### Supervisor round 38 — Caller ID provider-preset label + descriptions localized — COMPLETED

Closes round-37's provider-preset follow-up (supervisor independent grep flagged
`CallerIdSection.tsx:58 label: 'Generic SIP Europe'` as visible in the Settings > Devices provider
selector). Scoped to `CallerIdSection.tsx` + the 5 locale files. Palette/no-hover work from round 37
preserved (no styling changes).

- **Keys stored on each preset, rendered via `t()`** (the supervisor's endorsed option). Extended
  `ProviderPreset` with `labelKey?` + `descriptionKey`; the selector option renders
  `preset.labelKey ? t(preset.labelKey, preset.label) : preset.label` and the description renders
  `t(selectedPreset.descriptionKey, selectedPreset.description)`. The English literals stay only as the
  `t()` fallback args (same accepted pattern as every other `t(key, 'English')` in the codebase).
- **Generic preset label localized; brand labels kept.** `genericEurope` gets a `labelKey` →
  "Γενικό SIP Ευρώπης" / "Generisches SIP Europa" / "SIP générique Europe" / "SIP generico Europa"
  (SIP kept as acronym; Generic/Europe translated). The brand presets (3CX, FreePBX/Asterisk, Yeastar)
  keep their literal label (proper nouns, no `labelKey`).
- **All four descriptions localized** under `settings.peripherals.callerId.presets.<id>.description` in
  all 5 locales (the description renders under the selector, so it was also visible English).
- **+5 keys per locale (5869 → 5874).**
- Verified: `npm run locale:parity` → **pass (5874 keys, 0 missing/extra/support errors)**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean**; focused grep
  `rg -n 'Generic SIP Europe|Generic SIP|Advanced / Legacy PBX|hover:|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|bg-cyan|text-cyan|border-cyan'`
  over CallerIdSection + el/de/fr/it → **0 matches in el/de/fr/it** (preset label + setup labels all
  translated); in CallerIdSection the only matches are the English `t()` fallbacks (line 62 preset label
  consumed by `t(labelKey, label)`, plus setup lines 518/537) — no hover/blue/cyan; `git diff --check`
  → clean.

### Supervisor round 39 — Printer Setup Wizard full localization — COMPLETED (closes printer follow-up)

Live QA found the Settings → Printer discovery wizard leaking English in Greek ("Needs review",
"Unknown", "Discovered only. Verify before using.", "No saved profile yet.", etc.). Root cause: a large
part of `PrinterSetupWizard.tsx`'s `settings.printer.*` keys (verification / transport / quick-wizard
step families) were **never added to any locale** — only the early detect-step keys existed, so the whole
verify/style/save flow rendered inline English fallbacks in every language. The supervisor flagged 9
keys; an audit of the file found the gap was much wider, so the whole wizard was localized to genuinely
close the follow-up.

- **+64 keys per locale (5874 → 5938)** under `settings.printer`, covering: `transport*` (4),
  `verification*` (4), `unknown`, `optional`, `testing`, and the full `quickWizard*` verify/style/save
  families (titles, hints, sample/confirm states, ready/summary text, saved-summary labels, and the
  `quickWizardReadability` object `{label,small,normal,large}`). Translated for en/el/de/fr/it; SIP / PBX
  / TCP / RFCOMM / Windows / ESC-POS kept as technical terms; printer/brand/COM/IP values stay data.
- **Two minimal code fixes in `PrinterSetupWizard.tsx`** (no styling change):
  - The discovered-brand display (`{candidate.detectedBrand}`) now renders
    `detectedBrand === 'Unknown' ? t('settings.printer.unknown','Unknown') : detectedBrand` — the
    `'Unknown'` **sentinel stays in data/logic** (set at the recommendation fallback; compared in
    `PrinterSettingsModal:2207`), only the *display* is localized.
  - `quickWizardReadability` was used both as a string label (`'Readability'`) and as a parent for
    `.small/.normal/.large` (dynamic key) — an i18n collision. Made it an object and pointed the label at
    `quickWizardReadability.label`, so both resolve cleanly.
- **Palette/touch untouched:** no `hover:`/`blue`/`cyan`/`purple` added (grep over both printer files →
  0); the round 9–21 glass/amber + no-hover work is intact. `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` needed no key
  changes (its shared printer keys already exist; the leaks were wizard-owned).
- Verified: `npm run locale:parity` → **pass (5938 keys, 0 missing/extra/support errors in all
  locales)**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; focused grep for the named English phrases over
  PrinterSetupWizard/PrinterSettingsModal + el/de/fr/it → **0 matches in el/de/fr/it** (only English
  `t()` inline fallbacks remain in the .tsx, plus the `'Unknown'` data sentinel in PrinterSettingsModal);
  grep `hover:|blue|cyan|purple` over both printer files → **0**; `git diff --check` → clean.

### Supervisor round 40 — Caller ID status/toast strings localized — COMPLETED (closes round-38/39 follow-up)

Closes the remaining Caller ID i18n follow-up noted in rounds 37–39: `CallerIdSection.tsx` still returned
visible English directly from `toReasonMessage()` and from three toast `catch` fallbacks, which could leak
in non-English UI. Scoped to `CallerIdSection.tsx` + the five locale files; no behavior/styling/layout
change.

- **`toReasonMessage(reason)` → `toReasonMessage(reason, t)`** (the module-level helper now takes `t`,
  mirroring `PrinterSetupWizard`'s `transportLabel`/`verificationLabel`). All seven branches
  (`auth_failed`, `timeout`, `unsupported_provider`, `port_in_use`, `invalid_config`, `network_error`,
  default) return `t('settings.peripherals.callerId.reason.<key>', '<English fallback>')`; both call sites
  pass `t`. Added `import type { TFunction } from 'i18next'` (type-only; no new dependency).
- **Three toast `catch` fallbacks** ("Connection test failed", "Failed to save Caller ID settings",
  "Failed to disable Caller ID") now use `settings.peripherals.callerId.testFailed` / `saveFailed` /
  `disableFailed`.
- **+10 keys per locale (5938 → 5948)** under `settings.peripherals.callerId`: a `reason` object (7 keys)
  + the 3 toast keys, translated in en/el/de/fr/it (SIP/POS/PBX/port/firewall/router kept as technical
  terms).
- Verified: `npm run locale:parity` → **pass (5948 keys, 0 missing/extra/support errors in all locales)**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean**; grep of the listed English phrases in `CallerIdSection.tsx` → all
  appear **only as `t(key, 'fallback')` args** (reason branches + toasts), no bare English left;
  `git diff --check` → clean.

### Supervisor round 41 — Order-type selector icons (pickup bag + table/chairs) — COMPLETED

Live POS review rejected both order-type chooser icons: the pickup hand/bag mark (the custom
`HandBagIcon`, which kept reading as an ambiguous hand) and the table crossed-utensils mark (read as
"food", not "table"). Scoped to the two choosers' icons only — no card colors, labels, translations,
modal layout, or order-taking workflow changed.

- **Pickup → lucide `ShoppingBag` only.** Removed the custom `HandBagIcon` component/file entirely (the
  "HandBag" name no longer exists) and replaced its usage with the stock lucide `ShoppingBag` — a clean,
  unambiguous bag (no hand, no hybrid). White, centered, `w-full h-full`, `strokeWidth 1.6`.
- **Table → new `TableOrderIcon`** (`components/icons/TableOrderIcon.tsx`): a top-down central rounded
  table with **four rounded-rectangle chair/seat blocks** (top/bottom/left/right), line-art (24×24
  viewBox, `currentColor`, `fill="none"`, round caps/joins). The seat blocks (an earlier single-line
  version read as a square with tick marks) make it read as "table with seating" rather than utensils.
  White, centered, sized to fill the viewBox to match the pickup bag at `strokeWidth 1.6`.
- **Both choosers wired consistently** — `OrderDashboard.tsx` and `OrderFlow.tsx` import the same shared
  components; the unrelated floor-plan `UtensilsCrossed` (OrderDashboard "no tables configured" empty
  state) was deliberately left untouched. Pickup card stays green, table card stays blue; no hover added
  (existing `active:scale` tap feedback kept).
- **Live POS check (Greek / light theme):** the **Παραλαβή** card shows the white bag and the **Τραπέζι**
  card shows the white table+chairs, both aligned and matched in size.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` → clean for the touched files.

### Supervisor round 42 — Z Report / close-day hover purge + palette cleanup — COMPLETED

Live review: the Z Report / close-day flow is readable and glassy but still violated the touchscreen
no-hover rule and carried decorative cyan/blue accents outside the base palette. Scoped to
`modals/ZReportModal.tsx` and `modals/StaffShiftCheckoutFooterActions.tsx` — no business logic, data
loading, submission, printing, exporting, filters, date handling, or modal layout changed; blurry glass
+ rounded containers preserved; buttons stay icon+text centered.

- **Hover purge (both files).** Removed every Tailwind hover utility and replaced with tap/active
  feedback: footer print button `hover:border/​bg` → `active:` (light + `dark:active:`); footer checkout
  button `hover:from/to/shadow` → `active:from-red-700 active:to-red-800 active:scale-[0.98]` (stays red);
  ZReport `glassControlClass` `hover:bg-white/[0.18]` → `active:`; the two order/payment filter rows'
  `hover:bg-white/[0.1]` → `active:`; the Submit-report button `hover:bg-emerald-500` → `active:`.
- **Palette re-theme (ZReportModal cyan/blue → app palette).** Card/payment "card sales" accents
  `text-blue-600/300` → amber (matches the cash accent). Cash-movement amounts (driver returned / cash to
  return) `text-cyan-600/300` → neutral slate. Role + drawer-status badges: the `cyan` (server/waiter,
  drawer-active) and the adjacent decorative `indigo` (driver) → neutral slate glass (cashier/manager keep
  amber; reconciled/closed/active-shift keep their existing emerald/slate; needs-attention keeps amber).
  Stat-card icons `text-cyan-500` → the neutral `softTextClass` (consistent with the money-card icons);
  pending-checklist `RefreshCw` `text-cyan-500` → neutral slate. Order-type filter selected state
  `bg-blue-600` → `bg-amber-500 text-black` (app selected accent); the payment-method filter's selected
  `emerald` was left as-is (allowed). Submit-result note box `cyan` → emerald (admin-ready confirmation).
  Out-of-scope semantic colors (emerald success, rose/red destructive, amber warnings, orange given-cash)
  were not chased.
- Verified: `rg -n "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|bg-cyan|text-cyan|border-cyan|ring-blue|ring-cyan"`
  over both files → **0 matches**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` → clean.

Live review: the payment, split-payment, single-collection, void/refund, edit-refund-settlement,
edit-settlement-delta, and ECR payment dialogs still carried desktop `hover:` utilities and decorative
blue/cyan/purple/sky accents that read as non-semantic on a touchscreen POS. Scoped to
`modals/PaymentModal.tsx`, `modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx`, `modals/SinglePaymentCollectionModal.tsx`,
`modals/RefundVoidModal.tsx`, `modals/EditOrderRefundSettlementModal.tsx`,
`modals/EditSettlementDeltaModal.tsx`, and `ecr/PaymentDialog.tsx` — no payment/refund business logic,
handlers, amount calculations, labels, translations, IPC calls, submit/confirm behavior, or modal
open/close behavior changed; blurry glass + rounded containers preserved; buttons stay icon+text centered.

- **Hover purge (all 7 files).** Removed every Tailwind hover utility and replaced with tap/active
  feedback: method-card gradients → `active:scale-[0.98]`; confirm/cash/collect buttons
  `hover:bg-emerald-*` → `active:`; void/refund buttons `hover:bg-red-*/orange-*` → `active:` (semantics
  kept); white/grey neutral buttons `hover:bg-white/10` / `hover:bg-gray-700` → `active:`; the
  SplitPayment confirm `hover:scale-[1.02] hover:bg-emerald-600/30` → `active:bg-emerald-600/30
  active:scale-[0.98]`; ECR close/retry/done and adjustment-history toggle `hover:` → `active:`.
- **Palette re-theme (blue/cyan/purple/sky → app palette).** Non-semantic payment accents off the base
  palette were neutralized: PaymentModal card method (blue) and split method (purple) → slate (cash stays
  green, room-charge stays amber); SplitPayment card/method/info-box blue → slate; SinglePaymentCollection
  card icon `text-sky-300` → slate; RefundVoid card method icon + Close button (blue) → slate and the
  fetch spinner `border-blue-500` → `border-amber-500` (processing accent); ECR PaymentDialog processing
  icon/label `text-blue-400` → amber and the Retry button (blue) → slate. Semantic colors were left as-is:
  emerald/green success/paid/collect, red/rose void/destructive, amber/orange refund/warning/adjustment.
- Verified: `rg -n "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|bg-cyan|text-cyan|border-cyan|ring-blue|ring-cyan|bg-purple|text-purple|border-purple|ring-purple|from-blue|to-blue|from-cyan|to-cyan|from-purple|to-purple"`
  over all 7 files → **0 matches**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` → clean.

### Supervisor round 44 — Dine-in / table order icon redesign (elevation view) — COMPLETED

Live review: the custom top-down (plan-view) table/chairs glyph still read too abstract at the rendered
64px size in the order-type chooser. Scoped to `icons/TableOrderIcon.tsx` only — no behavior, layout, or
caller props changed; the consuming `OrderDashboard.tsx` / `OrderFlow.tsx` usages already pass
`className="w-full h-full text-white"` + `strokeWidth={1.6}` and were left untouched (table card icon
stays white, the title stays blue, the Greek label stays `Τραπέζι`). Pickup `ShoppingBag` unchanged.

- **Redraw as an elevation view.** Replaced the top-down table + four chair blocks with a front-elevation
  dining table flanked by a side-profile chair on each side (two diners across a table) — the universal
  "dine-in" read. Each chair is a real chair silhouette (back post floor→top, seat, front leg floor→seat);
  the table is a tabletop + two legs between them. Not crossed utensils, not cake.
- **Same icon contract.** Kept the lucide line style: `24x24` viewBox, `currentColor` stroke, `fill="none"`,
  round caps/joins, default `strokeWidth={1.6}`. The drawing fills roughly the same footprint (~x3–21,
  y3–21) as the neighbouring `ShoppingBag`, so the two order-type icons stay visually equal in size.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` over the icon + both consumers + this
  tracker → clean.

### Supervisor round 45 — Dine-in / table order icon redo (top-down + plate) — COMPLETED (supersedes round 44)

Live visual QA rejected the round-44 side-elevation glyph: at the real order-type card size it read as
vertical bars, not a dining table. Redrawn to a **top-down restaurant table** per the approved direction.
Scoped to `icons/TableOrderIcon.tsx` only — pickup `ShoppingBag`, text, colors, layout, order-flow
behavior, and translations untouched; table card icon stays white, the title stays blue, the Greek label
stays `Τραπέζι`.

- **Top-down plan view.** A rounded square table (`rect 7,7 10x10 rx2.3`), a small centre plate/ring
  (`circle 12,12 r2`) so it reads as dining/table service, and four clear seat blocks around it
  (top `8,2.75`, bottom `8,18`, left `2.75,8`, right `18,8` — each `8`-long, `3.25`-thick, `rx1.4`). All
  within the 24x24 viewBox with even margins.
- **Stroke weight.** Same lucide line contract (`24x24` viewBox, `currentColor`, `fill="none"`, round
  caps/joins). The default is `1.8`, but because both consumers (`OrderDashboard.tsx`, `OrderFlow.tsx`)
  pass `strokeWidth={1.6}` — which a default cannot override — the effective stroke is floored at `1.75`
  via `Math.max(strokeWidth, 1.75)` so the glyph matches the pickup `ShoppingBag` weight at 64px without
  touching the (out-of-scope) call sites. An explicitly heavier value is still respected.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` over `icons/TableOrderIcon.tsx` + this
  tracker → clean.

### Supervisor round 46 — Split payment internal scrollbar cleanup — COMPLETED

Live QA on the running POS: the split-payment by-amount tab showed a thick native white vertical scrollbar
on the right of the internal scroll pane, clashing with the blurry glass modal and violating the
scrollbar requirement. Scoped to `modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx` only — no payment logic, confirm
behavior, translations, colors, totals, method buttons, or layout structure changed; no hover styles
added.

  `index.css` / `renderer/styles/globals.css` / `renderer/styles/glassmorphism.css` as
  `scrollbar-width: none` + `::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none }`, already used across 20+ POS
  components) to the three internal `overflow-y-auto` panes — scrolling still works, the native bar is
  gone:
  - `renderByAmountTab` people list: `max-h-[380px] space-y-3 overflow-y-auto pr-1` → `… pr-1 scrollbar-hide`.
  - `renderByItemsTab` order-items column: `space-y-2 overflow-y-auto pr-1` → `… pr-1 scrollbar-hide`.
  - `renderByItemsTab` people column: `space-y-2 overflow-y-auto pr-1` → `… pr-1 scrollbar-hide`.
- Verified: `rg -o "overflow-y-auto[^\"]*"` over the file → all three panes now end with `scrollbar-hide`;
  `hover:` count in the file → **0**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` over
  `modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx` + this tracker → clean. (The optional `rg -nP` lookahead form was not
  runnable — this ripgrep build lacks PCRE2 — so the panes were confirmed manually as above.)

### Supervisor round 48 — Orders bulk action bar palette/touch cleanup — COMPLETED

Live QA on the running POS Orders dashboard (Greek view, selected order #00001): the selected-order bulk
action bar showed a blue selected-count circle, purple/indigo Edit/View buttons, and desktop hover-scale
styling — off the white/black/grey/yellow base palette and violating the touchscreen no-hover rule. (This
is the bulk action bar, not the protected order tab/navbar neon area.) Scoped to `BulkActionsBar.tsx`
only — OrderDashboard, order-taking flow, translations, handlers, and business logic untouched.

- **Hover purge.** `getButtonStyles` baseStyles dropped `hover:scale-105` (kept `active:scale-95` tap
  feedback); every variant's `hover:bg-*` → `active:bg-*`. No `dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` were present.
- **Off-palette variants re-themed.** `primary` (assign/driver) blue → neutral filled slate; `info`
  (edit + view) indigo → neutral slate glass (edit-neutral is allowed and avoids a double-amber clash
  next to `delivered`); `map` teal → neutral slate glass. Semantic variants kept: `warning` (delivered)
  amber, `danger` (cancel) red, `success` emerald, `secondary`/`neutral` slate — hover→active only. All
  `shadow-blue/indigo/teal` and `ring-blue/indigo/teal` likewise neutralized.
- **Selected-count circle** blue (`bg-blue-50/80 border-blue-500/60 text-blue-700` / dark
  `bg-blue-500/10 …`) → neutral slate. **Progress indicator** `bg-blue-500` → `bg-amber-500`.
- **Smooth rounded + centered.** Buttons `rounded-lg` → `rounded-xl`; baseStyles gained `justify-center`
  so the new icon+label pairs stay centered.
- **Mojibake icons / manual SVG → lucide-react.** Removed the dead `ActionConfig.icon` string field
  (source of the `✎ ✕ ↶ 🚗 🗺️` mojibake) and gave the rendered buttons real lucide icons: assign/driver
  `User`, delivered `CheckCircle`, edit `Pencil`, cancel `X`, map `Map` (imported `as MapIcon` to avoid
  shadowing the JS global), view `Eye`; the clear button's hand-rolled `<svg>` X-path → lucide `X`. Labels
  and behavior unchanged.
- Verified: `rg -n "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|ring-blue|shadow-blue|bg-indigo|text-indigo|border-indigo|ring-indigo|shadow-indigo|bg-teal|text-teal|border-teal|ring-teal|shadow-teal|bg-purple|text-purple|border-purple|ring-purple|shadow-purple"`
  over the file → **0 matches**; `Grep` for residual mojibake/`<svg>` → **0**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `git diff --check` over `BulkActionsBar.tsx` + this tracker → clean.

### Supervisor round 49 — Dine-in / table order icon redo (side view, table-for-two) — COMPLETED (supersedes round 45)

navbar/tabs neon, and unrelated icons untouched; the Greek label stays `Τραπέζι` and the table card title

  anchor, flanked by a chair on each side facing the table. Each chair has a **leaning (angled) backrest**
  (`M2.5 3.5 4 13 …` / mirrored) plus a seat and two legs — the slant distinguishes the backrests from the
  vertical legs so the glyph no longer collapses into the "vertical bars" look that got round 44 rejected.
  Balanced/symmetric about x=12, filling ~x2.5–21.5 / y3.5–20.5 to match the pickup `ShoppingBag` footprint.

  round caps/joins. Stroke default `1.8` with the `Math.max(strokeWidth, 1.75)` floor kept, so the weight
  matches the pickup bag at 64px even though the call sites pass `strokeWidth={1.6}`.

### Supervisor round 58 — Remove native Windows title/menu chrome from the POS shell — COMPLETED

Live visual QA: the running POS window still showed native desktop chrome - a Windows titlebar and a
File/Edit/View/Window/Help menu row - above the app, which is unacceptable for a touchscreen POS that
already has custom in-app controls. `tauri.conf.json` already sets `decorations: false`, but config alone
does not strip a window menu (decorations and menu are separate window attributes in Tauri 2), so the
shell contract is now also enforced at runtime. Scoped to `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` only - no renderer,
order flow, auth, payments, printing, sync, or customer-display changes.

- **Runtime shell-chrome enforcement in `setup()`.** At the top of the Tauri `setup` hook (desktop-gated),
  best-effort cleanup of the main window: `app.remove_menu()` (drops any app-level/default menu),
  `main_window.remove_menu()` (drops the window menu), and `main_window.set_decorations(false)`
  (re-asserts no native titlebar on the live/debug window). The window is resolved via
  `app.get_webview_window("main")` (default label - the config window has no explicit `label`).
- **Non-fatal.** Each step logs a `warn!` on failure and never aborts startup; if the main window is
  missing, it warns and continues. No `?`/early-return added to the setup path.
- **Preserved.** Custom titlebar/window commands (`commands::system_ui::window_*`), the external/customer
  display windows (which set their own `decorations(false)` in `commands/system_ui.rs`), and the unrelated
  business `menu::sync_menu` path are all untouched. `tauri.conf.json` was not modified.
- Verified: `cargo check --manifest-path pos-tauri/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` -> **clean** (exit 0, APIs
  compile); `npm run type-check` -> **clean**; `git diff --check` over `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` + this
  tracker -> clean; `rg` for `remove_menu|hide_menu|set_decorations|decorations|menu(` confirms the
  runtime enforcement is present and config `decorations: false` is intact.

### Supervisor round 60 — Native Windows menu/titlebar still returned after webview load — COMPLETED (follow-up to round 58)

Live QA after a clean `cargo build` + relaunch: the initial blank window had no chrome, but once the login
UI loaded, the native File/Edit/View/Window/Help menu row and the OS titlebar reappeared. The round-58
one-shot `setup()` cleanup (`app.remove_menu()` + `main_window.remove_menu()` + `set_decorations(false)`)
was therefore insufficient.

- **Root cause.** The renderer never touches decorations/menu (rg over `src/renderer` for
  `setDecorations|decorations|getCurrentW|appWindow|set_menu|.menu(` -> 0 matches) and
  `commands/system_ui.rs` only builds the *external* display window (its own `decorations(false)`), so
  nothing in app code re-enables chrome. It is the Tauri framework re-applying its **default** menu as the
  webview finishes loading. `setup()` runs too early - before the first navigation completes - so removing
  the menu there loses the race and the default re-attaches after the login page renders; on Windows the
  menu also forces a hosting titlebar, which is why both came back together.
- **Fix (scoped to `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`).** Install an explicit EMPTY app menu at the builder lifecycle
  point - `tauri::Builder::default().plugin(...).menu(|handle| tauri::menu::Menu::new(handle))` - so Tauri
  never generates its default menu and there is nothing to re-attach after load. On Windows an empty menu
  yields no menu bar (and no menu-induced titlebar). The round-58 `setup()` cleanup (`remove_menu` +
  `set_decorations(false)`) is kept as defense-in-depth. `tauri.conf.json` `decorations: false` is
  unchanged.
- **Preserved.** Custom in-app titlebar/window commands (`commands::system_ui::window_*`), the
  external/customer display window (`commands/system_ui.rs`, its own `decorations(false)`), order flow,
  auth, payments, printing, sync, and business logic - all untouched. No renderer changes.
- Verified: `cargo check --manifest-path pos-tauri/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` -> **clean** (exit 0, the
  `Builder::menu` / `Menu::new` API compiles); `npm run type-check` -> **clean**; `git diff --check` over
  `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` + this tracker -> clean; `rg` confirms `.menu(|handle| ... Menu::new ...)` at the
  builder plus the retained `setup()` cleanup and config `decorations: false`. Final confirmation requires
  a live relaunch of the rebuilt `the-small-pos.exe` (cannot be verified from static checks alone).

### Supervisor round 61 — Native Windows menu/titlebar STILL showed after round-60 rebuild — COMPLETED (corrects round 60)

Live QA of the round-60 rebuild still showed the native File/Edit/View/Window/Help row plus the OS
titlebar on the login screen. Round 60's empty-menu sentinel + retained `setup()` cleanup was the wrong
shape. I inspected the actual Tauri 2.10.2 / muda 0.17.1 source in `~/.cargo/registry` (not assumptions)
to find the real lifecycle.

- **Corrected root cause (verified from dependency source).** Three facts settle it:
  1. Config-defined windows (the `main` window) are built **inside** Tauri's internal `setup()` *before*
     the user `.setup()` closure runs (`tauri-2.10.2/src/app.rs` `fn setup`: it loops
     `config.app.windows` building each, *then* calls the user closure). A window created without its own
     menu inherits the app-wide menu (`src/window/mod.rs:365-369`: `is_app_wide = self.menu.is_none()`,
     fall back to `app_handle().menu()`).
  2. On Windows, muda attaches menus with the **native** `SetMenu`/`DrawMenuBar` Win32 calls
     (`muda-0.17.1/src/platform_impl/windows/mod.rs:349-356`); `window.remove_menu()` schedules the
     `SetMenu(hwnd, null)` removal as a **deferred** `run_on_main_thread` closure that runs only after the
     setup closure returns. That is the race round 60 still lost.
  3. Tauri auto-creates the default File/Edit/View/Window/Help menu **only on macOS**
     (`src/app.rs:2095-2100`, `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]`). On Windows the default is never
     auto-generated. So round 60's empty `Menu::new()` was itself the menu being attached (then raced for
     removal), and any path that left an app-wide menu non-`None` produced a native `SetMenu` on the
     borderless window.
- **Fix (scoped to `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`).** Prevent the menu at the source instead of racing cleanup:
  - Removed `.menu(|handle| tauri::menu::Menu::new(handle))` and added `.enable_macos_default_menu(false)`.
    With no `.menu(...)` call and the macOS default disabled, `AppHandle::menu()` stays `None`, so every
    window — the `main` window **and** the runtime-created customer/external display window in
    `commands::system_ui::display_open_window`, which also inherits the app menu — is created with `None`
    for its menu. muda's `SetMenu` is therefore never invoked: no native menu bar, and no menu-hosting
    titlebar, can exist to be removed.
  - Removed the round-58/60 racing cleanup (`app.remove_menu()` + `main_window.remove_menu()`) from
    `setup()`. Kept a single idempotent `main_window.set_decorations(false)` re-assertion (best-effort,
    warn-only) so the borderless contract is actively enforced on the live/debug window in addition to
    `tauri.conf.json` `decorations: false` (unchanged, source of truth).
- **Preserved.** Custom in-app titlebar/window commands (`commands::system_ui::window_*`), the
  external/customer display window (its own `decorations(false)`), order flow, auth, payments, printing,
  sync, translations, icons, and business logic — all untouched. No renderer changes.
- **Exact files.** `pos-tauri/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` (builder + `setup()` shell block) and this tracker.
- Verified: `cargo check --manifest-path pos-tauri/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` -> **clean**;
  `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean**; `git diff --check` over the touched files -> clean;
  `rg` confirms `.enable_macos_default_menu(false)` at the builder, no remaining `.menu(`/`remove_menu`
  calls, the retained single `set_decorations(false)`, and config `decorations: false`. Final sign-off
  still needs a live relaunch of a freshly rebuilt `the-small-pos.exe` (ensure the prior process is fully
  stopped so the new exe is not a stale copy — the persistence across rounds is consistent with a locked
  exe receiving the old binary).

### Supervisor round 62 — The File/Edit/View/Window/Help row was renderer UI (CustomTitleBar), not native chrome — COMPLETED (corrects rounds 58-61)

Rounds 58-61 all targeted **native** Tauri/muda chrome (default app menu + `decorations`). Fresh live QA
after round 61 still showed the row, and the supervisor isolated the real source: the row is the React
`CustomTitleBar` component, rendered by `FullscreenAwareLayout`. The decisive evidence — the WebView
localhost error page had **no** row; the row appeared only once the React login UI mounted — proves it is
app-rendered, not OS/Tauri native chrome. The round 58-61 native-shell work was chasing the wrong layer.

- **Root cause (renderer).** `App.tsx` wrapped every screen in `FullscreenAwareLayout`, which
  unconditionally rendered `<CustomTitleBar />` and reserved a `pt-8` top strip. `CustomTitleBar.tsx`
  (842 lines) is an Electron-era desktop window chrome reimplemented in React: a File/Edit/View/Window/
  Help dropdown row, `-webkit-app-region: drag`, Windows min/max/close buttons, and many `hover:*` desktop
  states. On a borderless touchscreen POS that row is exactly the unwanted "native-looking" menu/titlebar.
- **Fix (renderer-only, exact files):**
  - `src/renderer/components/FullscreenAwareLayout.tsx` — reduced to a pure layout wrapper. Removed the
    `CustomTitleBar` import + render, the `useWindowState`/`isFullScreen` padding logic, and the `pt-8`
    gap. Content now fills the borderless window from the top edge. Kept `children`/`className` so the
    three call sites need no structural change.
  - `src/renderer/App.tsx` — removed the (already dead) `import CustomTitleBar`, and stripped the
    titlebar-only props (`updateAvailable` / `onCheckForUpdates` / `onOpenSettings`) from both
    `FullscreenAwareLayout` call sites (login + main). All three call sites are now prop-less
    `<FullscreenAwareLayout>`.
  - `src/renderer/components/index.ts` — removed the unused `CustomTitleBar` barrel export (no consumer).
  - Deleted `src/renderer/components/CustomTitleBar.tsx` (now referenced nowhere; git history retains it).
- **Behavior preserved.** Login, routing, order dashboard/order-taking, modals, shutdown/restart overlay,
  update state (the `autoUpdater`-driven `UpdateDialog` is mounted independently of the titlebar), and the
  settings/recovery modals all still work. In the main POS flow, connection settings/recovery are reached
  via `RefactoredMainLayout` `onOpenConnectionSettings` (sidebar/routes) and `SyncRecoveryModal`; terminal
  reset remains available via `ConnectionSettingsModal` (factory reset) and the RecoveryPanel. No
  order-taking internals, business logic, auth, payments, printing, sync, translations, or table/pickup
  icons were touched.
- **Adjacent behavior changes (consequence of removing the bar — flagged for the founder):**
  1. The **login screen loses its only visible settings entry** (the bar's File→Settings). The
     `ConnectionSettingsModal` is still mounted on login and can be opened programmatically via the
     `pos:recovery-route` custom event, but there is no on-screen login button. If desired, a small
     touch-friendly settings affordance on login is a clean follow-up (not desktop hover chrome).
  2. The bar's **global custom edit shortcuts** (its own Ctrl+Z/Y/X/C/V/A handler + undo/redo stack) and
     its `pos:edit-command` dispatch are gone. The `pos:edit-command` event had **no listeners**, and
     WebView2 provides native Ctrl+C/V/X/Z/A editing inside inputs, so standard text-field editing is
     unaffected.
- **Rust shell (round 61) retained.** `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` (`enable_macos_default_menu(false)`, no app
  menu, single `set_decorations(false)` re-assertion) is left in place as correct native hardening — it
  was never harmful, just aimed at the wrong layer for this symptom. No Rust touched this round.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  the touched files -> clean; `rg` confirms zero `CustomTitleBar` references in code (only a history note
  in the layout comment), `FullscreenAwareLayout` renders no titlebar and no `pt-8`, and `App.tsx` no
  longer imports `CustomTitleBar`. Rust untouched, so `cargo check` was not re-run this round (round-61
  state already verified clean). Final visual sign-off still needs a live relaunch of a freshly rebuilt
  renderer (`npm run dev` / rebuilt exe).

### Supervisor round 63 — Touch-friendly pre-login Settings/Recovery affordance — COMPLETED (follow-up to round 62)

Round 62 removed `CustomTitleBar`, which also removed the only visible pre-login entry to connection/
settings recovery (the old File -> Settings). Login still had a programmatic path (`pos:recovery-route`
event), but staff/owner need an on-screen way to fix broken terminal config/auth **before** login. This
round adds that affordance — no desktop chrome reintroduced.

- **Renderer fix (exact files):**
  - `src/renderer/pages/LoginPage.tsx` — added an optional `onOpenSettings?: () => void` prop and a small
    touch-first button in the existing top-right control cluster (now a `flex items-center gap-2` row next
    to `ThemeToggle`). The button is icon-only (`lucide` `Settings`), 44x44 (`h-11 w-11`, meets touch
    minimum), `rounded-2xl` glass (`backdrop-blur-md` + translucent bg/border), with **tap feedback only**
    (`active:scale-95` + `active:bg-*`) and **no hover-dependent effects**. It renders only when
    `onOpenSettings` is provided and opens the existing `ConnectionSettingsModal`. `aria-label` + `title`
    use i18n.
  - `src/renderer/App.tsx` — login render site now passes
    `onOpenSettings={() => openConnectionSettings()}` (same `openConnectionSettings` callback already used
    by the main POS flow), so the button drives the existing modal that is already mounted in the login
    branch.
  - i18n: added `login.connectionSettings` to all five locales — `en` "Connection settings",
    `el` "Ρυθμίσεις σύνδεσης", `de` "Verbindungseinstellungen", `it` "Impostazioni di connessione",
    `fr` "Paramètres de connexion". (Key added to the authoritative second `login` block — the one with
    `loginButton`/`footer: "The Small POS"` that `LoginPage` actually resolves against; the legacy first
    `login` block "Staff Login" is unused dead weight, left untouched as out-of-scope cleanup.)
- **Preserved.** Login PIN logic, auth, the yellow orb `AnimatedBackground`, `ThemeToggle` behavior,
  order dashboard/order-taking, payments, printing, sync, and unrelated translations — all untouched. No
  `CustomTitleBar` reintroduced; no `pt-8` reintroduced in `FullscreenAwareLayout`.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); all five locale JSON
  files parse and expose `login.connectionSettings`; `git diff --check` over touched files -> clean; `rg`
  confirms the `onOpenSettings` prop + 44x44 `Settings` button + i18n `aria-label`/`title` in
  `LoginPage`, the `onOpenSettings={() => openConnectionSettings()}` call site in `App.tsx`, zero
  `CustomTitleBar` imports, and no `pt-8`/titlebar in `FullscreenAwareLayout`. Final visual sign-off
  still needs a live relaunch (`npm run dev` / rebuilt exe): button visible top-right on login next to the
  theme toggle, tap opens connection settings, content still flush to the top.

### Supervisor round 64 — Pre-login settings gear opened the printer wizard instead of Connection Settings — COMPLETED (fixes round 63 regression)

Live QA of the round-63 gear: it correctly called `App.openConnectionSettings()`, but
`ConnectionSettingsModal` auto-runs `maybeOpenPrinterQuickSetup` on every open. With no receipt printer
configured (typical pre-login / fresh terminal), that effect immediately opened
`PrinterSettingsModal`/`PrinterSetupWizard`, so the user saw **Printer Settings** instead of **Connection
Settings** — making the new gear feel broken.

- **Fix (exact files):**
  - `src/renderer/components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` — added `enablePrinterOnboarding?: boolean`
    to `Props` (defaults to `true`, so existing call sites are unchanged). The printer quick-setup effect
    (the `maybeOpenPrinterQuickSetup` block, ~line 359) now early-returns when the flag is `false`, and the
    flag was added to that effect's dependency array. No other behavior changed.
  - `src/renderer/App.tsx` — the **pre-login (no-user) `ConnectionSettingsModal` instance only** now passes
    `enablePrinterOnboarding={false}`. The logged-in instance is untouched (omits the prop → defaults to
    `true`), so the post-login printer onboarding flow is fully preserved.
- **Result.** Pre-login, the gear opens the modal on its default `activeSettingsSection = 'admin'` hub (the
  Connection Settings admin/hub view) and no longer auto-jumps into the printer wizard. The only remaining
  `setShowPrinterSettingsModal(true)` reachable pre-login is the explicit user-initiated button inside the
  hardware section (line ~2112), which is correct.
- **Preserved.** Yellow orb login `AnimatedBackground`, no `CustomTitleBar` restored, order-taking/dashboard
  order page untouched, and the Flaticon table icon work + attribution untouched (diff is limited to
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` + `App.tsx` + this tracker). Logged-in printer onboarding behavior
  unchanged.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  touched files -> clean; `rg` confirms the modal gates the effect on `enablePrinterOnboarding` (default
  `true`, early-return when false, in the dep array) and that **exactly one** `enablePrinterOnboarding={false}`
  exists in `App.tsx` — on the pre-login instance (line ~1366) — while the logged-in instance (line ~1449)
  carries no such prop. Final visual sign-off needs a live relaunch: pre-login gear opens Connection
  Settings (admin hub), not Printer Settings.

### Supervisor round 65 — Table order icon redraw to match the Flaticon reference (fuller dining set) — COMPLETED (supersedes round 59)

Live QA of the order-type chooser: the table card no longer looked like a cake (good), but the round-59
`TableOrderIcon` was too reduced/thin — it read like vertical bars/letters next to the Greek label. The
user re-supplied the exact Flaticon reference for the table icon:
- Page: https://www.flaticon.com/free-icon/chair_1012472 (id 1012472, by Freepik)
- PNG: https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1012/1012472.png

- **Fix (`src/renderer/components/icons/TableOrderIcon.tsx` only).** Redrew the glyph as a fuller, anchored
  silhouette that matches the reference: two high-backed dining chairs (tall backs leaning slightly
  outward, sweeping into seats, with splayed trapezoid legs + foot rails) flanking a central pedestal
  table (rounded tabletop bar, vertical column, flared trapezoid foot). The previous version was just
  vertical line segments, which the eye couldn't bind into furniture; the anchored back+seat L-shapes and
  leg trapezoids now read unambiguously as a dining set.
- **Constraints honoured.** Still an inline SVG using `currentColor` (no remote asset, no raster, no
  external load), 24x24 viewBox, lucide line-art style (round caps/joins, `fill="none"`) compatible with
  the neighbouring pickup `ShoppingBag`. Stroke floor lowered 1.75 -> 1.7 (icon-only) so it sits closer to
  the pickup's passed `strokeWidth={1.6}` while staying crisp at 64px; an explicitly heavier value is still
  respected. The Freepik/Flaticon attribution comment (incl. id 1012472 + PNG URL) is preserved in the
  file header.
- **Self-verified visually.** Rasterised the new paths with Playwright (system Edge) at 64px on a dark card
  (the live context), 64px on white, and a large 170px size, side-by-side against the Flaticon PNG. The
  64px live render is clearly two chairs + a pedestal table — no longer bars/letters. (Throwaway render
  script only; nothing added to the repo.)
- **Untouched.** Order-flow behaviour, translations, order-modal layout, navbar, tab colours, login
  background, `CustomTitleBar` (still absent), and settings. Diff is limited to `TableOrderIcon.tsx` + this
  tracker. Both call sites (`OrderDashboard.tsx`, `OrderFlow.tsx`) still import `TableOrderIcon`
  unchanged.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `TableOrderIcon.tsx` + this tracker -> clean; `rg` confirms the `flaticon`/`1012472` attribution in the
  icon and that `OrderDashboard.tsx` + `OrderFlow.tsx` still import `TableOrderIcon`. Final visual sign-off
  still needs a live relaunch of the order-type chooser.
  - Follow-up correction: the header comment originally used a non-ASCII em dash (`rg '[^\x00-\x7F]'` hit
    line 10); replaced with an ASCII hyphen. `TableOrderIcon.tsx` is now ASCII-only (`rg '[^\x00-\x7F]'`
    -> 0 matches), SVG geometry unchanged, `git diff --check` re-run clean.

### Supervisor round 66 — Remove remaining hover-dependent styling from OrderDashboard tables/floor-plan UI — COMPLETED

Touchscreen-first audit: `rg "hover:|group-hover:" OrderDashboard.tsx` still found desktop hover effects in
the tables/floor-plan UI (table status card styles, view/status/floor filter buttons, table cards and their
action buttons). Removed all of them, replacing feedback with touch-appropriate `active:` states where
useful; at-rest meaning is preserved.

- **Fix (`src/renderer/components/OrderDashboard.tsx` only):**
  - **Table status card styles** (`tableStatusConfig[*].card`, 6 statuses x light/dark): dropped
    `hover:border-*` / `hover:shadow-*`. The at-rest semantic border/bg (emerald=available, blue=occupied,
    amber=reserved, slate=cleaning/unavailable, orange=maintenance) is unchanged.
  - **View toggle (List/2D), status filter, and floor filter buttons:** inactive `hover:bg-*` overlays
    converted to `active:bg-*` so taps still flash feedback. Selected buttons keep `bg-blue-600 text-white`;
    inactive buttons stay grey at rest.
  - **Table card:** removed `group` and `hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-xl`; added a subtle
    `active:scale-[0.99]` press response. Existing `focus:ring` (keyboard) retained.
  - **Table action buttons** (Open check / New order / Pay / attention): `group-hover:bg-*` converted to the
    button's own `active:bg-*` (the correct touch translation — feedback on pressing the button, not on
    hovering the card). They already had `active:scale-95`.
- **Preserved.** Order behaviour, data fetching, table actions, order cards, translations, and the
  order-type chooser layout/icon (its three cards at lines ~6135/6170/6193 already used `active:scale-95`
  with no group-hover children, so they were left untouched; their inert `group` token is not a hover class
  and is out of scope). Navbar untouched. No business-logic changes.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `OrderDashboard.tsx` -> clean; `rg "hover:|group-hover:" OrderDashboard.tsx` -> **0 matches**. Final
  visual sign-off still needs a live relaunch of the tables/floor-plan view.

placeholder string lives under `roomsView.searchPlaceholder` — a different screen. The supervisor then

  - Coloured confirm buttons (check-in emerald, checkout amber, status blue, housekeeping purple):
    `hover:bg-*-600` -> `active:bg-*-600` (their `disabled:` states preserved).
  - `ActionButton` colour map (blue/purple/emerald/amber tint actions): `hover:bg-*-500/20` ->
    `active:bg-*-500/20`.
- **Preserved.** Room status colours, modal layouts, business logic (check-in/checkout/status/housekeeping
  mutations and queries untouched), and the order dashboard, order flow, navbar, order tabs, login
  background, settings, and table icon — none touched. Diff this round is limited to `RoomsView.tsx` + this

- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over

### Supervisor round 70 — Settings must not auto-open the Printer wizard — COMPLETED (extends round 64)

Live QA: from the logged-in POS, the sidebar Settings gear opened `ConnectionSettingsModal` but it then
immediately auto-opened `PrinterSettingsModal`/`PrinterSetupWizard` because no receipt printer was
configured — exactly the confusing "Settings gets lost in submodals" behaviour. Round 64 only disabled
this for the pre-login instance (via `enablePrinterOnboarding={false}`); the logged-in flow still
auto-opened. This round removes the auto-open for **all** Settings openings.

- **Fix (`src/renderer/components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** Removed the on-open
  `maybeOpenPrinterQuickSetup` effect entirely, so opening Settings never launches the printer wizard. With
  the effect gone the `enablePrinterOnboarding` prop was dead, so it was removed from `Props` and the
  component signature. A short comment now records that printer setup is reached deliberately from the
  Printer section, not on Settings open.
- **Preserved.** The Printer section's explicit "Configure Printer" button is unchanged: it still opens
  `PrinterSettingsModal` in quick mode with `autoStartWizard=false`. The
  `printerSettingsInitialMode`/`printerSettingsAutoStartWizard`/`showPrinterSettingsModal` state and the
  `PrinterSettingsModal` render are untouched. Pre-login Settings behaviour is unchanged (it already opened
  the Connection Settings hub; with the auto-open gone the prop is unnecessary).
- **`src/renderer/App.tsx`.** Removed the now-unnecessary `enablePrinterOnboarding={false}` from the
  pre-login `ConnectionSettingsModal` instance (no other App change). Net effect: Settings always opens to
  its hub/detail panel (default `activeSettingsSection = 'admin'`, or `database` when opened to recovery).
- **Untouched.** `PrinterSettingsModal`/`PrinterSetupWizard` internals, locale files, order dashboard/order
  flow/navbar/tabs/login background, table icon, printer CRUD/test logic, and business logic.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` + `App.tsx` + this tracker -> clean; `rg` finds no
  `maybeOpenPrinterQuickSetup` / `enablePrinterOnboarding` / stale auto-open wording in
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` or `App.tsx`; the only `setShowPrinterSettingsModal(true)` left is the
  explicit Configure-Printer button handler (no on-open effect); `git diff --name-only` shows only those
  three files. Final visual sign-off still needs a live relaunch (logged-in Settings opens to the hub, not
  Printer Settings).

### Supervisor round 71 — Printer Settings auto-receipt switch rows: amber/yellow glass (drop emerald) — COMPLETED

Live QA: in Settings > Printer > Configure Printer, the Automatic Receipt Actions rows still used the old
emerald look (emerald borders/text + bright green pill switch). "Enabled" here is a setting state, not a
semantic success state, so it should use the app's neutral/yellow glass language; the rest of
`PrinterSettingsModal` already moved to amber/neutral, making these rows the most inconsistent part.

- **Fix (`src/renderer/components/modals/PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`, `renderReceiptActionSwitchRow` only).**
  Restyled the enabled/disabled rows and the switch control:
  - Enabled row: emerald -> subtle amber glass (`border-amber-400/45 bg-amber-400/[0.07]`, inset highlight
    kept; not over-glowing). Disabled row: unchanged neutral grey/white glass (`border-white/10
    bg-white/[0.03]`).
  - Icon accent: `text-emerald-200` -> `text-amber-300`. Main label when enabled: now neutral readable
    `text-white` (was `text-emerald-50`); disabled stays `text-white/70`.
  - State label + AUTO chip when enabled: emerald -> amber (`text-amber-200/90`, chip
    `border-amber-300/35 text-amber-200/90`). Disabled neutral unchanged.
  - Switch: bright green pill -> premium switch with amber ON track (`border-amber-400 bg-amber-500`),
    neutral dark OFF track (`border-white/12 bg-black/25`, dropped a stray no-op `text-white/45`), white
    knob unchanged. Row now uses `transition-all` + `active:scale-[0.99]` for touch press feedback.
- **Behaviour preserved exactly.** Same `receiptActions` state, `handleReceiptActionToggle`, `role="switch"`,
  `aria-checked`, `aria-label`, label/state-label text, AUTO chip, and all translation keys. Icon, label,
  state label, AUTO chip and switch stay vertically centred (`items-center`); label keeps
  `whitespace-normal break-words` so Greek does not clip. No hover classes.
- **Untouched.** Locale files, printer discovery/save/test logic, `PrinterSetupWizard`,
  `ConnectionSettingsModal`, `App`, order dashboard/flow, navbar/tabs, login background, table icon.
  Semantic green/red elsewhere in the modal (status/success/error counts) is out of scope and unchanged.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"
  PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` -> **0 matches**; `rg "border-emerald|bg-emerald|text-emerald|bg-green-500"`
  inside `renderReceiptActionSwitchRow` -> **none** (any remaining emerald/green in the file is status-only,
  outside this function); `git diff --name-only` shows only `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` + this tracker. Final
  visual sign-off by supervisor in the live POS.

translated, but the collapsible title still read "Configured Terminals". `PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx` uses
`t('ecr.configuredTerminals', 'Configured Terminals')`, but the `ecr` block in the locale files had no
`configuredTerminals` key, so i18next fell back to the hardcoded English even in Greek mode.

- **i18n fix.** Added `ecr.configuredTerminals` to all five locales (the `ecr` block already existed in
  each; key was just missing): en "Configured Terminals", el "Ρυθμισμένα τερματικά",
  de "Konfigurierte Terminals", it "Terminali configurati", fr "Terminaux configurés". The key is used only
  by `PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx`, so it was kept under the existing `ecr.*` namespace the component
  already references (no component key move needed, no API churn). With the key now present in el.json the
  English fallback can no longer render in Greek.
- **Touch cleanup (`PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx`).** Removed the two header hover styles: the back button's
  `hover:opacity-80` -> `active:opacity-80` (kept `transition-opacity`), and the refresh button's
  `hover:bg-white/10` -> `active:bg-white/10` (kept `transition-colors` + `disabled:opacity-50`). No
  behaviour or layout change.
- **Untouched.** Printer flows, login, order page, table icon, terminal discovery/save/test logic, theme
  tokens, and the semantic online/offline green/red status pill in the header (out of scope).
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  the touched files -> clean; all five locale JSONs parse and expose `ecr.configuredTerminals`;
  `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:" PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx` -> **0 matches**; Greek now resolves
  `ecr.configuredTerminals` to "Ρυθμισμένα τερματικά" so "Configured Terminals" can no longer leak.

### Supervisor round 73 — Settings Screen & Sound theme-label polish (de-emoji + aria-label) — COMPLETED

Live QA (Settings > Οθόνη & Ήχος): the icon-only Lucide Sun/Moon/Monitor theme buttons look good, but the
underlying `modals.connectionSettings.light|dark|system` labels still carried emoji in some locales, so the
button `title`/screen-reader text read like "☀️ Φωτεινό". The icon buttons also set `title` but no
`aria-label`.

- **i18n fix.** Removed the leading emoji from `modals.connectionSettings.light|dark|system` in the three
  affected locales (en, el, it); de and fr were already clean and were left unchanged. Visible language
  names and all unrelated copy are untouched. Resulting labels: en Light/Dark/System,
  el Φωτεινό/Σκοτεινό/Σύστημα, de Hell/Dunkel/System, it Chiaro/Scuro/Sistema,
  fr Clair/Sombre/Systeme.
- **Accessibility fix (`ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** Added an explicit `aria-label` to each of the three
  icon-only theme buttons, using the same clean localized label as the existing `title`
  (`t('modals.connectionSettings.light|dark|system')`). Icon-only controls now expose a proper accessible
  name.
- **Untouched.** Theme button layout, selected styling, language buttons, switch styling, terminal
  preference behaviour, settings navigation, and business logic. No hover classes added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on
  touched files -> clean; all five locale JSONs parse; `rg "☀|🌙|🖥"` across the five locales -> **0
  matches**; `rg` in `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` shows the three theme buttons each carry both `title` and
  `aria-label`. Final visual sign-off by supervisor in the live POS.

### Supervisor round 74 — Devices / Caller ID Greek copy clarity (locale pass) — COMPLETED

Live QA (Settings > Συσκευές > Αναγνώριση Κλήσεων): layout fine and `CallerIdSection.tsx` had no hover
matches, but the Greek strings still leaked English technical fragments ("Legacy", "SIP username/password
authentication", "IP-trusted PBX ροή", "SIP server, username και password", "ISP router account", "SIP
credentials", "Caller ID"), making the area feel unfinished. Pure locale/copy pass.

- **Fix (`src/locales/el.json`, `settings.peripherals.callerId` only).** Rewrote the visible Greek strings
  to natural Greek, keeping necessary acronyms (SIP, PBX, IP, VoIP, POS) and brand names (3CX, Asterisk,
  Yeastar). Mappings applied: Legacy -> παλαιού τύπου, server -> διακομιστής, username -> όνομα χρήστη,
  password -> κωδικός, authentication -> έλεγχος ταυτότητας, credentials -> στοιχεία σύνδεσης,
  router/router account -> δρομολογητής / λογαριασμός δρομολογητή, and non-label "Caller ID" -> αναγνώριση
  κλήσεων. Keys touched: `authUsernamePlaceholder`, `routerUnsupportedTitle`, `routerUnsupported`,
  `unsupportedProvider`, `legacyInfoTitle`, `legacyInfoBody`, `reason.unsupportedProvider`, `reason.failed`,
  `saveFailed`, `disableFailed`, `setup.providerPresetHelp`, `setup.genericHelp`, `setup.legacyHelp`.
- **Meaning/behaviour preserved.** Same keys, same meaning; no service code, inputs, modes, validation,
  presets, or business logic changed. Technical SIP/PBX field labels (e.g. `outboundProxy` "Outbound Proxy")
  were kept to match provider documentation. (`reason.timeout` keeps "θύρα (port)" and "firewall" as
  in-scope-excluded standard terms; not in the avoidable-fragment list.)
- **Parity (EN/DE/IT/FR).** Audited only. They are already natural in their own languages with the same keys
  and meaning, and the Greek change does not alter meaning, so they were left unchanged. No Greek-only
  hardcoded component fix was made.
- **Component.** `CallerIdSection.tsx` not modified: the keys exist in el.json so the English `t()` fallbacks
  cannot render in Greek; no fallback needed cleaning. No hover classes added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on
  touched files -> clean; all five locale JSONs parse; a block-scoped `rg` of the Greek
  `settings.peripherals.callerId` for `Legacy|username|password|authentication|credentials|IP-trusted|router
  account|Caller ID|server` -> **0 matches**; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:" CallerIdSection.tsx` ->
  **0 matches**. Final visual sign-off by supervisor in the live POS.

### Supervisor round 75 — Table/dine-in icon now uses the actual Flaticon 1012472 artwork (local mask asset) — COMPLETED (supersedes the round-69 approximation)

Founder: "Use this icon for tables !!!" Reference: https://www.flaticon.com/free-icon/chair_1012472 (id
1012472, "chair" by Freepik). The prior `TableOrderIcon` was a hand-drawn approximation; this round uses the
real artwork.

- **Implementation (preferred mask approach).** Saved the official PNG locally at
  `src/renderer/assets/table-order-icon.png` (from https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1012/1012472.png)
  and rewrote `src/renderer/components/icons/TableOrderIcon.tsx` to render it as a CSS mask
  (`mask-image`/`-webkit-mask-image: url(<bundled asset>)`, `mask-size: contain`, centred, no-repeat) painted
  with `background-color: currentColor`. The asset is imported via Vite (bundled/local) - it is NOT fetched
  from the network at runtime. Because the icon is painted in `currentColor`, the existing card classes
  (e.g. `text-white`) still recolour it white/blue exactly like the old inline SVG, while the shape now
  matches the supplied Flaticon artwork pixel-for-pixel. Verified by rendering the mask white-on-blue (the
  live order-type card context) and in blue, against the raw PNG (throwaway Edge render; nothing added to the
  repo).
- **API compatibility.** `TableOrderIcon` keeps the same props shape: `className` (drives size via the call
  sites' `w-full h-full`) and an accepted-but-ignored `strokeWidth` (the mask has no stroke). Both call sites
  - `OrderDashboard.tsx` (`<TableOrderIcon className="w-full h-full text-white" strokeWidth={1.6} />`) and
  `OrderFlow.tsx` (same) - are unchanged and still import/use the component.
- **Attribution.** Freepik/Flaticon attribution (id 1012472 + reference page + PNG URL + local asset path)
  kept in the component header and here. Flaticon Free license: free for personal/commercial use with
  attribution required.
- **Scope guard.** Did not touch the pickup icon, order-taking flow/layout, navbar/tabs, login, settings,
  locale strings, or unrelated style tokens. Diff this round: `TableOrderIcon.tsx` + the new
  `assets/table-order-icon.png` + this tracker.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`, PNG import + mask
  `CSSProperties` type-check); `git diff --check` on touched text files -> clean; the asset file exists
  (512x512 RGBA, ~14 KB) and the component references it via a local `import`; `rg` confirms both call sites
  still import and render `TableOrderIcon`. Final visual sign-off by supervisor in the live POS.

### Supervisor round 76 — Settings > Data/Sync Greek i18n leaks — COMPLETED

Live QA (Greek, Data settings): "Pre Recovery Action" (recovery list/accessibility tree), "Clear All
Orders" and "Clear" (destructive row) still rendered in English. Root cause: missing locale keys, so the
hardcoded English `t()` fallbacks leaked in Greek.

- **clear-all-orders keys (all five locales, `settings.database`).** Added `clearAllOrdersLabel`,
  `clearAllOrdersHelp`, `clearAllOrdersButton`, `allOrdersCleared`, `allOrdersClearFailed`. Natural per
  language; Greek: title "Εκκαθάριση όλων των παραγγελιών", button "Εκκαθάριση", help "Διαγράφει όλες τις
  παραγγελίες, συμπεριλαμβανομένων των σημερινών".
- **Interpolation (`ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** The `allOrdersCleared` success toast now passes
  `{ count: result.cleared, defaultValue: 'Cleared {{count}} orders' }` so `{{count}}` interpolates once the
  key exists. No behaviour change; no destructive action triggered.
- **recovery kind (all five locales).** Added `settings.recovery.kinds.pre_recovery_action`: EN "Before
  recovery action", EL "Πριν την ενέργεια ανάκτησης", DE "Vor Wiederherstellungsaktion", IT "Prima
  dell'azione di ripristino", FR "Avant l'action de récupération". So `RecoveryPanel.kindLabel()` no longer
  falls back to "Pre Recovery Action".
- **RecoveryPanel destructiveKinds.** Added `'pre_recovery_action'` to `destructiveKinds` so these points
  classify as protective (matching Rust), e.g. for "Latest protective snapshot". Stored recovery data is not
  mutated.
- **helpText emoji (scoped).** Removed the leading "⚠️ " from `settings.database.helpText` in all five
  locales (visible on the Data page). Did not touch other warning strings (`confirmClear`,
  `confirmFactoryReset`, etc. keep their warnings).
- **Scope guard.** No table icon, order dashboard/flow, login, navbar/tabs, printer flows, Supabase, or
  deletion/reset logic changed. No destructive buttons run. No hover additions.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on
  touched text files -> clean; all five locale JSONs parse; a Node check confirms every locale defines the
  five `clearAllOrders*` keys plus `settings.recovery.kinds.pre_recovery_action`; "Clear All Orders" /
  "Pre Recovery Action" now only exist as the EN locale values / source default fallbacks (cannot leak in
  Greek, which resolves the new el keys); hover audit of `RecoveryPanel.tsx` + `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`
  shows no new hover additions. Final visual sign-off by supervisor in the live POS.

  - it: "...in stato in attesa o in stampa..."
  - fr: "...à l'état en attente ou en cours d'impression..."
  - en left as-is (source language).

  touched text files -> clean; all five locale JSONs parse; a check confirms `restoreNote` in el/de/it/fr no
  longer contains raw "pending"/"printing" while en still does (source). Final visual sign-off by supervisor
  in the live POS.

### Supervisor round 78 — recovery restoreNote grammar polish — COMPLETED (follow-up to round 77)

Round 77 removed the raw statuses but the German sentence was grammatically awkward
("...im Status ausstehend oder wird gedruckt werden..."). Locale-only grammar polish; audited el/it/fr for
the same quality.

- **Fix (locale-only, `settings.recovery.restoreNote`).**
  - de: now reads naturally with the statuses as quoted labels - "...mit dem Status \"Ausstehend\" oder
    \"Wird gedruckt\" werden während der Wiederherstellung abgebrochen...".
  - el: smoothed the doubled preposition to the genitive form - "σε κατάσταση αναμονής ή εκτύπωσης" (was
    "σε κατάσταση σε αναμονή ή σε εκτύπωση").
  - it: fixed the doubled "in" - "in stato di attesa o di stampa" (was "in stato in attesa o in stampa").
  - fr: left unchanged - already natural ("à l'état en attente ou en cours d'impression").
  - en: unchanged (source language).
- **No code/behaviour change.** `RecoveryPanel.tsx`, recovery logic, destructive actions, table icon, order
  pages, login, settings layout, and unrelated strings untouched. No hover additions.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on
  touched text files -> clean; all five locale JSONs parse (incl. the escaped German quotes); el/de/it/fr
  `restoreNote` contain no raw "pending"/"printing" while en still does (source). Final visual sign-off by
  supervisor in the live POS.

### Supervisor round 79 — Payment Terminals / ECR UI polish (modal fit, hover purge, palette) — COMPLETED

Live QA at 1280x800: Settings > Μηχανές καρτών > Ρύθμιση > Προσθήκη Τερματικού opened `TerminalConfigModal`
too tall (form/actions cut off below the viewport), and the ECR settings still had hover effects and
off-palette blue/indigo styling. UI-only pass across the scoped ECR files.

- **TerminalConfigModal fit.** Moved the Cancel/Save actions out of the scrolling form body into the
  `LiquidGlassModal` `footer` prop (the shell is `flex flex-col`, max-height 92vh, with an `overflow-y:auto`
  content area and a `flex-shrink-0` footer). The form now has `id="terminal-config-form"`; the footer Save
  button keeps `type="submit"` via `form="terminal-config-form"` so submit/Enter behaviour is unchanged. The
  footer is always visible; the fields scroll. No fields removed.
- **Palette (non-semantic blue/indigo -> amber/neutral; green/red kept).**
  - TerminalConfigModal: all input `focus:ring-blue-500` -> `focus:ring-amber-500`; checkbox accents
    `text-blue-500` -> `text-amber-500`; Save button blue -> amber.
  - TerminalDiscoveryModal: selected connection-type toggles and the Search/Add buttons blue -> amber.
  - TerminalCard: default `ring-blue-500/50` -> `ring-amber-500/50`.
  - TerminalCardCompact: "busy" status `text-blue-400`/`bg-blue-500/20` -> amber.
  - PaymentTerminalsSection: the Total mini-stat card color `indigo` -> `amber` (key + type + usage).
  - Bluetooth/USB/Network protocol labels left as-is (names, not styling).
- **Hover purge -> tap feedback.** Removed every `hover:`/`dark:hover:` in the five files; interactive
  controls now use `active:` (and `active:scale-[0.98]` on the modal footer buttons). The compact card's
  decorative container hover was dropped (non-interactive wrapper); a non-functional `hover:liquid-glass-modal-text`
  utility was removed. Connect/disconnect stay green, delete/disconnect stay red.
- **Behaviour preserved.** No ECR bridge/API calls, validation, save/delete/connect/disconnect logic, data
  models, or translations changed. Order pages, login, navbar, tabs, and the Flaticon table icon untouched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
  touched files -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:|:hover"` on the five ECR files -> **0**;
  `rg` for `bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|ring-blue|indigo|sky-|cyan-|purple-|violet|fuchsia` styling on the
  five files -> **0** (Bluetooth string literals are not styling). Final visual sign-off by supervisor in the
  live POS.
  - Live QA follow-up: the footer Save button showed "Add" in Greek. Root cause: the ECR components called
    `t('common.add')` / `t('common.save')` / `t('common.cancel')`, but the locales define these under
    `common.actions.*` (flat `common.add` does not exist, so it fell back to the English default). Fixed by
    pointing the buttons at the existing `common.actions.add/save/cancel` keys (no new locale keys).
    `TerminalConfigModal.tsx` (footer Cancel/Save/Add) and `TerminalDiscoveryModal.tsx` (discovered-device
    Add). Also fixed the adjacent same-bug `common.saving` -> `common.actions.saving` ("Saving..." was
    leaking identically). Verified: `npm run type-check` clean; `git diff --check` clean;
    `rg "common\.(add|save|cancel)\b"` in the two ECR files -> 0; `rg "common.actions.(add|save|cancel)"`
    shows the replacements.

loyalty-reader notice.

  - `actionClass`: `hover:bg-zinc-800` / `hover:bg-gray-100` -> `active:bg-zinc-800` / `active:bg-gray-100`
    (tap feedback; at-rest neutral greys unchanged).
  - Header scan icon: `text-cyan-400` -> `text-amber-400`.
  - Loyalty-reader notice: purple (`border-purple-*/bg-purple-*/text-purple-*`, both themes) -> amber
    (`border-amber-*/bg-amber-*/text-amber-*`).
- **Preserved.** Panel layout, rounded glass-like modal feel, form behavior, file-input/camera behavior,
  hardware scanning, manual-code submit, `onClose`/`onScan`, hooks, and translations - all unchanged. No
  business-logic changes. Green stays for connected/success; no red/destructive states were affected.

  Coupons scan flow is not exposed), so the change is verified statically only; live visual sign-off is

- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
