### Supervisor round 81 — Settings > Devices switch polish (glass pill) — COMPLETED

Founder called the small mobile-style row toggles ugly/inconsistent with the glass Settings modal. Polished
the shared switch treatment.

- **`ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` `switchTrackClass`.** Replaced the small toggle (w-11 h-6, 20px knob) with
  a larger glass pill: `w-14 h-8` track (56x32 touch target), centered `h-6 w-6` white knob (4px inset all
  round), `shadow-inner` track + `shadow-md`/subtle-border knob, amber/yellow ON (`peer-checked:bg-yellow-500`),
  neutral grey OFF (`bg-gray-300 dark:bg-gray-700`), smooth `after:transition-all`, knob travel
  `peer-checked:after:translate-x-6` (geometry-matched so it stays centered at both ends). Focus ring kept
  (`peer-focus:ring-2 ring-yellow-500/50`); no hover. This one shared class updates every Settings switch
  (Audio, Auto-print, Session timeout, Weighing Scale, Customer Display, Serial Scanner, Card Reader,
  Loyalty/NFC) at once.
- **`CashRegisterSection.tsx` fiscal auto-print switch.** Aligned the `role="switch"` button to the same look
  (h-8 w-14 track, white `h-6 w-6` knob, `bg-yellow-500` ON / `bg-gray-300 dark:bg-gray-700` OFF,
  `translate-x-6`); kept its existing `active:scale-95` tap feedback and `focus-visible` ring; removed the
  dark-knob check glyph for consistency with the plain white knob (and dropped the now-unused `Check`
  import). `handleFiscalPrintToggle` behavior unchanged.
- **Behavior preserved.** Same checked-state variables and `onChange`/`onClick` handlers; no persistence,
  bridge, or live-toggling changes. All labels/descriptions/translations (EN/EL/DE/IT/FR) intact; nothing
  hard-coded.
- **Out-of-scope note.** The cash-register add/edit form's "Enabled" toggle (`CashRegisterSection.tsx`
  ~line 1101) still uses the old inline w-11 h-6 toggle; left untouched per the explicit fiscal-auto-print
  scope - flagged as a future consistency follow-up.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
  touched files -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:|:hover"` on both TSX files -> no new hover for
  this scope; `rg "cyan-|purple-|violet|fuchsia|pink-|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|focus:ring-blue|sky-"` on
  both -> no off-palette introduced. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): in the running POS at
  Settings > Devices, the top peripheral toggles and the cash-register fiscal auto-print switch render as
  the larger grey/yellow glass pills with aligned labels, and no small old mobile switch is visible.

### Supervisor round 82 — Cash register add/edit form polish (Enabled switch + spacing) — COMPLETED

Follow-up to round 81's out-of-scope note. In Settings > Devices > Cash Register > Add Device, the form's
"Enabled" control still used the old small `w-11 h-6` toggle, and the bottom Cancel/Add Device action row
felt clipped/cramped at 1280x800 after scrolling.

- **Enabled switch (`CashRegisterSection.tsx` `renderFormView`).** Replaced the old inline toggle with the
  same glass pill as round 81: `w-14 h-8` track, white `h-6 w-6` knob (`shadow-md` + subtle border),
  `shadow-inner` track, amber/yellow ON (`peer-checked:bg-yellow-500`), neutral grey OFF
  (`bg-gray-300 dark:bg-gray-700`), geometry-matched `translate-x-6`, yellow focus ring, smooth transition,
  no hover. `updateForm({ enabled })` behavior unchanged.
- **Default + Enabled tiles.** Wrapped both controls in matching rounded glass tiles
  (`rounded-xl border liquid-glass-modal-border bg-white/5 px-3 py-2.5`) so they read as one consistent row;
  the row is now `flex-wrap` so it never overflows at 1280x800. The "Set as default" control stays a
  **checkbox** (semantics unchanged) - bumped to `h-5 w-5` with `accent-yellow-500`, in a tile with
  `active:bg-white/10` tap feedback (no hover). Same `updateForm({ is_default })` + translation key.
- **Action-row breathing room.** Added `pb-4` to the form container so the Cancel/Save(Add Device) row is no
  longer flush against the modal bottom and scrolls fully into view with space beneath. Actions stay inside
  the component; save/cancel handlers unchanged. (If live QA still shows clipping, a local sticky footer is
  the documented follow-up; padding was preferred as the minimal change.)
- **Behavior preserved.** No bridge/API, device save/delete/test/connect/disconnect, translations, Supabase,
  or unrelated-page changes.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
  touched files -> clean; added lines contain no `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`/`:hover`; added lines
  introduce no `cyan/purple/violet/fuchsia/pink/blue/sky` off-palette styling. Live visual sign-off PASSED
  (Codex/operator), superseded/strengthened by round 87: in Settings > Devices > Cash Register > Add Device,
  Print Mode renders as two centered segmented glass tiles with an amber selected state, Set as default and
  Enabled render as matching grey/yellow glass switches, the action row has breathing room, and Cancel was
  clicked afterward so no device was saved.

### Supervisor round 83 — Settings section-change scroll reset — COMPLETED

Live QA: scrolling deep in Settings > Devices then clicking the left-rail Data section opened Data halfway
down (the right pane preserved the previous section's scroll offset), hiding the section header/top controls.

- **Fix (`ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` only).** Added a `detailScrollRef` (`useRef<HTMLDivElement>`) on the
  right detail pane (the `overflow-y-auto` column around line ~987) and a `useLayoutEffect` keyed on
  `activeSettingsSection` (and `isOpen`) that calls `detailScrollRef.current?.scrollTo({ top: 0 })`. Because
  every navigation path funnels through `activeSettingsSection` - left-rail `openSection()`, the
  `initialSection === 'recovery'` deep link (which sets `'database'`), and the programmatic jump into
  `'hardware'` from PaymentTerminalsSection / cash-register setup - a single effect resets all of them. Used
  `useLayoutEffect` (runs before paint) so there is no one-frame flash of the old scroll position; no
  `setTimeout` needed.
- **Behavior preserved.** No settings persistence, bridge/API, recovery, or device logic changes; no
  translations; no visual redesign. The reset only affects the right pane's `scrollTop` on section change.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
  touched files -> clean; added lines contain no `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`/`:hover`; added lines
  introduce no `cyan/purple/violet/fuchsia/pink/blue/sky` off-palette styling. Live visual sign-off PASSED
  (Codex/operator): from a deep Devices scroll position with Caller ID advanced content open, switching to
  Data opened at the Database Management top, and switching back to Devices opened at the Peripherals top.

### Supervisor round 84 — Navigation rail active-module scroll-into-view — COMPLETED

Live QA: after logging out and back in, the collapsed navigation rail can preserve a previous `scrollTop`,
so on the order screen the active module icon appears clipped/floating at the top edge instead of fully
visible inside the rounded sidebar - the selected-page indicator looks detached.

- **Fix (`NavigationSidebar.tsx` only).** Added one deterministic effect keyed on
  `[currentView, orderedNavigationModules, draggingModuleId]` that, when not dragging, measures the active
  module button (`moduleButtonRefs.current[currentView]`) against the rail scroll container
  (`scrollContainerRef`) via `getBoundingClientRect()` and nudges only the container's `scrollTop` by the
  minimum needed (with an 8px margin) so the active button is fully visible. It runs when the current view
  changes and when the module list/order settles after login. No `setTimeout`.
- **Respects drag/reorder.** Early-returns while `draggingModuleId` is set, so it never scrolls during a
  drag; it only moves the rail's own `scrollTop` (never the page).
- **Preserved.** No color/behavior changes: inactive icons stay neutral, only the active icon keeps its
  module neon (`getNeonClass` untouched); rounded/glass rail style unchanged; OrderTabsBar, the order-type
  table icon, and the login orb animation untouched. Touch-first; no hover added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
  touched files -> clean; added lines contain no `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`/`:hover`; diff limited
  to `NavigationSidebar.tsx` + this tracker. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): closed Settings,
  logged out to the PIN screen (restored login orb animation visible behind the glass panel), entered PIN
  111111 and returned to Orders - the active Orders nav icon sat fully inside the rounded left rail (not
  clipped/detached), inactive nav icons rendered black/neutral while the active icon kept its colored neon
  state, and order tabs were correct (active Orders tab green, unselected Tables/Cancelled tabs grey).

### Supervisor round 85 — Settings > Data maintenance area: repair vs danger regrouping — COMPLETED

Live QA (Settings > Data, below RecoveryPanel): the maintenance actions were a dense flat list of rows with
repeated "Clear" / "Εκκαθάριση" buttons; safe repairs and destructive deletions/factory-reset were visually
mixed, and at 1280x800 the lower rows felt clipped. Non-technical staff could not tell the danger level.

- **Fix (`ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` only, the block below `<RecoveryPanel />`).** Regrouped the six
  actions into two clearly labelled cards:
  - **Repair & sync tools** (neutral/yellow card, `Wrench` header): Clear Sync Queue, Clear Old Orders, Sync
    Deleted Orders - copy says these are safe and keep data. (Code-review follow-up: the safe buttons now
    use unique labels too - `repairQueueButton` "Fix queue", `repairOldOrdersButton` "Clean old orders",
    `repairDeletedOrdersButton` "Sync deleted orders" - replacing the old generic
    `clearSyncButton`/`clearOldOrdersButton`/`syncButton` so no button repeats a bare "Clear"/"Sync".)
  - **Danger zone** (red-bordered card, `AlertTriangle` header): Clear All Orders, Clear All Operational
    Data, Factory Reset - each row is a red sub-tile with a "Cannot be undone" badge and a **unique** button
    label (Delete all orders / Erase operational data / Factory reset) so there are no longer three
    identical "Clear" buttons.
  - RecoveryPanel itself unchanged; added `gap-4 pb-6` to the maintenance column so the factory-reset row is
    no longer flush/cut off in the Settings scroll pane.
- **Handlers preserved exactly.** Every `onClick` is verbatim: `bridge.sync.clearAll()`,
  `bridge.sync.clearOldOrders()`, `bridge.sync.clearAllOrders()`, `bridge.sync.cleanupDeletedOrders()`,
  `setShowClearOperationalConfirm(true)`, `handleClearDatabase`; the existing ConfirmDialog /
  privileged-PIN flows are untouched. Touch-first: `active:scale`/`active:bg` only, no hover. Palette: glass
  + white/black/grey/yellow base, semantic red for destructive; rounded cards, centered icon/text/buttons.
- **i18n.** Added 8 keys under `settings.database` in EN/EL/DE/IT/FR: `repairToolsTitle`, `repairToolsHelp`,
  `dangerZoneTitle`, `dangerZoneHelp`, `cannotUndo`, `dangerDeleteOrdersButton`, `dangerEraseDataButton`,
  `dangerFactoryResetButton`. Existing keys (labels/help/safe button text) reused where they fit; nothing
  hard-coded.
- **Untouched.** RecoveryPanel internals, bridge/API, recovery/device logic, order/login/navbar/table icon,
  Supabase/migrations.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on
  touched files -> clean; all five locale JSONs parse and expose the 8 new keys; added TSX lines contain no
  `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`/`:hover`; changed files limited to `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`, the
  five locale JSONs, and this tracker. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): checked in the running
  POS at Settings > Data - repair tools are clearly separated from the red danger zone, the safe buttons
  show unique labels (Fix queue / Clean old orders / Sync deleted orders), the destructive rows carry
  "cannot be undone"/not-reversible badges, the bottom spacing is good (factory-reset row not clipped), and
  switching away to another section and back resets the Data panel to the top.

### Supervisor round 86 — Add Payment Terminal modal: native checkboxes -> glass toggles — COMPLETED

Live QA (Settings > Μηχανές καρτών > Ρύθμιση > Προσθήκη Τερματικού): the Add Payment Terminal form still
showed three large native blue square checkboxes (`printOnTerminal`, `isDefault`, `enabled`) that broke the
app palette and were not touch-first.

- **Fix (`TerminalConfigModal.tsx` only).** Replaced the three native `<input type="checkbox">` rows with
  touch-first toggle rows: label text on the left, a rounded yellow/grey glass switch on the right. Added a
  module-level `SWITCH_TRACK_CLASS` mirroring `ConnectionSettingsModal`'s switch pill (yellow ON track /
  neutral grey OFF, centered white knob, `peer-focus` ring, no hover). Each row is a `<label>` wrapping an
  `sr-only peer` checkbox + the track span, `min-h-[44px]`, rounded glass tile, aligned, inside the existing
  scroll body (does not overlap the sticky footer). Removed the redundant `htmlFor` (the label wraps the
  input) to avoid a Chromium double-toggle.
- **Behavior preserved exactly.** Same state names/setters (`printOnTerminal`/`setPrintOnTerminal`,
  `isDefault`/`setIsDefault`, `enabled`/`setEnabled`), same input `id`s, same `onChange` handlers, same i18n
  keys (`ecr.config.printOnTerminal` / `ecr.config.setDefault` / `ecr.config.enabled`), and the save payload
  still carries `isDefault`, `enabled`, `settings.printOnTerminal`. No ECR bridge/API, validation, or layout
  changes; modal glass shell and field layout untouched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
  touched files -> clean; added TSX lines contain no `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`/`:hover`; `rg`
  confirms no native checkbox class (`w-4 h-4 rounded border-gray-...`) remains for
  printOnTerminal/isDefault/enabled - they now use `sr-only peer` + the glass pill. Live visual sign-off
  PASSED (Codex/operator): checked in the running POS at Settings > Card Machines > Add Payment Terminal -
  the native blue squares are gone, the three controls render as rounded yellow/grey glass toggles, a single
  row tap toggled exactly once, and Cancel discarded the test state.

### Supervisor round 87 — Cash Register add/edit: Print Mode tiles + Set-as-default switch — COMPLETED

Live QA (Settings > Devices > Cash Register / Fiscal Printer > Add Device): two unfinished native controls
remained - Print Mode used native radio dots, and "Set as default" was a native white square checkbox next
to the already-polished "Enabled" glass switch (mismatch looked unfinished).

- **Fix (`CashRegisterSection.tsx` only).**
  - **Print Mode**: replaced the two native radios with a 2-up segmented glass tile selector. Each tile is a
    `<label>` wrapping an `sr-only peer` radio (`name="printMode"`, accessibility/keyboard kept) + a styled
    tile; selected = amber border/tint (`peer-checked:border-yellow-500 peer-checked:bg-yellow-400/15`),
    unselected = neutral grey/black glass; `min-h-[44px]`, centered, `peer-focus` ring, no hover. Uses the
    existing labels `settings.peripherals.cashRegister.registerPrints` / `posSendsReceipt`.
  - **Set as default**: replaced the native square checkbox with the same rounded yellow/grey glass switch
    as "Enabled" (label left, switch right, ≥44px tile). Extracted the switch pill into a module-level
    `CASH_REGISTER_SWITCH_TRACK_CLASS` and reused it for both the Set-as-default and Enabled switches to
    avoid duplication; "Enabled" appearance/behavior is unchanged.
- **Behavior preserved exactly.** State fields/payload semantics unchanged: `form.print_mode`,
  `form.is_default`, `form.enabled` (same `updateForm({...})` calls, same `name`, same `checked`/`onChange`).
  No bridge/API, save/delete/test/connect, validation, tax-table, or translation/locale changes; existing
  i18n keys reused; nothing hard-coded. Smooth rounded edges, aligned, no clipping at 1280x800.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
  touched files -> clean; added TSX lines contain no `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`/`:hover`; `rg`
  confirms no visible native radio/checkbox accent class (`accent-yellow-500`, `h-5 w-5 rounded accent-...`)
  remains for Print Mode or Set-as-default (the inputs are now `sr-only peer`). Live visual sign-off PASSED
  (Codex/operator): checked in the running POS at Settings > Devices > Cash Register / Fiscal Printer > Add
  Device - Print Mode renders as two segmented glass tiles and tapping the inactive "POS sends receipt" tile
  moved the selected amber state exactly once; "Set as default" renders as a grey/yellow glass switch
  matching Enabled with no native white square checkbox remaining visually; Cancel was then clicked,
  discarding the test state with no device saved.

### Supervisor round 88 — Light/dark theme live sign-off — COMPLETED

Live visual QA only (Codex/operator) - no code changed; this round records the result of verifying the
restored theming across both themes in the running POS.

- **Light mode (Orders + Settings).** Selected-only nav behavior held: inactive nav icons black/neutral,
  active Orders icon colored; order tabs showed active Orders green and unselected Tables/Cancelled grey;
  the login PIN page still showed the restored yellow/orb animated background behind the glass panel; and
  Settings > Devices / Display & Sound used grey glass cards with yellow accents and aligned controls.
- **Dark mode (switched via Settings > Display & Sound).** Settings shell stayed black/grey glass with
  yellow accents; the icon-only theme buttons, language buttons, sliders, and glass switches were readable
  and aligned. The dark Orders screen stayed black/grey/yellow with the active Orders tab green, unselected
  tabs grey, inactive nav icons neutral/white while the active nav kept its colored state, and logout
  stayed semantic red.
- No tracker-listed regressions in either theme; closes the "verify light and dark theme styling" global
  task.

### Supervisor round 89 — Settings > About Greek i18n leak — COMPLETED

Live QA (supervisor screenshot, Greek Settings > Πληροφορίες): the About panel still showed English visible
labels - "About", "Version", "Build Date", "Git SHA", "Platform", and "Copy Info". Root cause: the About
labels called `t('settings.about.*')` / `t('modals.connectionSettings.about'|'aboutSubtitle')` with English
fallback defaults, but **none** of those keys existed in any locale (`settings.about` was empty everywhere),
so every language rendered the English default.

- **Fix.**
  - `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` (About JSX only): pointed every visible About label/button at the existing
    `modals.connectionSettings` namespace (the supervisor's preferred one, where the title/subtitle keys
    already lived) - `about`, `aboutSubtitle`, `aboutVersion`, `aboutBuildDate`, `aboutGitSha`,
    `aboutPlatform`, `aboutRust`, `aboutCopyInfo`, `aboutCopied` - and removed the inline English fallback
    literals so no visible English string is hardcoded in the About JSX. (`settings.about.*` was used only
    here, so no other consumer is affected.)
  - Added natural translations for all nine keys in EN/EL/DE/FR/IT under `modals.connectionSettings`.
    Technical names are intentionally kept verbatim across locales ("Git SHA", "Rust"); raw values
    (version, build timestamp, SHA, platform/arch, Rust version) are produced from `aboutData` and were not
    touched.
- **Behavior preserved.** No layout, handlers, copy/update behavior (`handleCopyAboutInfo` unchanged),
  colors, hover behavior, settings navigation, order flow, or table/pickup icon changes. `TableOrderIcon`
  (Flaticon 1012472 PNG mask) untouched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` on the
  touched files -> clean; all five locale JSONs parse and expose the nine `modals.connectionSettings.about*`
  keys; `rg` confirms the visible English literals ("About"/"Version"/"Build Date"/"Git SHA"/"Platform"/
  "Copy Info") are no longer hardcoded in the About JSX (labels are now bare `t(...)` calls). Live visual
  sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): in the running POS, Greek Settings > Πληροφορίες now shows Greek-visible
  title/labels/button - Πληροφορίες, Έκδοση, Ημερομηνία δημιουργίας, Πλατφόρμα, Αντιγραφή πληροφοριών - while
  raw technical values are unchanged and Git SHA / Rust remain as technical product/acronym names. Layout,
  colors, settings navigation, copy behavior, and the TableOrderIcon Flaticon 1012472 asset were unchanged.

### Supervisor round 90 — Tables dashboard hover removal (touch-first) — COMPLETED

Live review: the Tables page loads correctly, but `tables/TablesDashboard.tsx` still had 39 `hover:` classes -
hover-only visual effects don't belong on a touchscreen-first POS.

- **Fix (`tables/TablesDashboard.tsx` only).** Removed/neutralized all 39 `hover:` classes:
  - Interactive `hover:bg-*` (tab bar refresh + tab buttons, order/delivered/canceled cards, view/status/
    floor filter buttons, detail-panel close) -> `active:bg-*` so taps still flash.
  - `hover:text-*` (tab labels, refresh, close) -> removed; at-rest text colors preserved.
  - Status-card `hover:border-*` (the `statusConfig` available/occupied/reserved/cleaning/maintenance/
    unavailable cards) -> removed; at-rest semantic border/bg colors unchanged.
  - Clickable order card `hover:scale-[1.01]` -> `active:scale-[0.99]`; floorplan table card
    `hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-xl` -> `active:scale-[0.99]` (kept its `group` + focus ring).
  - The floorplan card's child action buttons used `group-hover:bg-*`; converted to `group-active:bg-*` so
    pressing the card still flashes the action color (touch equivalent of the parent-hover effect).
- **Preserved.** Selected/active/current status styling, semantic status colors, disabled states, existing
  `active:`/`focus-visible`/focus rings, layout dimensions, translations, data/filters/search/refresh/list/
  floorplan behavior, and all click/keydown handlers. No new colors outside the existing palette; table card
  contents and truncation logic untouched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `rg "hover:"
  TablesDashboard.tsx` -> **0 matches**; `git diff --check` over `TablesDashboard.tsx` + this tracker ->
  clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): in the running POS, Tables list mode rendered
  correctly after reload with stats cards / search / top controls / table cards aligned; floorplan mode
  rendered correctly and tapping the cleaning status filter reduced the floorplan to B01/B02, with a second
  tap restoring the full floorplan; tapping T01 opened the table action modal and closing it returned to the
  floorplan without layout damage; no data/state changes were saved.

### Supervisor round 91 — TableActionModal glass/touch/palette cleanup — COMPLETED

Live issue: tapping a table opened `TableActionModal` as a mostly solid white/gray panel (the app requires
blurry-glass modals), the file still had many `hover:` classes, and the reservation action used purple
(off-palette for this modal).

- **Fix (`tables/TableActionModal.tsx` only).**
  - **Glass shell:** replaced the solid `bg-white` / `bg-gray-900` panel with translucent glass +
    `backdrop-blur-2xl` and a subtle border - light `bg-white/75 border-white/50`, dark
    `bg-gray-900/70 border-white/10` (preserved the existing `rounded-2xl`). Softened the two solid inner
    surfaces to glass too (table-detail icon container and the covers `<input>` now `bg-white/70` in light).
    Status-detail card and action tiles already used translucent tints.
  - **Hover removal:** every `hover:` class removed - interactive `hover:bg-*` -> `active:bg-*` (close,
    guest +/- controls, all action tiles), `hover:text-*` removed (close button), `hover:border-*` removed
    (action tiles keep their at-rest 2px semantic border), and card `hover:scale-[1.02]` -> dropped (kept
    `active:scale-[0.98]`). `rg "hover:"` now returns 0.
  - **Palette:** the New Reservation action (button bg/border, icon container, `Calendar` icon, title,
    arrow) recolored purple -> amber to match Edit Reservation. `rg "purple|violet|fuchsia"` now returns 0.
    Kept semantic red (cancel), emerald (set-available), amber (reservation/edit), slate (no-show), blue
    (new order / occupied, unchanged - not in the purple/violet/fuchsia scope).
- **Preserved.** All callbacks and behavior: `onNewOrder`, `onNewReservation`, `onSetAvailable`, reservation
  edit/no-show/cancel, guest count +/- + numeric input clamping, Escape (topmost-dialog) + backdrop close,
  disabled/`aria-disabled` states, translations, ARIA labelling, and table status logic. Icon/text alignment
  centered; smooth rounded edges kept. No locale changes.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `rg "hover:"
  TableActionModal.tsx` -> **0**; `rg "purple|violet|fuchsia" TableActionModal.tsx` -> **0**; `git diff
  --check` over `TableActionModal.tsx` + this tracker -> clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator):
  in the running POS, T01 opened as a readable translucent/blurry glass TableActionModal with an amber
  reservation action and no purple in light mode, and stayed readable/glassy with amber reservation styling
  in dark mode; the B01 cleaning-table path opened and showed the set-available/cleaning controls without
  layout damage; no data/state changes were saved.

### Supervisor round 92 — Regression-lock pass (login orb + selected-only neon + order-type icons) — COMPLETED

Scoped inspection of the four user-locked requirements; only one minimal safety correction was needed.

- **Inspected (all already correct, preserved as-is):**
  - `NavigationSidebar.tsx`: `getNeonClass` returns `text-black` (light) / `text-white` (dark) for inactive
    icons and the module neon only when active; touch feedback is `active:scale-95`, no hover.
  - `OrderTabsBar.tsx`: `tabTextClass` returns `text-gray-600` (light) / `text-white/70` (dark) for inactive
    tabs and the colored/neon identity only when active; `active:`-only feedback, no hover.

    matching `w-16 h-16` boxes rendered `w-full h-full text-white` (same size, white), and the table label is
    blue (`text-[#60a5fa]`).

    (`assets/table-order-icon.png`) rendered as a CSS mask with `currentColor` (unchanged).
  - `AnimatedBackground.tsx`: the animated yellow/black orb / lava-lamp background is present for both themes
    (not reverted to a flat/static grid).
- **Changed (`AnimatedBackground.tsx` only):** added `pointer-events-none` to the fixed full-screen
  background root so it can never intercept touchscreen taps (the grain overlay already had it; the root did
  not). No animation, color, or layout change.
- **Untouched:** order-taking behavior/data logic, translations, and the already-correct
  NavigationSidebar/OrderTabsBar/OrderFlow/TableOrderIcon files. No hover added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `rg "pointer-events-none"
  AnimatedBackground.tsx` -> present on the root; `rg "hover:" AnimatedBackground.tsx NavigationSidebar.tsx
  OrderTabsBar.tsx` -> **0**; `git diff --check` over `AnimatedBackground.tsx` + this tracker -> clean. Live
  visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): in the running POS, the login screen showed the restored
  yellow/black lava-lamp orb animation and a screenshot 3s later showed the orb composition moved (visible
  animation), while the PIN keypad accepted six taps for 111111 and logged in (background no longer
  intercepts touches). After login, the Orders page showed selected-only nav neon (selected Orders icon
  blue/neon, other nav icons neutral black) and the dashboard tabs showed active Orders green/neon with
  inactive Tables/Canceled neutral grey. Opening New Order showed the order-type chooser: Pickup = simple
  white bag, Table = the white TableOrderIcon table/chairs mark (not the old cake-like mark), both the same
  size with a blue Table label; the chooser was closed without creating an order. No data/order state was
  changed beyond logout/login.

uses lucide cutlery/store marks.

  `OrderFlow.tsx` already uses `TableOrderIcon` for the Table card: it renders white

  `ShoppingBag`, and the Table title stays blue (`text-[#60a5fa]`). Pickup remains the green bag card. Both
  cards already use `active:scale-95` (no hover).

    `<Store>`; replaced with `<TableOrderIcon className="w-4 h-4" />`, removed the unused `Store` import, and
    added the `TableOrderIcon` import (pickup/delivery badges keep `ShoppingBag`/`Truck`).
- **Preserved.** Copy, translations, callbacks, module gating (`hasTablesModule`/`hasDeliveryModule`),
  selected-state styling, and order-flow behavior unchanged. Pickup bag + green card untouched.

  `<UtensilsCrossed>` as the Tables-tab **empty-state** placeholder ("No tables configured") - that is not an
  order-type card/selector, so it was not converted. `CustomerInfoForm.tsx` line ~299 keeps a `hover:` on the
  **Validate Address** action button - not an order-type button, so left per the round's scope. Both will
  appear in the rg report below.

### Supervisor round 103 — Fiscal auto-print switch redesign (premium glass) — COMPLETED

Redesigned the Auto Fiscal Print toggle in the Cash Register / Fiscal Printer settings card from a flat solid
yellow pill into a touch-first glass control.

- **Fix (UI-only, single file `CashRegisterSection.tsx`).** Reworked only the fiscal-print `<button
  role="switch">` and its knob span:
  - Track sized up to `h-9 w-16` (64x36) `rounded-full` with a hairline `border`. OFF reads as recessed glass
    (`bg-gray-200/80 dark:bg-gray-700/70` + `shadow-inner` + `backdrop-blur-sm` + `border-black/10
    dark:border-white/10`); ON reads as a refined yellow lozenge (`bg-gradient-to-b from-yellow-300
    to-yellow-500` + `border-yellow-400/60` + colored `shadow-md shadow-yellow-500/30`) - no heavy solid
    orange.
  - Knob is `h-7 w-7` white with its own `from-white to-gray-50` gradient, `shadow-md`, and `border-black/10`
    so it stays visibly separated from the track. Geometry is derived: travel = 64 - 2x4 inset - 28 knob =
    28px = `translate-x-7`, so it lands flush with an equal 4px gap at both ends; height 36 - 8 inset = 28 =
    knob height, so it is vertically centered.
  - Touch only: `active:scale-95` press, `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-yellow-500/50`. No hover
    classes; palette stays white/black/grey/yellow (no blue/cyan/purple).
  - Row stays compact and layout-neutral: the two-line title/help block is taller than the 36px switch and the
    switch keeps `shrink-0`, so the row does not resize or shift.
- **Preserved.** `fiscalPrintEnabled` state, `handleFiscalPrintToggle`, `bridge.settings.set`
  category/key/value, toast success/error, `type="button"`, `role="switch"`, `aria-checked`, and all
  translations. Added `aria-label` reusing the existing `fiscalPrintLabel` translation (no new visible text,
  multilingual intact).
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|group-hover:|dark:hover:|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|focus:ring-blue|cyan|purple|violet|indigo"`
  over `CashRegisterSection.tsx` -> **0**. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): opened the running
  POS app, Settings -> Devices, and scrolled to Cash Register / Fiscal Printer. The Auto Fiscal Print row
  rendered in Greek. The switch is now a 64x36 yellow/amber glass pill with a visibly separated white knob,
  a clean ~4px inset, centered vertically, and aligned with the row text; it no longer looks like the old flat
  orange blob from the user screenshot. No setting was toggled during the visual check, and the Settings modal
  was closed afterward.

### Supervisor round 104 — Settings switch consistency pass (premium glass everywhere) — COMPLETED

Propagated the round-103 premium switch visual language to every shared Settings switch so the modal no longer
mixes the old 56x32 pill with the new 64x36 fiscal switch.

- **Fix (UI-only).** Rewrote the three shared peer-checkbox switch constants to identical tokens (the
  `sr-only peer` + `after:` knob translation of the round-103 `role="switch"` button):
  - `modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` -> `switchTrackClass` (used by every switch in the modal).
  - `ecr/TerminalConfigModal.tsx` -> `SWITCH_TRACK_CLASS`.
  - `peripherals/CashRegisterSection.tsx` -> `CASH_REGISTER_SWITCH_TRACK_CLASS` (Set-as-default + Enabled
    switches in the add/edit form). The already-approved direct Auto Fiscal Print `role="switch"` button was
    left untouched.
  - New shared geometry/visuals: track `w-16 h-9` (64x36) `rounded-full` `shrink-0`; OFF =
    `bg-gray-200/80 dark:bg-gray-700/70` + `shadow-inner` + `backdrop-blur-sm` + `border-black/10
    dark:border-white/10` (recessed neutral glass); ON = `peer-checked:bg-gradient-to-b
    peer-checked:from-yellow-300 peer-checked:to-yellow-500` + `peer-checked:border-yellow-400/60` +
    `peer-checked:shadow-md peer-checked:shadow-yellow-500/30` (refined amber, not flat orange). Knob =
    `after:h-7 after:w-7` `after:bg-gradient-to-b after:from-white after:to-gray-50` + `after:shadow-md` +
    `after:border-black/10`, inset `after:top-1 after:start-1` (4px), travel
    `peer-checked:after:translate-x-7` (64 - 28 knob - 8 inset = 28px, lands flush + centered).
    Touch/focus only: `peer-focus:ring-2 peer-focus:ring-yellow-500/50`, no hover.
  - Updated the now-stale geometry comment above `ConnectionSettingsModal`'s `switchTrackClass` to describe the
    new 64x36 / translate-x-7 geometry.
- **Preserved.** No handlers, state variables, bridge calls, translations, labels, aria attributes, or
  save/toggle behavior changed - only the shared class strings (and one comment). Palette stays
  white/black/grey/yellow; no blue/cyan/purple in these constants.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  three files + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|group-hover:|dark:hover:|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|focus:ring-blue|cyan|purple|violet|indigo|w-14 h-8|h-6 after:w-6|after:h-6|translate-x-6"`
  over the three files -> **0** (all old 56x32 / translate-x-6 / after:h-6 tokens gone; no off-palette tokens).
  Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): Settings > Devices top peripheral switches (Scale, customer
  display, serial barcode scanner, MSR, loyalty/NFC) render as the new large 64x36 glass OFF-state toggles
  with centered knobs. The Cash Register / Fiscal Printer automatic fiscal print switch is the matching 64x36
  yellow glass ON-state with clean row alignment. The Add Cash Register modal switches (Set as default OFF,
  Enabled ON) match the same premium switch system, as do the card-terminal add modal switches (Print receipt
  on terminal ON, Set default OFF, Enabled ON). Dialogs were cancelled; no test terminal/cash-register records
  were created.

  changed.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  three files + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|group-hover:|dark:hover:|bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|focus:ring-blue|text-orange|bg-orange|border-orange|from-orange|to-orange|orange-"`
  over the three menu files -> **0** (exit 1). Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator): in the running POS
  Pickup order menu the customizable menu cards now show compact rounded amber/yellow glass chips, not naked
  orange Settings/sliders icons. Cart action icons were preserved - ticket/discount stay amber and loyalty
  remains greyed when unavailable; they were not forced into all-yellow. The compact customer popover
  (`MenuModal.tsx`, title "Στοιχεία Πελάτη (Προαιρετικά)") now has a green Save/Αποθήκευση button; the close X
  stays neutral because it is not a labelled Cancel. No cart items/customer/order data were saved or changed
  (popover closed with X), and the order-taking layout/workflow stayed intact.
- **Correction (founder, action semantics + cart icons).** Action buttons follow semantics over strict
  yellow: Save/Add/Confirm/Apply = green, Cancel = red, Delete/Void stay red.
  - `menu/MenuItemCard.tsx`: the quick-quantity overlay **Add** button was already semantic green
    (`bg-emerald-500`, kept); the labelled **Cancel** button was neutral grey -> now red
    (`bg-red-500 active:bg-red-600 text-white`).
  - `modals/MenuModal.tsx` (the visible Pickup customer popover from the screenshot, title "Στοιχεία Πελάτη
    (Προαιρετικά)" / "Customer Info (Optional)", ~lines 2538-2603): the single **Save/Αποθήκευση** button was
    yellow primary (`liquid-glass-modal-primary`) -> now semantic green (`liquid-glass-modal-success`); same

  - `modals/CustomerInfoModal.tsx` (additional shared modal, NOT the screenshot popover): kept the broader
    Save/Cancel semantic alignment - **Save** `variant="primary"` -> `variant="success"` (green via
    `liquid-glass-modal-success`); **Cancel** `variant="secondary"` -> `variant="error"` (red via
    `liquid-glass-modal-error`). Uses the shared `POSGlassButton` semantic variants, so no new colors and the

    round-105 yellow change applied only to the menu sync buttons (a non-form utility action), not to cart
    icons. The MenuItemGrid preview close control stays a neutral Close `X` (not a labelled Cancel), per the
    rule to not redden close affordances.

    `menu/MenuGrid.tsx`, and `menu/MenuCart.tsx` still contain orange/amber tokens (combo accent, flavor
    intensity chips, a price chip, and a cart warning banner). These are outside the three-file scope of this
    round and were not modified.

    `MenuItemCard.tsx`, `MenuModal.tsx`, `CustomerInfoModal.tsx`) + tracker -> clean; `rg` for hover/blue/orange
    over the three menu files -> **0**. Order-taking layout/workflow re-checked: unchanged (only button
    color classes / `POSGlassButton` variants changed). Live visual sign-off now PASSED (see the round-105
    sign-off evidence above, which covers the green MenuModal Save and preserved cart icons).

### Supervisor round 106 — Semantic action-button pass for customer/address modals — COMPLETED

Applied consistent action-button semantics across the five customer/address modals: Save/Add/Confirm/Apply =
green, labelled Cancel = red, Close `X` stays neutral, Delete/Void stay red. This supersedes the older
yellow-primary / neutral-cancel wording from rounds 94, 95, 98, and 99 for these specific buttons (the hover
removal and field/focus palette work from those rounds still stands; only the Save/Cancel/Add/Update button
colors changed here). Cart icons were explicitly left alone.

- **Shared palette.** Green = `bg-green-600 active:bg-green-700 text-white` (green-600 = `#16a34a`, matching the
  existing CustomerSearchModal Continue button); Red = `bg-red-500 active:bg-red-600 text-white`. Layout,
  sizing, centering, icons, and neutral-grey disabled states were preserved on every button.
- **Fix (UI-only, five files).**
  - `modals/AddCustomerModal.tsx`: Cancel (kept `liquid-glass-modal-button` base for layout) -> red; submit
    Save / Save Address / Save Changes -> green (`text-black`->`text-white`, `shadow-yellow`->`shadow-green`).
  - `modals/AddNewAddressModal.tsx`: Cancel -> red; Add Address -> green (Home icon + centering kept).
  - `modals/EditCustomerInfoModal.tsx`: `editCustomerSaveButtonClass` -> green (`!text-black`->`!text-white`);
    Cancel -> red.
  - `modals/EditAddressModal.tsx`: Cancel -> red (kept `disabled:opacity-50`); Update Address -> green.
  - `modals/CustomerSearchModal.tsx`: the Add New Customer CTA (creates a customer) -> green via inline
    `style` (`#16a34a`/white) + `active:bg-green-700 dark:active:bg-green-600/30 active:scale-[0.98]`,
    matching the Continue button. Continue (already green) and Delete (already red) unchanged; Add Address /
    Edit Customer icon buttons and cart icons left as-is.
- **Preserved.** Business logic, data flow, validation, translations, modal layout, cart icons, and order
  workflow unchanged - only action-button color classes (and one inline `style`) changed.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  five files + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|group-hover:|dark:hover:"` over the five files -> **0**; `rg`
  for `bg-yellow`/`active:bg-yellow` over the five files -> **0** (no Save/Add/Update/Cancel button remains
  yellow). This solid semantic-action pass was superseded by round 107's glass refinement and later live modal
  QA (including CustomerSearchModal round 276 and EditCustomerInfoModal round 309), so no open sign-off remains here.

### Supervisor round 107 — Glass semantic action refinement (form modals) — COMPLETED

Refines round 106: keeps the Save=green / Cancel=red semantics but swaps the **solid opaque fills** for the
modal system's native **glass** variants, so harmless form actions feel native to the blurry glass modals
instead of reading as heavy paint chips.

- **Design philosophy recorded (founder + supervisor).** The white/black/grey/yellow palette is the
  foundation for *unity*, not a ban on color. Purposeful semantic/functional accents are wanted: green =
  positive save/add/confirm, red = cancel/destructive, and blue/purple/etc. are fine where they identify
  tabs/nav/status and make the UI feel modern. Avoid random off-theme blue *primary* buttons; do not flatten
  the product into all-yellow or dead grey.
- **Fix (UI-only, four form-footer modals).** Each Save/Add/Update + Cancel button now composes the shared
  glass classes instead of `bg-green-600`/`bg-red-500` solids:
  - Save/Add/Update -> `liquid-glass-modal-button liquid-glass-modal-success flex-1 rounded-* disabled:opacity-50
    disabled:saturate-0 disabled:cursor-not-allowed` (green glass tint + tinted text from the variant).
  - Cancel -> `liquid-glass-modal-button liquid-glass-modal-error flex-1 rounded-*` (red glass tint).
  - The base `liquid-glass-modal-button` supplies equal-width-friendly `inline-flex` centering, `gap` for
    icon+text, padding, the touch-safe `:active { scale(.98) }` press (its `:hover` is CSS-only and
    `@media (hover:hover)`-gated, so it never fires on the touchscreen), and blur; an explicit `rounded-2xl`
    (AddCustomerModal) / `rounded-xl` (others) preserves each modal's current corner shape. Disabled now
    reads muted/neutral via `opacity-50 + saturate-0` (no logic added). Files: `AddCustomerModal.tsx`,
    `AddNewAddressModal.tsx`, `EditCustomerInfoModal.tsx` (`editCustomerSaveButtonClass` const + Cancel),
    `EditAddressModal.tsx`.
- **CustomerSearchModal.tsx:** unchanged this round - Continue is already semantic green, Delete already red,
  and the Add New Customer CTA is already a stronger green CTA (matching Continue) with no hover classes.
  Add Address / Edit Customer icon accents and cart icons left alone.
- **Preserved.** Validation, data flow, translations, modal layout, close `X` (neutral), cart icons, and
  order workflow unchanged - only action-button color classes changed.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  five files + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|group-hover:|dark:hover:"` over the five files -> **0**; `rg`
  for `bg-red-500|bg-green-600|bg-yellow` over the four form-footer modals -> **0** (no solid action fills or
  yellow buttons remain; the only `bg-yellow-*` in CustomerSearchModal is the not-found notice *panel*, not an
  action button). Later live QA covered the reachable customer/search/edit modal paths (rounds 276 and 309);
  no open sign-off remains from this refinement note.

SVG for pickup orders, which did not match the order-type chooser. Aligned it with the
OrderDashboard/OrderFlow chooser glyphs.

  Replaced the storefront SVG `else` branch with: dine-in/dine_in/table -> `TableOrderIcon`
  (`w-6 h-6` + `text-gray-700`/`text-gray-300` so table orders don't get the pickup bag); pickup/takeaway/
  default -> lucide `ShoppingBag` at `size={24}` `strokeWidth={1.6}` with the existing light/dark `iconColor`
  (`#374151`/`#D1D5DB`). Delivery truck SVG unchanged.
- **Preserved.** Order data, statuses, labels, layout spacing, card shape, and colors (other than the icon
  glyph) unchanged; no hover changes.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file -> clean; `rg` confirms the old storefront path/comment is gone and `ShoppingBag` + `TableOrderIcon`

  row (#00001 / Παραλαβή / 18,50 €) now shows the same shopping-bag glyph shape as the OrderDashboard/OrderFlow
  order-type chooser, and the old storefront/awning icon is no longer visible on the pickup row. Visual
  inspection only - no order data/status/payment/customer state was changed.

### Supervisor round 109 — Selected-order delivered action: warning amber -> success green — COMPLETED

Fix: in the Orders dashboard selected-order action bar, the "Παραδόθηκε" / delivered-completed action rendered
amber because it was configured/styled as `warning`. Marking delivered/completed is a positive action, so it
now uses the semantic green `success` style.

- **Fix (UI-only, `BulkActionsBar.tsx`).** In `getActionsForTab('orders')` the delivered action config
  changed `variant: 'warning'` -> `variant: 'success'`; both rendered delivered buttons (delivery and pickup
  `selectionType`) changed `getButtonStyles('warning')` -> `getButtonStyles('success')`. The `warning` style
  case and the variant type union are left intact (no other action currently uses warning).
- **Preserved.** Labels, handlers (`onBulkAction('delivered')`), selection logic, layout, spacing, icons
  (`CheckCircle`), and no-hover/touch behavior unchanged. Cancel stays red (`danger`); edit/view neutral
  (`info`); clear neutral. Delivered-tab color/counts and order logic untouched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg` for `delivered.*warning` / `getButtonStyles('warning')` over
  `BulkActionsBar.tsx` -> **0** (the only remaining `warning` is the `case 'warning':` style definition + the
  variant type union, neither of which is the delivered action). Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator,
  Greek/light, 1280x800): in the running POS Orders dashboard, selected order #00001 opened the action bar -
  Παραδόθηκε renders as a semantic green success button with a centered CheckCircle + label; Ακύρωση renders
  red/destructive; Επεξεργασία, Προβολή, and Καθαρισμός remain neutral glass/grey. Tabs stayed active Orders
  green and inactive Tables/Cancelled neutral grey. No Delivered/Cancel action was pressed and no order data
  changed. This confirms the founder request that the delivered/completed action read green, not warning amber.

Regression audit of the login/nav/order-taking UI slice. Most requirements were already satisfied by earlier

     button (`hover:bg-green-600/30`) -> changed to `active:bg-green-600/30` (kept green - it is a positive

     / `bg-blue-500/10 ring-blue-500/20`, `Lock text-blue-300`/`text-blue-600`) -> moved to amber

     so the decorative chrome sits with the app palette.
- **Files changed:** `pages/LoginPage.tsx` (Save PIN hover->active + Lock badge blue->amber) and this tracker.
  All other scoped files were already correct and left untouched.
- **Preserved.** Order-taking business logic, auth/PIN-setup logic, module logic, and translations unchanged -

- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  changed file + this tracker -> clean; `rg` proofs: `AnimatedBackground` imported/rendered in LoginPage;

  - PASSED - Orders dashboard tabs show active Orders green/neon and inactive Tables/Cancelled tabs neutral

  - PASSED - order-type modal shows Pickup with the shopping-bag glyph and Table with the table/chairs
    `TableOrderIcon` plus the Greek label "Τραπέζι"; no cake/cutlery/store glyph in the table card.
  - CODE-VERIFIED ONLY - the setup-PIN modal was not opened because this terminal already has a PIN
    configured, so the amber Lock badge and the Save PIN hover->active change are verified by diff/rg/

### Supervisor round 111 — StaffShiftModal disabled primary buttons read as neutral glass — COMPLETED

- **Problem.** In the Staff check-in flow (Greek, light theme), the PIN-step "Συνέχεια"/Continue button is
  correctly disabled (`enteredPin.length !== 4`) but stayed a strong yellow primary CTA dimmed only by
  `disabled:opacity-50`, so on a touchscreen it still looked tappable. The optional Staff QR "Scan QR" button
  had the same yellow+opacity disabled pattern (disabled when the QR value is empty).
- **Fix (UI-only, `modals/StaffShiftModal.tsx`).** Both buttons keep their enabled yellow/black look but now
  get an unmistakably neutral/muted glass disabled state instead of relying on opacity alone. Dropped
  `disabled:opacity-50` and added `disabled:bg-slate-200/70 disabled:text-slate-400 disabled:border
  disabled:border-slate-300/60 disabled:shadow-none` plus dark-theme variants `dark:disabled:bg-white/10
  dark:disabled:text-white/40 dark:disabled:border-white/10` (and kept `disabled:cursor-not-allowed`). The
  `:disabled` pseudo-class outranks the base `bg-yellow-400`, so the neutral repaint only applies while
  disabled; the enabled state is unchanged.
- **Preserved.** Enabled yellow/black styling, current sizing/rounded shape/icon-text centering, framer tap
  behavior (`getInteractiveMotion` on the PIN button), labels, handlers, PIN validation, QR resolve logic,
  shift business logic, and translations - only the two disabled class strings changed. No hover classes.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg` confirms both disabled yellow check-in buttons now include neutral
  `disabled:bg-slate-200/70` / `disabled:text-slate-400` / `disabled:border-slate-300/60` /
  `disabled:shadow-none` (+ dark variants) and no longer rely only on `disabled:opacity-50`. Live visual
  sign-off (Codex/operator): PASSED for the PIN-step "Συνέχεια"/Continue disabled button in the live POS - it
  reads as muted neutral glass while `enteredPin.length !== 4` and returns to yellow/black when enabled. The
  "Scan QR" disabled state is CODE-VERIFIED ONLY because the Staff QR UI was not visible in this org during the
  review.

### Supervisor round 112 — Shared pickup-bag icon (order row matches chooser) — COMPLETED

- **Problem.** Live review reported the Orders row pickup glyph (under the amount) still read like a little
  storefront/package; the founder wants the row pickup icon to be the exact same bag used in the order-type
  chooser. (Round 108 had already swapped OrderCard's storefront SVG for lucide ShoppingBag, but the row and
  chooser were independent call sites.)
- **Fix (UI-only).** Added a shared `components/icons/PickupOrderIcon.tsx` wrapping the chooser's lucide
  `ShoppingBag` treatment (`strokeWidth` defaults to 1.6), so the row and chooser provably render the
  identical glyph rather than two separate call sites:
  - `order/OrderCard.tsx`: pickup/takeaway branch now renders `<PickupOrderIcon size={iconSize} color={iconColor} />`
    (kept the light/dark `iconColor` + 24px size); dropped the now-unused `ShoppingBag` import.
  - `OrderDashboard.tsx` + `OrderFlow.tsx` order-type chooser: pickup card now uses
    `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-full h-full text-white" />`; dropped the now-unused `ShoppingBag` imports.
  - `pages/OrdersPage.tsx` `getOrderTypeIcon` pickup + default badge now use `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-4 h-4" />`
    (kept the `ShoppingBag` import: the empty-state "no orders" placeholder still uses it - decorative, not an
    order-type icon).
  - Table icons remain `TableOrderIcon`; delivery icons unchanged.
- **Preserved.** Business logic, labels, translations, order status/payment icons, click handlers, layout, and
  no-hover/touch-first behavior unchanged - only the pickup glyph source.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  touched files + this tracker -> clean; `rg` confirms the old storefront SVG/path/comment is gone from
  OrderCard and that `PickupOrderIcon` is shared by the order row (OrderCard, OrdersPage badge) and the chooser
  (OrderDashboard, OrderFlow). Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, light theme): in the running POS
  Orders page, the pickup row icon under the amount now renders as the same simple bag glyph as the order-type
  chooser Pickup card; no storefront/awning glyph is visible. Dark theme is code-verified through the shared
  `PickupOrderIcon` + OrderCard dark `iconColor` path, not separately live-clicked in this pass.

### Supervisor round 113 — OrderDetailsModal footer clearance on short viewport — COMPLETED

- **Problem.** Live QA at 1280x800 (Greek/light): Orders > #00001 > Προβολή opens the Order Details modal with
  the totals/subtotal section initially crowding under the sticky glass footer - it reads clipped/overlapped
  rather than intentionally scrollable (scrolling could reveal it, but the resting layout looked wrong). The
  scroll body (`flex-1 overflow-y-auto`) had only `pb-6` (1.5rem) bottom clearance against a ~4.8rem footer
  (`px-6 py-4` + a `liquid-glass-modal-button` min-h-44px row).
- **Fix (UI-only, `modals/OrderDetailsModal.tsx`).** Gave the scroll body footer-aware bottom clearance: the
  child scroll `<div>` class changed `px-6 pb-6 pt-16` -> `px-6 pt-16 pb-24 scroll-pb-24`. `pb-24` (6rem) is
  real layout space so the last panel scrolls fully clear of the footer; `scroll-pb-24` sets
  `scroll-padding-bottom` so scroll-into-view stops above the footer. `pt-16` and `px-6` unchanged. No shared
  modal shell change was needed (the underlying double `overflow-y-auto` in `.liquid-glass-modal-content` is a
  shared concern and was deliberately not touched).
- **Preserved.** Glass footer, footer buttons (Print neutral / Split amber / Close neutral) + labels,
  payment/print/refund/split handlers, `openPaymentOnMount`, item/payment calculations, translations, modal
  sizing, and centered touch-sized actions unchanged. No hover classes added (touch-first).
- **Correction (first attempt FAILED live).** Codex re-checked at 1280x800: the `pb-24`/`scroll-pb-24` patch
  helped deep scroll but did NOT fix first-open readability - Υποσύνολο was still partly behind the footer and
  Σύνολο was off-screen until scrolling. Root cause was vertical *budget*, not bottom padding: the content
  stacked a `pt-8` (injected by the shared shell when there is no title/header) on top of the scroll body's
  `pt-16` (~6rem of top padding), the shell capped at `max-height: 92vh`, and every section used `p-6` /
  `space-y-6`, so a one-item order pushed the totals panel below the fold.
- **Corrected fix (UI-only, `modals/OrderDetailsModal.tsx`).** Reclaimed vertical budget so the one-item
  products + totals sit clear of the footer on first open, keeping `pb-24`/`scroll-pb-24`:
  - Raised usable height: shell `className` `!w-[92vw] !max-w-6xl` -> `+ !max-h-[96vh]`.
  - Removed the stacked injected top padding: `contentClassName` `p-0 overflow-hidden` -> `p-0 !pt-0
    overflow-hidden` (cancels the shell's auto `pt-8`; the absolute close button is still cleared by the scroll
    body's own `pt-16`).
  - Compacted vertical rhythm modestly: outer `space-y-6` -> `space-y-4`; all three sections `p-6` -> `p-5`;
    Order History header `mb-5` -> `mb-4`; totals panel `mt-6 ... py-5` -> `mt-4 ... py-4`; info inset cards
    `${insetPanelClass} px-4 py-4` -> `px-4 py-3`. Blurry glass, rounded panels, and premium feel kept.
- **Preserved.** Glass footer + footer buttons (Print neutral / Split amber / Close neutral) and labels,
  print/split/refund/close handlers, payment calculations, order data loading, `openPaymentOnMount`, item
  rendering, and translations unchanged. No hover classes (touch-first).
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg` confirms no `hover:`/`group-hover:`/`dark:hover:`, the scroll body keeps
  `pb-24` + `scroll-pb-24`, and the new `!max-h-[96vh]` / `!pt-0` / `p-5` / `space-y-4` compaction classes are
  present. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): after the correction,
  Orders > #00001 > Προβολή first-open shows the products card and the full totals panel (Υποσύνολο and Σύνολο
  with 18,50 €) cleanly above the sticky footer; a downward scroll brings ΙΣΤΟΡΙΚΟ ΠΑΡΑΓΓΕΛΙΩΝ and "Δεν
  υπάρχει ιστορικό πελάτη" fully above the footer as well. Note: the first `pb-24`-only patch FAILED live
  verification (first-open still clipped); the passed version uses the local `!max-h-[96vh]` / `!pt-0` height
  fix plus compact spacing (`space-y-4`, section `p-5`, totals `mt-4`/`py-4`, info cards `py-3`) and the
  `pb-24`/`scroll-pb-24` footer scroll clearance.

### Supervisor round 114 — Add-customer optional-field contradiction (ringer/floor) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding).** Delivery > Customer Search > Add New Customer: the "Name on ringer" and "Floor
  number" fields showed a red required asterisk (and blocked submit) even though the Greek labels read
  "(προαιρετικό)"/optional - a UI vs validation contradiction that confuses touchscreen staff. The modal's own
  convention is that optional fields (Email, Notes) carry an "(optional)" marker and no asterisk, while
  required fields (Full Name, Phone, Address) carry " *" + a red asterisk.
- **Fix (UI + i18n, `modals/AddCustomerModal.tsx` + 4 locale files).** Made these two fields truly optional,
  matching the founder's preferred POS UX (only phone, address, full name present as required):
  - Removed the red asterisk span from the Name-on-ringer and Floor labels (they no longer render
    `{!isSpecialAddressMode && <span class="text-red-500">*</span>}`).
  - Removed the submit-blocking validation (`nameOnRinger`/`floorNumber` required checks) so empty values no
    longer block save. `isSpecialAddressMode` is still used elsewhere (delivery-zone skip), so no dead code.
  - Aligned labels to the modal's optional convention in every locale: EL already had "(προαιρετικό)"; added
    the matching optional marker to EN ("(optional)"), DE ("(optional)"), IT ("(facoltativo)", matching IT's
    Email label), and FR ("(facultatif)"). The now-unused `nameOnRingerRequired`/`floorRequired` message keys
    were left in place (harmless; may be referenced elsewhere).
- **Preserved.** Glass modal style, rounded controls, green save/add + red cancel semantics, phone/address/
  full-name still required, the fields still submit their values when filled, and multilingual i18n parity.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  touched files + this tracker -> clean; the 5 locale JSON files parse and the ringer/floor labels now read as
  optional in all of EN/EL/DE/IT/FR; `rg` confirms no required asterisk/validation remains for ringer/floor.
  Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): reopened the live POS after HMR via

  Full Name still shows the required red asterisk; Email, Name on ringer, Floor, and Notes all read

  glass Add Customer. Cancel was clicked afterward - no customer/order data was saved. Note: save-with-empty

  production-connected.

### Supervisor round 115 — Add-customer edit-mode "Save Changes" i18n leak — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding).** Orders > New Order > Delivery > Customer Search (seeded customer) > Επεξεργασία
  Πελάτη: the modal title/fields and red Cancel were Greek, but the green primary button showed English "Save
  Changes". Root cause: the edit-mode label uses `t('modals.addCustomer.saveChanges', 'Save Changes')`, but the
  `modals.addCustomer` block had no `saveChanges` key in ANY locale, so the inline English fallback rendered -
  a latent leak in EL/DE/IT/FR (EN only looked right by coincidence of the fallback).
- **Fix (i18n only, 5 locale files).** Added `modals.addCustomer.saveChanges` (after `saveAddress`) in every
  locale, reusing each language's existing wording from sibling blocks: EN "Save Changes", EL "Αποθήκευση
  Αλλαγών", DE "Änderungen speichern", IT "Salva modifiche", FR "Enregistrer les modifications". No component
  change needed - the existing `t(...)` call now resolves the key; new/add-address modes (`save`/`saveAddress`)
  were already localized and are untouched.
- **Preserved.** Glass styling, green save/add + red cancel semantics, no hover-only effects, and no data/
  behavior changes beyond the localized label.
- Verified: locale JSON parse for all five (EN/EL/DE/IT/FR) -> OK; `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) ->
  clean (no TS touched, run for safety); `rg` confirms `modals.addCustomer.saveChanges` now exists in all five
  and that the Greek edit path resolves to "Αποθήκευση Αλλαγών" (no English "Save Changes" leak); `git diff
  --check` over the touched files + this tracker -> clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator,
  Greek/light, 1280x800): reopened the live POS after HMR via Orders -> New Order -> Delivery -> Customer Search
  (seeded customer) -> Επεξεργασία Πελάτη and scrolled to the footer. The green primary button now reads
  "Αποθήκευση Αλλαγών" with no English "Save Changes" visible; Cancel remains red glass and was clicked
  afterward - no customer/order data was saved.

### Supervisor round 116 — Address-row pencil opens address-only edit (not full customer edit) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding).** Delivery > Customer Search > seeded customer: the small amber pencil inside an
  address row is visually an address-edit action and `CustomerSearchModal` calls
  `onEditCustomer({ ...customer, editAddressId: addr.id })`, but both parent handlers opened AddCustomerModal
  in the generic full "Επεξεργασία Πελάτη" (edit) mode - duplicating the separate full "Edit Customer" button
  below and confusing staff.
- **Root cause.** AddCustomerModal already fully implements `editAddress` mode (loads only the targeted
  address via `initialCustomer.editAddressId`, "Edit Address" title, address-only save), but neither parent
  ever selected it - both `handleEditCustomer` handlers hardcoded `edit`, and both mode-state unions even
  omitted the `editAddress` literal.
- **Fix (UI/mode-selection only, 2 files).**
  - `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`: widened `customerModalMode` union to include `"editAddress"`;
    `handleEditCustomer` now sets `customer?.editAddressId ? "editAddress" : "edit"`. The modal invocation
    already forwards `editAddressId`.
  - `pages/NewOrderPage.tsx`: widened `addCustomerMode` union to include `'editAddress'`; `handleEditCustomer`
    now sets `customer?.editAddressId ? 'editAddress' : 'edit'`; and the `initialCustomer` builder now forwards
    `editAddressId: existingCustomer.editAddressId` (it was previously dropped, so address mode would have had
    no address to edit).
  - The full "Edit Customer" button passes no `editAddressId` (`onEditCustomer(customer)`), so it still opens
    full edit in both parents.
- **Preserved.** Delete/save behavior, customer data logic, glass styling, red cancel / green save semantics,
  and no hover-only effects - only the selected modal mode (and the forwarded id in NewOrderPage) changed.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `rg` confirms both parents
  select `editAddress` when `editAddressId` is present and `NewOrderPage` forwards `editAddressId`; `git diff
  --check` over the touched files + this tracker -> clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator,
  Greek/light, 1280x800): Orders -> New Order -> Delivery -> Customer Search (seeded customer). The small
  address-row pencil opens the address-scoped modal (not full customer edit) - after the round-118 label fix it
  shows title "Επεξεργασία Διεύθυνσης" and primary action "Αποθήκευση Διεύθυνσης". The separate full
  "Επεξεργασία Πελάτη" button still opens full customer edit with title "Επεξεργασία Πελάτη" and primary action
  "Αποθήκευση Αλλαγών". Cancel/close only; no data was saved.

### Supervisor round 117 — Orders sidebar pickup type-chip enlarged to the shared bag (24px) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (user request).** "Change the pickup icon in the order so it has the same [glyph] as when choosing
  order type." The Orders sidebar row's pickup order-type chip still read like a tiny storefront/stand because
  it rendered at `w-4 h-4` (16px).
- **Findings.** Two row paths render pickup icons. The dashboard grid (`OrderGrid` -> `order/OrderCard.tsx`,
  the icon under the amount) already uses the shared `PickupOrderIcon` at 24px (round 112, verified) - left
  unchanged. The sidebar `pages/OrdersPage.tsx` `getOrderTypeIcon` (the inline type chip next to the order
  number) used `PickupOrderIcon` but at the tiny `w-4 h-4`.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/OrdersPage.tsx`).** Bumped all three `getOrderTypeIcon` glyphs from `w-4 h-4` ->
  `w-6 h-6` (24px): pickup/default = `PickupOrderIcon` (shared chooser bag), delivery = `Truck`, dine-in =
  `TableOrderIcon`. A row shows one type icon, so all three were sized together for cross-row consistency.
  Color stays `currentColor` (inherited `text-zinc-300` dark / `text-gray-700` light), so it reads on both
  themes. No Store/storefront/building pickup glyph remains in either row path.
- **Preserved.** Order-taking logic, labels, handlers, delivery/table glyph identity (only sizes changed in
  the chip), and OrderCard's already-correct 24px pickup bag.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `rg` confirms pickup/
  takeaway row icons use `PickupOrderIcon` and no `Store`/building pickup icon remains; `git diff --check` over
  the touched file + this tracker -> clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light,
  1280x800): reopened the live POS after HMR on the Orders dashboard - pickup order #00001's row now shows the
  clean shared bag glyph under the amount (not a storefront/stand); opened the New Order order-type chooser and
  confirmed the Pickup card uses the same bag glyph, then closed the chooser without selecting an order type.
  No orders/customers/data were created or modified.

### Supervisor round 118 — Edit-address mode title + primary button label fix — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding on round 116).** The address-row pencil now correctly opens address-only edit mode,
  but in Greek the modal title showed English "Edit Address" and the green primary button read "Προσθήκη
  Πελάτη" (Add Customer) instead of an address-save label.
- **Root cause.** (1) `getModalTitle` uses `t('modals.addCustomer.editAddressTitle', 'Edit Address')`, but the
  `modals.addCustomer` block had no `editAddressTitle` key in any locale -> non-EN fell back to English. (2) The
  footer label ternary only branched `addAddress` and `edit`; `mode === 'editAddress'` fell through to
  `t('modals.addCustomer.save')` = "Add Customer".
- **Fix.**
  - `modals/AddCustomerModal.tsx`: the primary-button label now treats editAddress like addAddress -
    `(mode === 'addAddress' || mode === 'editAddress') ? t('modals.addCustomer.saveAddress', 'Save Address')`
    - so it reads the address-save label (EL "Αποθήκευση Διεύθυνσης"). Submit behavior unchanged (the
    editAddress save path was already correct).
  - Locales EN/EL/DE/IT/FR: added `modals.addCustomer.editAddressTitle` after `addAddressTitle` - EN "Edit
    Address", EL "Επεξεργασία Διεύθυνσης", DE "Adresse bearbeiten", IT "Modifica indirizzo", FR "Modifier
    l'adresse". Existing `saveAddress`/`saveChanges`/`addAddressTitle` keys preserved.
- **Preserved.** Glass styling, red cancel / green save semantics, submit/data behavior, and the round-116
  mode routing - only the title key + the editAddress button-label branch changed.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); the 5 locale JSON files parse
  and `editAddressTitle` exists in all five; `rg` confirms the editAddress primary button now resolves to
  `saveAddress` (no fall-through to the add-customer `save` label); `git diff --check` over the touched files +
  this tracker -> clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): via Orders ->
  New Order -> Delivery -> Customer Search (seeded customer), the address-row pencil's edit-address modal no
  longer shows English "Edit Address" and no longer falls through to "Προσθήκη Πελάτη" - the title reads
  "Επεξεργασία Διεύθυνσης" and the green primary button reads "Αποθήκευση Διεύθυνσης". The full "Επεξεργασία
  Πελάτη" button remains title "Επεξεργασία Πελάτη" / action "Αποθήκευση Αλλαγών". Cancel/close only; no data
  was saved.

### Supervisor round 119 — Users page row action semantics + touch targets — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding).** On the Users page table, the View and Ban row action icons read as the same
  neutral/gold action, and the Ban action (destructive) did not read red. The icons were also tiny floating
  glyphs without a proper centered touch target.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/UsersPage.tsx`, table row actions only).**
  - View/details button: kept neutral/amber (`text-amber-500`), now a centered 40x40 icon button.
  - Ban action (`user.is_banned === false`): destructive **red** (`text-red-600 dark:text-red-400` +
    `active:bg-red-500/10`) - was orange.
  - Unban action (`user.is_banned === true`): **green** success (`text-green-600 dark:text-green-400` +
    `active:bg-green-500/10`) - kept.
  - Both buttons are now `inline-flex h-10 w-10 items-center justify-center rounded-xl` (40x40, icon centered
    vertically/horizontally, smooth rounded), icons bumped to `w-5 h-5`. Touch feedback only:
    `transition-transform active:scale-[0.96]` + an `active:bg-*/10` press tint; no hover classes on these
    buttons.
- **Preserved.** Layout (same `flex items-center gap-2` actions cell), labels/titles, `handleViewUser` /
  `handleToggleBan` handlers, the `user.type !== 'app_user'` gate, the conditional `Ban`/`CheckCircle` icon,
  and all data calls - no new mutations or customer-record changes. Rest of the Users page not redesigned.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `rg` confirms the row action
  buttons no longer use `hover:` classes (active/tap feedback only); `git diff --check` over the touched files
  + this tracker -> clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): the live Users
  page table now shows View/details as amber and Ban/block as red/destructive in each row; both are centered
  40x40 rounded touch targets with active-only feedback and no hover classes in the changed row-action block.
  No customer data was changed and no block/unblock actions were clicked.

### Supervisor round 120 — Users customer-details + address-delete modals: glass + semantic controls — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding).** The Users "Customer Details" modal read flat/vibecoded: plain white/gray shell
  (not glass), orange footer Ban button, blue address edit controls + blue focus rings + blue/hover suggestion
  dropdown; the address-delete confirmation modal was a flat dialog with hover classes.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/UsersPage.tsx`, two modal blocks only).**

    Unban stays **green** (`bg-green-600 active:bg-green-700`).

    red-tinted destructive-cancel (`bg-red-500/10 border-red-500/30 text-red-600`); both icon+text centered,
    `rounded-xl`, `active:scale-[0.98]`.

    centered 40x40 (`inline-flex h-10 w-10 items-center justify-center rounded-xl`) with `active:bg-*/10
    active:scale-[0.96]`.
  - Inputs/textarea: blue focus rings -> `focus:ring-yellow-500` (neutral glass fields kept).
  - Address suggestions dropdown: `hover:bg-blue-500/10` -> `active:bg-yellow-500/10` (neutral/yellow tap).
  - Address-delete modal: glass shell; **Delete** red (`active:bg-red-700`); **Cancel** neutral glass
    (`bg-white/5`÷`bg-black/5`, not flat) so it doesn't compete with the solid-red Delete.
  - Footer Close + all touched buttons: hover removed, active/tap feedback only (`active:scale` / `active:bg`).
- **Preserved.** Layout, all handlers (`handleSaveAddress`/`handleCancelEdit`/`handleEditAddress`/
  `handleDeleteAddress`/`handleToggleBan`/`confirmDeleteAddress`/`closeDetailsModal`), state shape,
  translations, and data/API calls - color/shape/feedback only. Users table/page outside these two blocks not

- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; scoped `rg` over the two modal blocks shows no `blue`/`orange`/`hover:`
  classes remain. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): the Users -> Customer

### Supervisor round 121 — UsersPage remaining hover cleanup (non-modal controls) — COMPLETED

    `active:scale-95` (touch press).
  - Filter trigger, status + loyalty filter options, clear-filters, table row: `hover:bg-white/10` /

    filter option keeps its `bg-yellow-400 text-black` highlight; loyalty tier / status colors untouched.
  - Pagination prev/next: `hover:bg-yellow-400 hover:text-black` -> `active:bg-yellow-400 active:text-black`
    (keeps the yellow fill-on-press accent; disabled opacity states unchanged).
- **Preserved.** Handlers, data calls, table columns, translations, state, and the round-119/120 row-action +
  modal designs - only the `hover:` -> `active:` swap on these controls. No table layout rewrite.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `rg "hover:|group-hover:"`

### Supervisor round 122 — Card Machines Add Terminal modal: neutral glass inner panels — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding).** Settings > Card Machines > Setup > Add Terminal: the modal shell was fine, but
  the two inner section panels ("Connection Details" and "Settings") read as a blue/slate block, and form
  controls used small `rounded-lg` corners - off the black/white/grey/yellow foundation.
- **Root cause.** Both panels used the shared `liquid-glass-modal-card` class, which bakes a slate tint
  (`rgba(241,245,249,0.8)` light / `rgba(30,41,59,0.8)` dark) into the surface - so it reads bluish even though
  no blue/slate Tailwind class is present.
- **Fix (UI-only, `ecr/TerminalConfigModal.tsx`).**
  - Both inner panels: `rounded-lg liquid-glass-modal-card` -> neutral translucent glass `rounded-2xl border
    border-black/10 dark:border-white/10 bg-white/40 dark:bg-white/5 backdrop-blur-sm` (existing `p-4`/`space-y-4`
    kept; padding unchanged since `p-4` already overrode the card class's padding).
  - All inputs/selects: `rounded-lg` -> `rounded-xl` (compact layout + amber focus rings preserved).
  - Footer Cancel (neutral) and Add/Save (amber) buttons: `rounded-lg` -> `rounded-xl`; both stay centered
    (`inline-flex items-center justify-center`) with `active:scale-[0.98]` touch feedback.
- **Preserved.** All behavior, handlers, validation, translations, form fields, and save payloads unchanged;
  no fields added/removed; no terminals created/saved. Amber/yellow accent + focus rings kept; no blue/purple/
  cyan/pink/orange introduced; no hover classes (file was already hover-free). Existing scroll/footer behavior
  intact for 1280x800.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg` over `TerminalConfigModal.tsx` shows no `hover:`/`group-hover:` and no
  `blue`/`cyan`/`sky`/`purple`/`pink`/`violet`/`fuchsia`/`orange` color classes. Live visual sign-off PASSED
  (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): the Settings -> Card Machines -> Setup -> Add Terminal modal now has
  neutral translucent inner panels instead of the earlier blue/slate card tint; inputs/selects/buttons are
  smoother rounded; the checkbox settings render as the same glass toggle rows used elsewhere; the lower
  settings section is reachable by scrolling and not clipped by the footer. Canceled the modal only - no
  terminal was created/saved and no device data changed. Independent checks re-confirmed: `npm run type-check`
  clean, `git diff --check` clean, and the `TerminalConfigModal.tsx` audit has no hover/group-hover, no
  off-palette color classes, no `rounded-lg`, and no `liquid-glass-modal-card`.

  knob). Unconditional dark-surface classes (no `dark:`) because this modal is dark-styled throughout (no
