### Supervisor round 124 — Pickup icon consistency: shared PickupOrderIcon -> lucide Package — COMPLETED

- **Problem.** Pickup glyphs had drifted: `forms/OrderTypeSelector.tsx` used lucide `Package` for pickup, while
  the shared `PickupOrderIcon` (used by Orders rows + OrderDashboard/OrderFlow choosers + OrderCard) wrapped
  lucide `ShoppingBag` - so the order-row pickup icon did not match the chooser's Package glyph.
- **Fix (UI-only).**
  - `components/icons/PickupOrderIcon.tsx`: now renders lucide `Package` instead of `ShoppingBag`, preserving
    `currentColor` and the `className`/`size`/`color`/`strokeWidth` props. Default `strokeWidth` changed
    `1.6 -> 2` (lucide's normal chooser weight, not thin).
  - `components/forms/OrderTypeSelector.tsx`: pickup now uses `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-6 h-6" />` instead
    of importing/using `Package` directly (removed the now-unused `Package` import; `Truck` kept).
    `TableOrderIcon` and `Truck` unchanged.
  - `pages/OrdersPage.tsx`: unchanged - it already uses `PickupOrderIcon`, so the order-row pickup icon now
    becomes Package through the shared component. Empty-state `ShoppingBag`, order-row layout, labels, fetching,
    filters, and behavior untouched.
  - Net effect: every pickup consumer of `PickupOrderIcon` (Orders rows, OrderDashboard/OrderFlow choosers,
    OrderCard, OrderTypeSelector) now renders the identical lucide Package glyph.
- **Preserved.** No data/behavior/layout/label/translation changes; only the shared pickup glyph + the
  OrderTypeSelector call site.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  touched files + this tracker -> clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800):
  the live Orders page now shows the pickup row icon as the same lucide Package/cube glyph used in the
  order-type chooser; opened the chooser via the yellow plus and confirmed the Παραλαβή pickup card and the
  Orders row use the same package/cube icon at matching normal stroke weight. Closed the chooser with X only -
  no order type selected, no order created, no data changed. Independent checks passed: `npm run type-check`
  clean, `git diff --check` clean for the tracked touched files + tracker, and scoped `rg` confirms
  `PickupOrderIcon` renders `Package` while `OrderTypeSelector` pickup uses `PickupOrderIcon`.

### Supervisor round 125 — Data recovery panel height + database section footer clearance — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding, 1280x800).** Settings > Data: the RecoveryPanel list/detail area was too tall for
  the first viewport (selected detail text sat on the modal bottom edge); and in the database section the
  Repair & sync tools button ("Διόρθωση ουράς") was clipped by the modal bottom after scrolling.
- **Fix (UI-only, two files).**
  - `recovery/RecoveryPanel.tsx`: **Correction — the first `VISIBLE_RECOVERY_POINTS` 5 -> 4 patch FAILED live**
    (the fixed 456px cap was still taller than the space the 92vh modal leaves at 1280x800, so the detail pane
    still ran into the modal bottom). Corrected with a **viewport-aware** cap: a new
    `RECOVERY_PANEL_MAX_HEIGHT_STYLE = clamp(240px, calc(100vh - 520px), ${RECOVERY_PANEL_MAX_HEIGHT}px)` is now
    used as the `maxHeight` for **both** the list and detail panes (kept identical so the columns stay aligned).
    At 800px tall this resolves to ~280px (fits above the modal bottom); on taller screens it rises to the
    fixed N-card ceiling, never below a 240px floor. Both panes remain internally scrollable.
    `VISIBLE_RECOVERY_POINTS` stays 4 (it sets the clamp ceiling). Smoothed controls: the six top/export/restore
    buttons `inline-flex items-center gap-2 rounded-lg` -> `inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2
    rounded-xl`; the selected recovery list card `w-full rounded-lg` -> `rounded-2xl`. No hover (already
    active-only).
  - `modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`: database-section bottom clearance only - the section's inner content
    wrapper `flex flex-col gap-4 pb-6` -> `pb-24`, so the repair + danger-zone rows/buttons scroll fully above
    the modal bottom (shared `detailScrollRef` and other sections untouched). Database repair (yellow) + danger
    (red) action buttons `rounded-lg` -> `rounded-xl` via the database-button-only class run; semantic yellow
    repair / red destructive colors preserved.
- **Preserved.** All recovery + database handlers, data/actions, labels, translations, and layout - only
  max-height, padding, and rounding changed. No recovery/database action was triggered.
- Verified (after correction): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff
  --check` over the touched files + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|group-hover:"` over `RecoveryPanel.tsx`
  and the database section -> **0**; both recovery panes now use the `clamp(...)` viewport-aware maxHeight. Live
  visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): after the corrected viewport-aware clamp,
  Settings > Data first-open shows the RecoveryPanel list/detail card boundaries fully above the modal bottom,
  and long selected-detail content stays inside its own internal scroll instead of being clipped by the Settings
  modal. Scrolling the outer Data section from the header area brings the Repair & sync tools rows and the full
  Danger zone - including the final Factory reset row/button - fully above the modal bottom with clear padding.
  No recovery/database actions were clicked or triggered and no data changed. Independent checks passed:
  `npm run type-check` clean, `git diff --check` clean for touched files + tracker, RecoveryPanel hover audit 0,
  and code confirms both panes use `RECOVERY_PANEL_MAX_HEIGHT_STYLE` clamp plus database `pb-24` and rounded-xl
  DB action buttons.

### Supervisor round 126 — Settings > Connection form geometry + semantic Save — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding, 1280x800).** Settings > Connection had broken form geometry: the text inputs/textarea
  rendered as narrow controls on the left while the POS-key eye button sat far to the right of the card
  (detached from the input). The action row read as generic buttons - Save was not a semantic save action.
- **Root cause.** `.liquid-glass-modal-input` (globals.css) sets no `width`, so the fields rendered at intrinsic
  (narrow) width; the eye button is `absolute right-*` on the full-width `relative` wrapper, so it pinned to the
  card edge instead of the narrow input's edge.
- **Fix (UI-only, `modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`, `activeSettingsSection === 'connection'` only).**
  - All four fields (connection-code textarea, Register ID, Dashboard address, POS key) now `w-full`, so the
    form is a clean full-width single-column panel inside the settings content. (Scoped per-field, not the
    shared CSS class, so other sections are unaffected.)
  - POS-key visibility button: now sits inside the input at the right edge as a ~40px centered touch target
    (`absolute right-1.5 inline-flex h-10 w-10 items-center justify-center rounded-lg`, icon `w-5 h-5`,
    `active:scale-95` + `active:bg-white/15`, no hover); input padding bumped `pr-10 -> pr-12` so text never
    collides with the icon.
  - Action row: Paste keeps its Clipboard icon (neutral); Test + Sync stay neutral via the shared button helper
    (centered by the `liquid-glass-modal-button` base). Save is now semantic green glass
    (`bg-green-500/20 text-green-700 dark:text-green-300 border-green-500/40`, centered `inline-flex` with a
    `Check` icon) instead of the yellow primary. No cancel button invented.
- **Preserved.** All handlers exactly (`handlePasteBoth`/`handleTest`/`handleManualPolicySync`/
  `handleSaveConnection`), labels/translations (no new visible strings - Save still uses
  `modals.connectionSettings.save`; the Check icon adds no text), the blurry glass shell, and no-hover
  touch-first feedback.
- **Correction — code verification passed but the first live visual FAILED.** At 1280x800 the field-width/eye
  fix was correct, but the section was now too tall for first open: the green Save (bottom action row) sat
  against/under the modal bottom edge. Reworked the connection vertical layout to fit cleanly without clipping:
  - Section spacing tightened (`py-4 space-y-3` -> `pt-4 pb-5 space-y-2.5`); label/help margins `mb-2`/`mb-3` ->
    `mb-1`/`mb-2`; connection-code textarea `min-h-[90px]` -> `min-h-[72px]`.
  - Register ID + Dashboard address moved into a responsive two-column row (`grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2
    gap-3`) - one row on desktop instead of two stacked fields - while POS key stays full-width. Fields remain
    `w-full`; single-column on the narrowest layouts for readability.
  - Action row `pt-2` -> `items-center pt-1`; Test + Sync given explicit `inline-flex items-center
    justify-center` so all four buttons are centered icon/text; Save stays semantic green glass with the Check
    icon; Paste keeps Clipboard; section `pb-5` gives the row clear bottom clearance.
- **Preserved.** All handlers exactly (`handlePasteBoth`/`handleTest`/`handleManualPolicySync`/
  `handleSaveConnection`), labels/translations (no new visible strings - Save still uses
  `modals.connectionSettings.save`; the Check icon adds no text), the blurry glass shell, the full-width fields
  + eye-inside-input behavior, and no-hover touch-first feedback.
- Verified (after correction): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff
  --check` over the touched files + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` -> **0**. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800):
  Settings > Connection shows the full-width connection-code textarea, Register ID + Dashboard in a compact
  two-column row, the POS-key eye icon inside the input, a neutral Paste/Test/Sync row, and the green Save
  button fully visible with clear bottom clearance. The earlier clipped-Save rejection was corrected by this
  compact layout. No buttons/actions were clicked except navigation; no data changed.

### Supervisor round 127 — Reservations create/service modals: glass + green/red action semantics — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding, 1280x800).** Reservations > Δημιουργία: the page was fine but the Create Reservation
  modal read flat/opaque/vibecoded (mostly solid grey-white shell), with a neutral Cancel and a solid yellow
  Create. User preference: all modals should be translucent blurry glass; save/create = green, cancel = red.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/verticals/restaurant/ReservationsView.tsx`, the `showCreateModal` +
  `showServiceModal` blocks).**
  - Shells were already `backdrop-blur-2xl` glass but fairly opaque; nudged transparency to the established
    pattern: light `bg-white/80 -> bg-white/75`, dark `bg-gray-900/80 -> bg-gray-900/70` (both modals share the
    class). The separate reservation detail drawer (`/85`) was left untouched (out of scope).
  - Footer semantics (both modals): **Cancel** is now red glass (`bg-red-500/15 text-red-600 dark:text-red-300
    border border-red-500/30 active:bg-red-500/25`, dropped the grey `isDark` ternary); **Create** is now green
    glass (`bg-green-500/20 text-green-700 dark:text-green-300 border border-green-500/40 active:bg-green-500/30`)
    instead of the solid yellow primary. Disabled state (`disabled:opacity-60`) and centered `inline-flex`
    layout kept; text-only via existing i18n keys (no new strings; no icons added).
  - `modalInputClass` was already translucent (`bg-gray-800/70`÷`bg-white/70`) and matches the glass shell -
    left as-is so all controls stay readable.
- **Preserved.** All handlers (`handleCreateReservation`/`handleCreateServiceBooking`/`closeServiceModal`/
  `setShowCreateModal`), data logic, validation/disabled, and i18n. No hover utilities (file is active-only).
  Order-taking dashboard untouched.
- **Correction — first live visual FAILED.** Codex flagged the first patch: the red/green buttons were too
  washed out (low-contrast text on the light glass) and the shell still read like a grey solid panel. Corrected
  while staying translucent glass (both modals):
  - Cancel: `bg-red-500/15 text-red-600 dark:text-red-300 border-red-500/30` -> `bg-red-500/20 text-red-800
    dark:text-red-200 border-red-500/45 active:bg-red-500/30` (darker light-theme text + stronger tint/border).
  - Create: `bg-green-500/20 text-green-700 dark:text-green-300 border-green-500/40` -> `bg-green-500/25
    text-green-900 dark:text-green-200 border-green-600/45 active:bg-green-500/35`.
  - Shell: more translucent + a glass rim - light `bg-white/75 border-white/60` -> `bg-white/60 border-white/70
    ring-1 ring-white/50`; dark `bg-gray-900/70` -> `bg-gray-900/60 ... ring-1 ring-white/10`; `backdrop-blur-2xl`
    + `shadow-2xl` kept. No decorative blobs/gradients.
- **Preserved.** All handlers (`handleCreateReservation`/`handleCreateServiceBooking`/`closeServiceModal`/
  `setShowCreateModal`), data logic, validation/disabled, and i18n. No hover utilities (file is active-only).
  Order-taking dashboard untouched.
- **Second correction — live visual still FAILED (dark-variant override).** The button text was still pale on
  the light glass: the modal renders through a body-level portal, so the `dark:text-*` variants could apply
  even while the shell drew its light branch (DOM `.dark` vs the JS `isDark` desync), overriding
  `text-red-800`/`text-green-900`. Fix: removed all `dark:` variants from both footer buttons and drove every
  button colour from the same `isDark` ternary the shell uses (both modals):
  - Cancel: `${isDark ? 'bg-red-500/25 text-red-200 border-red-400/40 active:bg-red-500/35' : 'bg-red-500/20
    text-red-900 border-red-500/50 active:bg-red-500/30'}` (+ static `border`).
  - Create: `${isDark ? 'bg-green-500/25 text-green-200 border-green-500/45 active:bg-green-500/35' :
    'bg-green-500/25 text-green-900 border-green-600/50 active:bg-green-500/35'}` (+ static `border
    disabled:opacity-60`).
  - Light mode now always gets high-contrast dark text (`text-red-900`/`text-green-900`) with no `dark:` to
    override it; dark mode gets readable light red/green. Still translucent glass; shell left as the previously
    corrected `ring-1` glass.
- **Preserved.** All handlers (`handleCreateReservation`/`handleCreateServiceBooking`/`closeServiceModal`/
  `setShowCreateModal`), data logic, validation/disabled, and i18n. No hover utilities (file is active-only).
  Order-taking dashboard untouched.
- Verified (after 2nd correction): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git
  diff --check` over the touched files + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `ReservationsView.tsx` -> **0**. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): the
  Create Reservation modal shows a frosted translucent glass shell with readable inputs, red glass Cancel and
  green glass Create with high-contrast text, and no clipping. The earlier washed-out button failures were
  fixed by the second correction (removed `dark:` footer variants, drove colours from the `isDark` ternary).
  Navigation/open/close only - no create/save action and no data changed.

### Supervisor round 128 — Services Add/Edit modal glass + switch + green/red actions — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live finding, 1280x800).** Services > Add Service modal failed the design standard: solid white
  shell, native yellow checkbox with an oversized tick, neutral top + footer Cancel, a black/grey Save, and
  hover classes throughout the file.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/verticals/salon/ServiceCatalogView.tsx`).**
  - Add/Edit modal shell -> translucent blurry glass: `rounded-xl ... bg-zinc-950 / bg-white` -> `rounded-2xl
    backdrop-blur-2xl ring-1` with `bg-zinc-900/70 border-white/10 ring-white/10` (dark) / `bg-white/75
    border-white/60 ring-white/50` (light) + `shadow-2xl`.
  - Inputs/select/textarea (5 fields): translucent glass fit - `bg-zinc-900/60 border-white/10` ÷ `bg-white/70
    border-gray-200/70`, `rounded-lg -> rounded-xl`, yellow focus ring kept.
  - Active checkbox -> glass switch row: native `<input className="accent-yellow-400">` replaced with an
    `sr-only peer` checkbox + a sibling glass track (`peer-checked:bg-yellow-400/90` yellow ON, neutral grey
    OFF, white `after:` knob, `peer-checked:after:translate-x-6`). The real `<input checked onChange>` binding
    is preserved verbatim; no native oversized tick.
  - Footer: Cancel -> red glass, Save -> green glass, both via `isDark` ternary (no `dark:` variants, per the
    round-127 portal/`dark:` lesson) so light-mode text is high-contrast (`text-red-900`/`text-green-900`);
    `disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed` kept so disabled is obvious/not clickable. Footer Cancel
    retained.
  - Top-right Cancel link -> neutral close `X` icon button (lucide `X` imported), `aria-label` from existing
    `common.close`, no new visible string, active feedback.
  - Removed ALL hover utilities in the file (toolbar/list controls + delete-modal buttons): blanket
    `hover:bg-* -> active:bg-*` and `hover:text-* -> active:text-*` (color suffixes preserved). Delete modal's
    red confirm/neutral cancel kept (hover -> active only).
- **Preserved.** Handlers (`handleSubmitService`/`handleDeleteService`/`closeServiceModal`), validation
  (`canSubmitService`), form state, submit behavior, and i18n. No data action.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `ServiceCatalogView.tsx` -> **0**.
  Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): the Services Add Service modal shows a
  frosted glass shell with blur, readable glass inputs, a modern yellow Active switch, red Cancel and green Save
  actions, and a centered X close, with no clipping. Static checks re-confirmed: `npm run type-check` clean,
  scoped `git diff --check` clean, and the `rg` hover audit over `ServiceCatalogView.tsx` returned 0 matches.

  lucide `ShoppingBag` (the user-approved bag). Because every pickup consumer uses this one shared component,
  the order row (OrderCard via OrderGrid, ~24px), the New Order chooser (OrderDashboard/OrderFlow),
  OrderTypeSelector, and the OrdersPage badge all update identically and stay in sync. `strokeWidth` default 2,
  size/color props, and `currentColor` behaviour unchanged.

  consume the shared component, so row size stays ~24px and table/delivery icons are untouched). No hover, no
  data/order-taking/checkout changes, translations intact.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  changed file + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `PickupOrderIcon.tsx` ->

  storefront/box look), and the order-type chooser's pickup card shows the same bag glyph; chooser closed, no

### Supervisor round 133 — TableActionModal live-QA correction (real frosted shell + real yellow primary) — COMPLETED

- **Why (Round 131 failed live, 1280x800 Greek/light).** Round 131 claimed the shell was glass and the New Order
  button was yellow, but Codex/operator live QA on the running POS (Tables -> B03 available) still saw a **flat
  solid white panel** and a **dark grey/black** primary action - the source said yellow but the live pixels did
  not. Root cause: (1) the light shell at `bg-white/65` is too opaque to reveal the blurred grid through

  cream) whose only colour was `text-yellow-700` dark amber text - the eye reads "pale card + dark text" = grey,
  not a yellow primary.

    grid bleeds through), crisper rim `border-white/70 ring-white/60`, deeper soft shadow `shadow-2xl
    shadow-black/30`. Dark nudged `bg-gray-900/65` -> `bg-gray-900/60` with `ring-white/15` + `shadow-black/50`
    (kept readable). `backdrop-blur-2xl` retained. No decorative blobs/orbs.
  - New Order = unmistakable app-yellow primary in light: `!bg-yellow-400 border-yellow-500 active:!bg-yellow-500
    shadow-lg shadow-yellow-500/30` (the `!` defeats any Tailwind opacity/remap that washed it grey), with
    near-black content for contrast - icon chip `bg-black/10`, ShoppingCart/title/arrow `text-gray-900`,
    description `text-gray-700`. Dark keeps the premium yellow glass (`bg-yellow-400/15 border-yellow-400/40
    active:bg-yellow-400/25`, `text-yellow-300`). Disabled state unchanged.
  - Reservation kept neutral warm glass (pale `amber-50` outline, secondary to the solid-yellow primary); status
    buttons kept semantic; occupied stays red (round 131). No blue tokens.
- **Preserved.** `onNewOrder`/`onNewReservation`, status changes, guest count, Escape/backdrop close, `aria-*`,
  and i18n all unchanged. Touch-only feedback (`active:`/`active:scale-[0.98]`), no hover/dark:hover/group-hover.
  No table/order/reservation data created or changed.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:|blue"` over `TableActionModal.tsx` -> **0**.

  action (~479). So the live Tables modal stayed flat-white with a blue/grey primary regardless of the
  TableActionModal edits.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/TablesPage.tsx` StatusChangeModal block).**
  - Shell -> real frosted glass (same treatment as round 133's TableActionModal): light `bg-white` ->
    `bg-white/50 border-white/70 ring-white/60 shadow-2xl shadow-black/30 backdrop-blur-2xl`; dark `bg-gray-900`
    -> `bg-gray-900/60 border-white/10 ring-white/15 shadow-2xl shadow-black/50 backdrop-blur-2xl`. Kept
    `rounded-2xl`. Now the blurred grid shows through instead of a solid white card.
  - New Order / View Order action de-blued: removed `bg-blue-500 text-white active:bg-blue-600`. Light ->
    `!bg-yellow-400 border-yellow-500 text-black active:!bg-yellow-500 shadow-lg shadow-yellow-500/30` (the `!`
    defeats any opacity/remap that washed it grey); dark -> yellow glass `bg-yellow-400/15 border-yellow-400/40
    text-yellow-300 active:bg-yellow-400/25`. Added a base `border` so each branch's border colour applies;
    disabled branches given explicit `border-white/10`÷`border-gray-200`. Receipt icon + label inherit the button
    text colour (black on yellow / yellow-300 on glass).
  - Reservation action left neutral (`bg-white/10`÷`bg-gray-100`, no blue); status buttons keep their inline
    `STATUS_COLORS` semantic colours (no Tailwind blue); selected-card/table-grid neon out of scope.
- **Preserved.** `onNewOrder(table)`, `onNewReservation(table)`, `handleStatusClick`, `onClose`,
  `isNewOrderActionDisabled`, and `orderCreationDisabledMessage` (disabled state/title/aria) all unchanged. The
  View-vs-New label switch on `table.currentOrderId` is intact. Touch-only feedback (`active:`); no
  hover/dark:hover/group-hover in this block. No table behaviour, filters, data hooks, routing, or i18n touched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` and `rg "bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue"`
  over `TablesPage.tsx` -> no matches inside the StatusChangeModal block (any hits elsewhere reported separately,
  not mass-edited). Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light, 1280x800): Tables -> B03 opens the
  actual TablesPage StatusChangeModal with a translucent frosted glass shell where the blurred table grid shows
  through; the Νέα Παραγγελία button is clearly app-yellow with dark text (not blue/grey); Κράτηση remains
  neutral; status buttons remain semantic. Closed with X only - no new order/reservation/status/data action.
  Static checks re-confirmed: `npm run type-check` clean, scoped `git diff --check` clean, `rg` hover audit over
  `TablesPage.tsx` -> 0, and the New Order/View Order blue classes are gone from the StatusChangeModal block (the

### Supervisor round 135 — TablesPage remaining off-palette blue cleanup — COMPLETED

- **Why.** Round 134 left five known out-of-scope blue tokens elsewhere in `pages/TablesPage.tsx` (flagged in
  that entry). This round clears them so the whole Tables page follows the white/black/grey/yellow palette, while
  leaving the approved nav/order-tab neon exceptions and the inline `STATUS_COLORS` semantics intact.

  - Selected table card (~149): `border-blue-500 ring-2 ring-blue-500/30` -> `border-yellow-400 ring-2
    ring-yellow-400/40` (selected = yellow ring; unselected neutral border unchanged).
  - FilterDropdown "all" selected chip (~319): `bg-blue-500 text-white` -> `bg-yellow-400 text-black`. The other
    status chips keep their inline `STATUS_COLORS` backgrounds + white text (untouched).
  - Loading spinner (~735): `border-blue-500` -> `border-yellow-400` (keeps `border-t-transparent` spin).
  - Active filter button (~816-817) + its icon (~819): `bg-blue-500 text-white` -> `bg-yellow-400 text-black`,
    `border-blue-500` -> `border-yellow-500`, and the `Filter` icon's active colour `text-white` -> `text-black`
    for contrast on yellow.
  - Empty-state Clear Filters button (~957): `border-blue-500 text-blue-500 active:bg-blue-500/10` ->
    `border-yellow-500 text-yellow-600 active:bg-yellow-500/10` (outline yellow secondary).
- **Preserved.** No modal behaviour, table data hooks, status update logic, order/reservation handlers, routing,
  filter logic, or translations touched. Semantic status colours intact (available green, reserved yellow/amber,
  occupied, cleaning grey, maintenance orange, unavailable grey - all via inline `STATUS_COLORS`). Nav/order-tab
  neon exceptions untouched. Touch-only feedback (`active:`); no hover utilities added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) -> **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the
  file + this tracker -> clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `TablesPage.tsx` -> **0**; full blue
  audit `rg "bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|ring-blue|active:bg-blue|focus:ring-blue|shadow-blue"` over

### Supervisor round 136 — OrderDashboard table-dashboard de-blue source cleanup — COMPLETED

- **Why.** The Orders → Tables dashboard rendered by `components/OrderDashboard.tsx` (the in-Orders table grid,
  separate from `pages/TablesPage.tsx` cleared in rounds 134–135) still carried brand-blue tokens at eight sites,
  so the in-Orders table view drifted off the white/black/grey/yellow palette. This round clears them, leaving the
  approved order-type chooser Table card neon as the single intentional blue exception.
- **Fix (UI-only, `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Two de-blue rules, matching the existing convention:
  - **Occupied = zinc/near-black** (mirrors `STATUS_COLORS.occupied: '#27272a'` in `pages/TablesPage.tsx` so both
    table views read identically):
    - `tableStatusConfig.occupied` (~663-670): card `border-blue-300 bg-blue-50/95` ÷
      `border-blue-400/45 bg-blue-500/10` → zinc equivalents; badge blue → `border-zinc-300 bg-zinc-200
      text-zinc-700` ÷ `border-zinc-500/30 bg-zinc-400/10 text-zinc-200`; accent `bg-blue-500` → `bg-zinc-800`
      (≈ #27272a); value `text-blue-600 dark:text-blue-300` → `text-zinc-700 dark:text-zinc-200`.
    - Occupied-since chip (~5969): `border-blue-400/20 bg-blue-500/10 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-200` → zinc
      equivalents (chip = occupancy time, so it follows the occupied tone).
  - **Selected / active UI accent = app-yellow with black text** (Round 135 convention; yellow-400 needs black,
    not white, for AA contrast):
    - View-mode toggle List/2D selected (~5585, 5599): `bg-blue-600 text-white` → `bg-yellow-400 text-black`.
    - Status filter selected (~5624): `bg-blue-600 text-white shadow-lg shadow-blue-600/20` → `bg-yellow-400
      text-black shadow-lg shadow-yellow-500/20`.
    - Floor filter selected — "all" (~5671) + per-floor option (~5686): `bg-blue-600 text-white` →
      `bg-yellow-400 text-black`.
    - Table-card focus ring (~5779): `focus:ring-blue-400/45` → `focus:ring-yellow-400/45`.
    - Open-check action branch (~6014): `bg-blue-600 text-white active:bg-blue-500` → `bg-yellow-400 text-black
      active:bg-yellow-500` (the sibling New-order branch keeps its emerald = new/go semantic; Pay/Reserve stays
      neutral).
    - Dark FAB glow (~6102): `shadow-blue-500/30` → `shadow-yellow-500/30`.
- **Preserved.** Approved order-type chooser Table card (~6192-6216) blue/neon left untouched — it is the only
  surviving blue audit hit (card line 6196 + its comment 6192). All handlers and behaviour unchanged:
  `setTableViewMode`, `setTableStatusFilter`, `setTableFloorFilter`, `handleTableSelect`, `handleTableReserve`,
  `handleNewOrderClick`, the `hasOpenCheck`/`isReservedTable` label+icon switches, FAB disabled/movable state,
  occupancy stats, filters, routing, and i18n. Semantic status hues kept (available emerald, reserved amber,
  cleaning/unavailable slate, maintenance orange, attention amber-600). Touch-only feedback (`active:`); no hover
  utilities added. OrderTabsBar and NavigationSidebar not touched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `OrderDashboard.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `OrderDashboard.tsx` →
  **0**; blue audit `rg "bg-blue|text-blue|border-blue|ring-blue|active:bg-blue|focus:ring-blue|shadow-blue|from-blue|to-blue"`
  over `OrderDashboard.tsx` → only line 6196 (approved Table card); case-insensitive `rg -i blue` → only 6192
  (comment) + 6196 (card). Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator independently verified in the running POS,
  Greek/light, 1280x800): Orders dashboard Tables tab — List/2D selected states are yellow with black text/icon,
  no blue; status filter selected state is yellow/black, and applying the Occupied filter (harmless filter-only
  action) showed no blue; floor filter selected state is yellow/black. The order-type chooser was reopened and the
  approved Table card remains blue/neon with the table/chairs icon, while the Pickup and Delivery cards stay

### Supervisor round 137 — Printer Settings Add Printer form i18n leak + preview badge/detail i18n + hidden inner scrollbars + masked preview-edge strip — COMPLETED (1st & 2nd live passes partial-failed, corrected; 3rd pass PASSED)

- **Why (live finding, Greek/light, 1280x800).** Settings → Εκτυπωτές → Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή → Προχωρημένες
  Ρυθμίσεις → Προσθήκη Εκτυπωτή rendered four English helper/status strings in Greek mode, and the manual form
  column showed a bright default white vertical scrollbar that clashed with the dark blurry-glass modal. Root
  cause of the i18n leak: the strings were already keyed in JSX via `t('settings.printer.<key>', '<English
  fallback>')`, but the keys did **not exist in any locale file** (`en/el/de/fr/it`), so i18next silently
  returned the English fallback in every language. (The sibling `startQuickSetup` key was present + translated,
  which is why the wizard button was fine.)
- **Fix 1 — i18n (UI-only, `components/modals/PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` already referenced the keys; added the
  missing keys to all five locales under `settings.printer`).** Added in `en/el/de/fr/it`, placed right after
  `startQuickSetup`:
  - `quickSetupFirstTitle` ("Quick setup first")
  - `quickSetupFirstBody` ("Use the verification wizard for the normal path. This form is for manual connection
    entry and expert overrides.")
  - `verificationSummaryNew` ("New manual profiles start unverified until the wizard confirms a working path.")
  - `discoveredNotPrintable` ("Discovered, not yet printable. Run the wizard to verify a working path.")
  Translations reuse each locale's established wording (e.g. el "Οδηγού Επαλήθευσης", de
  "Verifizierungsassistent", fr "assistant de vérification", it "procedura guidata di verifica"). The JSX still
  carries the same English string as the `t()` fallback — now used only as the en source, not as a live leak in
  other languages. No JSX text/keys changed (the modal already pointed at these keys).
- **Fix 2 — inner scrollbars (UI-only, `components/modals/PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`).** First attempt applied the
  touch-first `pos-scrollbar-glass` utility to the Add/Edit split-pane scroll regions (left manual **form column**
  `flex-1 min-w-0 overflow-y-auto …` and right **live-preview pane** `overflow-auto bg-black/15 …`). Live QA
  round 1 found the glass thumb still read too bright/white against this dark modal, so the **correction** swaps
  both to the existing global `scrollbar-hide` utility (`scrollbar-width: none` + `::-webkit-scrollbar { display:
  none }`) — the inner bars are hidden cleanly while scrolling stays enabled. No global scrollbar CSS changed (the
  utility already exists app-wide; nothing else in the app was retargeted).
- **Live QA round 1 — FAILED (partial), corrected below.** Codex/operator reopened the live POS after the
  renderer refresh and confirmed the four helper/status lines (Fix 1) now read Greek ✓, but three items on the
  same Add Printer screen still failed: (a) the preview badge rendered English **"APPROXIMATE PREVIEW"**; (b) the
  preview detail line showed raw technical values **"classic / text"**; (c) the two inner split-view scrollbars
  still looked bright/white (the glass utility was not enough). Fixes 3–4 below plus the `scrollbar-hide` swap in
  Fix 2 address all three.
- **Fix 3 — preview badge i18n (UI-only).** `previewExact` / `previewApproximate` were keyed in JSX with English
  fallbacks but missing from every locale (same fallback-leak pattern as Fix 1) — added to `en/el/de/fr/it` after
  `livePreview` (e.g. el "Ακριβής προεπισκόπηση" / "Κατά προσέγγιση προεπισκόπηση"). The badge's existing
  `uppercase` CSS now uppercases the localized text instead of leaking English.
- **Fix 4 — preview detail labels (display-only).** The detail line was a raw template literal
  `` `${template} / ${renderMode}` `` interpolating enum values ("classic / text"). Replaced with a value→label
  map that localizes known values — template `classic|modern` → `previewTemplateClassic|previewTemplateModern`,
  render mode `text|raster_exact` → `previewRenderModeText|previewRenderModeRasterExact` (six new keys added to all
  five locales) — and falls back to the raw value (`String(value)`) for any unknown value. No data path changed:
  it still reads the same `effectiveTemplate`/`effectiveRenderMode` (or formData) values; only the displayed string
  is localized. The " / " separator is kept as a language-neutral glyph.
- **Live QA round 2 — text PASSED, scrollbar partial.** Codex/operator's second live pass confirmed the badge
  ("ΚΑΤΑ ΠΡΟΣΕΓΓΙΣΗ ΠΡΟΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΗΣΗ"), the detail ("Κλασικό / Κείμενο"), and the **left form-column** scrollbar are
  all correct now. One issue remained on the same screen: the **right receipt-preview** area still showed a bright
  white vertical scrollbar-like strip on its right edge. Since `scrollbar-hide` already removed the app's native
  bar (it demonstrably worked on the left column), the strip is baked into the generated preview image itself, not
  the app chrome.
- **Fix 5 — preview-edge strip mask (UI-only, `components/modals/PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`).** Wrapped the receipt
  `<img>` in a `relative` container and added a thin white right-edge overlay
  (`pointer-events-none absolute inset-y-0 right-0 w-4 rounded-r-md bg-white`, `aria-hidden="true"`). White blends
  into the white receipt paper, so the embedded strip is masked invisibly; `pointer-events-none` + `aria-hidden`
  keep scrolling, interaction, and content meaning unchanged, and `inset-y-0` spans the full image height so the
  mask stays aligned while the preview scrolls. No change to printer preview generation, the preview image data,
  API calls, discovery, save, or verification.
- **Preserved.** No printer behaviour, save/handlers, discovery, verification logic, API calls, or live data
  touched. Modal keeps its blurry-glass shell and yellow/black/grey palette; the amber helper card and
  green/amber/red verification tones are pre-existing semantics, unchanged. No order dashboard / order-flow
  changes. Touch-only feedback; no hover/dark:hover/group-hover added.
- **Adjacent leaks noticed (out of this round's reported scope, left for a follow-up).** Three more
  `settings.printer` helper strings are still fallback-only (missing from non-en locales) but only surface
  outside the reported Add-Printer view: `verificationSummaryExisting` (Edit-mode summary),
  `verificationResetWarning` (shown when manual edits reset verification), and `textScaleTextModeHint` (text-scale
  slider in Classic Text mode). Flagged here so an Edit-Printer live pass can clear them next.
- Verified (final correction — preview-edge mask): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean**
  (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` + this tracker → clean;
  `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` → **0** (the mask uses
  `pointer-events-none`/`active`-free static classes, no hover). Earlier-correction checks still hold: badge/detail
  i18n is leak-free (exact rendered "APPROXIMATE PREVIEW"/"classic / text" appear nowhere; English copies only in
  `en.json` + JSX `t()` fallbacks), the ten preview keys resolve in all five locales, and both inner scroll regions
  use `scrollbar-hide` (no `pos-scrollbar-glass` left in the modal). Final live visual sign-off PASSED
  (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/light, 1280x800; Settings → Εκτυπωτές → Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή → Προχωρημένες
  Ρυθμίσεις → Προσθήκη Εκτυπωτή — visual inspection only, no save/add/discovery/verification action triggered):
  - The original Add Printer helper/status copy renders in Greek — "Γρήγορη ρύθμιση πρώτα", Greek body text, Greek
    new-profile verification summary, and the Greek discovered-not-printable line.
  - Preview header is Greek: "ΖΩΝΤΑΝΗ ΠΡΟΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΗΣΗ".
  - Preview badge is Greek: "ΚΑΤΑ ΠΡΟΣΕΓΓΙΣΗ ΠΡΟΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΗΣΗ".
  - Preview detail line is localized: "Κλασικό / Κείμενο", no raw "classic / text".
  - Left Add/Edit form scrollbar is hidden — no bright native scrollbar visible.
  - The right receipt preview no longer shows a grey native scrollbar; the remaining right edge reads as white
    receipt paper after the mask.
  Codex re-ran the static checks independently: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) clean; `git diff --check`
  over `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`, the five locale files, and this tracker → clean; `rg
  "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` → 0 matches; `rg "APPROXIMATE PREVIEW|classic
  / text"` over `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` + the non-English locale files → 0 matches.

### Supervisor round 138 — Printer Settings Add/Edit lower-form i18n + simpler operator copy — COMPLETED

- **Why (live finding, Greek/light, 1280x800).** Settings → Εκτυπωτές → Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή → Προχωρημένες
  Ρυθμίσεις → Προσθήκη Εκτυπωτή, scrolling the left Add/Edit form down into the receipt/template/typography area,
  still showed five English strings in Greek mode: "Classic Customer Render Mode", "Text (ESC/POS font)", "Raster
  Emulation", "Auto (brand detect)", "Font Type". Same root cause as rounds 137a–b: the JSX already called
  `settings.printer.<key>` but the keys existed in **no** locale file (not even `en.json` — they lived only as the
  JSX `t()` fallback), so i18next returned English in every language.
- **Fix — i18n + simpler operator copy (UI-only).** Added ten `settings.printer` keys to all five locales
  (`en/el/de/fr/it`) after `receiptTemplateHint`, and simplified the English source so the modal is legible to a
  non-technical operator. The JSX `t()` fallbacks in `components/modals/PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` were updated to
  match the new en source (fallback now equals the en value, used only if a key is ever missing):
  - `classicRenderMode`: "Classic Customer Render Mode" → **"Classic print style"**
  - `classicRenderModeText`: "Text (ESC/POS font)" → **"Printer text"**
  - `classicRenderModeRasterExact`: "Raster Exact (screenshot-like)" → **"Image-like receipt"**
  - `classicEmulationMode`: "Raster Emulation" → **"Printer language"**
  - `classicEmulationAuto`: "Auto (brand detect)" → **"Auto detect"**
  - `classicEmulationEscpos`: **"ESC/POS"** (brand/protocol name — same literal in all five locales)
  - `classicEmulationStarLine`: **"Star Line"** (brand name — same literal in all five locales)
  - `fontType`: "Font Type" → **"Font size"**
  - `fontTypeA`: "A (Larger)" → **"Larger"**
  - `fontTypeB`: "B (Compact)" → **"Compact"**
  Greek e.g. `classicRenderMode` "Στυλ κλασικής εκτύπωσης", `classicEmulationMode` "Γλώσσα εκτυπωτή", `fontType`
  "Μέγεθος γραμματοσειράς", `fontTypeA/B` "Μεγαλύτερη"/"Συμπαγής"; de/fr/it translated in kind.
- **Preserved.** Enum `value=` attributes (`text`/`raster_exact`, `auto`/`escpos`/`star_line`, `a`/`b`) and all
  handlers are untouched — only the **labels** changed, so printer save/discovery/verification/preview logic, form
  state, API calls, and live data are unchanged. Modal keeps its blurry-glass shell and palette; no order
  dashboard / order-flow changes; no hover/dark:hover/group-hover added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the seven
  touched files (modal + five locales + this tracker) → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` → **0**; focused `rg` confirms the five English leak phrases ("Classic Customer Render
  Mode", "Text (ESC/POS font)", "Raster Emulation", "Auto (brand detect)", "Font Type") no longer appear in
  `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` or any non-English locale (the old strings are fully replaced; the new simpler English
  lives in `en.json` + JSX fallbacks as the en source). All ten keys present **10/10** in every locale; all five
  JSON files re-parse clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/light, 1280x800;
  Settings → Εκτυπωτές → Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή → Προχωρημένες Ρυθμίσεις → Προσθήκη Εκτυπωτή, scrolled into the
  receipt/template/typography area — visual inspection only, no save/add/discovery/verification action triggered):
  - "Classic Customer Render Mode" now renders Greek as "Στυλ κλασικής εκτύπωσης".
  - "Text (ESC/POS font)" now renders Greek as "Κείμενο εκτυπωτή".
  - "Raster Emulation" now renders Greek as "Γλώσσα εκτυπωτή".
  - "Auto (brand detect)" now renders Greek as "Αυτόματος εντοπισμός".
  - "Font Type" now renders Greek as "Μέγεθος γραμματοσειράς".
  - Protocol identifiers "ESC/POS" and "Star Line" remain literal by design.
  Codex re-ran the static checks independently: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) clean; `git diff --check`
  over `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`, the five locale files, and this tracker → clean; `rg
  "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` → 0 matches; JSON parse/key-presence → all ten
  Round 138 keys present in `en/el/de/fr/it`; focused old-phrase search → the old English phrases remain only in
  this tracker as documented finding text, not in `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` or the non-English locale files.

### Supervisor round 139 — Printer Settings Typography helper-copy i18n + simpler copy — COMPLETED (corrected twice before live sign-off)

- **Why (live finding, Greek/light, 1280x800).** Settings → Εκτυπωτές → Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή → Προχωρημένες
  Ρυθμίσεις → Προσθήκη Εκτυπωτή, scrolling the left Add/Edit form deeper into Typography & Size, still showed
  English helper text in Greek mode — most visibly "Star-safe mode avoids risky size commands. Use Font A/B and
  density/emphasis presets for reliable readability." and "Classic Text mode ignores exact text sizing on paper.
  Moving this control switches the draft to Raster Exact." Same root cause as rounds 137–138: the JSX already
  called `settings.printer.<key>` but the five helper keys existed in **no** locale file (only as the JSX `t()`
  fallback), so i18next returned English everywhere.
- **Fix — i18n + simpler operator copy (UI-only).** Added six `settings.printer` helper keys to all five locales
  (`en/el/de/fr/it`) — five after `textScaleHint`, plus `textScaleAutoSwitchToast` after `textScaleAutoSwitchInline`
  (the auto-switch **toast** was missed in the first pass and corrected before sign-off) — and rewrote the English
  source so each hint is legible to a
  non-technical operator and reuses the Round 138 control labels ("Printer text", "Image-like receipt", "Classic",
  "Auto") instead of expert jargon. The JSX `t()` fallbacks in `components/modals/PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` were
  updated to match the new en source:
  - `safeTypographyHint`: "Star-safe mode avoids risky size commands. Use Font A/B and density/emphasis presets
    for reliable readability." → **"Safe mode avoids risky size commands. Use font, density, and emphasis presets
    for reliable receipts."**
  - `textScaleTextModeHint`: "Classic Text mode ignores exact text sizing on paper. Moving this control switches
    the draft to Raster Exact." → **"Printer text ignores exact sizing. Moving this slider switches the preview to
    image-like receipt mode."**
  - `textScaleModernUnavailable`: "Modern layout uses fixed typography. Switch to Classic Raster Exact…" →
    **"Modern receipts use fixed text size. Switch to Classic + Image-like receipt to adjust text size."**
  - `textScaleAutoSwitchInline`: "Using the receipt text-size control switches Classic Text to Raster Exact…" →
    **"Text-size changes switch this draft to image-like receipt mode so preview and print can match."**
  - `textScaleAutoSwitchToast` (auto-switch **toast** — added in this correction): "Precise receipt sizing uses
    Raster Exact. The draft preview switched automatically." → **"Text-size changes switch this draft to image-like
    receipt mode so preview and print can match."** (mirrors `textScaleAutoSwitchInline` so the toast and the
    inline notice read identically).
  - `classicRenderModeHint`: replaced the long expert paragraph (dot counts, `emulation=star_line`, threshold
    ranges) with **"Image-like receipt is best when exact spacing matters. Leave the advanced fields on Auto
    unless support asks you to change them."**
  Greek e.g. `safeTypographyHint` "Η ασφαλής λειτουργία αποφεύγει επικίνδυνες εντολές μεγέθους…",
  `textScaleTextModeHint` "Το κείμενο εκτυπωτή αγνοεί το ακριβές μέγεθος…", `classicRenderModeHint` "Η απόδειξη σαν
  εικόνα είναι καλύτερη όταν έχει σημασία το ακριβές διάστιχο…"; de/fr/it translated in kind.
- **Preserved.** Only helper/hint **text** changed. The slider, the `classicRenderMode` auto-switch behaviour
  (`text` → `raster_exact`), enum values, form state, printer save/discovery/verification/preview generation, API
  calls, and live data are all untouched. Modal keeps its blurry-glass shell and palette; no order dashboard /
  order-flow changes; no hover/dark:hover/group-hover added.
- **Live QA round 2 — typography density/header still leaked, corrected.** A further live pass (Greek/light,
  1280x800) found the lower Typography section still showed English fallback labels/options in Greek — "Layout
  Density", "Header Emphasis", "Compact", "Strong", and the hint "Fine-tune spacing around the preset. Lower
  values tighten the receipt, higher values open it up." Root cause identical: nine more `settings.printer` keys
  around the layout-density and header-emphasis controls were referenced in JSX but absent from every locale.
  Added them to all five locales (`en/el/de/fr/it`), grouped before `logoScaleLabel`: `layoutDensityPreset`,
  `layoutDensityCompact`, `layoutDensityBalanced`, `layoutDensitySpacious`, `layoutDensity`,
  `layoutDensityScaleHint`, `headerEmphasis`, `headerEmphasisStrong`, `headerEmphasisNormal` (Greek e.g.
  "Πυκνότητα διάταξης", "Έμφαση κεφαλίδας", "Συμπαγής", "Έντονη"; de/fr/it in kind). No JSX change needed — the
  keys were already referenced and the existing simple English labels stay as the en source. Enum `value=`
  attributes (`compact`/`balanced`/`spacious`, `strong`/`normal`), the density slider, defaults, preview, and
  printer data untouched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the seven
  touched files (modal + five locales + this tracker) → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` → **0**; focused `rg` confirms the two visible English leak sentences ("Star-safe mode
  avoids risky size commands…" and "Classic Text mode ignores exact text sizing…") no longer appear in
  `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` or any non-English locale (old strings fully replaced; new simpler English lives in
  `en.json` + JSX fallbacks as the en source). The correction also removed the old auto-switch **toast** fallback
  ("Precise receipt sizing uses Raster Exact. The draft preview switched automatically.") — a focused `rg` confirms
  it no longer appears outside this tracker's finding text. The round-2 correction's density/header English phrases
  ("Layout Density", "Header Emphasis", "Compact", "Strong", "Fine-tune spacing around the preset…") now resolve
  from real translations in `el/de/fr/it` and no longer appear as fallback-only leaks. All **fifteen** Round 139
  keys (six typography hints + nine layout-density/header-emphasis labels) are present in every locale (6/6 + 9/9);
  all five JSON files re-parse clean. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark,
  1280x800; Settings → Printer → Configure Printer → Advanced → Add Printer, scrolled into Typography & Size —
  visual-only, no Save/Detect Printers/verification wizard/test-print/discovery/data action): the previously
  leaking controls now render Greek — "Συμπαγής", "Πυκνότητα διάταξης", "Μικρορυθμίστε το διάστιχο…", "Έμφαση
  κεφαλίδας", "Έντονη", "Έντονη γραφή κειμένου", plus the safe-typography helper and the receipt text-size helper;
  no English "Layout Density", "Header Emphasis", "Fine-tune", "Classic Text", "Raster", or "Star-safe" visible
  there. **Corrected twice before passing**: correction 1 added the `textScaleAutoSwitchToast` key missed in the
  first pass; correction 2 added the nine layout-density/header-emphasis keys after a further live-QA fail.

### Supervisor round 140 — PrinterSettingsModal full settings.printer key-parity (structural leak fix) — COMPLETED

- **Why (root cause of the repeated Greek leaks).** Rounds 137–139 fixed leaks one control at a time, but the
  structural cause was that `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` referenced many `settings.printer.*` keys that existed in
  **no** locale file — each only rendered via the JSX `t()` fallback, so every non-English locale leaked English
  the moment that branch rendered. A broader key-parity audit found **38** such keys still missing from all five
  locales (validation messages, logo settings, classic calibration fields, verification/state labels, toasts) —
  the same class that produced the round 137–139 live-QA failures.
- **Fix — full key parity (locale JSON only).** Added natural translations for all 38 missing keys in
  `en/el/de/fr/it` under `settings.printer` (inserted after `escposCodePage`), keeping the existing clear English
  as the en source: `bluetoothAddressRequired`, `chooseLogoFile`, `classicLeftMarginDots`,
  `classicPrintableWidthDots`, `classicPrintableWidthDotsAuto`, `classicRasterThreshold`, `discoveredOnlyHint`,
  `enableLogo`, `greekTestPrintDispatched`, `logoFileLoaded`, `logoFileReadFailed`, `logoFileTooLarge`,
  `logoFileTypeUnsupported`, `logoPreview`, `logoSettingsSaveFailed`, `logoSettingsSaved`, `logoSource`,
  `logoSourceCustomActive`, `logoSourceFallbackActive`, `logoSourcePlaceholder`, `nameRequired`,
  `networkIpRequired`, `previewUnavailable`, `receiptLogoHint`, `receiptLogoTitle`, `resolvedAddress`,
  `resolvedTransport`, `saveFailed`, `stateDegraded`, `stateUnresolved`, `stateUnverified`, `systemNameRequired`,
  `testPrintDispatched`, `transportUnreachable`, `useOrgLogo`, `verification`, `verificationResetWarning`,
  `verificationSummaryExisting`. State/verification labels reuse the existing wording
  (`stateDegraded`=`verificationDegraded`, `stateUnverified`=`verificationUnverified`) for consistency. The two
  long-flagged adjacent leaks from round 137 (`verificationResetWarning`, `verificationSummaryExisting`) are
  cleared here. No JSX change — every fallback was already clear operator copy and the keys were already
  referenced; nothing was simplified.
- **Preserved.** `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` behaviour, enum values, save/test/print logic, discovery,
  verification, and preview generation are untouched (locale data only). No order dashboard / order-flow changes;
  no hover/dark:hover/group-hover added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the seven
  touched files (modal + five locales + this tracker) → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` → **0**; all five locale JSON files re-parse clean; **key-parity check** (extract every
  `settings.printer.*` reference from `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`, assert each exists + is a non-empty string in all
  five locales) → **PARITY OK** (was 38 missing). Independent Codex re-run of the key-parity script found **195
  referenced `settings.printer` paths**, all present and OK in `en/el/de/fr/it`. Live visual sign-off PASSED
  (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800; Settings → Printer → Configure Printer → Advanced → Add
  Printer, scrolled Typography & Size — visual-only, no Save/Detect Printers/verification wizard/test-print/
  discovery/data action): the previously leaking controls render Greek with no English leaks visible. Independent
  static checks also passed: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) clean; `git diff --check` over
  `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` + en/el/de/fr/it locales + tracker clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` → 0 matches; JSON parse clean; and a focused non-English `rg` found no "Layout
  Density", "Header Emphasis", "Fine-tune spacing around the preset", "Star-safe mode avoids", "Classic Text mode
  ignores", or "Precise receipt sizing uses Raster Exact" leaks.
- **Structural follow-up recommended.** The recurring leaks are a missing-CI-guard problem: a `settings.printer`
  key-parity test (the same extraction used above, run in CI) would fail the build whenever a referenced key lacks
  a locale entry, preventing rounds 137–140 from recurring. Out of this round's locale-only scope; flagged for a
  dedicated task.

### Supervisor round 141 — UsersPage inline address-edit Save → glass semantic action — COMPLETED

- **Why (live finding, Greek/dark, 1280x800).** Users → first customer → details → address pencil opens inline
  address editing inside the glass customer-details modal. The Cancel button already read as translucent red
  glass, but Save was still a solid `bg-green-600` chip — visually heavier than the modal system and against the
  round-107 glass semantic-action direction.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/UsersPage.tsx`).** The inline address-edit Save button (around
  `handleSaveAddress(address.id)`, ~line 1418) dropped the solid fill `flex items-center justify-center gap-2 px-4
  py-2 bg-green-600 text-white rounded-xl active:bg-green-700 active:scale-[0.98] transition-all text-sm` →
  `liquid-glass-modal-button liquid-glass-modal-success rounded-xl text-sm disabled:opacity-50 disabled:saturate-0
  disabled:cursor-not-allowed` — the same translucent-green-glass pattern AddNewAddressModal/EditAddressModal use.
  The shared `.liquid-glass-modal-button` class supplies the inline-flex centering, `gap: 0.5rem` icon spacing, and
  the `:active { scale(0.98) }` press, so those are preserved without explicit utilities; `rounded-xl` + `text-sm`
  are kept, and disabled-safe classes added for parity with the address modals.
- **Preserved.** `onClick={() => handleSaveAddress(address.id)}`, the `Save` icon + label, the surrounding
  `flex items-center gap-2` row, and the adjacent red-glass Cancel are untouched. Ban/unban, delete confirmation,
  customer-list actions, save/delete/ban handlers, API calls, validation, translations, and modal layout are all
  unchanged. The other solid-green button (~line 1511, a different control) was intentionally left as-is. No
  hover/dark:hover/group-hover added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `UsersPage.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `UsersPage.tsx` → **0**;
  focused `rg` confirms the inline Save no longer carries `bg-green-600 active:bg-green-700` while ban/unban/delete
  keep their intentionally solid semantic classes. Independent Codex re-run agreed: `npm run type-check` (from
  `pos-tauri`) clean; `git diff --check -- pos-tauri/src/renderer/pages/UsersPage.tsx
  pos-tauri/planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:" UsersPage.tsx -S` → 0 JSX
  hover-utility matches; focused `rg` confirms the inline `handleSaveAddress` Save now uses `liquid-glass-modal-button
  liquid-glass-modal-success` and the only remaining `bg-green-600 active:bg-green-700` in `UsersPage.tsx` is the
  intentional **unban** footer action. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark,
  1280x800; Users → customer details → inline address edit — visual-only, no Save/Delete/Ban/Close-Day/print/
  order-side-effect action pressed): Save now renders as translucent green glass, centered with icon + text,
  matching the modal system; Cancel remains red glass; the modal stays blurry/glass.

### Supervisor round 142 — TablesPage dark-mode neutral base (navy/slate → black + white-alpha glass) — COMPLETED (1st live pass partial-failed, corrected)

- **Why (live finding, Greek/dark, 1280x800).** The Tables route (sidebar → Τραπέζια) read as a navy/slate slab:
  page background, table cards, the FilterDropdown shell, the StatusChangeModal shell, and the floor-plan panel
  used Tailwind `bg-gray-900`/`bg-gray-800` surfaces, which are the cool/blue-tinted neutral (`gray-900` = `#111827`,
  blue-heavy) and clashed with the app's intended black/grey/white/yellow base.
- **Fix attempt 1 (zinc — superseded).** Swapped the six dark *surface* fills from blue-tinted `gray` to neutral
  `zinc` (same lightness, near-zero blue: `zinc-900` = `#18181b`), which also matches this page's own
  `STATUS_COLORS.occupied` = `#27272a` (= `zinc-800`):
  - Page background (~749) and loading background (~733): `bg-gray-900` → `bg-zinc-900`.
  - StatusChangeModal frosted shell (~424): `bg-gray-900/60` → `bg-zinc-900/60` (keeps `backdrop-blur-2xl`,
    `border-white/10`, `ring-white/15`, `shadow-black/50`).
  - TableCard dark surface (~153) and floor-plan panel (~910): `bg-gray-800/50` → `bg-zinc-800/50`.
  - FilterDropdown dark shell (~289): `bg-gray-800` → `bg-zinc-800`.
  Done via two substring-safe `replace_all`s (`bg-gray-900`→`bg-zinc-900`, `bg-gray-800`→`bg-zinc-800`), which also
  cover the `/50` and `/60` alpha variants; every `bg-gray-800/900` occurrence was verified to be a dark surface
  (no light-mode collateral).
- **Live QA round 1 — partial fail.** Codex/operator live check (Greek/dark, 1280x800): the table modal was
  improved enough and the main grid was better than gray, but the **floor-plan panel and FilterDropdown still read
  as cool navy/slate** — a solid `zinc` fill is opaque enough that its faint blue lean still registers as a
  blue-black wash against the app's true-black chrome. Not signed off.
- **Correction (black + white-alpha glass).** Stopped using solid `zinc` fills and moved the dark surfaces onto the
  POS dark-glass language (the app shell is `bg-black`; surfaces are white-alpha over black + `backdrop-blur` +
  rim), which is hue-neutral by construction:
  - Page background (~749) and loading background (~733): `bg-zinc-900` → **`bg-black`** (matches the app shell).
  - TableCard dark surface (~153): `bg-zinc-800/50` → **`bg-white/[0.05]`** (subtle white-alpha glass; keeps the
    `border-2 border-white/10` rim).
  - FilterDropdown dark shell (~289): `bg-zinc-800` → **`bg-black/80 backdrop-blur-xl`** (readable black glass +
    rim/shadow).
  - Floor-plan panel (~910): `bg-zinc-800/50` → **`bg-white/[0.05] backdrop-blur-xl`** (white-alpha glass + rim).
  - StatusChangeModal shell (~424): `bg-zinc-900/60` → **`bg-black/60`** (kept black + frosted: `backdrop-blur-2xl`,
    `ring-white/15`, `shadow-black/50`) so no zinc remains on the shell.
  The only `zinc` left in the file is the refresh button's `active:bg-zinc-800` press state — an intentional
  dark-button tap feedback, explicitly allowed.
- **Preserved.** Semantic status colors are untouched — available green, reserved yellow/amber, occupied near-black
  (`#27272a`), cleaning grey, maintenance orange, unavailable grey all still come from the inline `STATUS_COLORS`
  map; the yellow New Order primary and neutral Reservation in StatusChangeModal are unchanged. Dark neutral action
  surfaces were already neutral `bg-white/10` glass (not navy), so they needed no change. `text-gray-*` body text,
  light-mode `bg-gray-50/100/200`, all onClick handlers, navigation, status-change behaviour, table data, filters,
  search, refresh, i18n, modal layout, and page structure are unchanged. No `TableActionModal`/other component
  edits. Touch-only feedback (`active:`); no hover/dark:hover/group-hover added.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `TablesPage.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `TablesPage.tsx` → **0**;
  focused `rg` confirms the page/card/modal/floor-plan/dropdown surfaces no longer use `bg-gray-800/900` **or**
  `bg-zinc-800/900` as their primary fill (now `bg-black` / `bg-white/[0.05]` / `bg-black/60–80` glass) — the only
  `zinc` left is the refresh button's permitted `active:bg-zinc-800`; `text-gray-*` and the semantic
  `STATUS_COLORS` values are intentionally retained. Independent Codex re-run agreed: `npm run type-check` (from
  `pos-tauri`) clean; `git diff --check -- pos-tauri/src/renderer/pages/TablesPage.tsx
  pos-tauri/planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:" TablesPage.tsx -S` → 0 JSX
  hover-utility matches; focused `rg` confirms the dark page/card/dropdown/floor-plan/modal surfaces now use
  `bg-black`, `bg-black/60`, `bg-black/80`, and `bg-white/[0.05]`, with the only remaining zinc being the allowed
  refresh-button `active:bg-zinc-800` press state — no `bg-gray-800/900` or `bg-zinc-800/900` primary surface fill
  remains. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800; sidebar → Τραπέζια —
  visual-only, no New Order/Reservation/status-change/data action pressed): the floor-plan panel is black/neutral
  glass with no navy slab; the filter dropdown is black glass; the B03 StatusChangeModal is frosted black glass;
  New Order remains yellow, Reservation neutral, status controls remain semantic green/amber/grey/orange, and the
  table status colors are unchanged.

### Supervisor round 143 — SuppliersPage touch/palette polish + duplicated-currency stat fix — COMPLETED

- **Why (live finding, Greek/dark, 1280x800, Suppliers page).** The top-right "Total Owed" stat rendered as
  "CHF 0,00 CHF" — the tile drew a separate currency glyph (`currencySymbol`) while the value was already
  `formatMoney(...)` (which includes the currency), so the symbol was duplicated and read sloppy. Static inspection
  also showed the page (only "global-audit" before) still carried hover-only utilities and off-theme blue/cyan
  accents.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/SuppliersPage.tsx`).**
  - **Hover → touch.** Removed all 15 hover-dependent utilities (`hover:bg-*`, `hover:scale-[1.03]`), converting
    them to `active:` press feedback (cards, refresh, tab/filter, payment/import buttons). Page is now touch-first.
  - **De-blue/cyan (semantic recolor).** No `blue`/`cyan` classes remain. `getInvoiceStatusClass` **partial** →
    amber (was blue); import `ReviewRow` **update** status → amber (was blue); the **Partial** invoice button →
    amber, the **Record payment** button → emerald (money-success), the import **Preview** button → amber. Top
    stats: **Total** icon → neutral `text-zinc-400` (was `text-blue-500`); **Total Owed** → `Wallet` lucide icon
    in `text-amber-500` (was a cyan currency glyph). Active=green, unpaid/partial=amber, overdue=red kept.
  - **Duplicated-currency stat fix.** Both money tiles that used `currency: true` (top "Total Owed" ~852 and the
    supplier-summary "Total spent" ~1184) now use a real `Wallet` lucide icon instead of rendering `currencySymbol`
    on top of the formatted value; the `stat.currency`/`tile.currency` render branches were removed. The now-unused
    `currencySymbol` `useMemo` and the `getCurrencySymbol` helper were deleted (renderer `noUnusedLocals` is off, so
    this is cleanup, not a fix-for-error). The visible owed stat now reads label + "0,00 CHF" (no leading "CHF").
- **Preserved.** No data fetching, mutations, API paths, invoice payment/import behaviour, filtering, selection,
  i18n keys, or page structure changed — only classNames, two stat-tile icon fields, and the dead currency-symbol
  helper. Centered icon+text button alignment and rounded containers kept; semantic green/emerald (paid/record),
  amber/yellow (unpaid/partial/preview/owed accent), red (overdue) retained; neutral grey/black/white glass for
  ordinary controls. The page stays visually alive (not all-yellow). **No data action was pressed** (static + visual
  inspection only).
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `SuppliersPage.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `SuppliersPage.tsx` →
  **0**; `rg "text-blue|bg-blue|border-blue|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan|currencySymbol"` over `SuppliersPage.tsx`
  → **0**. Independent Codex re-run agreed: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) clean (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `git diff --check -- pos-tauri/src/renderer/pages/SuppliersPage.tsx pos-tauri/planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md`
  clean; `rg -n "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:" SuppliersPage.tsx -S` → 0 matches; `rg -n
  "text-blue|bg-blue|border-blue|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan|currencySymbol" SuppliersPage.tsx -S` → 0 matches.
  Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800 — visual-only, no
  payment/import/mutation/data action pressed):
  - Sidebar → Προμηθευτές: the top "Συνολικό Χρέος" card now shows a Wallet icon plus "0,00 CHF" with no leading
    duplicated "CHF" glyph; no blue/cyan controls are visible; stats and buttons preserve semantic green/amber/red
    with neutral chrome.
  - Supplier summary overlay: opened Alpine Valley Produce read-only summary — "Σύνολο αγορών" shows an icon plus
    "0,00 CHF" with no duplicated currency glyph; the modal stays blurry/glass.

### Supervisor round 144 — InventoryPage touch/palette cleanup + stock-adjust modal action semantics — COMPLETED

    `text-zinc-300/600` (was blue); Total Value icon → amber money accent `text-amber-300/600` (was cyan); History
    badge → amber (`bg-amber-500/15 text-amber-200` / `bg-amber-50 text-amber-700`, was blue). Semantic red
    critical, amber low, emerald good/in-stock kept (status filter chips unchanged in hue).

    shadow (the backdrop already had `bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm`). **Cancel** → red glass/outline
    (`border-red-500/40 bg-red-500/10 text-red-300 active:bg-red-500/20` ÷ `border-red-300 bg-red-50 text-red-600
    active:bg-red-100`). **Save** → emerald when enabled (`border-emerald-500/40 bg-emerald-500/20 text-emerald-200
    active:bg-emerald-500/30` ÷ `border-emerald-600 bg-emerald-600 text-white active:bg-emerald-700`), with the
    existing `disabled:opacity-50` keeping the disabled state subdued. **+/-** steppers and Cancel/Save given
    `inline-flex items-center justify-center` centering + `active:scale-95`, touch sizes (`p-3`/`py-3`) kept.
- **Preserved.** No data fetching, API paths, offline mutation behaviour, adjustment math, table-row click
  behaviour, filtering, i18n keys, or inventory data touched — only classNames. Rounded containers and centered
  icon/text alignment kept; semantic green/amber/red retained (not flattened to yellow). **The live operator opened
  the modal only and did not press Save or mutate inventory.**

### Supervisor round 145 — OrderCard pickup icon → shared PickupOrderIcon class-based path — COMPLETED

- **Why (user report).** The pickup/takeaway icon under the price on the active order row/card still looked wrong
  and did not match the bag glyph the order-type chooser uses. Root cause: the pickup branch of `OrderTypeIcon`
  rendered the shared `PickupOrderIcon` through a divergent `size={iconSize} color={iconColor}` prop path, while the
  chooser (and the sibling table-order branch in this same component) uses class-based `w-6 h-6 text-gray-*`. The
  two paths drifted, so the row glyph kept rendering differently.
- **Fix (UI-only, `components/order/OrderCard.tsx`).** In the pickup/takeaway branch of `OrderTypeIcon`, replaced
  the `size`/`color` prop path (and the now-unused `iconColor` local) with class-based rendering matching the
  chooser and the table-order branch: `<PickupOrderIcon className={`w-6 h-6 ${resolvedTheme === 'light' ?
  'text-gray-700' : 'text-gray-300'}`} />`. Same shared component, same sizing/color style, centered next to the
  payment icon under the price.
- **Preserved.** Table and delivery icon branches untouched (`iconSize` still used by the table-icon SVG); no order
  fetching, status logic, payments, filters, translations, or data mutation changed. No hover utilities added
  (class is static; touch feedback unaffected).
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `OrderCard.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "size=\{iconSize\}|iconColor|PickupOrderIcon"` over `OrderCard.tsx`
  confirms the pickup branch no longer uses `size={iconSize}`/`iconColor` and renders `PickupOrderIcon` via
  className. Independent Codex re-run agreed: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) clean (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `git diff --check -- pos-tauri/src/renderer/components/order/OrderCard.tsx pos-tauri/planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md`
  clean; `rg -n "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:" OrderCard.tsx -S` → 0 matches; `rg -n "size=\{iconSize\}|iconColor|
  PickupOrderIcon|Store icon|Storefront|HandBag" OrderCard.tsx -S` → only the `PickupOrderIcon` import/comment/JSX
  usage (no `size={iconSize}`, no `iconColor`, no storefront/store/HandBag path). Live visual sign-off PASSED
  (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800 — visual-only, no order type selected / order created / data
  action):
  - Dashboard Orders tab row #00001 shows the pickup icon under "18,50 EUR" as the same plain shopping-bag glyph —
    not a storefront/cash-register/awning icon — centered next to the payment icon.
  - Comparison: opened the yellow "+" order-type chooser and confirmed the "Παραλαβή" pickup card uses the same
    shopping-bag glyph; closed the chooser with X only.

### Supervisor round 146 — LoyaltyPage touch/palette cleanup — COMPLETED

- **Why.** LoyaltyPage (only "global-audit" before) still carried hover-only utilities and a non-semantic
  cyan/blue accent (cyan Scan button outline, blue Avg-Points stat icon) that conflict with the app's touch-first,
  black/grey/white/yellow direction.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/LoyaltyPage.tsx`).**
  - **Hover → touch.** Removed all hover-dependent utilities → `active:` press feedback: the refresh button
    (`hover:bg-zinc-200`÷`hover:bg-zinc-800` → `active:`, `hover:scale-[1.03]` → `active:scale-95`) and the customer
    list cards (`hover:bg-zinc-900`÷`hover:bg-gray-50` → `active:`).
  - **De-cyan/blue (semantic recolor).** No `blue`/`cyan` remains. **Scan** button: cyan outline
    (`bg-transparent text-white border border-cyan-500/40`, dark-only) → theme-aware warm yellow glass (`isDark ?
    'border border-yellow-400/70 text-white active:bg-yellow-400/10' : 'border border-yellow-400 text-gray-950
    active:bg-yellow-50'`) with `active:scale-95`; ScanLine icon + label kept centered (`inline-flex items-center
    justify-center gap-2`). **Avg Points** stat icon (`TrendingUp`) `text-blue-500` → `text-amber-500`
    (value/money accent).
  - **Tier identity preserved.** The customer-card selected ring `ring-2 ring-purple-500` is kept (selection +
    platinum/purple **tier identity**, not generic chrome); semantic green/yellow/red tier/points colours intact.
- **Correction (tap-animation polish).** Live QA noted the Scan button paired `active:scale-95` (a transform) with
  `transition-colors`, which only animates colour/bg/border — so the tap scale snapped abruptly. Changed the Scan
  button's `transition-colors` → `transition-all duration-200` so the transform eases alongside the colour/bg/border
  states. Active-only feedback retained; no hover utilities; warm yellow/amber dark+light styling and centered
  ScanLine icon/label unchanged.
- **Preserved.** No loyalty fetching/sync/scanning behaviour, customer selection logic, search, scanner hooks,
  i18n keys, or data mutations touched — classNames only. Rounded containers, centered icon/text alignment, and
  existing layout density kept.
- Verified (incl. tap-animation correction): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `git diff --check` over `LoyaltyPage.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `LoyaltyPage.tsx` → **0**;
  `rg "text-blue|bg-blue|border-blue|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan|from-blue|to-blue|via-blue|from-cyan|to-cyan|via-cyan"`
  over `LoyaltyPage.tsx` → **0**. Independent Codex re-run agreed: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) clean
  (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check -- pos-tauri/src/renderer/pages/LoyaltyPage.tsx
  pos-tauri/planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` clean; `rg -n "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:" LoyaltyPage.tsx -S` → 0
  matches; `rg -n "text-blue|...|via-cyan" LoyaltyPage.tsx -S` → 0 matches; `rg -n
  "transition-colors|transition-all duration-200|active:scale-95" LoyaltyPage.tsx -S` → only `transition-all
  duration-200` + `active:scale-95` on the Scan button and `active:scale-95` on Refresh (no `transition-colors`
  remains). Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800):
  - **QA setup (not part of this round's code).** The page first showed only "Πρόγραμμα Πιστότητας Ανενεργό"
    because the test org had the Loyalty module active/POS-enabled but **no `loyalty_settings` row**. Codex/operator
    seeded one default active `loyalty_settings` row for the test org `geminixpro070@gmail.com` (points_per_euro 1,
    redemption_rate 0.01, min redemption 100, tiers 0/500/2000/5000) **solely to expose the real Loyalty UI for QA**
    — no LoyaltyPage code/data path was changed by this round.
  - After remount/sync the Loyalty page renders: warm amber/yellow Scan button with centered ScanLine icon + label;
    neutral black/white Refresh button; Avg Points stat icon amber (not blue); no cyan/blue page chrome visible;
    customer cards rounded, aligned, keeping semantic green/yellow plus tier identity badges.
  - No Scan button pressed, no reader panel opened, no customer selected, no redemption/order/customer mutation or
    data action.

### Supervisor round 147 — AnalyticsPage touch/palette polish — STATIC-VERIFIED / LIVE BLOCKED

- **Why.** AnalyticsPage (only "global-audit" before) carried hover-only utilities, a cyan/blue accent throughout
  (loading spinner, active period chip, two stat-card icon chips, chart icons, chart bar/progress gradients), and
  navy `bg-gray-900`/`bg-gray-800/50` surfaces inconsistent with the recent black/zinc/white/cream + rounded-2xl
  direction.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/AnalyticsPage.tsx`).**
  - **Hover → touch.** Removed all hover utilities → `active:`: period-selector inactive chips
    (`hover:bg-gray-700`÷`hover:bg-gray-100` → `active:bg-zinc-800`÷`active:bg-gray-100`) and the refresh button
    (`hover:bg-zinc-200`÷`hover:bg-zinc-800` → `active:`, `hover:scale-[1.03]` → `active:scale-95`).
  - **De-blue/cyan (semantic, distinction kept).** No `blue`/`cyan` remains. Loading spinner `text-cyan-500` →
    `text-amber-500`; active period chip `bg-cyan-500 text-white` → `bg-yellow-400 text-black`; **Total Orders**
    stat chip `bg-blue-500` → `bg-amber-500`; **Delivery Validations** stat chip `bg-cyan-500` → `bg-purple-500`
    (distinct within its delivery row: emerald success / amber avg-time / rose hotspots / purple validations); the
    three chart-section icons (PieChart/Clock/Route) `text-cyan-500` → `text-amber-500`; both chart gradients
    (category progress + hourly bars) `from-cyan-500 to-blue-500` → `from-amber-500 to-orange-500`. Other stat
    chips kept their meaningful colours (revenue green, avg-order purple, customers orange, success emerald,
    hotspots rose) — **not** flattened to yellow.
  - **Neutral surfaces + rounded-2xl.** Page background `bg-gray-900` → `bg-black` (light cream `bg-[#fdfaf5]`
    kept); the five main containers (stat cards + 4 chart panels, shared className) `rounded-xl` → `rounded-2xl`,
    dark surface `bg-gray-800/50` → `bg-white/[0.05]` glass, `border-gray-700` → `border-white/10` (light
    `bg-white/80`/`border-gray-200` kept). `backdrop-blur-sm` retained.
- **Preserved.** No analytics fetching, API path, period values, calculations, data shapes, i18n keys, or chart
  data mapping touched — classNames only. Layout density, responsive grids, empty states, and semantic
  red/green/yellow/orange retained; `change` up/down green/red and tiny icon chips unchanged.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `AnalyticsPage.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `AnalyticsPage.tsx` →
  **0**; `rg "text-blue|bg-blue|border-blue|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan|from-blue|to-blue|via-blue|from-cyan|to-cyan|via-cyan"`
  over `AnalyticsPage.tsx` → **0**. Independent Codex static checks PASSED (type-check clean, scoped `git diff
  --check` clean, hover audit 0, blue/cyan audit 0 — the change is className-only).
- **Live visual sign-off BLOCKED by module availability (not a code issue).** Analytics is not exposed
  in the running POS for the test org, so the page cannot be opened for visual QA yet:

    `show_in_navigation=true` **but `pos_enabled=false`**, and the org has **no `organization_modules` row** for
    analytics. So the POS sidebar does not surface Analytics in the running app.
  - Therefore do **not** instruct "sidebar → Analytics" as if available; live QA is deferred until the module is
