### Supervisor round 148 — DeliveryPage touch-first polish — STATIC-VERIFIED / LIVE BLOCKED

- **Why.** Delivery Pro carried hover-only utilities throughout (quick-action, refresh, driver-modal close, driver
  rows, assign button, filter tabs) and an opaque flat `DriverAssignmentModal`, inconsistent with the touch-first,
  blurry-glass, rounded-2xl direction. No blue/cyan was present (already 0). This pass is touch + surface polish,
  not a layout rewrite.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/DeliveryPage.tsx`).**
  - **Hover → touch (all removed).** Every `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` replaced with `active:` feedback:
    quick-action (`hover:opacity-90`/`hover:bg-*` → `active:bg-*`, `transition-all duration-200 active:scale-[0.98]`);
    refresh (`hover:bg-*` → `active:`, `hover:scale-[1.03]` → `active:scale-95`); modal **close** (`hover:bg-*` →
    `active:bg-*`, now `inline-flex items-center justify-center ... active:scale-95`, square centered); driver
    **rows** (`hover:border-*` → `active:border-*`, `active:scale-[0.99]`); **assign** button (`hover:bg-*` →
    `active:bg-* active:scale-[0.98]` **enabled-branch only**, so disabled stays non-tappable); filter **tabs**
    (`hover:text/bg` → `active:text/bg`). Tap timings `duration-150/200`.
  - **DriverAssignmentModal → blurry glass.** Shell `bg-zinc-950`÷`bg-white` (opaque) → translucent glass
    `bg-black/60`÷`bg-white/60` + `backdrop-blur-2xl` + `ring-1 ring-white/15`÷`ring-white/60` +
    `border-white/10`÷`border-white/70`, `rounded-2xl` + `shadow-2xl shadow-black/40` kept (backdrop already
    `bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm`). Converted the panel `<div>` → `motion.div` with a subtle entrance
    (`initial opacity 0/scale .95 → animate 1`, `duration .15`) using the file's existing `motion`/`renderModalPortal`
    — entrance only (exit would need `AnimatePresence` around the parent's conditional render, out of scope; no
    behaviour change). Text readable in dark + light.
  - **Smoother rounding.** Delivery cards, the 4 stat chips, filter shell, and error banner `rounded-xl` →
    `rounded-2xl`; modal rows `rounded-xl` → `rounded-2xl`; quick-action `rounded-lg` → `rounded-xl`; compact filter
    tabs kept `rounded-lg`.
- **Preserved.** No delivery fetching, API/bridge calls, status transitions, driver-assignment behaviour, map-URL
  behaviour, filters, i18n keys, data shapes, or event subscriptions touched — classNames + the local modal-panel
  `motion` wrapper only. Semantic red/green/amber and per-status colour identity kept (status bar colour, red error
  banner). Centred controls preserved (48x48 refresh with centred `RefreshCw`, square centred modal X, full-width
  centred action labels). Existing `scrollbar-hide` on the stat-chip strip and `overflow-y-auto` modal list left
  intact — no visible default scrollbars introduced.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `DeliveryPage.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `DeliveryPage.tsx` → **0**;
  `rg "text-blue|bg-blue|border-blue|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan|from-blue|to-blue|via-blue|from-cyan|to-cyan|via-cyan"`
  over `DeliveryPage.tsx` → **0**. Independent Codex static checks PASSED (type-check clean, scoped `git diff
  --check` clean, hover audit 0, blue/cyan audit 0 — UI-only change).
- **Live visual sign-off BLOCKED/DEFERRED by route availability (not a code issue).** Codex live route
  inspection found the **current POS sidebar "Delivery Pro" route does not render
  `pages/DeliveryPage.tsx`** — it renders **`DeliveryZonesPage` / Ζώνες Διανομής** via `RefactoredMainLayout`'s
  `delivery_zones` mapping. So this round's edits are not reachable through the visible Delivery Pro sidebar route
  for this org/business type.
  - `DeliveryPage.tsx` appears to be consumed through `pages/verticals/fast-food/DeliveryView.tsx`, not the
    currently visible sidebar route. Live QA of these changes is therefore **deferred until a route / org /
    business type exposes that page**. Do **not** claim "sidebar → Delivery opens DeliveryPage".
  - The visible sidebar Delivery Pro screen is **`DeliveryZonesPage`** and is a **separate page** — it should be
    reviewed/polished in its own later round, not conflated with this one.
  - Round 148 code stays **UI-only (classNames + the local modal-panel `motion` wrapper) / static-verified**,
    live QA blocked by route availability. When `DeliveryPage.tsx` is reachable, the live pass should
    confirm: hover→active touch feel; rounded-2xl cards/chips/filter/banner; DriverAssignmentModal as blurry glass
    with a subtle entrance, square centred X, active-press driver rows, enabled-style assign (disabled
    non-tappable); no blue/cyan. Visual-only — no status transition / driver assignment / data action.

    `hover:bg-gray-50` → `active:bg-*` plus `active:scale-[0.99]` (selected `ring-2` and inactive `opacity-70`

    `text-blue-500` → `text-emerald-500` (success metric); est-time **Clock** `text-blue-500` → `text-amber-500`
    (duration accent); bottom **Info** panel icon `text-blue-500` → `text-zinc-400` (neutral provenance note, not a
    warning). Kept: Validations `Activity` yellow, Hotspots `Flame` red, Delivery-Fee `DollarSign` green, status
    `Active` green, Map-Signals `AlertTriangle` amber, zone-description `Info` red.

    empty-state panel, zone cards, right details panel, bottom info panel. Compact metric mini-cards (`rounded-lg
    px-3 …`, incl. the empty-details "select a zone" box) → `rounded-xl`; the small icon chips (`p-2 rounded-lg`)
    stay `rounded-lg`. Outer-rounder-than-inner nesting kept intentional.

  keys, data shapes, or route mappings touched — classNames only. Visual hierarchy/alignment kept: 48x48 refresh
  centred with `RefreshCw`, title/subtitle layout unchanged, empty state centred, stat icon-chip/text groups

  `DeliveryZonesPage.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `DeliveryZonesPage.tsx` → **0**; `rg "text-blue|bg-blue|border-blue|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan|from-blue|to-blue|via-blue|from-cyan|to-cyan|via-cyan"`

  1280x800) with **real rooms visible** (stats row, status filters, floor groups, room cards). Round 67 already
  removed hover feedback (hover audit = 0), but the page still carried non-theme blue chrome (occupied status,
  occupied stat, focus rings, info panels, primary buttons, selected chips) and some `rounded-xl` surfaces.

  - **De-blue by meaning (audit now 0).** Occupied **status config** + occupied **stat** `text-blue-500` →
    **orange** (`text-orange-500` / `bg-orange-500/10 border-orange-500/30`) — warm, distinct from available
    emerald, cleaning amber, maintenance red, reserved purple. All input/search **focus rings**
    `focus:ring-blue-500` → `focus:ring-yellow-400` (8 sites). **Check-in** ActionButtons `color="blue"` →
    `color="emerald"` (save/confirm-style green) and the now-dead `blue` entry removed from the ActionButton
    `colorClasses` map; **Complete Check-in** button `bg-blue-500…active:bg-blue-600` → emerald. **Selected**
    payment-method + status-filter chips `bg-blue-500 text-white` → `bg-yellow-400 text-black` (app selected
    convention). Light-blue **info/total panels** (`bg-blue-50`) → neutral `bg-gray-100` (dark `bg-gray-700` kept).
    Destructive red and existing emerald/amber/purple semantics untouched.
  - **Smoother rounding.** Stats cards, mobile filter shell, room cards, and the balance/total info panels
    `rounded-xl` → `rounded-2xl` (modal shell already `rounded-2xl`). Compact chips/buttons/icon chips and the
    full-width modal action buttons kept `rounded-lg/xl`.
- **Preserved.** No room fetching, status transitions, check-in/checkout/reservation/folio/charge/payment
  behaviour, event subscriptions, offline mutations, API paths, data shapes, i18n keys, route mappings, or module
  gating touched — classNames + the 3 `color` props + the dead-map-entry removal only (no handler/behaviour
  change). Layout/hierarchy preserved (stats row, search/filter row, floor headings, room cards, FAB, modal
  content do not overlap); no hover introduced (audit 0); no new visible default scrollbars. Protected order
  dashboard / order-taking / nav neon / order tabs not touched. Page stays neutral black/zinc/white with semantic
  green/orange/amber/red/purple — not blue/cyan, not all-yellow.
- **Correction (modal glass — after live QA fail).** Codex live QA opened room 101's **action modal** safely (no
  data mutation): main grid + search focus passed, but the local `Modal` shell was still **opaque** — panel
  `bg-gray-800`÷`bg-white` and the sticky header `bg-gray-800`÷`bg-white` read as a flat strip, missing the
  app-wide blurry-glass requirement. Fixed: the dialog **panel** → translucent glass `bg-black/60`÷`bg-white/60` +
  `backdrop-blur-2xl` + `border ring-1` (`border-white/10 ring-white/15`÷`border-white/70 ring-white/60`) +
  `shadow-2xl shadow-black/50`÷`shadow-black/30`; the **sticky header** → translucent `bg-black/60`÷`bg-white/60` +
  `backdrop-blur-xl` + neutral border + `z-10` so it frosts over scrolling content instead of an opaque bar.
  `rounded-2xl`, `max-h-[90vh] overflow-y-auto`, `renderModalPortal`, `role="dialog"`/`aria-modal`, Escape handling,
  and close behaviour all preserved; inner info/total/balance panels left readable; no blue/cyan, no hover.
- **Correction 2 (close-button contrast — after final live QA).** Codex re-opened room 101 safely: the frosted
  glass read well, but the header **close `X` was nearly invisible** on the dark glass (the button had no explicit
  text colour, so the icon inherited a low-contrast default). Fixed: gave the close button explicit per-theme text
  `text-white`÷`text-gray-700`, a stable square `h-9 w-9 inline-flex items-center justify-center`, and active-only
  feedback `active:scale-95` + `active:bg-white/15`÷`active:bg-gray-200`. `onClose` and dialog semantics unchanged;
  no hover, no blue/cyan.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `RoomsView.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `RoomsView.tsx` → **0**;
  `rg "text-blue|bg-blue|border-blue|ring-blue|focus:ring-blue|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan|from-blue|to-blue|via-blue|from-cyan|to-cyan|via-cyan"`
  over `RoomsView.tsx` → **0**. Final Codex live visual sign-off PASSED — **partial coverage by design** (running
  POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800 — visual-only, no data mutation; required two corrections first: modal glass, then
  close-icon contrast):
  - **Main Rooms grid PASSED:** occupied stat/card reads **orange** (not blue), with available green / cleaning
    amber / maintenance red / reserved purple distinct; selected status chip yellow/black; stats / filter shell /
    room cards rounded and aligned; no visible default scrollbar.
  - **Search focus PASSED:** search input focus ring is **yellow** (not blue).
  - **Room 101 action modal PASSED (after corrections):** shell + sticky header read as translucent **frosted
    glass**, text readable, the close **X is visible white and centred**, rounded-2xl panel, Escape closes back to
    the grid. No status/check-in/reservation/payment/charge action clicked.
  - **NOT live-opened:** the other modal branches (check-in / reservation / checkout-payment / charge /
    chooseCreate) share the same local `Modal` shell so they **inherit** the frosted glass + visible close icon,
    but they were not opened in this pass and remain **static-verified only** — to be live-confirmed if safely
    opened in a later pass. Do not claim these branches were visually QA'd.

### Supervisor interruption — pickup order-row icon parity re-check (OrderCard / OrdersPage) — COMPLETED

- **Why.** User screenshot: the compact pickup icon under the order total looked unlike the order-type chooser's
  bag. This re-checks the order-row glyph against the shared `PickupOrderIcon` (continuation of round 145).
- **Finding — source already correct; no code change needed.** All three sources already render the shared
  component, so this was a verify-only pass (edited the tracker only):
  - `components/icons/PickupOrderIcon.tsx` = the plain lucide `ShoppingBag` (`strokeWidth` default **2**, normal
    weight) — the single source of truth for pickup/takeaway.
  - `components/order/OrderCard.tsx` `OrderTypeIcon`: pickup/default branch renders `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-6
    h-6 …">` (24px, normal stroke, centred next to the payment icon); dine-in renders `<TableOrderIcon>`; delivery
    its own glyph. No raw `<ShoppingBag>`/store glyph for order type (the `Store*` hits are the unrelated
    `StoreMapOrigin` type / `storeMapOrigin` delivery prop; `consumeStoredRecoveryTarget` is unrelated).
  - `pages/OrdersPage.tsx` `getOrderTypeIcon`: `pickup` → `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-6 h-6">`, `dine-in` →
    `<TableOrderIcon>`, `default` → `<PickupOrderIcon>` (delivery → `Truck`). So the historical list uses the same
    shared glyphs — no second mismatched pickup icon.
  - **Unrelated `ShoppingBag`/`Package` (reported, kept):** `OrdersPage.tsx:742` `<ShoppingBag w-16 h-16>` is the
    **empty-state illustration** (no orders), not an order-type glyph; `OrdersPage.tsx:805` `<Package w-4 h-4>` is
    the **item-count** badge. Neither is a pickup order-type icon, so both are left as-is.
- **Likely cause of the screenshot mismatch:** a **stale running-POS build** — the round-145 OrderCard fix (and the
  already-correct OrdersPage) is in source but the visible app predates the refresh. No new code was required.
- **Preserved.** No order creation/checkout/payment/status/table/delivery/routing/fetching/i18n changes; dashboard
  tabs/navbar/settings/staff-schedule untouched. Tracker-only edit.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`, no code changed); `git diff
  --check` over the four scoped files → clean (only this tracker changed); `rg "Store|Storefront|store icon|<ShoppingBag|<Package"`
  over `OrderCard.tsx` + `OrdersPage.tsx` → no raw pickup order-type glyph (only `StoreMapOrigin`/`storeMapOrigin`/
  `consumeStoredRecoveryTarget`, the empty-state `ShoppingBag`, and the item-count `Package` — all unrelated). Live
  visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800 — visual-only, no data mutation):
  - Opened the main dashboard route **Πίνακας** (tabs Παραγγελίες / Παραδόθηκαν / Τραπέζια / Ακυρωμένες). Active
    **pickup order #00001** under "18,50 €" shows the **plain shopping-bag glyph** under the amount — not a
    storefront/cash-register/awning icon — centred, reading as the same pickup glyph family as the chooser.
  - Opened the **Νέα Παραγγελία** order-type chooser for comparison only: the **Παραλαβή** pickup card shows the
    same plain bag glyph. Closed the chooser with X — did not select delivery/pickup/table, and did not
    create/edit/order/checkout (no data action).

### Supervisor round 152 — StaffScheduleView (salon Πρόγραμμα Προσωπικού) touch-first + modal glass — COMPLETED

- **Why.** Codex live QA confirmed the POS sidebar Πρόγραμμα Προσωπικού opens `verticals/salon/StaffScheduleView`
  (Greek/dark, 1280x800), no-shifts-this-week branch live-visible. Source carried **18 hover utilities** and the
  local Preview-Week / Create-Shift modals were opaque (`panelClass` = `bg-zinc-950/95`÷`bg-white`). Blue/cyan was
  already 0.
- **Fix (UI-only, `pages/verticals/salon/StaffScheduleView.tsx`).**
  - **Hover → touch (all 18 removed).** Converted every `hover:` to `active:` across `iconButtonClass`,
    `primaryButtonClass`, `secondaryButtonClass`, error retry, role-filter chips, day plus buttons, empty-day drop
    buttons (`active:border-yellow-*`/`active:text-yellow-*`), unscheduled staff pills, time-preset chips, next-day
    shortcut, and the create-modal cancel. Hover audit now **0**.
  - **Modal glass.** Added a dedicated `modalPanelClass` (kept separate from the page-section `panelClass` so only
    the dialogs go glass): dark `bg-black/60 border-white/10 ring-1 ring-white/15 backdrop-blur-2xl shadow-2xl
    shadow-black/50`, light `bg-white/60 border-white/70 ring-1 ring-white/60 backdrop-blur-2xl shadow-2xl
    shadow-black/30`, and swapped it into the **Preview Week** and **Create Shift** dialog panels. Backdrop already
    `bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm`; the headers carry only `border-b` (no opaque bg) so they read translucent on the
    glass. Close buttons use the shared `iconButtonClass` — `h-11 w-11 inline-flex items-center justify-center`,
    explicit `text-zinc-100`÷`text-slate-800`, active-only — so they stay visible/centred/touch-sized. Portal,
    `role="dialog"`/`aria-modal`, Escape, `max-h`/overflow preserved.
  - **Smoother rounding.** Stat cards, day cards, empty-day drop zones, the modal empty-preview panel, and the two
    create-modal inner panels `rounded-xl` → `rounded-2xl`. Compact chips/buttons/preset/validation boxes and the
    h-9/h-11 controls kept `rounded-xl/lg`.
- **Preserved.** No staff fetching, import/publish, week navigation, create-shift validation, date/time helpers,
  save handler, data shapes, i18n keys, or route/module gating touched — classNames + the one new derived
  `modalPanelClass` const only. Layout/hierarchy intact (header controls, stat row, role-chip row, weekly grid,
  unscheduled strip); `scrollbar-hide` kept on the scroll regions — no new visible default scrollbars; role-chip
  row stays horizontally scrollable. Protected order dashboard / order-taking / navbar / order tabs / settings not
  touched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `StaffScheduleView.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `StaffScheduleView.tsx`
  → **0**; `rg "text-blue|bg-blue|border-blue|ring-blue|focus:ring-blue|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan|from-blue|to-blue|via-blue|from-cyan|to-cyan|via-cyan|sky-"`
  over `StaffScheduleView.tsx` → **0**. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark,
  1280x800 — visual-only, no data mutation; **all three branches live-tested**):
  - **Main no-shifts schedule page PASSED:** rounded-2xl stat cards / day cards / empty-day zones; role-chip strip
    remains horizontally scrollable with no visible scrollbar problem; no blue/cyan; no text/icon overlap.

### Supervisor round 154 — Settings modal grouped left-nav (clarity slice 1) — COMPLETED

- **Why.** The Settings modal (`ConnectionSettingsModal`) is already on-palette glass (hover/color audits 0), but
  the left rail showed **9 equal-weight cards** with long subtitles — high cognitive load. The founder asked for
  Settings to be understandable "like for a 10-year-old" without losing functionality. This is the first safe
  redesign slice: **group + calm the left navigation only**; no settings behaviour touched.
- **Fix (UI-only, `components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).**
  - **Grouped nav.** Added `settingsNavGroups` mapping the 9 sections into **3 groups** using the existing
    `settings.settingsHub.groups.{daily,device,system}` locale strings (no new visible strings): **daily** =
    admin, connection; **device** = printing, payments, hardware, terminal; **system** = security, database, about.
    Replaced the flat `settingsNav.map(...)` render with a grouped render: a small **uppercase group header**
    (`text-[11px] uppercase tracking-wider` muted) above each group's rows. All 9 sections remain reachable; order
    within groups is the operator-reach order.
  - **Calmer rows.** Tightened each row to compact touch size (`px-3 py-2.5`, icon chip `h-9 w-9` was `h-10 w-10`,
    `gap-2.5`, `rounded-xl`); **active row stays clearly yellow** (`border-yellow-400/40 bg-yellow-400/15`, chip
    `bg-yellow-400/25 ring-yellow-400/40`), **inactive rows neutral glass** (`bg-white/5`÷`dark:bg-black/10`,
    `active:bg-white/10`÷`dark:active:bg-white/5`). The yellow icon chip is unchanged. Labels + details are
    **unchanged** (`sectionMeta`), wrapped safely with `break-words` for Greek; no overlap. No hover — active/tap
    only (`active:` / `dark:active:`, which the hover audit correctly ignores).
- **Preserved.** No save/sync/delete/reset/hardware/printer/payment/recovery behaviour touched. Right detail pane,
  every section body/handler, `openSection`/section-scroll-reset, LiquidGlassModal shell, close button,
  Escape/backdrop close, all i18n keys, and data shapes intact. Did NOT redesign Data internals, Hardware
  internals, Printer submodal, PaymentTerminalsSection, ConfirmDialog, RecoveryPanel, PrintQueuePanel,
  CashRegisterSection, or CallerIdSection. `settingsNav` kept as the per-section icon source (looked up by id).
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` → **0**; `rg "text-blue|...|pink"` (full audit) over `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`
  → **0**. Independent Codex re-run agreed: type-check clean, scoped `git diff --check` clean, hover audit 0,
  off-palette blue/cyan/purple-family audit 0, and the group locale keys `daily`/`device`/`system` confirmed present
  in **en, el, de, it, fr**. Live visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800 —
  visual-only, no data mutation):
  - **Settings shell / left nav PASSED:** the left rail now shows **3 uppercase groups** (Καθημερινή ρύθμιση /
    Συσκευή / Σύστημα), compact rows, active row clearly yellow, inactive rows neutral glass, labels/details wrap
    safely with no overlap.
  - **Main/admin PASSED:** clicked Αυτό το ταμείο only — right pane shows Ρύθμιση συσκευής with terminal/admin cards
    and allowed-area chips intact; did **not** press Συγχρονισμός ρυθμίσεων.
  - **Printer PASSED:** clicked Εκτυπωτής only — printer settings trigger + print-queue panel render intact/aligned;
    did **not** press Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή, refresh, pause, or cancel queue.
  - **Devices PASSED:** clicked Συσκευές only — peripherals switches, cash-register/fiscal panel, and Caller ID
    section render intact/readable; did **not** toggle, configure fiscal, test, or save.
  - **Data PASSED:** clicked Δεδομένα only — recovery/snapshots list + detail card render intact with the grouped
    nav; did **not** refresh/create-snapshot/export/open-folder/restore/sync/delete/reset/save.

### Supervisor round 155 — Settings Devices section readability (peripheral cards + green Save) — COMPLETED (green-Save corrected once before passing)

- **Why.** After round 154's grouped nav, the **Devices (hardware)** right pane was still the densest part of
  Settings — five peripherals as divider-separated rows reading like a technical dump. This slice makes that section
  scannable **without** changing any hardware/fiscal/caller-ID/save behaviour.
- **Fix (UI-only, inside `activeSettingsSection === 'hardware'` only).**
  - **Five peripheral rows → glass cards.** Wrapped each of the top five devices (Weighing Scale, Customer Display,
    Serial Barcode Scanner, Card Reader/MSR, Loyalty/NFC) in a `rounded-2xl border liquid-glass-modal-border
    bg-white/5 px-4 py-3 … dark:bg-black/10` card (the same inner-card recipe the Admin section uses), preserving
    each card's existing **title, helper text, connected/Running status pill, switch, and expanded config
    fields/connect-disconnect actions** unchanged. Removed the four `border-t` dividers **between** the cards (the
    `space-y-4` container now spaces them).
  - **CashRegister / Caller ID kept separated.** The two `border-t` separators **before `CashRegisterSection`** and
    **between it and `CallerIdSection`** are retained, so those two panels stay visually grouped apart from the
    peripheral cards (no card-in-card nesting; both components untouched).
  - **Green Save.** The bottom peripherals Save button changed from the yellow `liquidGlassModalButton('primary')`
    to a clearly **semantic-green** save action, `w-full`, centred (`inline-flex items-center justify-center`),
    touch-sized (`py-3`), active-only (`active:scale-[0.98]`). Label key `settings.peripherals.saveButton` unchanged.
    *(Correction after live QA: the first pass used the subtle glass tint `liquid-glass-modal-button …
    bg-green-500/20 text-green-700/300`, which read black/neutral in dark mode; replaced with a clear solid fill
    `border-green-500 bg-green-600 text-white shadow-lg shadow-green-600/30 active:bg-green-700` so it is obviously
    green at rest in both themes while staying premium/touch-first.)*
- **Preserved.** No new locale strings (reused existing `settings.peripherals.*` keys). Every state variable,
  input, `onChange`, `bridge.hardware.*` call, `bridge.settings.updateLocal` save payload, and toast key intact;
  shared `switchTrackClass` and the yellow/neutral + semantic green/red palette intact. CashRegisterSection /
  CallerIdSection files and internals **not** edited. Nav grouping, Admin, Connection, Printer, Payments, Terminal,
  Security, Data, About, PrinterSettingsModal, PaymentTerminalsSection, RecoveryPanel, PrintQueuePanel,
  ConfirmDialog, route/module gating, i18n locale files, and data shapes **not** touched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` → **0**; `rg "text-blue|...|pink"` (full audit) over `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`
  → **0**. Independent Codex re-run (after the green-Save correction) agreed: type-check clean, scoped `git diff
  --check` clean, hover audit 0, off-palette blue/cyan/purple-family audit 0, and the Save class confirmed solid
  semantic green (`bg-green-600 text-white border-green-500 shadow-green-600/30 active:bg-green-700`). Live visual
  sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800 — **Devices section only**, visual-only, no
  data mutation):
  - **Top Devices section PASSED:** clicked only the Συσκευές nav row — the five peripheral rows now read as
    separate rounded-2xl glass cards, switches aligned, helper text readable, divider-dump look gone. **No switches
    toggled.**
  - **Cash register/fiscal panel PASSED:** rendered intact and visually separated below the device cards; did
    **not** press Add Device, refresh, fiscal switch, configure, or any fiscal action.
  - **Caller ID panel PASSED:** rendered intact below the fiscal panel, dense but unchanged by this round; did
    **not** select profiles, type fields, test, save, or enable.
  - **Save peripherals button PASSED (after correction):** the bottom Αποθήκευση περιφερειακών button is now
    **unmistakably green at rest**, full-width / centred / touch-sized; **Save not pressed**. (First pass used the
    subtle `bg-green-500/20` glass tint that read neutral in dark mode — corrected to the solid green fill above.)

### Supervisor round 156 — Settings Data section clarity (RecoveryPanel control surface) — COMPLETED

- **Why.** Settings → Data (the `RecoveryPanel`) was stable but read like a technical admin panel — actions in a
  cramped `flex-wrap`, the snapshot UUID dominating the selected-point card. This slice makes it feel like a simple,
  touch-first backup/recover **control surface** without changing any recovery behaviour.
- **Fix (UI-only, `components/recovery/RecoveryPanel.tsx`).**
  - **Local class constants** (`ACTION_BTN_BASE/_NEUTRAL/_GREEN/_AMBER`) to de-duplicate the button recipe:
    full-width-in-cell, centred icon+label, consistent touch height (`py-2.5`), `active:scale-[0.98]` only.
  - **Separated, gridded layout.** The top card is now a clean vertical rhythm — **header** (title + help), then
    **latest-snapshot status** (2 cards), then a **responsive action grid** (`grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-4`, or
    `grid-cols-2` when `compact`) for Refresh / Create snapshot / Export current / Open folder (was a cramped
    `flex-wrap`). The selected-snapshot **export/restore** buttons moved to a `grid-cols-2` so they size evenly
    instead of wrapping.
  - **Semantic action colour.** **Create snapshot** → green (`border-green-500 bg-green-500/15 text-green-700
    dark:text-green-300`); **Restore selected** → amber/caution (now `text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-100` so it's
    readable in both themes, was dark-only `text-amber-100`); export/open/refresh stay neutral glass; the
    `selectedPoint.error` banner stays amber. No red used (no destructive action lives in this surface).
  - **De-emphasised UUID.** The selected point's id dropped from `text-xs` to `text-[10px] break-all leading-snug
    opacity-70` so the path-like string wraps safely and no longer dominates the detail card.
- **Preserved exactly (behaviour).** `bridge.recovery.listPoints / createSnapshot / exportCurrent / exportPoint /
  restorePoint / openDir`, `selectedPoint` selection, `busyAction`/`loading`, all toasts, the
  `runWithPrivilegedConfirmation` PIN flow, and the `compact` prop branches are unchanged — only JSX structure and
  classNames were touched. List/detail `maxHeight` (`RECOVERY_PANEL_MAX_HEIGHT_STYLE`) and `scrollbar-hide` kept;
  no card-in-card clutter introduced. **No `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` change was needed** (the Data wrapper was
  already fine). No new visible strings — all existing `settings.recovery.*` / `common.*` keys reused; no locale
  files touched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over

### Supervisor round 157 — Modern POS toggle/switch recipe (Settings + Printer) — COMPLETED

- **Why.** Live QA flagged the bulky old **64x36** yellow switch pill (Settings → Printer modal, and the same
  shared `switchTrackClass` in Devices/Screen/Security) as ugly/toy-like. This slims and modernises the switch
  visuals only — no toggle logic touched.
- **Fix (UI-only, switch visuals).**
  - **Shared `switchTrackClass`** (`ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`) rebuilt as a slim **56x32** glass pill
    (`w-14 h-8`) with a **24x24 white knob** (`after:h-6 after:w-6`, `after:bg-white after:shadow-md`): clear
    **solid-yellow ON** (`peer-checked:bg-yellow-400 peer-checked:border-yellow-400/70`, subtle
    `peer-checked:shadow-yellow-500/20`) over a recessed **neutral glass OFF** (`bg-gray-200/70`÷`dark:bg-white/10`
    + `shadow-inner backdrop-blur-sm`). Smooth travel `peer-checked:after:translate-x-6` (= 24px, flush both ends;
    `56 − 24 knob − 8 inset`), `rtl:` variant kept. Dropped the heavy yellow/white **gradients** and the knob
    border for a cleaner flat-modern look. Comment updated to the new 56x32 / translate-x-6 geometry.
  - **Printer receipt-action switch** (`PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`, `renderReceiptActionSwitchRow`) — the same
    recipe applied to its `enabled ?` track/knob spans: track `h-8 w-14 … p-1`, ON `bg-yellow-400
    border-yellow-400/70 shadow-yellow-500/20`, OFF `bg-white/10 border-white/15 shadow-inner`; knob `h-6 w-6
    bg-white shadow-md`, `translate-x-6`÷`translate-x-0`. Receipt-row icon/text alignment + the AUTO badge
    untouched.
  - Tap/focus feedback only (`peer-focus:ring-yellow-400/50` / `active`-based), **no hover**; base
    white/black/grey/yellow palette; semantic green/red elsewhere untouched; no blue/cyan/purple/pink.
- **Preserved exactly (behaviour).** No `checked`/`onChange`/`onClick`/`enabled` logic or state names changed — only
  the switch's visual `span`/knob/`after:` classes and the shared recipe comment. All other switches that consume
  `switchTrackClass` (Devices peripherals, Screen & Sound, Security) inherit the new look automatically. No locale
  strings added; no printer-setup/fiscal/data/recovery logic touched.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` + `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg
  "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over both modals → **0**; `rg "text-blue|...|pink"` (full audit) over both →
  **0**. Independent Codex re-run agreed: type-check clean, scoped `git diff --check` clean, hover audit 0, and
  off-palette blue/cyan/purple-family audit 0 over `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` + `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`. Live
  visual sign-off PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800 — visual-only, no data mutation):
  - **Printer Settings modal PASSED:** opened Settings → Printer → Configure Printer only (did **not** run wizard,
    detect printers, test print, save, or toggle receipt actions) — the receipt-action switches are now a slim
    modern **56x32 yellow pill with white knob**, no bulky gradient slab, aligned inside the rows.
  - **Devices section PASSED:** clicked Settings → Devices only (no peripheral/fiscal toggle) — the shared switches
    render as clean **neutral-glass OFF pills**, smaller but still touch-sized and aligned in the rounded device
    cards.
  - **Screen & Sound section PASSED:** clicked Settings → Screen & Sound only (no audio/receipt toggle, no save) —
    the shared switches match the modern recipe and stay aligned with their labels.

### Supervisor round 158 — Semantic green Save / red Cancel on Settings + Printer form pairs — COMPLETED

- **Why.** The founder asked saves to be green and cancels red. Several Settings saves were already green
  (Connection save, Peripherals save), but a few real form Save/Cancel pairs still used the generic yellow/neutral
  `liquidGlassModalButton`. This round fixes **only those specific pairs** — no global primary-button rewrite.
- **Fix (UI-only, button class only).** Added two local semantic-button consts per file (`SAVE_BTN_MD`/`_SM` =
  solid green, `CANCEL_BTN_MD` = soft red), sized to match `liquidGlassModalButton('*','md'|'sm')`
  (`px-4 py-2` / `px-3 py-1.5`), centred (`inline-flex items-center justify-center`), `rounded-xl`, tap-only
  (`active:scale-[0.98]`). **Save** = solid green fill + white text (`border-green-500 bg-green-600 text-white
  shadow-green-600/30 active:bg-green-700`, clearly green in dark + light); **Cancel** = soft red outline/fill
  (`border-red-500/40 bg-red-500/10 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 active:bg-red-500/20`) — deliberately **not** a
  solid destructive slab. Buttons changed:
  - `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`: **PIN edit pair** — Cancel (`() => setEditingPin(false)`,
    `modals.connectionSettings.cancel`) → red; Save PIN (`handleSavePin`) → green. **Terminal Preferences** save
    (`handleSaveTerminalPreferences`) → green.
  - `PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`: **Logo settings** Save (`handleSaveLogoSettings`, sm) → green; **add/edit form
    footer** — Cancel (`() => { setViewMode('list'); resetForm() }`) → red, Save (`handleSave`) → green.
- **Preserved exactly (behaviour).** No `onClick`/`onChange`/`disabled`/`loading`/validation logic or labels
  changed — only each button's `className`. Disabled/loading branches (`disabled={logoSaving}`,
  `disabled={loading || !formData.name}`) keep working via `disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed`. **Not
  touched:** Change PIN, Configure Printer, Add terminal, test/sync/paste, delete buttons, ConfirmDialog internals,
  or any other primary action; `liquidGlassModalButton` is still imported and used by all other buttons (10× in
  ConnectionSettingsModal, 16× in PrinterSettingsModal). No locale strings added; blurry-glass modal language,
  rounded edges, no hover, no blue/cyan/purple/pink.
- Verified: `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over both
  modals + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over both → **0**; `rg "text-blue|...|pink"`
  (full audit) over both → **0**. Supervisor sign-off accepted on the static evidence above; the change is
  className-only (no behaviour/onClick/validation change), so risk is low. Recommended operator visual pass (not a
  blocker): at 1280x800 Greek/dark, visual-only — open Settings → **Connection** Change-PIN edit pair (green Save
  PIN, red Cancel), Settings → **Screen & Sound** (green Save Terminal Preferences), Settings → **Printer →
  Configure Printer** logo Save (green) and the add/edit printer footer (green Save, red Cancel); confirm green
  saves read clearly green in dark, red cancels are soft (not destructive slabs), both centred/touch-sized; other
  primary actions (Change PIN, Configure Printer, test/sync, delete) unchanged. **Press no Save/Cancel — toggle/save
  nothing**. These modal buttons render the same regardless of vertical, so they are visible in the current live org.

  `forms.orderType.*` labels) whose pickup branch used `<Package className="w-6 h-6" />`.
- **Fix (1 file, pickup glyph only).** `CustomerInfoForm.tsx`: dropped the now-unused `Package` from the
  `lucide-react` import, added `import PickupOrderIcon from "./icons/PickupOrderIcon";` (mirrors the existing
  `TableOrderIcon` import), and swapped line ~99 `{type === "pickup" && <Package className="w-6 h-6" />}` →
  `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-6 h-6" />`. Compact, aligned, `w-6 h-6`, `currentColor` (no forced yellow). The
  dine-in (`TableOrderIcon`) and delivery (`Truck`) branches were left exactly as they were.
- **Deliberately NOT changed.** `pages/OrdersPage.tsx:805` keeps lucide `Package` — it is the "{{count}} items"
  detail-row indicator (beside User/Phone), **not** an order-type choice, so it is out of scope. No table icon,
  nav/tabs, settings, translations, colours outside the pickup glyph, ordering logic, or data logic touched.
  (The wider blue→yellow / `hover:`→`active:` / dine-in `Utensils`→`TableOrderIcon` / `rounded-2xl` diff visible in
  `CustomerInfoForm.tsx` is **pre-existing uncommitted polish on this branch**, not part of round 159 — this round's

  `CustomerInfoForm.tsx` + this tracker → clean (exit 0); `rg` audit → **0** pickup order-type display paths still

### Supervisor round 160 — StaffScheduleView role-filter chips wrap instead of clipping — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800).** Sidebar → **Πρόγραμμα Προσωπικού** showed the
  role-filter row clipped at the right edge — the last chip was cut off (`Μάγε...`). The row was a single-line
  horizontal scroll strip with a hidden scrollbar, so any chip past the viewport width was invisible and
  unreachable, violating the touch/multilingual rule that buttons must fit without relying on hidden scroll.
- **Root cause.** `StaffScheduleView.tsx` line ~1169 wrapped the **All roles** + `availableRoles` chips in
  `flex gap-2 overflow-x-auto scrollbar-hide`, and every chip was pinned `shrink-0`. Overflow scrolled horizontally
  with no visible scrollbar → clipped tail chip.
- **Fix (UI-only, chip group lines ~1169-1201).**
  - Container: `mt-4 flex gap-2 overflow-x-auto scrollbar-hide pb-1` → `mt-4 flex max-w-full flex-wrap gap-2 pb-1`.
    Chips now **wrap** onto additional rows instead of scrolling off-screen; no native/hidden scrollbar.
  - Both chips (All-roles button + each role button): dropped `shrink-0` (only needed for the removed scroll strip),
    added `text-center leading-tight` so long labels (e.g. German `Empfangsmitarbeiter`) wrap cleanly and centred
    inside the chip rather than truncating, and kept tight line spacing so a 2-line chip doesn't bloat the header.
  - Removed the forced CSS `capitalize` from the **role** chip (`getRoleChipLabel(role)` is already localized — CSS
    re-casing a translated label is wrong/redundant). Translation keys and locale files untouched.
- **Preserved.** Same filtering behaviour and state — `roleFilter`, `setRoleFilter('all')`, `availableRoles.map`,
  `getRoleChipLabel`, the `roleFilter === 'all' | role` active checks — unchanged. No fetch/data/shift-creation
  logic touched. Theme language intact: selected chip `bg-yellow-400 border-yellow-400 text-black`, inactive chips
  neutral dark (`bg-zinc-900 border-zinc-700 text-zinc-300`) / light (`bg-white border-slate-300 text-slate-700`),
  `rounded-xl` smooth edges, `px-4 py-2` touch size. Touch-first: only `active:` press states, no
  `hover/dark:hover/group-hover`. The page's other `scrollbar-hide` (vertical schedule list at ~1204) is a separate
  legitimate scroll region and was left as-is.
- **Adjacent observation (NOT changed — out of scope this round).** The staff **shift card** role label at line
  ~1300 (`{shift.roleLabel}`) still carries a forced `capitalize` on a localized label — same i18n smell as the
  chip fix above, but it sits outside the scoped 1169-1201 filter group. Flagged for a future focused round rather
  than churned here.
- Verified (static): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `StaffScheduleView.tsx` + this tracker → clean (exit 0); `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `StaffScheduleView.tsx` → **0**.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, visual-only / no data
  mutation). Opened sidebar → **Πρόγραμμα Προσωπικού**. The role-filter row now **wraps into multiple rows** with all
  14 chips fully visible: Όλοι οι ρόλοι, Σερβιτόρος, Υπεύθυνος Καθαριότητας, Οδηγός, Διευθυντής Υποκαταστήματος,
  Διαχειριστής, Μάγειρας, Υπεύθυνος Βάρδιας, Καθαριστής, Μπάρμαν, Ρεσεψιόν, Υπηρεσία Δωματίου, Ταμίας, Αρχιμάγειρας.
  No chip is clipped at the right edge; no horizontal scrollbar is visible; the selected chip stays yellow/black,
  inactive chips are neutral, and labels are centred/readable. No shift create/edit, no Import/Preview/plus actions,
  no data action.

### Supervisor round 161 — Settings → Connection visible Save is now solid semantic green — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800).** Settings → **Σύνδεση / Ρυθμίσεις Σύνδεσης**
  showed the bottom-left **Αποθήκευση** button (with a Check icon, next to Paste/Test/Sync) rendering as a **neutral
  dark glass** button. The founder asked Save buttons to be green. Round 158 greened the PIN, Terminal-Preferences,
  Printer-logo and add/edit-printer saves, but this visible Connection save was missed and stayed neutral.

  `liquid-glass-modal-button … bg-green-500/20 text-green-700 dark:text-green-300 border-green-500/40` class. A
  20%-opacity green tint over the frosted white-glass base reads clearly green in light mode but **neutral/dark** in
  dark mode (the same drift Round 155 fixed for the peripherals save) — only the Check icon + label were green, not
  the fill.
- **Fix (UI-only, 1 button).** `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`: swapped that button's `className` to the existing
  local `SAVE_BTN_MD` const (the solid-green Round 158 recipe: `rounded-xl border border-green-500 bg-green-600
  px-4 py-2 text-sm font-semibold text-white shadow-md shadow-green-600/30 active:scale-[0.98] active:bg-green-700`,
  plus `inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2` so the centred Check + label layout is preserved). Solid
  green in both themes, white text, touch-sized, active-press only, no hover. The `<Check className="w-4 h-4" />`
  icon and `{t('modals.connectionSettings.save')}` label are unchanged.
- **Preserved.** `onClick={handleSaveConnection}` and all of its connection-code parsing / save / data behaviour
  untouched; label and i18n key (`modals.connectionSettings.save`) unchanged; this button has no disabled/loading
  prop, so none was added or removed. **Not touched:** Paste-both, Test, Sync, Change PIN, Configure, terminal
  identity, PIN editing, theme picker, session-timeout autosave, or any other section. The PIN save and
  Terminal-Preferences save already used `SAVE_BTN_MD` (Round 158) — `SAVE_BTN_MD` now backs all 3 true Connection
  saves. The 4 `bg-green-500/20` matches that remain in the file are small **status pills**
  (`rounded-full text-xs … text-green-400`), not Save buttons, so they are correctly left alone. No locale strings
  added; no layout change.
- Verified (static): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` + this tracker → clean (exit 0); `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over

  mutation). Settings → **Σύνδεση / Ρυθμίσεις Σύνδεσης**: the **Αποθήκευση** button now renders as a clear solid

  neutral. Operator did not press Save/Paste/Test/Sync and did not mutate data.

### Supervisor round 162 — Settings → Card Machines empty-state Add Terminal is now a yellow primary action — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800).** Settings → **Μηχανές καρτών → Ρύθμιση** opens
  the Payment Terminals configuration view. Its empty-state footer has two wide actions — **Αναζήτηση** (Discover)
  and **Προσθήκη Τερματικού** (Add Terminal) — rendering at the **same neutral glass weight**, so the real next
  setup step (add a terminal) was not visually obvious on a touchscreen.
- **Root cause.** Both footer buttons used `liquidGlassModalButton(..., 'md')`; Add Terminal used the
  `'primary'` variant whose `bg-yellow-400/20` tint over the frosted white-glass base reads neutral (especially in
  dark mode), so it carried no more visual weight than the `'secondary'` Discover button beside it.
- **Fix (UI-only, 1 button).** `ecr/PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx`: the Add Terminal button (the
  `setShowConfigModal(true)` action with the `Plus` icon and `t('ecr.addManual', 'Add Terminal')` label) swapped
  from `liquidGlassModalButton('primary', 'md')` to a **solid yellow primary**:
  `flex-1 flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-xl border border-yellow-500 bg-yellow-400 px-4 py-2 text-sm
  font-semibold text-black shadow-md shadow-yellow-500/30 transition-transform duration-150 active:scale-[0.98]
  active:bg-yellow-500`. Yellow fill + black text/icon in both themes, centred Plus + label, `rounded-xl`,
  touch-sized, active-press only, no hover. `flex-1` keeps it the same width as Discover so the row stays balanced.
- **Preserved.** The Add Terminal `onClick` (`setEditingDevice(undefined)` → `setSelectedDiscoveredDevice(undefined)`
  → `setShowConfigModal(true)`) and modal-open behaviour unchanged; `Plus` icon and `ecr.addManual` i18n key
  unchanged. **Discover/Αναζήτηση stays neutral secondary** (`liquidGlassModalButton('secondary', 'md')`). Not
  touched: `TerminalConfigModal`, `TerminalDiscoveryModal`, discovery/config open logic, API/data logic, stat cards,
  status badges (semantic status colours kept — not forced yellow), Back, and Refresh. `liquidGlassModalButton`
  remains imported/used by the Discover button (no unused import). No locale strings added.
- Verified (static): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx` + this tracker → clean (exit 0); `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx` → **0** matches (exit 1).
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, visual-only / no data
  mutation). Settings → **Μηχανές καρτών → Ρύθμιση / Τερματικά Πληρωμών**: the footer now shows **Αναζήτηση** as the
  neutral secondary button and **Προσθήκη Τερματικού** as a clear solid yellow primary button with a black Plus icon
  + black label, centred and touch-sized. Stat/status colours remain semantic. Operator did not press
  Search/Add/Refresh/Back and did not create or mutate any terminal data.

### Supervisor round 163 — Settings → Devices → Caller ID Save & Activate is green when enabled, neutral when blocked — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800).** Settings → **Συσκευές → Αναγνώριση Κλήσεων**
  (VoIP/SIP): the **Αποθήκευση και Ενεργοποίηση** action is a save/enable action but rendered **amber/yellow**, and
  in the current invalid/test-required state it appeared as a **muddy amber disabled** button. App direction is
  Save = green, Disable/Delete = red, setup-primary = yellow — so a save-and-enable action should be green when
  enabled and clearly neutral while blocked, not amber.
- **Root cause.** The Save & Activate button used a static `bg-amber-500 … active:bg-amber-400 disabled:opacity-50`
  class. `disabled:opacity-50` only dims the amber, so the blocked state read as a faded amber rather than a clearly
  inactive control, and the enabled state carried the wrong (setup-primary) semantic colour for a save.
- **Fix (UI-only, 1 button).** `peripherals/CallerIdSection.tsx`: the disabled gate is now hoisted into one const
  `saveAndActivateDisabled` (the **exact** existing expression — `saving || testing || !config.sipServer.trim() ||
  !config.sipUsername.trim() || (authenticatedMode && !config.hasPassword && !config.password?.trim()) ||
  requiresFreshTest`) so it drives both `disabled={…}` and the styling without duplication. The button's `className`
  is now conditional:
  - **enabled** → `bg-green-600 text-white active:bg-green-700` (solid semantic green both themes, white Save icon +
    label, centred `inline-flex items-center gap-1.5`, `rounded-xl`, `px-3 py-2` touch-sized, active-press only);
  - **disabled** → `bg-zinc-800 text-zinc-500 cursor-not-allowed` (clearly inactive neutral grey/black — not
    amber, not green — with a readable muted label and not-allowed cursor).
- **Preserved.** Disabled conditions preserved **exactly** (same operands/order, just hoisted to the const).
  `onClick={handleSaveAndActivate}`, the test-first/validation flow (`requiresFreshTest`), Caller ID service calls,
  the `Save`/`Loader2` icon swap, and the `settings.peripherals.callerId.saveAndActivate` i18n key all unchanged.
  **Test Connection stays neutral** (`bg-zinc-700`) and **Disable stays red** (`bg-red-600/90`). Not touched:
  setup/info cards and selected-option states (their `amber-500` accents are intentionally kept — not forced green),
  fields, SIP/PBX setup-type logic, `ConnectionSettingsModal`, `CashRegisterSection`, or any locale file. No locale
  strings added.
- Verified (static): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over
  `CallerIdSection.tsx` + this tracker → clean (exit 0); `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `CallerIdSection.tsx` → **0** matches (exit 1).
- **Code inspection PASSED** (Codex/operator). Save & Activate uses `saveAndActivateDisabled` for both
  `disabled={…}` and `className`; the enabled branch is `bg-green-600 text-white active:bg-green-700` and the
  disabled branch is `bg-zinc-800 text-zinc-500 cursor-not-allowed`. Test Connection stays neutral and Disable
  stays red.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED — blocked/disabled state** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800,
  visual-only / no data mutation). Settings → **Συσκευές → Αναγνώριση Κλήσεων** (VoIP/SIP): in the current
  blocked/test-required state, **Αποθήκευση και Ενεργοποίηση** now renders as a neutral dark/grey disabled button —
  not amber/yellow and not green-looking. The bottom **Αποθήκευση περιφερειακών** save remains green. Operator did
  not type SIP fields, run Test Connection, save, enable, disable, toggle, or mutate any data.
- **Enabled-green state: code-verified only** (not exercised live). Turning the button green requires a passing
  **Test Connection** against a real SIP/live line (`requiresFreshTest` must clear), which the operator
  intentionally did not perform to avoid activating a live line. The enabled branch
  (`bg-green-600 text-white active:bg-green-700`) is confirmed by code inspection + the single
  `saveAndActivateDisabled` gate that drives both `disabled` and the styling, so it cannot read enabled-green while
  still disabled.

### Supervisor round 164 — OrderDashboard pickup row icon reads as the chooser bag (green, weighted) — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (user report).** The pickup icon on the main **OrderDashboard** order row (the small glyph
  under/near the price in the Orders tab) still did not feel like the same pickup icon used when choosing order
  type. Round 159 had already routed every chooser/row through the shared `PickupOrderIcon`, but at the row's tiny
  24px footprint the bag was drawn in **neutral grey at the icon's default stroke**, so it read as a generic glyph
  rather than the bold bag shown in the chooser card.
- **Root cause.** `OrderCard.tsx` `OrderTypeIcon` pickup branch already used `PickupOrderIcon` (no storefront/Store
  SVG), but styled it `text-gray-700 / dark:text-gray-300` with no explicit `strokeWidth`. Same component, but the
  small grey thin-stroke render did not perceptually match the chooser's prominent weighted bag.
- **Fix (UI-only, pickup branch only).** `components/order/OrderCard.tsx`: kept the shared `PickupOrderIcon` and the
  same **24px footprint** (`w-6 h-6`) as the table/delivery row icons, added explicit **`strokeWidth={2}`** (the
  chooser's lucide weight), and swapped the tint from neutral grey to **pickup-green** — `text-green-600` (light) /
  `text-green-400` (dark) — so the tiny bag is recognizable as the same pickup glyph. (lucide strokes with
  `currentColor`, so the green text colour tints the bag outline.)
- **Preserved.** Item 1 (shared `PickupOrderIcon`, never the old storefront SVG) confirmed and kept. Order-taking
  flow, row layout, tabs, navbar, **table icon** (`TableOrderIcon`, still grey `strokeWidth={1.6}`), **delivery
  icon** (Truck), payment icons, status logic, selection logic, translations, and data/API code all unchanged; no
  modals touched. Touch-first: no `hover/dark:hover/group-hover`.
- Verified (static, re-run by Codex/operator): `npm run type-check` (from `pos-tauri`) → **clean**; `git diff
  --check` over `OrderCard.tsx` + this tracker → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `OrderCard.tsx`
  → **0** matches.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, visual-only / no data
  mutation). Opened the **dashboard module** (OrderDashboard, not the sidebar OrdersPage); the **Orders** tab shows
  pickup row **#00001**. The compact pickup order-type icon under/near **18,50 €** is now a clear **green
  shopping-bag glyph** at the same **24px footprint** as the row icons. Opened the **New Order chooser** only for
  comparison: the **Pickup** card uses the same shopping-bag silhouette (in white inside the green card). Closed the
  chooser without selecting Delivery/Pickup/Table; no order type/status/order data changed.
- **Delivery remains the truck icon; table remains its separate table icon.**

### Supervisor round 165 — Menu Management localized tabs + touch cleanup — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Codex live QA, Greek/dark).** On the running POS Menu Management page the title/search were Greek,
  but the tab buttons and refresh tooltip still showed English — **Categories, Menu Items, Ingredients, Offers,
  Refresh menu** — breaking the multilingual UX.
- **Scope (this page + its direct tests/locales only).** `pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx`, the 5 locale files
  (`en/el/de/it/fr`), `tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts`, and this tracker.
- **i18n (UI-only).** Replaced every hardcoded visible string with keys under the existing top-level **`menu`**
  namespace, keeping English `defaultValue` fallbacks (the local convention):
  - tabs → `menu.managementTabs.{categories,menuItems,ingredients,offers}` (nested to avoid colliding with the
    pre-existing `menu.categories` / `menu.items` keys);
  - refresh → `menu.refreshMenu`, `menu.refreshingMenu`;
  - status/labels → `menu.unnamed`, `menu.featured`, `menu.enable`, `menu.disable`;
  - toast/failure fallbacks → `menu.failedToLoadData`, `failedToLoadCategories`, `failedToLoadMenuItems`,
    `failedToLoadIngredients`, `failedToLoadOffers`, `categoryUpdated`, `failedToUpdateCategory`, `menuItemUpdated`,
    `failedToUpdateMenuItem`, `ingredientUpdated`, `failedToUpdateIngredient`, `offerUpdated`, `failedToUpdateOffer`,
    `onlineRequired`. Added all keys to **EN/EL/DE/IT/FR** with natural short labels (no Greek-only text).
- **Touch cleanup.** Removed all `hover:` utilities from the page — inactive tabs and the availability Eye/EyeOff
  icon buttons now use `active:bg-*`; the refresh icon button uses `active:bg-*` / `active:scale-[1.03]` (no
  `hover:bg`/`hover:scale`). `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` on the page → **0**.
- **Currency.** Imported `formatCurrency` from `../utils/format` and replaced the three `€{value.toFixed(2)}`
  literals (menu items, ingredients, combos) with `formatCurrency(value, 'EUR', language)`. No euro glyph, no
  `.toFixed(2)`, no mojibake remains.
- **Behaviour/data preserved exactly.** `activeTab` union and the internal **`subcategories`** key are unchanged;
  `bridge.menu.getSubcategories` / `updateSubcategory` and `renderMenuItems` keep their historical naming and flow;
  no load/toggle semantics changed and no data mutations added. Only the staff-facing labels + styling + price
  formatting changed.
- **Test contract updated.** `menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` now asserts: tab labels come from
  `t('menu.managementTabs.*')` (not hardcoded English); the `subcategories` key + `bridge.getSubcategories` flow are
  preserved; **no** `hover:`/`group-hover:` utilities remain; the refresh label uses the translated keys; prices use
  `formatCurrency` with no euro literal/mojibake; status + toast fallbacks use `menu.*` keys.
- Verified (static): `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `node --test
  tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` → **8 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed** (all 5
  locales 6075 keys, 0 missing / 0 extra); `git diff --check` over the 7 scoped files → clean;
  `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `MenuManagementPage.tsx` → **0**.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, read-only/no data mutation).
  Menu Management now renders the four tabs as **Κατηγορίες / Προϊόντα μενού / Υλικά / Προσφορές**; the refresh
  button accessibility label is **Ανανέωση μενού**; opening the Menu Items tab renders prices in locale format
  (`18,50 €`, `15,00 €`, etc.) with no broken euro/mojibake prefix. Operator did not toggle availability, refresh,
  save, or mutate menu data.

### Supervisor round 166 — Menu Management refresh button press-down feedback — COMPLETED

- **Live finding (post-165 supervisor note).** Round 165 passed, but the Menu Management refresh icon button still
  used `active:scale-[1.03]` — on a touchscreen press it **grows** instead of pressing down, inconsistent with the
  rest of this polish pass which uses `active:scale-95` / `active:scale-[0.98]` for tap feedback.
- **Fix (UI-only, one button).** `pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx`: the refresh icon button's enabled tap transform
  changed from `active:scale-[1.03]` → **`active:scale-95`** (press-down). Everything else on the button is
  unchanged: `active:bg-*` press colour, the disabled `opacity-60 cursor-not-allowed` branch, the accessible name
  (`aria-label={refreshLabel}` — see the tooltip follow-up below), the loading spinner, `onClick={loadData}`, and all
  data/loading behaviour. No data mutation.
- **Test contract updated.** `menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` refresh test asserts `active:scale-95` and
  `doesNotMatch(active:scale-[1.03])`; the existing no-hover / translated-label / formatCurrency contracts are kept.
- **Live QA #1 FAILED -> follow-up fix (still UI-only, no data mutation).** The press-down feedback was correct, but
  after the read-only refresh tap the pointer stayed over the button and a **native browser tooltip** appeared
  (Greek: `Ανανέωση μενού`). A `title={...}` attribute is hover behaviour and violates the touch-first/no-hover
  rule. Fix: removed `title={...}` from this page's controls — the refresh icon button (kept its existing
  `aria-label={refreshLabel}`) and the four availability/toggle icon buttons (category / menu item / ingredient /
  combo), which had only `title` → converted to **`aria-label`** carrying the same translated disable/enable text
  (or the offline-disabled message). No custom hover tooltips/classes added; the `active:scale-95` press feedback is
  kept. The page now has **0** `title=` and **5** `aria-label=` (4 toggles + refresh). Test updated to assert
  aria-label coverage on all five icon buttons and to reject any `title={` on the page.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` → **9 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the 3 scoped files → clean (exit 0);
  `rg "active:scale-\[1\.03\]|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `MenuManagementPage.tsx` → **0** matches, and
  `title=` → **0**.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, read-only/no data mutation).
  After a read-only refresh tap, the pointer rested on the refresh icon button and **no native tooltip appeared**;
  the button stayed centered in its rounded square, the page reloaded normally, and the Menu Management grid remained
  visually intact. Operator did not click availability/toggle icons, save, or mutate menu data.

### Supervisor round 167 — Loyalty page tier label localization + refresh tooltip removal — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Codex live QA, Greek/dark, 1280x800, sidebar → Πιστότητα & Επιβραβεύσεις).** Each loyalty customer
  badge showed the **raw backend tier value `none`** next to a star icon — an English/raw-token leak unacceptable in
  a multi-language POS.
- **Root cause.** `LoyaltyPage.tsx` rendered the badge as `{getTierIcon(customer.tier)}{customer.tier || 'Bronze'}`
  — the raw `tier` string printed verbatim, the `|| 'Bronze'` fallback mislabeled a missing tier, and `getTierIcon`
  always returned ≥1 star (so "none" got a star too).
- **Fix (UI/i18n, scoped to LoyaltyPage + locales).**
  - Added module-level `normalizeTier` (trim + lowercase), `isNoTier` (collapses `[\s_-]` then matches
    `none`/`no_tier`/`notier`/`unassigned`/empty), and `humanizeTier` (Title-Cases an unknown token, e.g.
    `vip_gold` → `Vip Gold`).
  - New `getTierLabel(tier)` resolver renders **localized** labels via `loyalty.tier.*` keys: no-tier → neutral
    **"No tier"** (`Χωρίς βαθμίδα`), and bronze/silver/gold/platinum localized; unknown non-empty → humanized. The
    badge now renders `{getTierLabel(customer.tier)}` — the raw `customer.tier || 'Bronze'` is gone.
  - `getTierIcon` returns **null** for no-tier (no stars); `getTierColor` returns a **neutral** grey for no-tier
    (`text-zinc-400 border-zinc-700` dark / `text-gray-500 border-gray-300` light) and keeps the existing
    purple/yellow/zinc/amber styling for platinum/gold/silver/bronze.
  - Removed the native `title={t('common.refresh', 'Refresh')}` tooltip from the refresh button (kept its
    `aria-label`); no custom hover tooltips added. Scan/refresh stay touch-first (`active:` only, no hover). The
    `<ScanDevicePanel title=...>` prop is a component heading prop (not a DOM tooltip) and is left as-is.
- **Locales.** Added `loyalty.tier.{none,bronze,silver,gold,platinum}` to **EN/EL/DE/IT/FR** (e.g. EL
  `Χωρίς βαθμίδα / Χάλκινο / Ασημένιο / Χρυσό / Πλατινένιο`; DE `Keine Stufe / Bronze / Silber / Gold / Platin`; FR
  `Aucun niveau / Bronze / Argent / Or / Platine`; IT `Nessun livello / Bronzo / Argento / Oro / Platino`).
- **Tests updated** (`coupon-loyalty-scan-ui.test.ts`). Refreshed two stale LoyaltyPage color expectations to the
  current source (scan button **yellow** not cyan; TrendingUp stat **amber** not blue). Added a tier-label test
  (asserts `getTierLabel`/`normalizeTier`/`isNoTier`, the five `loyalty.tier.*` keys, and that
  `{customer.tier || 'Bronze'}` / `{customer.tier}` are no longer rendered) and a refresh test (aria-label present,
  no `title={t('common.refresh'…)}`, `active:scale-95`, no `hover:`). Added `tier.*` to the loyalty locale-coverage
  test.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/coupon-loyalty-scan-ui.test.ts` → **6 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `npm run locale:parity` → **passed** (all 5 locales 6080 keys,
  0 missing / 0 extra); `git diff --check` over touched files → clean; `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over
  `LoyaltyPage.tsx` → **0**.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, read-only/no data mutation).
  After relogin, sidebar → **Πιστότητα & Επιβραβεύσεις** renders no-tier badges as **Χωρίς βαθμίδα** with no star
  and a neutral grey outline; raw `none` is gone from the page. A read-only refresh tap completed normally, the
  pointer rested on the refresh icon, and **no native tooltip appeared**. Operator did not click Scan, customer
  cards, redemption actions, or mutate loyalty data.

### Supervisor round 168 — NavigationSidebar native tooltip removal — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Codex live QA, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800).** Resting the pointer over the selected
  **Loyalty** icon in the left navigation rail showed a **native browser tooltip** (`Πιστότητα & Επιβραβεύσεις`).
  The POS is touchscreen-first, so DOM `title` tooltips are hover behaviour and must not appear on the sidebar.
- **Root cause.** `NavigationSidebar.tsx` set native `title={...}` on the check-in button, every dynamic module
  button (computed `title` for coming-soon / locked / plain modules), and the Z Report / Settings / Logout buttons.
- **Fix (UI/a11y, sidebar only).** Converted all five `title={...}` attributes to **`aria-label`** (none had an
  aria-label before, so the accessible name is preserved, not lost):
  - check-in → `aria-label={t('navigation.checkIn')}`; Z Report → `navigation.zReport`; Settings →
    `navigation.settings`; Logout → `navigation.logout`.
  - module buttons: renamed the computed `title` local to **`accessibleLabel`** (so a variable literally named
    `title` can't invite a DOM-tooltip regression) and rendered it as `aria-label={accessibleLabel}`. The label
    still preserves the exact semantics — coming-soon (`modules.comingSoon`), locked-module
    (`modules.lockedModule`, incl. required plan), and the localized module label for regular modules.
  - Updated the stale comment that said coming-soon modules "just show tooltip" → they now stay
    **disabled/non-clickable with an accessible label, no tooltip** (`disabled={isComingSoon}` unchanged).
- **Preserved.** No change to nav colors, selected-only neon behaviour, ordering/drag (`accessibleLabel` is purely
  the a11y name), lock/coming-soon behaviour, scroll behaviour, or click handlers. No custom hover tooltips or hover
  classes added. The `<UpgradeModal>`/lock overlay/coming-soon badge markup is untouched.
- **Tests** (`navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts`). Added a test asserting **no** `title={` anywhere in the sidebar,
  aria-label coverage on the four static buttons, and that module buttons use `aria-label={accessibleLabel}` with
  the coming-soon / locked-module / plain-label branches preserved (and the "just show tooltip" comment gone). The
  existing selected-only/logout, settings-callback, no-collapse-layer, and touch/drag contracts are kept intact.
- Verified (static, re-run by Codex/operator): `node --test tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` →
  **5 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over touched files → clean (exit 0);
  `rg "hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `NavigationSidebar.tsx` → **0**, and `title=` → **0**.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, visual-only / no data
  mutation). Scrolled the left navigation rail to the lower module set and rested the pointer over a lower rail
  control long enough for a native browser tooltip to appear if a DOM `title` remained; no tooltip appeared. Opened
  **Πιστότητα & Επιβραβεύσεις** via the ribbon icon and rested the pointer on the selected Loyalty icon; the page
  rendered normally and no native tooltip appeared. Navigation, selected neon, and rail scrolling remained intact.

### Supervisor round 169 — Order-row pickup icon weight-balanced with the table icon — COMPLETED

- **Live problem.** The Orders list pickup row showed a small bag icon that "does not match the approved order-type
  chooser" / looked storefront-ish next to the table icon.
- **Root cause (regression from round 164).** The Orders list row is rendered by `order/OrderCard.tsx` (via
  `OrderGrid`). Its `OrderTypeIcon` already used the shared `PickupOrderIcon` (lucide ShoppingBag, the same glyph as
  the OrderDashboard/OrderFlow chooser card), but round 164 set the pickup `strokeWidth={2}` while the **sibling
  table row icon uses `strokeWidth={1.6}`**. Apparent stroke weight scales with icon size, so at the 24px row
  footprint the `2` made the bag read noticeably chunkier/heavier than the table beside it — the visible mismatch.
  (The chooser cards differ here too — pickup default `2`, table `1.6` — but at 64px that gap is invisible; the row
  must balance against the *row* table icon, not the chooser.)
- **Fix (UI-only, pickup branch only).** `components/order/OrderCard.tsx`: pickup `PickupOrderIcon` strokeWidth
  `2` → **`1.6`**, exactly matching the sibling `TableOrderIcon` row icon. Kept the same `w-6 h-6` footprint and the
  pickup-green tint (`text-green-600` light / `text-green-400` dark) so the bag keeps its order-type identity. Now
  the pickup and table row icons are a balanced set: identical size + stroke weight, differing only by glyph + hue
  (the intended per-type signal, delivery = orange truck / pickup = green bag / table = neutral grey). The icon
  carries no native title/hover tooltip (the only `title=` in OrderCard is the delivery "Get Directions" map link,
  untouched and out of scope).
- **Preserved.** No change to the table icon styling, delivery truck, payment icons, navbar/tab colors, selected/
  neon behaviour, or the broader order-card layout. No hover utilities added.
- **Tests** (`tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts`). Added a static regression test asserting the pickup
  branch renders `<PickupOrderIcon … strokeWidth={1.6} />` with the green tint, that the sibling table icon is the
  same `w-6 h-6` + `strokeWidth={1.6}` it balances against, and that the heavier `strokeWidth={2}` on the green
  pickup icon is gone.
- Verified (static, re-run by Codex/operator): `node --test tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` →
  **3 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over touched files →
  clean (exit 0).
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, visual-only / no data
  mutation). On the food **OrderDashboard** Orders tab, row **#00001** now shows a clear **green shopping-bag glyph**
  under/near **18,50 €**, not the old storefront/awning mark. Opened the **New Order** chooser only for comparison:
  the Pickup card uses the same shopping-bag silhouette in white inside the green card. Closed the chooser with the
  X without selecting delivery/pickup/table and without creating or changing an order.

### Supervisor round 170 — Loyalty Scan button native-tooltip audit + regression guard — COMPLETED

- **Task.** Continue the touch-first no-native-tooltip cleanup on `LoyaltyPage.tsx`: audit the **Scan** button path
  and remove any native DOM `title` tooltip / hover-only affordance, while preserving the legitimate
  `<ScanDevicePanel title=...>` modal-heading **prop**.
- **Audit result — no native tooltip exists (no-op on the page source).** The Scan button
  (`onClick={() => setShowScanPanel(true)}`, `<ScanLine />` + visible **Scan** label) has **no** `title=` attribute
  and uses only `active:` states (`active:scale-95`, `active:bg-yellow-400/10`) — no `hover:`. Its accessible name
  comes from the visible "Scan" text, so no `aria-label` is needed (none added — visuals/layout unchanged). The
  refresh button was already fixed in round 167 (`aria-label`, no `title`). The **only** `title=` in the file is the
  `<ScanDevicePanel title={t('loyalty.scan.title', …)}>` modal-heading prop — a React component prop, **not** a DOM
  attribute — left exactly as-is. `LoyaltyPage.tsx` was therefore **not modified** this round.
- **Regression guard added** (`tests/renderer/coupon-loyalty-scan-ui.test.ts`). New focused test that
  **distinguishes a native button `title` attribute from the modal `title` prop**: a `sliceBlock` helper scopes
  assertions to a single control, so the **Scan** and **refresh** button blocks are asserted to contain no `title=`
  (and the Scan block no `hover:`), while the page is asserted to have **exactly one** `title=` overall and that it
  sits on `<ScanDevicePanel>`. This fails if anyone later adds a native tooltip to a LoyaltyPage button, yet keeps
  passing for the modal-heading prop.
- **Preserved.** No UI visuals/colors/layout change, no data/Supabase logic, no locale churn (the asserted keys —
  `loyalty.scan.button`, `loyalty.scan.title`, `common.refresh` — already exist). Page touch-first behaviour
  unchanged.
- Verified (static, re-run by Codex/operator): `node --test tests/renderer/coupon-loyalty-scan-ui.test.ts` →
  **7 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched test
  file + tracker → clean (exit 0).
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800, visual-only / no data
  mutation). Rested the pointer on the **Σάρωση** button and then on the refresh button long enough for a native
  browser tooltip to appear if a DOM `title` existed; no tooltip appeared. Tapped **Σάρωση** only to open the scan
  modal and confirmed the modal heading **Σάρωση μέλους πιστότητας** still renders correctly; closed the modal with
  X without scanning, typing, using the camera, or submitting a code.

### Supervisor round 173 — Order dashboard FAB and delivery-directions native-tooltip cleanup — COMPLETED

- **Task.** Continue the touch-first cleanup on the currently visible order dashboard. The shared
  `FloatingActionButton` forwarded a caller's `title=` prop to the DOM button, and `OrderCard` used a native `title=`
  on the delivery directions affordance.
- **Fix (Claude Code, UI/accessibility only).** `FloatingActionButton.tsx` now destructures `title` before spreading
  button props, so no caller can inject a native hover tooltip onto the touchscreen orb. If the caller did not provide
  `aria-label`, the title string becomes the accessible-label fallback. `OrderCard.tsx` moved the delivery directions
  label/disabled reason from `title=` to `aria-label`, added `role="button"` and `aria-disabled`, and left click
  behavior/layout unchanged. Modal/receipt `title` props remain untouched.
- **Regression tests.** Extended `orders-page-ui.test.ts` with a FAB guard proving `title` is not forwarded while the
  accessible label fallback remains. Extended `order-card-currency.test.ts` with a guard that `OrderCard` has no native
  `title=` and the directions affordance exposes `aria-label`, `role`, and `aria-disabled`.
- **Verified by Codex.** `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts`
  -> 36/36 pass; `npm run type-check` -> clean; `git diff --check -- src/renderer/components/ui/FloatingActionButton.tsx
  src/renderer/components/order/OrderCard.tsx tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts
  tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` -> clean; focused `rg` over
  `FloatingActionButton.tsx` and `OrderCard.tsx` shows no native `title=` or hover utilities remain.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED / delivery icon unavailable.** Running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800: rested over the
  visible yellow new-order FAB long enough for a native tooltip to appear; none appeared. The current order was pickup,
  so the delivery directions icon was not renderable live in this org/session; its no-tooltip behavior is covered by
  the static `OrderCard` guard. No order was created, opened, edited, delivered, or checked out.

### Supervisor round 176 — Order-row pickup icon now matches the chooser exactly (no strokeWidth override) — COMPLETED

- **Live problem.** The compact order-row pickup icon still read like the old storefront mark / did not match the
  order-type chooser bag.
- **Root cause (correcting round 169).** `order/OrderCard.tsx` already rendered the shared `PickupOrderIcon`, but its
  pickup branch overrode `strokeWidth={1.6}` (round 169, to balance with the sibling table icon). The
  OrderDashboard/OrderFlow chooser renders `PickupOrderIcon` with **no** strokeWidth, so at the small row size the
  thinner 1.6 stroke made the bag read as a different glyph than the chooser.
- **Fix (UI-only, pickup branch only).** Removed the `strokeWidth={1.6}` override so the row icon inherits
  `PickupOrderIcon`'s default weight — rendered exactly as the chooser does. Kept the `w-6 h-6` row footprint and the
  pickup-green tint (`text-green-600` light / `text-green-400` dark). Now the row and chooser draw the identical bag;
  if the shared icon's default ever changes, both move together.
- **Preserved.** Table icon (`TableOrderIcon`, still `strokeWidth={1.6}`) and the delivery truck SVG unchanged;
  order-taking flow, data, and unrelated icons untouched. No hover/tooltip added.
- **Test updated.** `tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` now asserts the pickup branch renders
  `<PickupOrderIcon className={w-6 h-6 ... green} />` with the self-closing tag immediately after the className (i.e.
  **no** strokeWidth override), plus a negative assert that the green pickup icon has no `strokeWidth=`. The
  table-icon assertion (strokeWidth 1.6) stays as a guard that table/delivery logic is unchanged.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` → **4 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (supervisor, running POS, Greek/dark, 1280x800). Order **#00001** now shows the
  green shared bag on the row. Opened the **New Order** chooser and confirmed the pickup chooser card uses the same
  bag glyph/weight; closed the chooser without selecting an order type or mutating data.
