### Supervisor round 180 — SuppliersPage native tooltip cleanup — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Greek/dark POS QA).** The Suppliers **refresh** button exposed a native browser tooltip
  (`title="Ανανέωση"`). Static audit also flagged `title=` at the empty states and the disabled import-save guidance.
- **Fix (UI/accessibility only, `pages/SuppliersPage.tsx`).** Whole-file `title=` is now **0**:
  - **Refresh** icon button: removed native `title={t('common.refresh', 'Refresh')}`; its existing
    `aria-label={t('common.refresh', 'Refresh')}` stays as the accessible name.
  - **Disabled import-save** button: removed native `title={!importDraft ? t('suppliers.import.saveRequiresPreview',
    …) : undefined}`. Disabled behaviour is unchanged (`disabled={saving || !importDraft || importDraft.rows.some(...
    === 'error')}`), and its **visible label already flips to "Save after preview"** when no preview exists, so the
    precondition is communicated without a hover tooltip (no aria-describedby needed).
  - **Empty states:** the four flagged `title=` were **not** DOM tooltips — they are props on the local `EmptyState`
    component, which renders a **visible `<h3>` heading**. Removing them would delete visible copy, so the prop was
    **renamed `title` → `heading`** across the interface, destructure, the `<h3>`, and all 4 call sites. The visible
    heading text (same i18n keys/values) and layout are unchanged; this also removes the recurring `rg title=`
    false-positive that triggered this round.
- **Preserved.** No change to supplier/invoice/import/payment data logic, API calls, barcode scan, upload, or
  modal open/close behaviour; palette/layout unchanged; no hover utilities introduced.
- **Test.** `tests/renderer/suppliers-page-ui.test.ts`: added a focused guard asserting the page has **no** `title=`,
  the EmptyState renders a visible `<h3>{heading}</h3>` (prop renamed, copy preserved), the refresh + close controls
  keep their `aria-label` (refresh keeps `onClick={fetchData}`), and the import-save button has no `title=` while
  keeping its disabled logic + visible "Save after preview" label. Also refreshed three stale assertions left from
  earlier SuppliersPage refactors so the whole file passes: refresh `hover:*` → `active:bg-zinc-200/zinc-800` +
  `active:scale-95`; the removed `currencySymbol` span → the current Wallet-icon stat contract (`Wallet` import +
  owed stat `icon: Wallet`); and the yellow import button `hover:bg-yellow-400/10` → `active:bg-yellow-400/10`.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/suppliers-page-ui.test.ts` → **16 pass / 0 fail** (full file,
  incl. the focused no-native-title guard); `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check`
  over the touched files → clean; `rg "title=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `SuppliersPage.tsx` → **0**.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (supervisor, running POS, Greek/dark, Suppliers page). Clicked the refresh control
  (read-only) and rested the pointer on it: **before** HMR/reload, accessibility reported "Ανανέωση Description:
  Ανανέωση" (the native `title` description); **after** the refresh it reported only "button Ανανέωση" and **no
  native tooltip appeared**. No supplier/invoice/import/payment data was changed — refresh only reloaded data.

  the four icon-only row/address buttons had **only** `title`, so each `title` was converted to `aria-label` using
  the existing keys (`users.viewDetails`, `users.unban`/`users.ban`, `customer.actions.editAddress`,
  `customer.actions.deleteAddress`). Whole-file `title=` is now **0**.
- **Preserved.** No change to layout, colors, active/tap feedback, data logic, modal behaviour, or the
  ban/delete/save handlers (`handleViewUser`, `handleToggleBan`, `handleEditAddress`, `handleDeleteAddress`,
  `loadUsers`); existing dirty UI work untouched; no hover utilities. No locale keys added (all referenced keys
  already exist).
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/users-page-ui.test.ts`: added a focused guard asserting UsersPage has **no `\btitle=`**,
  refresh keeps its handler + `aria-label`, and the row view-details / ban-unban / address edit / address delete
  buttons keep their handlers and localized aria-labels. Also refreshed one stale hover assertion
  (`hover:bg-zinc-900`/`hover:bg-gray-200` → the current `active:bg-zinc-900`/`active:bg-gray-200`) to match the
  touch-first source.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/users-page-ui.test.ts` → **19 pass / 0 fail** (incl. the new

  clean; `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `UsersPage.tsx` → **0**.

### Supervisor round 185 — InventoryPage native tooltip + cyan-stat cleanup — COMPLETED

- **Live problem (Greek/dark POS QA).** Inventory page icon-only controls exposed native `title` descriptions in
  accessibility — refresh read "Ανανέωση Description: Ανανέωση", each item row read "Προβολή ιστορικού τιμών και
  κινήσεων Description: ...", each adjust-stock icon read "Προσαρμογή Αποθέματος Description: ...". Native `title`
  tooltips are hover-dependent and shouldn't exist in this touchscreen POS.
- **Fix (UI/accessibility only, `pages/InventoryPage.tsx`).** Removed all **4** native `title=` attributes (whole-file
  `title=` now **0**):
  - **Refresh** — removed `title`; kept its existing `aria-label={t('common.refresh', 'Refresh')}`.
  - **History row** (clickable `<tr>`) — `title` → `aria-label={t('inventory.history.open', 'View price and movement
    history')}`.
  - **Adjust-stock icon button** — `title` → `aria-label={adjustAction.message || t('inventory.adjustStock', 'Adjust
    Stock')}`.
  - **Adjust modal Save button** — removed `title={adjustAction.message || undefined}`; added a reason-aware
    `aria-label={adjustAction.message ? \`${t('common.save','Save')}: ${adjustAction.message}\` : t('common.save',
    'Save')}` while the visible **Save** text is unchanged.
- **Palette.** Replaced the **Total Value** stat card's cyan top border (`border-t-cyan-400`/`-500`) with the
  page's **amber** palette (`border-t-amber-400`/`-500`) — matching that card's existing amber `BarChart3` icon — and
  (follow-up) neutralized the **Total Items** stat card's leftover blue top stripe (`border-t-blue-400`/`-500`) to
  **zinc** (`border-t-zinc-400`/`-500`) to match its already-neutral `Boxes` icon. The semantic stock-status colours
  (red/amber/green) are **unchanged**.
- **Preserved exactly.** `fetchInventory`, `openHistoryModal(item)`, `event.stopPropagation()` + `openAdjustModal(item)`,
  `handleAdjustStock()`, all `disabled` gates (`adjustmentQty === 0 || adjustAction.disabled`), modal close/Escape,
  data loading/calculations. No hover utilities (the page was already `active:`-only; the stale test assertions that
  expected refresh `hover:` were refreshed to `active:`).
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/inventory-page-ui.test.ts`: added a focused guard (no `\btitle=`, no
  `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`; refresh / history row / adjust icon / Save button keep their handlers +
  localized aria-labels). The off-palette scan now covers the **blue/cyan/purple family**: explicit
  `border-t-cyan`/`text-cyan`/`bg-cyan`/`border-cyan` **and** `border-t-blue`/`text-blue`/`bg-blue`/`border-blue`
  checks, plus a broad utility-token scan rejecting any `(border-t-|border-|text-|bg-|ring-|from-|to-|via-)` +
  `blue|cyan|purple|violet|pink|sky|indigo` in the InventoryPage source (matched against the source, so test prose is
  not flagged). Refreshed two stale assertions to current source: refresh `hover:*` →
  `active:bg-zinc-200`/`active:bg-zinc-800`/`active:scale-95`, and the adjustment-modal panel class (loosened to
  assert the `text-white`/`text-gray-950` readable-text intent through the glass chrome).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/inventory-page-ui.test.ts` → **12 pass / 0 fail** (incl. the new
  no-title/cyan guard); `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files →
  clean; `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:|border-t-cyan|text-cyan|bg-cyan|border-cyan"` over
  `InventoryPage.tsx` → **0**.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, real Tauri POS after reload + login, Greek/dark Inventory page).
  Accessibility now reports **"button Ανανέωση"**, the row **"Προβολή ιστορικού τιμών και κινήσεων"**, and **"button
  Προσαρμογή Αποθέματος"** with **no native Description/title text**. The stock-adjust modal was opened read-only; Save
  reports as **disabled "Αποθήκευση"** with no native Description/title text, and was closed with **Escape**. Visual
  stat cards show **Total Items neutral/zinc** and **Total Value amber** — no blue/cyan stripe visible. No inventory
  quantity, notes, stock, row, filter, or settings data was changed.

  `onClick={() => onOpenSettings()}`, and the `Settings` icon are unchanged; whole-file `title=` is now **0**.

  native `title` tooltip + `hover:` text classes (accessibility showed a "Description" entry). Replaced
  `title={t('app.themeToggle.title', { theme })}` with the same value as **`aria-label`**, and removed the `hover:`
  classes in favour of touch feedback — `active:scale-95` + `active:text-white`/`active:text-black` (and
  `inline-flex items-center justify-center` to keep the glyph centred). The cycle behaviour
  (`auto → dark → light → auto`) and the visible **A / Sun / Moon** states are unchanged; whole-file `title=`/`hover:`
  now **0**.
- **Verified-already-correct (no source change, now test-guarded):**
  - **AnimatedBackground** — the yellow/black lava-lamp orb is intact (framer-motion `motion.div` blobs,
    `radial-gradient` orbs in the amber palette `#eab308`/`#facc15`/`#fde68a`/`#d97706`, `repeat: Infinity`, multiple
    "Lava lamp blob"s); `LoginPage` renders `<AnimatedBackground />`. Not simplified/removed.
  - **NavigationSidebar** — `getNeonClass` returns **neutral** for inactive (`text-white` dark / `text-black` light)
    and the per-module neon color + glow only when active (`isActive = currentView === module.id`). The green
    sync-health icon is outside nav and untouched.
  - **OrderTabsBar** — `tabTextClass` returns neutral for inactive (`text-white/70` dark / `text-gray-600` light); the
    active tab gets its distinct neon (green/orange `#f97316`/blue `#3b82f6`/red) + glow only when active.
  - **OrderDashboard order-type modal** — pickup card uses `PickupOrderIcon` and the table card uses the supplied
    `TableOrderIcon`, both at the same large `w-full h-full` footprint; no Utensils/fork/cake glyph inside the chooser
    cards (the `UtensilsCrossed` elsewhere in OrderDashboard is unrelated and out of the chooser region).
- **Tests added.**
  - **NEW** `tests/renderer/login-page-ui.test.ts`: LoginPage uses AnimatedBackground + the yellow orb markers remain;
    pre-login settings button has `aria-label` and **no** `title=`; and (follow-up) **ThemeToggle** has `aria-label`,
    **no** `title=`, **no** `hover:` (uses `active:scale-95`/`active:text-*`), with the cycle + A/Sun/Moon states intact.
  - `tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts`: inactive unlocked icons neutral (dark white / light black), active
    uses per-module neon; **explicitly documents** that black-in-dark would be inaccessible so dark inactive stays
    white (`doesNotMatch` on a dark `text-black` inactive branch).
  - `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: OrderTabsBar inactive neutral + active neon-only; order-type modal table
    card uses `TableOrderIcon` at the pickup `w-full h-full` footprint with no Utensils/fork/cake in the chooser region.
- **Preserved.** Source changes were limited to removing native tooltip/hover affordances from the **LoginPage
  settings button** and **ThemeToggle** (native `title` → `aria-label`, `hover:` → `active:` touch feedback); no
  order/dashboard workflow or data behaviour changed, and no locale keys added.

### Supervisor round 190 — Navigation sidebar + order tabs inactive-neutral restoration — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** In dark mode the navigation-rail icons were reading too bright/white, so inactive (non-current)
  modules looked nearly as prominent as the active route. The same over-bright treatment leaked into the order-tab
  selector: inactive tab labels/counters returned `text-white/70`, washing out the "only the active tab glows" intent.
- **Fix (UI/accessibility only).**
  - **`components/NavigationSidebar.tsx`** — `getNeonClass` inactive branch now returns a dark-safe neutral grey
    (`theme === 'dark' ? 'text-zinc-400' : 'text-black'`) instead of `text-white`, so every inactive unlocked icon is
    quiet; only the active/current route keeps its per-module neon color + glow. Because the helper is the single
    leverage point, this also neutralizes the Z-Report-when-closed icon, the dragged-module icon, and the module icons.
    The **Check In** button switched from a hardcoded `text-zinc-100/text-zinc-900` to `getNeonClass('yellow', false,
    resolvedTheme)` so it follows the same inactive-neutral rule (Z-Report already used `getNeonClass('yellow',
    !!isZReportOpen, …)` and Settings already used `getNeonClass('yellow', false, …)`). Removed the **Settings icon's**
    nested `text-zinc-100/text-zinc-900` override (`<Settings className="w-5 h-5" />`) so the wrapper's getNeonClass color
    controls it. **Logout stays its own red** (`border border-red-500/70 bg-transparent text-red-500`, red `LogOut`).
    No native `title=` tooltips, no `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` utilities introduced.
  - **`components/OrderTabsBar.tsx`** — `tabTextClass` inactive branch now returns `isDark ? 'text-zinc-400' :
    'text-gray-600'` (was `text-white/70` in dark), so inactive labels/counters are neutral grey in both themes. Active
    tabs keep their distinct neon identity + glow (orders green, delivered orange `#f97316`, tables blue `#3b82f6`,
    canceled red) via `TAB_ACTIVE_TEXT` / `TAB_GLOW`.
- **Preserved.** The login `AnimatedBackground` orb (restored earlier) is untouched and its `login-page-ui` guard still
  passes — not replaced by any subtler grid/wash background. No order/data/payment/workflow behaviour changed; this is
  purely the inactive-icon/tab color treatment.
- **Tests.** Updated the two round-186 restoration guards to the new neutral values:
  `tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` now asserts the inactive branch returns **`text-zinc-400`** in dark
  (comment-aware regex), **rejects** both `text-black` (invisible on the rail) and `text-white` (over-bright) for the
  inactive dark branch, and additionally guards that Check In + Settings use `getNeonClass('yellow', false, …)`, that
  the Settings icon has **no** nested `text-zinc-100/900` override, and that logout keeps its red outline.
  `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` now asserts the inactive tab branch returns **`text-zinc-400`** in dark and
  **does not** return `text-white/70`, with the active green/orange/blue/red palette intact.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` → **6 pass / 0 fail**;
  `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **37 pass / 0 fail**;
  `node --test tests/renderer/login-page-ui.test.ts` → **3 pass / 0 fail** (orb guard unchanged);
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean;
  `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `NavigationSidebar.tsx` + `OrderTabsBar.tsx` → **0**.
- **Live visual sign-off PASSED** (Codex/operator, real Tauri POS, Greek/dark 1280×800). Verified on the order dashboard
  that only the current route icon shows neon color/glow; inactive navigation icons, Check In, Z-when-closed, and
  Settings read as quiet neutral grey; logout remains red; order dashboard inactive tab labels/counters are grey while
  the active Orders tab is green; the restored login yellow/orb background renders after logout; logged back in with PIN
  111111; no order/payment/settings/data mutation was performed.

### Supervisor round 191 — ReservationsView top Create/Refresh native tooltip cleanup — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the Reservations page (sidebar → Κρατήσεις, Greek/dark 1280×800), the accessibility tree
  reported native **Description** text on the two top controls — `Δημιουργία Description: Δημιουργία Κράτησης` (Create)
  and `Ανανέωση Description: Ανανέωση` (Refresh). These came from DOM `title=` attributes, which are hover-dependent
  tooltips that violate the touchscreen/no-hover rule.
- **Fix (UI/accessibility only).** In `pages/verticals/restaurant/ReservationsView.tsx`:
  - **Create button** — converted the native `title={…}` to `aria-label={…}`, keeping the exact 3-way branching name
    the title used: service-disabled reason (`reservationsView.createServiceInAppointments`) → service booking
    (`reservationsView.createServiceTitle`) → generic create (`reservationsView.createTitle`). The
    `disabled={activeTab === 'services' && !hasServiceReservations}` gate, the `Plus` icon, the visible
    `reservationsView.create` label, the click handler (open service modal vs. open create modal + date prefill), and
    the yellow/`active:` styling are all unchanged.
  - **Refresh button** — converted `title={t('reservationsView.refresh', …)}` to `aria-label={…}`. The tab-aware
    refetch handler (`refetchAppointments()` on services, else `refetch()`), the spin-on-loading
    `RefreshCw` icon (`isActiveLoading ? 'animate-spin' : ''`), and the styling are unchanged.
  - Whole-file `title=` is now **0** — no other DOM tooltips remain in this view, and no hover utilities were
    introduced. No reservation creation/submission/data-fetch/filter/date/modal logic changed.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/reservations-view-i18n.test.ts`: **added** a focused guard asserting the view has **no
  `\btitle=`** and **no** `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`; that Create keeps its branching `aria-label` (same
  3-way ternary) plus its `disabled` logic and `Plus` icon/label; and that Refresh keeps its localized `aria-label`,
  tab-aware `refetchAppointments`/`refetch` handlers, and the spin-on-loading `RefreshCw` icon. **Updated** the
  round-186-era service-tab test (`…disabled tooltip no longer blocks them`) from asserting `title={…}` to
  `aria-label={…}` for the same branching expression, and corrected the stale "tooltip" wording in two comments to
  "accessible name".
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/reservations-view-i18n.test.ts` → **24 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean;
  `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `ReservationsView.tsx` → **0 matches**.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, real Tauri POS, Greek/dark 1280×800). On the Reservations page (sidebar
  Κρατήσεις), top Create now appears in the accessibility tree as button **Δημιουργία Κράτησης** and Refresh as button
  **Ανανέωση**, with no Description text on either top control. Visible labels/icons/layout remain intact. No
  Create/Refresh action was clicked and no reservation/data mutation was performed. Note: native date input internals
  still expose their browser-generated Day/Month/Year descriptions; those are outside this top-control title cleanup

- **Problem (founder screenshot / live QA).** The small pickup icon under the total on an OrderDashboard order row still
  read like a storefront/badge rather than the plain bag the order-type chooser shows. Traced to
  `components/order/OrderCard.tsx` (NOT `pages/OrdersPage.tsx`): the `OrderTypeIcon` pickup/takeaway/default branch
  wrapped the shared `PickupOrderIcon` in a small rounded **green chip** (`rounded-lg bg-green-600` with a white
  `w-4 h-4` bag) — a round-187 treatment that made the row icon read as a distinct coloured badge instead of the same
  bag glyph used by every chooser/modal call site.
- **Fix (UI only).** In `components/order/OrderCard.tsx`, removed the chip wrapper and now render the shared
  `PickupOrderIcon` **directly** at the same 24px (`w-6 h-6`) footprint as the OrderDashboard/OrderFlow order-type
  chooser bag and the sibling table icon, with theme-aware grey
  (`resolvedTheme === 'light' ? 'text-gray-700' : 'text-gray-300'`) so it stays clear in both light and dark themes. No
  `Store`/`Package`/storefront glyph and no separate direct `ShoppingBag` render — pickup still flows through the shared
  `PickupOrderIcon` wrapper (default `strokeWidth` 2, matching the chooser). **Delivery** (orange truck SVG,
  `stroke="#d97706"`) and **dine-in/table** (`TableOrderIcon`, `strokeWidth={1.6}`) icons are unchanged. Row layout,
  tap behaviour, and the items-count `<Package>` indicator elsewhere in the row are untouched; no hover utilities or
  native `title=` introduced. Updated the now-stale chip comment to describe the plain-bag rendering.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: **added** a focused OrderCard guard (new `orderCardSource`
  reader) that scopes to the `OrderTypeIcon` body and asserts pickup renders `PickupOrderIcon` directly at `w-6 h-6`
  with the theme-aware grey, that there is **no** `rounded-lg bg-green-600`/`bg-green-600`/`text-white` chip, **no**
  `<Store`/`<Package`/`<Storefront` and **no** direct `ShoppingBag`, and that delivery (`stroke="#d97706"`) + table
  (`TableOrderIcon` `strokeWidth={1.6}`) remain covered, with no `title=`/`hover:` in the icon path. **Updated** the
  round-187 green-chip guard in `tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` (same OrderCard source) to the new
  plain-bag contract so it no longer asserts the removed chip — it now requires the direct render and rejects the chip.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **38 pass / 0 fail**;
  `node --test tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` → **4 pass / 0 fail** (updated guard);
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean;
  `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `OrderCard.tsx` → **0 matches**.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, real Tauri POS, Greek/dark 1280×800). On the dashboard order list (sidebar
  Πίνακας), the pickup order row now shows the plain bag glyph under the total, matching the order-type chooser glyph
  and with no green chip/badge/storefront. The row still shows #00001 / Παραλαβή / 18,50 €, the order tabs remain in
  place, and no order/data mutation was performed. Standalone Orders page also still shows the shared plain bag beside
  Παραλαβή.

  order-type chooser already uses).

  `../../../components/icons/TableOrderIcon` and replaced both `UtensilsCrossed` renders with it: the tab-loop icon
  (`const Icon = tab === 'rooms' ? BedDouble : tab === 'services' ? Scissors : TableOrderIcon`) and the single-module
  tables header (`<TableOrderIcon className={`w-6 h-6 shrink-0 ${isDark ? 'text-yellow-400' : 'text-yellow-600'}`} />`).
  `TableOrderIcon` is `className`/`currentColor`-driven, so the existing `w-5 h-5`/`w-6 h-6` sizing and yellow accents
  carry over unchanged. Removed the now-unused `UtensilsCrossed` from the `lucide-react` import. **Rooms** stays
  `BedDouble` and **Services** stays `Scissors` (both tab + header). Tab labels, counts, active/inactive styling, tap
  behaviour, modules logic, reservation data flow, and create/refresh behaviour are all untouched; no hover utilities or

  imports `TableOrderIcon` from the canonical path, uses it for the tables tab fall-through (`… : TableOrderIcon`) and
  the single-module tables header, that `UtensilsCrossed` is **fully gone** from the view (no leftover import/render),
  and that `BedDouble`/`Scissors` remain (both as imports and in the header renders), with no `title=`/`hover:`

### Supervisor round 194 — Settings "This register" overview redesign (operator-friendly admin section) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** In the real Tauri POS the Settings modal is glassy and functional, but the admin/"This register"
  section read too dense/vibecoded: register ID, dashboard URL, allowed-action pills, active-area pills and sync status
  all had near-equal visual weight, so an operator couldn't tell at a glance what mattered. Goal: make it understandable
  by a 10-year-old while preserving every behaviour and piece of information.
- **Fix (UI only, admin section of `components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** Redesigned only the
  `activeSettingsSection === 'admin'` body into a plain-language operator overview (marked
  `data-settings-register-overview`):
  1. **Status card** (`data-register-status-card`) — a plain-language title/help
     (`settings.deviceSetup.overview.statusTitle` / `.statusHelp`) plus the existing sync-health tone dot (`syncToneClass`)
     and the `syncStatus`/`syncHealthLabel` line. **The primary Sync settings action
     (`data-register-sync-action`, calling `handleManualPolicySync`) lives here** as a compact button, so the main
     action is visible in the first viewport (see live-QA correction below).
  2. **Three large summary tiles** (`data-register-summary-tile` ×3) — **Register type**
     (`overview.registerType`, reusing `managedTerminalSummary`), **Sync** (`overview.syncState` with the tone dot +
     `syncHealthLabel`), and **PIN** (`overview.pinStatus` with a green/yellow dot, the short
     `overview.pinResetShort`/`pinOkShort` status, and the full existing `pinResetRequired`/`pinResetClear` sentence
     underneath).
  3. **Quiet technical details** (`data-register-technical-details`) — register ID + dashboard address moved below the
     summary on a recessed surface in muted text (`break-all text-xs liquid-glass-modal-text-muted`), still visible/
     readable with the same `runtimeTerminalId || terminalId || …` and `runtimeAdminUrl || adminDashboardUrl || …`
     fallbacks. Laid out as a compact 2-column grid (`sm:grid-cols-2`) to keep the section short.
  4. **Calmer Allowed actions / Active areas** (`data-register-allowed-actions` / `data-register-active-areas`) — each
     now shows a small **count badge** plus the individual localized labels rendered inline
     (`enabledFeatureLabels.join(' · ')` / `enabledModuleNames.join(' · ')`) instead of a wall of equal-weight pills.
     `enabledModuleNames` is still built via `resolveNavigationLabel(t, module.module.id, module.module.name)`, so the
     localized labels and information are preserved.
  - Preserved behaviour/data: `handleManualPolicySync` (Sync settings button), all terminal/runtime/admin-URL data,
    `enabledFeatureLabels`, `enabledModuleNames`, `pinResetRequired`, sync health, and every other settings section are
    untouched. Existing glass language kept (`rounded-2xl`, subtle borders, light/dark-aware, base white/grey/yellow with
    semantic green/red/yellow status dots). No `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` and no native `title=` in the admin
    section (the 3 whole-file `title=` matches are pre-existing **`ConfirmDialog` component props** = visible headings in
    the database section, not DOM tooltips, and untouched).
  - i18n: added `settings.deviceSetup.overview.{statusTitle,statusHelp,registerType,syncState,pinStatus,pinResetShort,
    pinOkShort,technicalDetails}` (8 keys) with real translations in **all five** locales (en/el/de/fr/it) — no
    Greek-only hardcoding.
- **Live-QA correction (round 194 follow-up, Codex/operator, 1280×800 Greek/dark).** First pass looked cleaner but the
  primary "Συγχρονισμός ρυθμίσεων" / Sync settings action was pushed **below the visible panel** (it sat in a
  bottom-centered row), so on a touchscreen overview the main action required guessing there was hidden scroll. **Fix:**
  moved the Sync button **into the top status card** as a compact primary action (`data-register-sync-action`,
  right-aligned, wraps under on narrow widths) and **removed the old bottom-centered duplicate**; also compacted the
  technical details into a 2-column grid so the section is not taller. Same `handleManualPolicySync` behaviour, exactly
  one Sync button now.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`: **added** two focused guards — (a) the admin section carries
  `data-settings-register-overview`, has the status card + exactly 3 summary tiles + the quiet (muted) technical-details
  area still rendering register id/dashboard address, the calm count+joined-labels allowed-actions/active-areas (and the
  old `enabledFeatureLabels.map`/`enabledModuleNames.map` pill walls are gone), `enabledModuleNames` is still built via
  `resolveNavigationLabel`, and there is no `title=`/`hover:` in the section; **the Sync action
  (`data-register-sync-action`) is asserted to live inside the top status card, before the technical details, with
  exactly one Sync button (no bottom duplicate, `flex justify-center` gone from the section)**;
  (b) the 8 new `overview.*` keys exist non-empty in en/el/de/fr/it and Greek differs from English for the prose keys.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **14 pass / 0 fail** (12 prior + 2 new);
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` → only the 3 pre-existing
  `ConfirmDialog` `title` **props** (visible headings), **0** in the admin section / **0** hover; `git diff --check`
  over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, real Tauri POS, Greek/dark 1280×800). Opened Settings → Αυτό το ταμείο. The
  redesigned admin overview shows the clear status card at the top, the primary "Συγχρονισμός ρυθμίσεων" button is
  visible in the first viewport inside the status card (no hidden scroll needed to reach the main action), the three
  summary tiles render cleanly (register type / sync / PIN), allowed-actions and active-areas are calmer count+text
  summaries, and technical details are secondary/muted. No settings/data mutation was performed; Sync was not clicked.

### Supervisor round 195 — Settings → Connection action bar hierarchy fix (deliberate Save slot) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the real Tauri POS (Greek/dark, 1280×800), Settings → Σύνδεση was functional but the action
  layout was sloppy: the secondary actions (Paste connection code, Test, Sync settings) filled a single flat
  `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2 pt-1` row and the green **Save** button wrapped onto its own left-aligned line
  beneath them, so the primary action read like leftover wrapped content instead of a deliberately placed button.
- **Fix (UI only, Connection section of `components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** Restructured only the
  connection action area into a marked action bar (`data-connection-action-bar`,
  `flex flex-col gap-2 pt-2 sm:flex-row sm:items-center sm:justify-between`):
  - **Secondary cluster** (`data-connection-secondary-actions`, `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2`) groups **Paste**
    (keeps its `pasteBothTooltip` aria-label + `Clipboard` icon), **Test** (still gated behind `allowManualCredentials`)
    and **Sync settings** as neutral `liquidGlassModalButton('secondary', 'md')` glass buttons.
  - **Primary Save slot** (`data-connection-primary-action`, `sm:ml-auto`) holds the green Save button on its own,
    end-aligned on desktop and full-width on narrow (`SAVE_BTN_MD + ' w-full sm:w-auto'`), so it reads as the deliberate
    primary action and never wraps under the secondary row.
  - Behaviour/handlers preserved exactly: `handlePasteBoth`, `handleTest`, `handleManualPolicySync`,
    `handleSaveConnection`, the show/hide API key control, the inputs, and the `allowManualCredentials` gating are all
    unchanged. Save stays green (`SAVE_BTN_MD`); secondary actions stay neutral glass. No new copy or locale keys (reused
    existing labels). Glass/rounded/touch language kept; no `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` and no native `title=`
    in the connection section.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 195 guard that slices the connection section
  (`settings-section-connection` → `settings-section-security`) and asserts the `data-connection-action-bar` marker +
  its `sm:flex-row … justify-between` layout, the `data-connection-secondary-actions` cluster (Paste/Test-when-allowed/
  Sync, neutral glass, **no** Save/`SAVE_BTN_MD` inside it), the `data-connection-primary-action` Save slot
  (`sm:ml-auto`, `SAVE_BTN_MD + ' w-full sm:w-auto'`, `handleSaveConnection`, save label), that the old
  `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2 pt-1` flat row is **gone**, and that there is no `title=`/`hover:` in the section.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **15 pass / 0 fail** (14 prior + 1 new);
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` → only the 3 pre-existing
  `ConfirmDialog` `title` **props** (visible headings, database section), **0** in the connection section / **0** hover;
  `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, real Tauri POS, Greek/dark 1280×800). Opened Settings → Σύνδεση. The secondary
  actions (Paste connection code / Test / Sync settings) are grouped on the left/start, and the green Αποθήκευση Save
  button is visually separated on the right in its own primary slot. Save no longer looks like a left-wrapped fourth
  secondary action. No settings/data mutation was performed; Save, Test, Paste, and Sync were not clicked.

### Supervisor round 196 — Settings → Screen & Sound terminal controls accessible labels — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the real Tauri POS (Greek/dark, 1280×800), Settings → Οθόνη & Ήχος looked fine but the
  accessibility tree exposed the terminal preference controls as **unnamed** generic controls (spin button, combo box,
  slider, checkbox, checkbox): visible labels existed but were not programmatically bound to the actual controls.
- **Fix (semantic/accessibility only, terminal section of `components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** Gave the
  five controls stable accessible names without changing behaviour, layout, colours, or copy:
  - **Screen-timeout number input** — visible `<label>` now `htmlFor="terminal-screen-timeout"` bound to the input's new
    matching `id` (label text stays `settings.terminal.screenTimeout`).
  - **Touch-sensitivity `<select>`** — `<label htmlFor="terminal-touch-sensitivity">` bound to the select's `id`
    (`settings.terminal.touchSensitivity`).
  - **Display-brightness range input** — `<label htmlFor="terminal-display-brightness">` bound to the range's `id`
    (`settings.terminal.displayBrightness`).
  - **Audio switch checkbox** — the `sr-only` input's visible title sits in a separate `<div>`, so the title `<div>` got
    `id="terminal-audio-label"` and the checkbox `aria-labelledby="terminal-audio-label"` (reuses the visible
    `settings.terminal.audioEnabled` text as the name).
  - **Receipt auto-print switch checkbox** — same pattern: title `<div id="terminal-receipt-autoprint-label">` +
    checkbox `aria-labelledby="terminal-receipt-autoprint-label"` (`settings.terminal.receiptAutoPrint`).
  - No new locale keys (reused the existing `settings.terminal.*` calls). All state setters
    (`setScreenTimeoutMinutes`/`setTouchSensitivity`/`setDisplayBrightness`/`setAudioEnabled`/`setReceiptAutoPrint`) and
    the save handler are unchanged. Glass style/layout untouched; no native `title=` and no
    `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` introduced.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 196 guard that slices the terminal section
  (`settings-section-terminal` → `settings-section-database`) and asserts all five controls are labeled — the
  number/select/range via `htmlFor` + matching `id`, the two switch checkboxes via `aria-labelledby` to their visible
  title `<div>` ids — that the existing state setters remain wired, and that there is no `title=`/`hover:` in the
  section.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **16 pass / 0 fail** (15 prior + 1 new);
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` → only the 3 pre-existing
  `ConfirmDialog` `title` **props** (visible headings, database section), **0** in the terminal section / **0** hover;
  `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, real Tauri POS, Greek/light app with dark/glass Settings modal, 1280×800).
  Settings → Οθόνη & Ήχος now exposes all five terminal controls with proper accessible names in the accessibility tree:
  - spin button **Χρονικό όριο οθόνης (λεπτά)** — ID `terminal-screen-timeout`
  - combo box **Ευαισθησία αφής** — ID `terminal-touch-sensitivity`
  - slider **Φωτεινότητα οθόνης** — ID `terminal-display-brightness`
  - checkbox **Ήχος ενεργός**
  - checkbox **Αυτόματη εκτύπωση αποδείξεων**

  Visual layout/glass styling unchanged. No settings/data mutation was performed; Save and controls were not
  changed/clicked.

### Supervisor round 197 — Settings → PIN & Lock session-timeout controls accessible labels — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the real Tauri POS (Greek/dark Settings modal, 1280×800), Settings → PIN & Κλείδωμα had the
  same hidden defect as round 196: the **session-timeout switch** was exposed as an unnamed checkbox and the **timeout
  minutes input** as an unnamed spin button (disabled when timeout is off). Visible labels existed but were not
  programmatically bound to the controls.
- **Fix (semantic/accessibility only, session-timeout card of `components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** The
  `activeSettingsSection === 'security'` block renders two cards (PIN + session-timeout); only the session-timeout card
  (headed by `t('modals.connectionSettings.security')`) was touched and now carries a `data-session-timeout-card`
  marker. Both controls' visible titles are sibling `<span>`s (not wrapping `<label>`s), so each is bound via
  `aria-labelledby`:
  - **Session-timeout switch checkbox** — the visible title `<span>` got `id="session-timeout-label"` and the `sr-only`
    checkbox `aria-labelledby="session-timeout-label"` (reuses the `modals.connectionSettings.sessionTimeout` text).
  - **Timeout-duration number input** — the visible title `<span>` got `id="session-timeout-duration-label"` and the
    input `aria-labelledby="session-timeout-duration-label"` (reuses `modals.connectionSettings.timeoutDuration`); the
    name applies even while the input is `disabled={!sessionTimeoutEnabled}`.
  - No new locale keys (reused existing `modals.connectionSettings.*` calls). The `disabled` behaviour, the
    `handleToggleSessionTimeout` toggle, `setSessionTimeoutMinutes` + `onBlur={handleSaveSessionTimeout}`, the quick
    presets, glass layout, and colours are all unchanged. No native `title=` and no `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:`
    introduced.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 197 guard that slices the session-timeout
  card via its `data-session-timeout-card` marker (so it targets that card, not the PIN or terminal cards) and asserts
  the switch checkbox + minutes input are bound by `aria-labelledby` to their visible-title `<span>` ids, the
  disabled-when-off behaviour and the existing handlers are preserved, and there is no `title=`/`hover:` in the card.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **17 pass / 0 fail** (16 prior + 1 new);
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` → only the 3 pre-existing
  `ConfirmDialog` `title` **props** (visible headings, database section), **0** in the session-timeout card / **0**
  hover; `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.

  Settings → PIN & Κλείδωμα, navigated away and back to force the section re-render, and the accessibility tree now names
  both controls: checkbox **Χρονικό Όριο Συνεδρίας** and disabled spin button **Διάρκεια Χρονικού Ορίου**. Visual
  layout/glass styling and disabled-when-off behaviour were unchanged. No settings/data mutation was performed; the
  timeout switch and minutes field were not changed/clicked.

### Supervisor round 198 — Settings → Data recovery-point selection accessible state — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the real Tauri POS (Greek/dark Settings modal, 1280×800), Settings → Δεδομένα → Local
  Recovery shows recovery snapshot cards as a single-select list driving the right-side detail panel. The selected card
  has the yellow border/background **visually**, but the accessibility tree exposed each snapshot only as a plain button
  (e.g. "button Προγραμματισμένο …") with **no selected/pressed/current state**, so operators using assistive tech could
  not tell which recovery point was selected.
- **Fix (semantic/accessibility only, `components/recovery/RecoveryPanel.tsx`).** Added `aria-pressed={isSelected}` to
  each recovery-point `<button>` so the existing single-select state is announced to assistive tech. `isSelected` is the
  same `point.id === selectedPointId` flag that already drives the yellow selected styling, so the programmatic pressed
  state and the visual state can't diverge. Chose `aria-pressed` over a `role="listbox"`/`option` pattern deliberately:
  it keeps the elements as plain buttons (their current Tab/Enter behaviour is already correct) and avoids implying an
  arrow-key navigation contract the list doesn't implement. Also added a `data-recovery-point-list` marker on the list
  container (test hook only, no visible copy). `onClick={() => setSelectedPointId(point.id)}`, the selected styling, the
  list/detail layout, and all export/restore/create/open handlers are unchanged; no native `title=` and no
  `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` introduced.
- **Tests.** **New** `tests/renderer/recovery-panel-ui.test.ts`: asserts each recovery-point button keeps its
  `setSelectedPointId(point.id)` handler and now carries `aria-pressed={isSelected}` (on the same button, before the
  className), that `isSelected` is still derived from `selectedPointId`, that the yellow selected styling is preserved,
  that the `data-recovery-point-list` marker exists, and that RecoveryPanel introduces no `title=`/hover utilities.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/recovery-panel-ui.test.ts` → **2 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `rg "\btitle=|hover:|dark:hover:|group-hover:"` over `RecoveryPanel.tsx` → **0**;
  `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED:** In the running POS Settings → Data → Local recovery panel (Greek/dark glass modal), the selected
  recovery point kept the yellow selected styling and each recovery point is exposed in the Windows accessibility tree as
  a toggle button, verifying the `aria-pressed` selected-state mapping. No recovery/export/restore/create actions were
  clicked; only navigation/read-only inspection.

### Supervisor round 199 — Shared glass modals isolate background accessibility — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** With Settings open (ConnectionSettingsModal via `LiquidGlassModal`), the Windows accessibility
  tree still exposed the background POS app controls before/around the modal — sidebar buttons, order tabs, the order
  row, expenses / New Order. That breaks modal containment and makes Settings harder to understand for assistive-tech
  users (the dialog should be the only reachable surface while open).
- **Fix (shared, UI-semantics only, `components/ui/pos-glass-components.tsx`).** Added a shared, ref-counted background
  isolation used by **both** `POSGlassModal` and `LiquidGlassModal`:
  - Both modal viewport roots now carry a stable `data-liquid-glass-modal-viewport` attribute.
  - A module-level manager (`acquire`/`release` + `applyBackgroundIsolation`/`restoreBackgroundIsolation`) hides every
    `document.body` child that is **not** a modal viewport root from assistive tech (`aria-hidden="true"`) and makes it
    unfocusable (`inert`, feature-detected via `'inert' in HTMLElement.prototype` so older webviews still get
    `aria-hidden`). Modal viewport roots are skipped, so sibling/nested dialogs stay reachable.
  - Multiple/nested modals are safe: a `backgroundIsolationCount` ref count plus a `Map<HTMLElement,
    SavedBackgroundA11yState>` capture each touched element's **original** `aria-hidden`/`inert` exactly once and only
    restore it when the **last** glass modal releases — so closing a nested dialog never un-hides the app while another
    glass modal is open, and a modal never restores a state another modal imposed.
  - Wired via a `useBackgroundAccessibilityIsolation(active)` hook: `POSGlassModal` passes `isOpen`, `LiquidGlassModal`
    passes `mounted && !isServerRender` (stays isolated through the closing animation). The effect acquires on
    activation and releases on cleanup.
  - Preserved exactly: `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, the focus trap + `isTopMostDialog` Escape/topmost handling,
    `ReactDOM.createPortal` portal behaviour, enter/exit animations, body scroll lock, and `useBlockerRegistration`. No
    visual styling/layout/copy/colour or business behaviour changed; no native `title=` and no
    `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` introduced.
- **Tests.** **New** `tests/renderer/liquid-glass-modal-a11y.test.ts` (source-level, like the other renderer tests)
  proves: (1) both viewport roots carry `data-liquid-glass-modal-viewport` and both components call the hook; (2) the
  shared code scans `document.body.children` and sets `aria-hidden`+`inert` on non-modal children; (3) it skips modal
  viewport roots before hiding anything; (4) the ref count + saved-state map restore previous `aria-hidden`/`inert` only
  when the last modal releases. A fifth test guards that dialog semantics, portal behaviour, focus trap, and the
  no-hover rule are preserved.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/liquid-glass-modal-a11y.test.ts` → **5 pass / 0 fail**;
  `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **17 pass / 0 fail** (no regression);
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, real Tauri POS, Greek/dark glass modal, 1280×800): With Settings open, the Windows
  accessibility tree now starts at the Settings dialog and no longer exposes the background sidebar, order tabs, order
  row, Expenses, or New Order. Opening the nested Printer Settings modal kept both modal dialogs reachable while the POS
  background stayed hidden. Closing the nested modal left Settings isolated; closing Settings restored the background app
  controls to the tree. Visual styling, focus, Escape/close behavior, and modal blur were unchanged. No
  printer/recovery/settings action buttons were executed; only read-only navigation and modal open/close were used.

### Supervisor round 200 — Order type chooser card copy and accessible names — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** In the running POS order-type chooser modal (OrderDashboard → `LiquidGlassModal`), the delivery
  and pickup cards looked good but in **Greek** their subtitles repeated the title (Παράδοση / Παράδοση, Παραλαβή /
  Παραλαβή), and the Windows accessibility tree exposed duplicate button names — "button Παράδοση Παράδοση", "button
  Παραλαβή Παραλαβή". Root cause: `modals.orderTypeSelection.pickupDescription`/`deliveryDescription` in `el.json` were
  set to the bare titles, and the chooser buttons had no explicit `aria-label`, so the browser auto-built the accessible
  name by concatenating the `<h3>` title + `<p>` description (→ "title title").
- **Fix (UI/i18n/accessibility only, `components/OrderDashboard.tsx` + `el.json`).**
  - Added a pure module-level `composeOrderTypeAriaLabel(title, description)` that returns just the title when title and
    description are equal (case-insensitive) and otherwise `"{title}. {description}"`, so the accessible name can never
    repeat itself even if a future locale re-introduces equal strings.
  - The chooser's three cards now localize their title + description **once** into consts (via an IIFE in the modal
    body) and reuse them for both the visible text and an explicit `aria-label={composeOrderTypeAriaLabel(...)}`. Added
    `type="button"` to all three. Removed the leftover hover-only `group` class (no `group-hover` remained); kept the
    glass cards, neon accents (delivery yellow / pickup green / table blue), the shared pickup bag icon, the table/chair
    icon, `active:scale-95` tap feedback, and subtle transitions. No `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` and no native
    `title=`.
  - i18n: only `el.json` was duplicative — set `modals.orderTypeSelection.pickupDescription` →
    "Ο πελάτης παραλαμβάνει από το κατάστημα" and `deliveryDescription` → "Στη διεύθυνση του πελάτη" (the actually-used
    keys; the similar `modals.orderType.*` block was left alone to avoid a conflicting second key). `orderFlow.tableDescription`
    in Greek was already "Σερβίρισμα στο τραπέζι". en/de/fr/it already had distinct, kid-clear descriptions for all three
    cards, so they were left unchanged. All five locales verified non-empty and distinct from their titles. JSON valid.
- **Tests.** Extended `tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` (reusing its `LOCALES`/`loadLocale`/`get` helpers):
  (a) all five locales have non-empty pickup/delivery/table descriptions that differ from their titles; (b) Greek
  pickup/delivery descriptions differ from `orderFlow.pickupOrder`/`deliveryOrder` and are real Greek; (c) OrderDashboard
  has the `composeOrderTypeAriaLabel` helper, all three chooser buttons are `type="button"` with explicit
  `aria-label={composeOrderTypeAriaLabel(...)}`, the leftover `group` class is gone, and the neon accents + `active:scale`
  feedback remain.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` → **10 pass / 0 fail** (7 prior + 3 new);
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek). Reopened the POS in Greek after logging in with test PIN
  111111; opened the order-type chooser from the dashboard FAB; did not select any order type or create an order.
  - **Visual check passed:** Delivery card subtitle is "Στη διεύθυνση του πελάτη", Pickup subtitle is
    "Ο πελάτης παραλαμβάνει από το κατάστημα", Table remains "Σερβίρισμα στο τραπέζι". No duplicated title/subtitle text;
    visuals unchanged (glass modal, delivery yellow, pickup green, table blue, bag/table icons).
  - **Windows accessibility tree passed:** buttons read as "Παράδοση. Στη διεύθυνση του πελάτη",
    "Παραλαβή. Ο πελάτης παραλαμβάνει από το κατάστημα", and "Τραπέζι. Σερβίρισμα στο τραπέζι"; no duplicate
    "Παράδοση Παράδοση" or "Παραλαβή Παραλαβή" names; background remained hidden behind the modal.
  - **Codex independently reran:** `node --test tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` → **10 pass / 0 fail**;
    `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` over the touched round-200 files → **clean**.

### Supervisor round 201 — Dashboard shell selected-state semantics — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the running POS dashboard after login, the sidebar navigation and order-status tabs have
  readable labels but the accessibility tree exposes them as **plain buttons only** — it does not indicate which sidebar
  route is the current page or which order tab is selected. A dashboard-shell accessibility bug; colours/neon/touch
  styling must stay exactly as-is.
- **Fix (accessibility-only, no visual/behaviour change).**
  - **`components/NavigationSidebar.tsx`** — the module nav button now exposes `aria-current={isActive ? 'page' :
    undefined}` (built from the existing `isActive = currentView === module.id` flag), so the current route announces as
    the current page and inactive modules get **no** `aria-current`. Colours, icons, drag behaviour, and
    locked/coming-soon gating are unchanged; no native `title=` added.
  - **`components/OrderTabsBar.tsx`** — the strip wrapper is now `role="tablist"` with a localized
    `aria-label={t('dashboard.tabs.tablistLabel', 'Order status tabs')}` (English fallback via `t`), and each tab button
    is `type="button"` + `role="tab"` + `aria-selected={activeTab === tab.id}`. Labels, counters, neon active palette,
    and `active:scale-95` are untouched; no hover classes, no native `title=`.
- **Tests.**
  - `tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts`: **added** a guard asserting the module button sets
    `aria-current={isActive ? 'page' : undefined}` from the `currentView === module.id` flag, that it is never the
    hardcoded always-on `aria-current="page"`, and that no native `title=` was introduced.
  - `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: **added** a guard asserting the OrderTabsBar wrapper is `role="tablist"`
    (with the localized aria-label), each tab is `type="button"` + `role="tab"` with `aria-selected={activeTab ===
    tab.id}` (derived, not hardcoded), and the neon active palette + `active:scale` are preserved with no `title=`/hover.
- **Live QA correction (first pass, Codex/operator).** With the ARIA roles in place the strip read as a tab container
  with tab items, but the **Windows accessibility tree still did not expose selected/current state** — it showed
  "button Πίνακας", "tab item (selectable) Παραγγελίες 1", etc. with no selected/current marker (Windows UIA does not
  reliably surface `aria-current`/`aria-selected` as a state for these controls). **Correction:** keep the ARIA
  attributes but also bake the state into the accessible **name**:
  - **NavigationSidebar** — the active module's `aria-label` now uses `t('navigation.currentPage', { label: moduleLabel,
    defaultValue: '{{label}} — Current page' })` (applied only in the `isActive` branch); inactive modules keep the
    plain `moduleLabel`. `aria-current` is retained.
  - **OrderTabsBar** — each tab now has an `aria-label` derived from `activeTab === tab.id`: selected →
    `t('dashboard.tabs.selectedTab', '{{label}} {{value}} — Selected')`, inactive →
    `t('dashboard.tabs.tab', '{{label}} {{value}}')`. `role`/`aria-selected` are retained; the visible label/counter are
    unchanged.
  - i18n: added `navigation.currentPage`, `dashboard.tabs.tab`, `dashboard.tabs.selectedTab`, and
    `dashboard.tabs.tablistLabel` to **all five** locales (en/el/de/fr/it). No visual or behaviour change.
  - Tests extended: the nav guard now asserts the `isActive`-only `navigation.currentPage` accessible name (and the key
    interpolates `{{label}}` in every locale); the OrderTabsBar guard asserts the per-tab `aria-label` branches on
    `activeTab === tab.id` using the selectedTab/tab keys (derived, not hardcoded) and that both keys exist with the
    `{{label}}`/`{{value}}` placeholders, with `selectedTab` differing from the plain `tab` label, in every locale.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`
  → **48 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files
  → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS dashboard). The Windows accessibility tree now reads the active sidebar
  route as "Πίνακας — Τρέχουσα σελίδα" while inactive routes remain plain labels, and the selected order tab as
  "Παραγγελίες 1 — Επιλεγμένο" while the other tabs stay plain ("Παραδόθηκαν 0", "Τραπέζια 0", "Ακυρωμένες 0"). The visual
  dashboard shell is unchanged: inactive nav icons neutral, active icon neon, order tabs keep the intended active-neon /
  neutral-inactive state. Codex independently reran `node --test tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts
  tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **48 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check`
  over the touched Round 201 files → **clean**.

### Supervisor round 202 — TableActionModal background accessibility isolation — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the running POS (Dashboard → Tables tab → table #TB03 card), the TableActionModal looks
  correct (frosted glass shell, yellow New Order primary, neutral New Reservation, no data action taken), but the
  Windows accessibility tree still exposed the **background app** before/around the dialog — sidebar buttons, order tabs,
  table filters/cards, Expenses, New Order all appeared before "dialogue Ενέργειες τραπεζιού". TableActionModal already
  had `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` but did not participate in the shared background isolation verified in
  round 199 for `LiquidGlassModal`/`POSGlassModal`.
- **Fix (accessibility-only, conservative, no visual/behaviour change).**
  - Exported the round-199 shared isolation from `components/ui/pos-glass-components.tsx` — `const` →
    `export const useBackgroundAccessibilityIsolation` and `export const MODAL_VIEWPORT_ATTR` — so consumers reuse the
    ref-counted manager instead of duplicating it. (Both round-199 viewport divs and tests are unaffected; the existing
    `const …` regex guards still match the `export const …` form.)
  - `components/tables/TableActionModal.tsx`: imported the shared hook + marker; marked its app-level portal root (the
    `fixed inset-0 z-[1200]` div) as a viewport root via `{...{ [MODAL_VIEWPORT_ATTR]: '' }}` so the isolation
    skip-check never hides the modal itself; and called `useBackgroundAccessibilityIsolation(isOpen)` (before the
    `if (!isOpen) return null` early return) so it acquires isolation while open and releases on close. The shared ref
    count means this composes with any already-open glass modal (nested) and only un-hides the app when the last modal
    closes.
  - Preserved exactly: all colours/layout/icons/text, the New Order / New Reservation / set-available / reservation
    action logic, the topmost-`[role="dialog"]` Escape gate, the app-level `createPortal(document.body)` + `z-[1200]`,
    and the no-document fallback. No hover utilities and no native `title=` introduced. `render-modal-portal.ts` was not
    touched (TableActionModal uses `createPortal` directly).
- **Tests.**
  - `tests/renderer/tables-action-modal-portal.test.ts`: **added** a guard that TableActionModal imports the shared
    `useBackgroundAccessibilityIsolation` + `MODAL_VIEWPORT_ATTR`, calls the hook with `isOpen`, marks its portal root
    with the shared marker, does **not** redefine the ref-count logic locally, and still exposes `role="dialog"` /
    `aria-modal` / the app-level portal / `z-[1200]` with no `title=`/hover.
  - `tests/renderer/liquid-glass-modal-a11y.test.ts`: **added** a guard that the hook + marker are now exported (the
    reuse contract), since the extraction changed their visibility (the code still lives in pos-glass-components).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/tables-action-modal-portal.test.ts
  tests/renderer/liquid-glass-modal-a11y.test.ts tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **56 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS). Dashboard → Tables tab → table #TB03 opens TableActionModal; with the
  modal open the Windows accessibility tree starts at "dialogue Ενέργειες τραπεζιού" and no longer exposes the sidebar
  buttons, order tabs, table grid/cards, Expenses, or New Order. The visible modal is unchanged (frosted glass shell,
  yellow New Order primary, neutral New Reservation, Escape closes); no New Order / New Reservation / set-available /
  reservation action was clicked and no data was changed. After Escape closes the modal, the background accessibility tree
  returns normally. Codex independently reran `node --test tests/renderer/tables-action-modal-portal.test.ts
  tests/renderer/liquid-glass-modal-a11y.test.ts tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **56 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` over the touched Round 202 files → **clean**.

### Supervisor round 203 — OrderCard pickup row icon green accent (match the chooser bag) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (founder screenshot).** On the OrderDashboard Orders tab, pickup order row #00001's small pickup icon under
  the total still read like a faint storefront. The round-192 neutral-grey 24px shared bag is technically correct but
  visually too weak; the founder wants the row pickup icon to unmistakably match the pickup icon in the order-type
  chooser.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/order/OrderCard.tsx` `OrderTypeIcon` pickup/default branch).** Kept the shared
  `PickupOrderIcon` rendered directly (no chip/badge, no `Store`/`Package`/storefront, no direct `ShoppingBag`), but gave
  it the **pickup-green accent** and the chooser's explicit stroke weight: `className={`w-6 h-6 ${resolvedTheme ===
  'light' ? 'text-green-600' : 'text-green-400'}`}` with `strokeWidth={2}`. The dark `text-green-400` matches the
  chooser card's green title; light uses `text-green-600` for contrast on the cream row. Footprint stays `w-6 h-6` (24px)
  so it remains aligned with the delivery truck and `TableOrderIcon` row icons and the payment icon beside it, and the
  row does not shift. **Delivery** (orange truck `#d97706`) and **dine-in/table** (`TableOrderIcon`, `strokeWidth={1.6}`)
  are unchanged, as are the payment icon, layout, order tabs/navbar neon colours, and the order-taking workflow. No
  `hover:`/`dark:hover:`/`group-hover:` and no native `title=` in the icon path.
- **Tests.** Updated the two OrderCard guards to the new visible contract:
  `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` and `tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` now assert the pickup branch
  renders `PickupOrderIcon` with `w-6 h-6` + the `text-green-600`/`text-green-400` accent + `strokeWidth={2}`, that it is
  no longer the old grey (`text-gray-700`/`text-gray-300`), and keep the existing negatives (no `rounded-lg bg-green-600`
  chip, no white `w-4 h-4` bag, no `<Store`/`<Package`/`<Storefront`/`<ShoppingBag`) and the unchanged delivery
  (`stroke="#d97706"`) + table (`strokeWidth={1.6}`) assertions, with no `title=`/hover in the icon path.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts`
  → **44 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files
  → clean.

  #00001 now shows the compact pickup order-type icon under 18,50 € as a green shopping-bag glyph, no grey/storefront look
  and no chip/badge. Opened the New Order order-type chooser only for comparison: the Pickup card uses the same
  shopping-bag silhouette with the green pickup identity; closed the chooser with Escape without selecting

### Supervisor round 204 — Tables view-mode toggle localized (List/2D) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On Dashboard → Tables tab (Greek/light), the table view toggle showed the hardcoded English
  "List" while every surrounding control was Greek ("Όλα", "Διαθέσιμο", "Όλοι οι όροφοι", …). The source already calls
  `t("tablesDashboard.viewMode.list", "List")` and `t("tablesDashboard.viewMode.floorPlan", "2D")` (in
  `OrderDashboard.tsx` and the standalone `components/tables/TablesDashboard.tsx`), but the real locale source —
  `src/locales/overlays/*.table-check.json` — defined `tablesDashboard` **without** a `viewMode` block, so the toggle
  fell through to the English default value.
- **Fix (i18n only).** Added `tablesDashboard.viewMode.list` and `tablesDashboard.viewMode.floorPlan` to all five
  table-check overlay files with short, compact labels so the segmented toggle stays tight: en `list="List"`, el
  `list="Λίστα"`, de `list="Liste"`, fr `list="Liste"`, it `list="Lista"`; `floorPlan="2D"` in every locale. No
  OrderDashboard/TablesDashboard styling, colour, workflow, or data changes — only the missing locale keys.
- **Tests.** Strengthened the existing `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` "table dashboards expose list and 2D
  floor-plan modes" test: it keeps the source assertions (both dashboards call the `viewMode.list`/`floorPlan` keys and
  open in List mode) and now also parses all five `overlays/*.table-check.json` files, asserting
  `tablesDashboard.viewMode.list`/`floorPlan` exist as non-empty strings, that Greek `list` is real Greek and not "List",
  and that no non-English locale leaves `list` as the English fallback "List".
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **40 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.

  compact and aligned, surrounding Greek controls remained unchanged ("Όλα", "Διαθέσιμο", "Κατειλημμένο", "Κρατημένο",
  "Καθαρισμός", "Όλοι οι όροφοι"), and no table/order/payment/status data was changed. Codex independently reran

### Supervisor round 205 — Tables floor labels keep localized case (drop forced all-caps) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On Dashboard → Tables tab (Greek/light), the floor label surfaces were forced into all caps via
  Tailwind `uppercase`, rendering "ΌΡΟΦΟΣ" and "ΌΡΟΦΟΣ 1" on the floor filter prefix and the per-table floor labels —
  harsh/vibecoded and inconsistent with the neighbouring floor button that already reads natural Greek "Όροφος 1". Bad
  for Greek and the other localized languages.
- **Fix (UI only, scoped to floor label surfaces).** Removed only the `uppercase` utility from the floor label
  className(s); kept the `<Layers>`/`<LayoutGrid>` icon, gap/spacing, `text-xs`/`text-[11px]` size, `font-bold`/
  `font-semibold` weight, `tracking-wide`, colour, rounded containers, active/tap behaviour and scrollbar behaviour:
  - `components/OrderDashboard.tsx` — the floor filter prefix (`t("tablesDashboard.floor", "Floor")`) and the per-table
    card floor label (`getTableFloorLabel(getTableFloorValue(table))`).
  - `components/tables/TablesDashboard.tsx` — the floor filter prefix (`t('tablesDashboard.floor')`), the per-table card
    floor label (`floorLabel(getFloorValue(table))`), and the selected-table detail floor label
    (`floorLabel(getFloorValue(selectedTable))`).
  - Did **not** touch stats/status/metadata uppercase (Due, badges, covers/waiter headers, etc.), the floor buttons
    (already natural case), locale strings, handlers, table/order actions, colours, or layout structure.
- **Tests.** Strengthened `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` with a focused guard: for each of the five floor
  label render tokens it extracts the nearest enclosing `className={…}` (the label container) and asserts it does **not**
  carry the `uppercase` utility while it **does** keep `tracking-wide` (proving only uppercase was removed); it also
  asserts the floor buttons stay present and that both components still use `uppercase` somewhere (stats/status not
  globally stripped).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **41 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.

  already reads "Όροφος 1" and now matches the labels. The view toggle still reads "Λίστα" / "2D", status filters and

### Supervisor round 206 — TablesPage compact cards show section, not raw notes/seed text — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the sidebar Tables page (Greek/light), the compact table cards rendered raw `table.notes` as
  primary microcopy, leaking English/admin seed text — "Bar table seeded for testing.", "Terrace table seeded for
  testing.", "Dining Room table seeded for testing.". The grid search also filtered by `t.notes`, even though the
  localized search placeholder says table number or section and `RestaurantTable` already carries structured
  `section`/`floor` fields.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/TablesPage.tsx`).**
  - The compact `TableCard` no longer renders `table.notes`; it shows the structured `table.section` on the same
    `MapPin` secondary line, gated on `table.section &&` so it only appears when a section is present. The seats line,
    status chip, card sizing, icon sizing, truncation (`truncate max-w-[60px]`), spacing, active tap behaviour, and
    theming are unchanged.
  - Grid search now matches the visible table number **+ `t.section`** instead of `t.notes`, aligning with the existing
    `tables.filters.search` placeholder. The case-insensitive table-number match is preserved
    (`t.tableNumber.toString().toLowerCase().includes(term)`).
  - `notes` is **not** removed from the data model (`types/tables.ts`) or from any modal/details path (e.g.
    `TableActionModal` still renders `table.notes`); only this page's primary card + grid search changed. No table data,
    API/schema, status-change modal, new-order/reservation handler, colour, radius, or layout-structure change.
- **Tests.** Strengthened `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: a new guard asserts the compact card renders
  `{table.section}` on the `{table.section && (` gated `MapPin` line and **not** `{table.notes}`, and that grid search
  uses `t.section?.toLowerCase().includes(term)` but **not** `t.notes?.toLowerCase().includes(term)`. The existing
  case-insensitive table-number search assertion is kept.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **42 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek/light, visual-only/no data mutation). On the sidebar Tables
  page, compact cards no longer show raw notes such as "Bar table seeded for testing.", "Terrace table seeded for

  small MapPin line (Bar, Terrace, Dining Room) while seats, status chips, card sizing, spacing, active/tap behavior, and
  theme stayed unchanged. Codex typed "Terrace" into the search field and the grid filtered to the Terrace section tables

### Supervisor round 207 — TablesPage removes native title tooltips (touch-first) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** The sidebar Tables page accessibility tree exposed icon buttons with hover/native tooltip
  descriptions — "Description: Εναλλαγή σε κάτοψη", "Description: Φίλτρα Τραπεζιών", "Description: Ανανέωση". `TablesPage.tsx`
  carried **five** DOM `title=` attributes (filter-dropdown close button, disabled New Order button, view toggle, filter
  button, refresh button). Native `title` tooltips are hover-dependent (useless on a touchscreen) and surface as doubled
  "Description:" entries in the Windows UIA tree.
- **Fix (UI/accessibility only, `pages/TablesPage.tsx`).** Removed all five DOM `title=` attributes. The four icon
  buttons (view toggle, filter, refresh, close) already had matching `aria-label`s, which are preserved as the accessible
  names. The disabled **New Order** button had only the `title` removed — its `disabled`/`aria-disabled` logic and its
  visible inline disabled message (`{isNewOrderActionDisabled && (<p>{orderCreationDisabledMessage}</p>)}` below the
  action row) are unchanged, so the disabled reason stays visible (not gated behind a hover that can't fire on touch).
  No table data, API/schema, search behaviour, status-change/new-order/reservation handler, colour, radius, or
  layout-structure change; no Round 206 section work touched.
- **Tests.** Updated the `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` TablesPage icon-button test: removed the two old
  assertions that **required** `title=`; it now asserts the view-toggle/filter/refresh/close `aria-label`s remain, that
  there is **no `\btitle=`** anywhere in TablesPage, and that the disabled New Order button keeps its
  `disabled`/`aria-disabled` logic and the visible `orderCreationDisabledMessage` inline message.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **42 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean; whole-file `title=` count in
  `TablesPage.tsx` → **0**.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator). After a real app reload and login with the test PIN, the running POS sidebar
  Tables page in Greek/light showed the view-toggle, filter, and refresh controls as named buttons with no duplicated
  "Description" native tooltip text; search and cards still rendered normally; no table/order/reservation/payment/day-close
  data action was performed. Codex independently reran `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **42 pass**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` over `TablesPage.tsx`, `orders-page-ui.test.ts`, and
  `POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` → **clean**.

### Supervisor round 208 — TablesPage compact card metadata readability (section wraps, no 60px cap) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the sidebar Tables grid (Greek/light), the compact `TableCard` truncated section names badly —
  "Dining Room" rendered as "Dining R…" — because the section span was hard-capped at `max-w-[60px]` with `truncate`
  inside a single `justify-between` row competing for width with the seats line.

  metadata stack** with stable vertical rhythm (`space-y-1.5`): the seats line first, then the structured `table.section`
  line. Removed the `truncate max-w-[60px]` cap; the section span now uses the available card width and wraps gracefully
  (`min-w-0 break-words leading-snug`). The `MapPin` icon is `shrink-0` and top-aligned (`items-start`, `mt-0.5`) so it
  stays anchored to the first line when the section wraps. Seats line, icons, text sizes/colours, theming, the
  `table.section`-only value from Round 206, and the card's smooth edges / touch-first `active:` feedback are unchanged.
  No `hover:` classes, no DOM `title=`, and no data/search/handler/status behaviour change.
- **Tests.** Updated the Round 206 guard in `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` to the Round 208 contract: it still
  asserts `table.section` is rendered (gated on presence) and `table.notes` is not, and that grid search uses `t.section`
  not `t.notes`; it now asserts the section span is the wrapping `min-w-0 break-words leading-snug` render and that the
  card no longer contains the `max-w-[60px]` truncation pattern.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **42 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean; whole-file `max-w-[60px]` count in
  `TablesPage.tsx` → **0**, no `title=`/hover introduced.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator). The running POS sidebar Tables page in Greek/light now shows the compact
  table-card metadata as a clear two-line stack; the Dining Room, Terrace, and Bar section values are fully readable
  instead of being clipped to "Dining R…"; seats and status chips still render normally; the view/filter/refresh buttons
  remain named controls with no native tooltip descriptions; no table/order/reservation/payment/day-close data action was
