### Supervisor round 209 — TablesDashboard native tooltip cleanup (refresh + Add Order FAB) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (static audit).** `components/tables/TablesDashboard.tsx` still carried source-level native `title=` on the
  refresh button and passed `title=` to the Add Order `FloatingActionButton`. The shared FAB already converts `title`
  → `aria-label` (round 173), but this component should be clean/touch-first itself rather than relying on the FAB's
  fallback — native `title` tooltips are hover-dependent and surface as "Description:" entries in the Windows UIA tree.
- **Fix (UI/accessibility only, `components/tables/TablesDashboard.tsx`).** Removed the refresh button's DOM `title=` and
  gave it an `aria-label` using the same `tablesDashboard.refresh` translation; changed the Add Order
  `FloatingActionButton` call to pass `aria-label={t('tablesDashboard.addOrder', …)}` instead of `title`. The FAB's
  props already accept `aria-label` (it destructures `'aria-label': ariaLabel` and prefers it over `title`), so the
  accessible name is unchanged. `onClick` (`handleRefresh` / `onAddOrder`), the refresh `disabled`
  (`isRefreshing || ordersLoading || tablesLoading`) logic, the `RefreshCw` spin icon, active/tap styling, layout, and
  all table/order handlers are preserved. Whole-file `title=` in TablesDashboard → **0**. Did **not** touch OrderDashboard
  modal `title` props or ReceiptPreviewModal `title` props (those are visible component headings, not DOM tooltips).
- **Tests.** Added a focused guard in `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (via `tablesDashboardSource`): TablesDashboard
  has **no source-level `\btitle=`**, the refresh button keeps its `aria-label` (`tablesDashboard.refresh`) +
  disabled/icon, and the Add Order `FloatingActionButton` passes `aria-label` (`tablesDashboard.addOrder`) with its
  `onClick`, not `title`.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **43 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS at 1282×802, Greek/light).** Checked both the mounted Dashboard →
  Tables tab and the sidebar Tables route. The accessibility tree no longer exposes native tooltip "Description:" entries
  on the table refresh/Add Order-style controls; the visible controls still read with clear names such as `Ανανέωση` and
  `Νέα παραγγελία`, and the route remains visually stable.

### Supervisor round 210 — OrderDashboard New Order FAB title-source cleanup (mounted dashboard) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (static audit).** Round 209 cleaned the standalone `TablesDashboard`, but that component is not mounted in
  the current renderer paths — the visible dashboard is `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`. Its New Order
  `FloatingActionButton` (the one with `positionStorageKey="pos-orders-new-order-fab-position"`) still passed a native
  `title={…}`. The shared FAB converts `title` → `aria-label`, so this was **not** a live DOM-tooltip bug, but the
  mounted dashboard source should be clean/touch-first itself.
- **Fix (UI/accessibility only, `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Changed only that New Order `FloatingActionButton`
  from `title={…}` to `aria-label={…}`, preserving the **exact conditional label text** (`orders.startShiftFirst` when
  `!isShiftActive`, else `orders.newOrder`). Because the FAB already carried a static
  `aria-label={t("orders.newOrder")}` that always won (`resolvedAriaLabel = ariaLabel ?? title`, so the conditional
  `title` was effectively dead at runtime), the redundant static `aria-label` was folded into the single conditional
  one — so the disabled "Start a shift first…" message now actually reaches the accessible name. `onClick`
  (`handleNewOrderClick`), `disabled={!isShiftActive}`, `movable`, `positionStorageKey`, `className`, and the FAB's
  default icon/behaviour are unchanged; no modal/order/table handlers touched. Did **not** touch the `LiquidGlassModal`
  (`orderFlow.selectOrderType`) or `ReceiptPreviewModal` (`orderDashboard.receiptPreview`) `title` props — those are
  visible component headings, not DOM tooltips.
- **Tests.** Added a focused guard in `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (via `orderDashboardSource`) that slices the
  mounted New Order FAB block (by its `onClick={handleNewOrderClick}` + `pos-orders-new-order-fab-position`) and asserts
  it exposes the **conditional `aria-label`** (start-shift-first / newOrder), has **no `\btitle=`**, and keeps
  `onClick`/`disabled`/`movable`/position key — while the two visible modal heading `title` props remain present/allowed.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **44 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator). The running POS Dashboard in Greek/light showed the mounted New Order FAB as a
  named **Νέα Παραγγελία** button with no duplicated "Description" native-tooltip text; the Dashboard Tables tab and
  table cards still rendered normally; no order modal was opened and no table/order/reservation/payment/day-close data
  action was performed. Codex independently reran `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **44 pass**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `git diff --check` over `OrderDashboard.tsx`, `orders-page-ui.test.ts`, and
  `POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` → **clean**.

### Supervisor round 211 — mounted Dashboard table-card waiter text readability (wrap, no truncate) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On Dashboard → Tables (Greek/light), the mounted table-card waiter/assignment tile clipped its
  value as "Χωρίς αν…" — the waiter value used `className="mt-1 truncate font-black"` inside a half-width tile of the
  `grid grid-cols-2` metadata grid in `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`, so "Χωρίς ανάθεση" (Unassigned) ellipsized.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Let the waiter value use the available tile width and wrap cleanly
  instead of ellipsizing: replaced `mt-1 truncate font-black` with `mt-1 break-words leading-tight font-black` on the
  value, and added `min-w-0` to the waiter tile (the grid item) so it honors its half-column and the value wraps inside
  it. Grid's default stretch keeps both tiles the same height when the waiter value grows to two lines, so cards stay
  compact and stable. The covers tile, the `tablesDashboard.covers`/`tablesDashboard.waiter` labels, the
  `{guestCount}/{table.capacity}` and `{waiterName}` renders, status colours/semantics, table selection,
  new-order/reservation buttons, status actions, and all data/search/filter handlers are unchanged. No `hover:` classes
  and no DOM `title=` introduced.
- **Tests.** Added a focused guard in `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (via `orderDashboardSource`): the waiter
  value renders with `mt-1 break-words leading-tight font-black` ahead of `{waiterName}` (no `mt-1 truncate font-black`),
  the waiter tile carries `min-w-0`, and the covers/waiter labels + `{guestCount}/{table.capacity}` + `{waiterName}`
  renders are preserved.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **45 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator). On the running POS Dashboard → Tables in Greek/light, the table-card waiter
  assignment value now visually wraps as the full "Χωρίς ανάθεση" over two lines instead of showing the clipped
  "Χωρίς αν…"; the covers and waiter tiles stayed aligned, and no action buttons, status chips, or handlers changed.

### Supervisor round 212 — mounted Dashboard table card accessibility/touch hierarchy (non-interactive wrapper) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On Dashboard → Tables, each list-mode table card wrapper was exposed as a large `role="button"`
  while it *contained* inner action buttons (New order, New reservation, Mark cleaned, Pay/Edit). The accessibility tree
  read each card as a giant button **plus** nested buttons — invalid interactive nesting and poor touch clarity.
- **Fix (UI/accessibility only, `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Made the list-mode table card wrapper a
  non-interactive semantic container: changed the `<div>` to `<article>` and removed `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`,
  the outer `onClick={() => handleTableSelect(table)}`, the outer `onKeyDown` (Enter/Space → `handleTableSelect`),
  `cursor-pointer`, the focus-ring styling (`focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-yellow-400/45`), and the press
  feedback (`active:scale-[0.99]`) — a passive container must not animate on tap. The card keeps its static visual
  classes (`min-h-[230px] rounded-2xl border p-4 backdrop-blur-xl transition-all duration-200` + `visual.card`). All
  inner visible action buttons and their handlers are preserved exactly, **including their own `active:scale-95` press
  feedback** — attention / new-or-open order still call `handleTableSelect`, reserve/pay/edit still branch to
  `handleTableReserve` or `handleTableSelect`. No `TableFloorPlanView`, order-taking, colours, copy, data handlers, or
  modal logic changed.
- **Tests.** Added a focused guard in `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (via `orderDashboardSource`): the card
  wrapper is the non-interactive `<article>` (asserted shape, no `active:scale`), and a slice of the card region has
  **no** `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, `cursor-pointer`, `focus:ring-yellow-400/45`, `onKeyDown`, or
  `active:scale-[0.99]` on the wrapper, while the inner action buttons still carry their `active:scale-95` and still call
  `handleTableSelect` (≥2×) and `handleTableReserve`.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **46 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA PASSED** (Codex/operator). After reloading the running POS and logging back in, Dashboard → Tables in
  Greek/light exposes each table card as an **article**, not a giant button; the inner buttons (Καθαρίστηκε, Νέα
  παραγγελία, Νέα κράτηση) remain normal buttons; no "Description" tooltips appeared and no data/action button was
  clicked.

### Supervisor round 213 — Dashboard Orders row pickup icon = green holder + white bag (echo chooser) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (founder screenshot, interruption).** On the Dashboard → Orders tab, the pickup/takeaway order row's
  order-type icon under the total still read like the wrong/storefront glyph. The round-192 grey bag and the round-203
  green-accent bag were both the *shared* `PickupOrderIcon`, but rendered bare at 24px they still looked faint/storefront-y
  on the row. The founder wants the row pickup icon to *visually* match the order-type chooser's pickup card, not just
  share the same source glyph.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/order/OrderCard.tsx` `OrderTypeIcon` pickup/takeaway/default branch).** The branch now
  echoes the OrderDashboard/OrderFlow order-type chooser's **green pickup card** (`OrderFlow.tsx` pickup button: green
  card + `PickupOrderIcon className="… text-white"`): the shared `PickupOrderIcon` is rendered as a centered **white** bag
  (`className="w-4 h-4 text-white"`, `strokeWidth={2}`) inside a compact **green rounded holder** — a ~28px squircle
  `<span className="inline-flex h-7 w-7 items-center justify-center rounded-[10px] bg-green-600">`. Solid `bg-green-600`
  + white bag stays clearly visible on both the cream/light row (`#fffaf1`) and the dark row. The holder has **no hover**
  and **no active scale** (a passive indicator under the amount, alignment stable next to the payment icon). Still the
  shared `PickupOrderIcon` (no raw `ShoppingBag`, no `Store`/`Package`/`Storefront`, no custom SVG). **Delivery** (orange
  truck `#d97706`) and **dine-in/table** (`TableOrderIcon`, `strokeWidth={1.6}`) icons, the payment icon, the row layout,
  order tabs/navbar, OrderDashboard modal, OrderFlow, OrdersPage, translations, and the order-taking workflow are all
  unchanged. The screenshot path is `OrderCard.tsx` (the OrderDashboard order row); `OrdersPage.tsx` is a separate page
  and was intentionally left as-is.
- **Tests.** Updated the two focused OrderCard guards from the previous bare-green-line contract to the new
  green-holder + white-bag contract: `tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` and
  `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` now assert the pickup branch renders the
  `inline-flex h-7 w-7 items-center justify-center rounded-[10px] bg-green-600` holder wrapping
  `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-4 h-4 text-white" strokeWidth={2} />` (green holder + white bag), that the round-192 grey
  and round-203 green-accent 24px direct renders are gone, that there is no `Store`/`Package`/`Storefront`/raw
  `ShoppingBag`, and that delivery (`stroke="#d97706"`) + table (`strokeWidth={1.6}`) icons and the no-`title=`/no-`hover:`
  icon path are unchanged.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts`,
  `npm run type-check` (`tsc --noEmit`), and `git diff --check` over the touched files — see the run results in the
  session report.
- **Live QA PASSED (Codex/operator).** On the running POS Dashboard → Orders tab in Greek/light, order `#00001`
  now shows the pickup/takeaway order-type icon under the total as a white shopping-bag inside a small green rounded
  holder. Opening the New Order order-type chooser confirmed the row now echoes the chooser's green Pickup card and
  uses the same shared bag shape. The chooser was closed with the close button only; no order type was selected and no
  order/status/payment data was mutated. Delivery/table rows were not changed by this round; the visible payment icon
  and row layout stayed aligned.
- **Carry-over note resolved.** The previous table-card compaction prompt was completed in the follow-up round 214;
  this round only addressed the Orders-row pickup icon.

### Supervisor round 214 — mounted Dashboard table-card compaction (no clipping at 1280×800) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, carry-over from round 213).** At 1280×800 Greek/light, the Dashboard → Tables list cards were
  visually cropped: the first visible row (#TB01/#TB02/#TB03) started correctly but the lower status/action area was cut
  by the bottom of the scroll pane, so staff couldn't see the Mark cleaned / New order / New reservation / Pay/Edit
  buttons without scrolling. The card was too tall because it **duplicated** the top status chip with a bulky
  `mt-4 rounded-xl border px-3 py-3` panel for `needsAttention` ("Needs cleaning" / "Out of service") and
  `readyForGuests` ("Ready for guests").
- **Fix (UI only, mounted list-mode card in `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Replaced the two bulky bordered status
  panels with a **compact inline status row** (`mt-2 flex items-center gap-1.5 text-sm font-black` + the existing
  amber/emerald text colour and `AlertTriangle`/`CheckCircle2` icon) — the top chip already carries the status, so the
  lower copy no longer consumes a full card panel. The card keeps the passive `<article>` wrapper from round 212 (no
  `role="button"`, no `tabIndex`, no outer `onClick`/`onKeyDown`, no `cursor-pointer`, no wrapper `active:scale`). All
  real inner action buttons stay centered, keep `active:scale-95`, and keep their handlers (`handleTableSelect` /
  `handleTableReserve`) exactly. Semantic green/amber/yellow/neutral colours, locale strings, `TableFloorPlanView`,
  order/order-taking flows, data handlers, navbar/tabs, the pickup/table icons, and modal logic are untouched. No
  `hover:` utilities.
- **Follow-up (first live QA FAILED — card still clipped at 1282×802).** The inline-status pass alone was not enough; the
  card was genuinely shortened across multiple axes: wrapper `min-h-[230px]`→`min-h-[180px]` and `p-4`→`p-3`; table
  number `text-4xl`→`text-2xl`; metadata + action grids `mt-3`→`mt-2`; metadata tiles `px-3 py-2`→`px-3 py-1.5`; status
  inline rows `mt-3`→`mt-2`; and the occupied-only chips row (`occupiedSince` / `currentOrderId`) is now rendered
  **conditionally** (`{occupiedSinceLabel || table.currentOrderId ? (…) : null}`) so available/clean cards no longer
  reserve that band. Net: an available/clean card drops from ~250px to ~200px. The occupied open-check balance branch
  (its `text-3xl` Due figure) is intentionally left prominent. Still touch-friendly (action buttons keep their `py-2`
  height + `active:scale-95`).
- **Follow-up #2 (second live QA FAILED — re-layout, judged by the real viewport).** Trimming was still not enough, so the
  card was **re-laid out** to be genuinely short (~150px): (1) the two boxed Covers/Waiter tiles are replaced by a compact
  **one-line two-chip info strip** (icon + value), where the covers chip is fixed (`shrink-0`) and the waiter chip takes
  the remaining width (`min-w-0 flex-1`) so a value like "Χωρίς ανάθεση" reads on one line without wrapping into a tall
  tile (truncating only if pathologically long); (2) the **duplicate lower status line** (Ready for guests / Needs
  cleaning / Out of service) is **removed** — the top status badge already carries it and the attention action button is
  the cleaning CTA — and the now-unused `CheckCircle2` import was dropped; (3) the Tables header stack was tightened
  Tables-only (`flex-col gap-4`→`gap-3`, header `space-y-3`→`space-y-2`) to give the grid pane more height. The card keeps
  the passive `<article>`, the chips row stays conditional, and the action buttons stay centered with their handlers +
  `active:scale-95`. `TableFloorPlanView`, order-taking, data handlers, locale strings, navbar/tabs, pickup/table icons,
  and modal logic are untouched; semantic colours preserved; no `hover:` utilities.
- **Tests.** The focused guard in `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (slicing the card `<article>` region) asserts
  the re-laid compact contract: the wrapper is `min-h-[180px] … p-3` (the old `min-h-[230px]` is gone); the bulky
  `mt-4 rounded-xl border px-3 py-3` panel **and** the duplicate lower status lines (`text-sm font-black text-amber-700`/
  `text-emerald-700`) are **gone**; the metadata is the one-line chip strip (`mt-2 flex items-center gap-1.5 text-xs` with
  a fixed `shrink-0` covers chip + a `min-w-0 flex-1` waiter chip whose `{waiterName}` is in a `truncate` span) and the
  boxed `px-3 py-1.5` tiles are gone; the table number is `text-2xl` (not `text-3xl`/`text-4xl`); the chips row is
  conditional; the wrapper stays the passive `<article>` (no `role="button"`/wrapper `active:scale`); and the inner
  action buttons keep `active:scale-95` (≥2×) + `handleTableSelect` (≥2×) + `handleTableReserve`. The round-211 (waiter
  one-line chip), round-212 (article slice anchor) and "controls fixed" (header `space-y-2`/`gap-3`) guards were updated
  to the new layout.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **47 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — COMPLETED (Codex/operator, after two failed passes).** On the running POS Dashboard → Tables at 1282×802
  Greek/light, the first visible table-card row (#TB01/#TB02/#TB03) now fits with the **action buttons fully visible**
  without scrolling: Mark cleaned / New order / New reservation labels and icons are centered/readable, not clipped; the
  card keeps the one-line covers/waiter chip strip, status is read from the top badge, and colours/copy remain unchanged.

### Supervisor round 215 — New Order order-type chooser widened for 3 options (no cramped skinny cards) — COMPLETED

- **Regressions re-verified OK (do not change).** Live verification already confirms these are correct and were left
  untouched this round: the login `AnimatedBackground` warm yellow lava/orb motion; `NavigationSidebar` inactive icons
  neutral (black in light, dark-safe grey in dark) with only the selected route neon; `OrderTabsBar` inactive tabs grey
  with only the active tab neon; the table order-type uses `TableOrderIcon` (not Cake/Utensils).
- **Problem (live QA).** On Dashboard → New Order with delivery + pickup + table all enabled, the order-type chooser was
  too narrow at 1282×802 — the three cards sat in a `max-w-lg` modal, so the Greek descriptions wrapped into skinny
  vertical columns and the chooser felt cramped.
- **Fix (UI only, chooser in `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Made the chooser premium/touch-friendly for the
  three-option case while keeping 1/2-option choosers compact:
  - The `LiquidGlassModal` width is now conditional on the same flag that drives the 3-column layout:
    `className={hasDeliveryModule && hasTablesModule ? "!max-w-3xl" : "!max-w-lg"}` — a wider glass shell (with the same
    blur/rounded style) only when all three cards show, so each card gets breathing room; one/two cards keep `max-w-lg`.
  - The grid is responsive: the 3-option case is `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3` with `gap-4 sm:gap-5` (stacks full-width on
    tiny viewports, 3-up at the POS's 1282px) instead of the always-skinny fixed `grid-cols-3`; 1/2-option grids are
    unchanged.
  - Card descriptions read better: `text-xs` → `text-sm leading-snug` on all three cards so long Greek copy is legible
    and doesn't feel like a skinny column. Titles, the delivery yellow / pickup green / table blue (`#60a5fa`) semantic
    identity, `PickupOrderIcon`/`TableOrderIcon` at the same `w-full h-full` footprint (centered), the Greek copy, the
    `active:scale-95` tap feedback, and the `composeOrderTypeAriaLabel` accessible names are all unchanged. No `hover:`
    utilities, no order-taking/data/modal-internal/table-grid/icon/locale changes.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 215 guard asserting the 3-option chooser uses the
  conditional `!max-w-3xl`/`!max-w-lg` width and the responsive `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3` grid (the old fixed
  `grid-cols-3` is gone), the descriptions are `text-sm leading-snug`, the `PickupOrderIcon`/`TableOrderIcon` full
  footprints + yellow/green/blue title identity are unchanged, and all three cards keep a `composeOrderTypeAriaLabel`
  aria-label. The existing order-type-modal `TableOrderIcon`/no-Cake-Utensils guard, the inactive-grey OrderTabsBar
  guard, the neutral-sidebar-icon guard, and the login `AnimatedBackground` orb guard remain (all already covered, so no
  new login guard was needed).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts
  tests/renderer/login-page-ui.test.ts` → **59 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS at 1282×802, Greek/light).** Opened Dashboard → New Order with delivery,
  pickup, and table enabled. The chooser now appears as a wider blurry-glass modal where the three cards have breathing
  room, Greek descriptions read cleanly instead of skinny columns, and the delivery yellow / pickup green / table blue
  colours, centered icons, and `active:scale-95` tap feedback are preserved.

### Supervisor round 216 — Order-list pickup order-type icon = green holder + white bag — COMPLETED

- **Problem (founder screenshot + parity audit).** The founder screenshot was the Dashboard → Orders tab row, where pickup
  must read as the same green-pickup identity used by the order-type chooser. The dashboard `OrderCard` path already had
  the round-213 green holder, but the standalone Orders route (`pages/OrdersPage.tsx`) still had a parallel bare pickup
  row icon path: `getOrderTypeIcon()` rendered pickup/default as `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-6 h-6" />`, an outline bag
  in the row's neutral text colour that can read ambiguously at row scale. The fix brings that remaining order-list surface
  into parity so the icon cannot drift back toward a storefront/shop impression.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/OrdersPage.tsx`).** `getOrderTypeIcon()` now renders pickup **and** the default fall-through as
  a shared `pickupRowIcon`: the shared `PickupOrderIcon` as a centered **white bag inside a compact green rounded holder**
  (`inline-flex h-7 w-7 items-center justify-center rounded-[10px] bg-green-600` wrapping
  `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-4 h-4 text-white" strokeWidth={2} />`) — the same green-pickup identity as the order-type
  chooser and the dashboard `OrderCard` (round 213), sized for a row. **Delivery** (`<Truck className="w-6 h-6" />`) and
  **dine-in** (`<TableOrderIcon className="w-6 h-6" />`) are unchanged; the icon stays centered/aligned next to its label
  in the `flex items-center` row. Never a `Store`/`Package`/storefront glyph or a raw `ShoppingBag` for the order-type
  icon (the items-count `<Package>` and empty-state `<ShoppingBag>` elsewhere on the page are untouched). No `hover:`
  utilities, no locale/data-flow/modal/sidebar/tab/login/other-page changes. `components/order/OrderCard.tsx` already
  used this exact green-holder treatment (round 213), so it needed no change and was left untouched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: **updated** the OrdersPage order-type-icon guard (the old assertion
  required the bare `w-6 h-6` pickup render) to assert — scoped to the `pickupRowIcon` holder + `getOrderTypeIcon` switch
  body — that pickup/default render the `inline-flex h-7 w-7 … rounded-[10px] bg-green-600` holder wrapping
  `<PickupOrderIcon className="w-4 h-4 text-white" strokeWidth={2} />`, that pickup + default both reuse `pickupRowIcon`,
  that delivery `<Truck>` / dine-in `<TableOrderIcon>` are unchanged, and that the icon region has no
  `Package`/`Store`/`<ShoppingBag>`. The round-213 OrderCard green-holder guard remains.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **48 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS at 1282×802, Greek/light).** On the Dashboard → Orders tab, pickup order
  `#00001` now shows the white bag inside the small green rounded holder, matching the chooser/dashboard pickup identity
  and no longer reading as a storefront/shop mark. Row text and price remain aligned, no order/data behaviour was changed,
  and the standalone `OrdersPage` row path is covered by the updated static guard.

### Supervisor round 217 — Settings "This register" sync-health label localized (no raw English runtime state) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** On the running POS at 1282×802 (Greek/light), Settings → Αυτό το ταμείο showed the raw English
  sync status **"stale"** in two places — the status line ("Συγχρονισμός: stale") and the sync summary tile. The cause:
  `ConnectionSettingsModal`'s `syncHealthLabel = t(`settings.managedByAdmin.syncHealth.${runtimeSyncHealth}`, {
  defaultValue: runtimeSyncHealth })` falls back to the **raw runtime value** when the locale lacks a key, and the
  `syncHealth` block had only `offline/polling/websocket/online/healthy/error/blocked/unknown` — so runtime states like
  `stale` leaked through untranslated.
- **Fix (i18n only — no source/layout change).** Added the missing runtime health states to
  `settings.managedByAdmin.syncHealth` in **all five** POS locales (en/el/de/fr/it): `stale, failed, fail, disconnected,
  connected, good, live, ok, synced, degraded` — the values the modal's `syncToneClass` recognises (green:
  healthy/online/ok/synced/connected/good/live; red: offline/error/failed/fail/disconnected/stale) plus `degraded`.
  Greek renders `stale` as the short operator label **"Παλιό"** (concise for the summary tile), not English. No change to
  `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` was needed — it already resolves the label through the `syncHealth.${runtimeSyncHealth}`
  key, so adding the keys fixes the fall-through; the visual layout, the red/yellow/green tone-dot behaviour, the Sync
  button, all handlers, and the glass styling are untouched. No unrelated settings sections, sidebar/tabs/login/order
  pages, data flows, or module names were touched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 217 guard asserting every POS locale defines
  the new `settings.managedByAdmin.syncHealth.{stale,failed,fail,disconnected,connected,good,live,ok,synced,degraded}`
  keys as non-empty strings, that Greek `stale` is real Greek (not the raw "stale" fallback), and that the modal still
  derives `syncHealthLabel` from `settings.managedByAdmin.syncHealth.${runtimeSyncHealth}` (not raw display text).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **18 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS at 1282×802, Greek/light).** Reopened Settings → Αυτό το ταμείο after
  the locale update. The live runtime status had changed from `stale` to `polling`, and the modal rendered it in Greek as
  "Συγχρονισμός: Περιοδικός έλεγχος" plus "Περιοδικός έλεγχος" in the summary tile, with the same yellow tone dot and no
  raw English fallback visible. The original `stale` path and the other runtime states are covered by the new locale
  regression guard.

### Supervisor round 218 — Order-row pickup icon = plain bag, both surfaces (drop the green badge/holder) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (founder correction + supervisor review).** The pickup/takeaway order-row icon was a white bag inside a compact
  green rounded badge (`h-7 w-7 … rounded-[10px] bg-green-600` chip) on **two** order-row surfaces: the standalone Orders
  route `pages/OrdersPage.tsx` (round 216) **and** the live Dashboard order row `components/order/OrderCard.tsx` (round
  213, rendered via `OrderGrid` → `OrderDashboard`). On the running POS the filled green chip still reads as a **separate
  boxed treatment** — a chip standing apart from the unboxed neutral `Truck` / `TableOrderIcon` siblings, so pickup looks
  like a different *kind* of thing rather than just another order type. A first pass changed only `OrdersPage.tsx`; the
  supervisor confirmed via the live screenshot (the Dashboard row after closing Settings) that `OrderCard.tsx` still showed
  the badge, so this round fixes **both** surfaces. The ask: render the shared pickup bag as a **plain bag silhouette at
  row scale**, centered and readable, with no green badge/holder — and without reverting to a
  `Store`/`Package`/storefront/raw-`ShoppingBag` glyph.
- **Fix (UI only, both order-row surfaces).**
  - `pages/OrdersPage.tsx` — `pickupRowIcon` now renders the shared `PickupOrderIcon` directly with no wrapper:
    `<PickupOrderIcon className={`w-6 h-6 ${isDark ? 'text-green-400' : 'text-green-600'}`} strokeWidth={2} />`.
  - `components/order/OrderCard.tsx` — the `OrderTypeIcon` pickup/default branch now renders the same plain bag, using the
    file's local theme idiom: `<PickupOrderIcon className={`w-6 h-6 ${resolvedTheme === 'light' ? 'text-green-600' :
    'text-green-400'}`} strokeWidth={2} />` (matching the sibling dine-in `<TableOrderIcon>` branch directly above it).
  - On both, the bag is sized **`w-6 h-6`** to sit at the same row scale as the sibling delivery `<Truck className="w-6
    h-6" />` and dine-in `<TableOrderIcon className="w-6 h-6" />` (the old chip rendered a 16px `w-4 h-4` bag inside a 28px
    box). A **theme-aware semantic green stroke** (`text-green-600` light / `text-green-400` dark) keeps the bag readable on
    the cream/light and dark rows while preserving the green-pickup identity. The green badge/holder is gone on both: no
    `bg-green-600` chip, no `rounded-[10px]` wrapper, no `text-white`. Pickup **and** the default fall-through reuse the
    plain bag; **delivery** and **dine-in** icons are unchanged; the icon stays centered/aligned next to its label in the
    `flex items-center` row. No `hover:` utilities, no tap animation on the icon, no locale/data-flow/modal/sidebar/tab/
    login/other-page changes; the items-count `<Package>` and empty-state `<ShoppingBag>` elsewhere on `OrdersPage` are
    untouched. (The KitchenDisplay screen renders its own separate local card and is not part of this order-list round.)
- **Tests.**
  - `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: the OrdersPage guard ("…plain bag at row scale, no green badge") asserts —
    scoped to the `pickupRowIcon` definition + `getOrderTypeIcon` switch body — pickup/default render `<PickupOrderIcon
    className={`w-6 h-6 ${isDark ? 'text-green-400' : 'text-green-600'}`} strokeWidth={2} />`, the badge is gone (no
    `bg-green-600`/`rounded-[10px]`/`text-white`), and delivery/table are unchanged. **Plus** the OrderCard guard in the
    same file was flipped from the round-213 green-holder expectation to the plain-bag treatment (scoped to the
    `OrderTypeIcon` body): the plain `w-6 h-6` green-stroke render, no `bg-green-600`/`rounded-[10px]`/`text-white`, delivery
    `stroke="#d97706"` + dine-in `<TableOrderIcon>` unchanged, no `title=`/`hover:`.
  - `tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts`: the duplicate OrderCard green-holder guard was flipped the same way — it
    now asserts the plain `w-6 h-6` green-stroke `PickupOrderIcon`, that the green-chip span and the white-bag render are
    gone, no `Store`/`Package`/`ShoppingBag`, and the table icon treatment unchanged.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` →
  **52 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS at 1282×802, Greek/light).** Verified **both** order-row surfaces after
  hot reload: the Dashboard order row (`Πίνακας` → `Παραγγελίες`) shows pickup as a plain green bag under the price with
  no filled green chip/badge, and the standalone Orders route (`Παραγγελίες`) shows the same plain green bag inline next
  to `Παραλαβή`. In both views the icon is readable, centered/aligned at the same row scale as the delivery/table icons,
  and delivery/table icons remain unchanged.

### Supervisor round 219 — Orders filters "Clear all filters" is a real touch button, not loose text — COMPLETED

- **Problem (supervisor design QA, live Greek POS).** On the standalone Orders route, opening the filters panel revealed
  the "Clear all filters" action as a tiny plain-text button under the filter grid (`pages/OrdersPage.tsx`): `<button
  className="mt-2 text-sm …">` with no `type`, no sizing, and no icon. In the live Greek POS it rendered as small loose
  text "Καθαρισμός όλων των φίλτρων" — easy to miss and far below a reliable touchscreen target size.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/OrdersPage.tsx`).** Replaced that naked text button with a real touch-sized control while keeping
  `handleClearFilters` and the locale key unchanged: `type="button"`, `inline-flex min-h-[44px] items-center
  justify-center gap-2 rounded-2xl border px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition active:scale-[0.97]` with a theme-aware
  amber/yellow neutral skin (light `border-amber-300 bg-amber-100/70 text-amber-800 active:bg-amber-200`; dark
  `border-yellow-300/30 bg-yellow-400/10 text-yellow-200 active:bg-yellow-400/20`). The button now centers a lucide
  **`RotateCcw`** reset icon (`w-4 h-4`, newly imported from `lucide-react`) next to the localized label
  (`t('orders.filters.clearAll', …)`), so it reads as an obvious "reset filters" tap target. Press feedback is
  active-only (`active:scale` + `active:bg`); **no** `hover:` utilities, no emoji, no custom SVG. The filter-panel grid
  layout above is unchanged (only the trailing button swaps text → button, `mt-2` → `mt-3` for breathing room). No change
  to search/filter behaviour, pagination, rows, order data, or the round-218 pickup icon.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 219 guard ("…real touch-sized button, not a naked
  text link") that confirms `handleClearFilters` is still defined, `RotateCcw` is imported from `lucide-react`, and —
  scoped to the clear-filters `<button>` (anchored on its `onClick`) — that it has `type="button"`,
  `onClick={handleClearFilters}`, an `inline-flex` / `min-h-[44px]` / `rounded-2xl` touch target, centered
  `items-center`/`justify-center`/`gap-2`, a `<RotateCcw>` icon (and no `<svg`), `active:` press feedback with no
  `hover:`, the existing `orders.filters.clearAll` key, and no naked `>Clear all filters<` text.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **49 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS at 1282×802, Greek/light).** Opened the standalone Orders route filters
  panel and confirmed "Καθαρισμός όλων των φίλτρων" now renders as a clear amber rounded touch button with a `RotateCcw`
  reset icon, not loose text. Changed the status filter to `εκκρεμεί` (list dropped from 21 to 3 orders), tapped the new
  clear button, and verified the status/type/date controls reset to all/default and the list returned to 21 orders after
  the refresh settled. Search/filter layout, rows, pagination, and the round-218 pickup icons remained unchanged.

### Supervisor round 220 — Orders row status label is a compact semantic pill (not tiny loose text) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (supervisor design QA).** On the standalone Orders route, the row status label rendered as tiny loose text
  (`<span className="text-xs font-semibold ${getOrderStatusTextClasses(order.status)}">`) — just a small coloured word that
  looked weak and easy to miss next to the strong `font-mono font-bold text-lg` order number and the price. It needed to
  read as an intentional status chip without bloating the row.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/OrdersPage.tsx`).** Replaced the loose-text status styling with a compact **status pill**. The
  status `<span>` is now `inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full border px-2.5 py-1 text-xs font-semibold`
  plus per-status skin classes. The old text-only `ORDER_STATUS_TEXT_CLASSES` map / `getOrderStatusTextClasses` were
  repurposed into `ORDER_STATUS_PILL_CLASSES` / `getOrderStatusPillClasses`, a data-driven `Record` that gives each status
  a readable **light + dark** skin via the `dark:` Tailwind variant (the file already uses `darkMode: 'class'` + the theme
  context's root `.dark` toggle): `pending` amber, `confirmed` blue, `processing`/`preparing` purple, `ready` green,
  `out_for_delivery` cyan, `completed`/`delivered` neutral grey, `cancelled` red; unknown statuses fall back to the yellow
  pending skin (the white/black/grey/yellow app-palette base with small semantic accents). The pill stays compact (`py-1`,
  `text-xs`) so it sits within the order-number line box and does **not** grow the row. **No hover utilities and no
  active/press feedback** — the pill is informational, and taps still bubble to the row's existing `onClick` (open order).
  Row behaviour, the order-type icon/label, the round-218 pickup bag icon, filters, pagination, and data flow are all
  unchanged. The dashboard order-taking page and `OrderCard` were not touched this round.
- **Live-QA correction (too quiet/tiny → strengthened, UI only, `pages/OrdersPage.tsx`).** First-pass live QA found the
  pill present but still too quiet and small at 1282×802. Strengthened the pill treatment only: the span is now
  `inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full border px-3 py-1.5 text-[13px] leading-none font-bold
  whitespace-nowrap` (was `px-2.5 py-1 text-xs font-semibold`). `leading-none` keeps the taller/bolder text inside the
  `text-lg` order-number line box so the row still does **not** grow, and `whitespace-nowrap` stops long localized labels
  (e.g. Greek `out_for_delivery`) wrapping inside the rounded pill. The per-status skins in `ORDER_STATUS_PILL_CLASSES` were
  deepened for contrast: light text `-700 → -800/-900` on `-100 → -200` fills with `-300` borders; dark foregrounds lifted
  `-300 → -200` with `/10 → /20` fills and `/45` borders. **Completed/delivered grey** specifically moved off the faded
  `gray-100 / text-gray-600` (which read as disabled) to solid `bg-gray-300 text-gray-900` (light) /
  `bg-zinc-600/60 text-zinc-100` (dark). `pending` amber, `ready` green, `cancelled` red, `confirmed` blue,
  `processing`/`preparing` purple, `out_for_delivery` cyan stay semantic; unknown still falls back to the yellow pending
  skin. Still **no hover utilities and no active/press feedback** on the pill. Nothing else changed — order number, price,
  order-type icon/label, the round-218 pickup bag, filters, pagination, data flow, `OrderCard`, and the dashboard
  order-taking page are all untouched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: the row-chips guard now requires the **strong** pill `<span>`
  (`inline-flex … rounded-full border px-3 py-1.5 text-[13px] leading-none font-bold whitespace-nowrap
  ${getOrderStatusPillClasses(order.status)}`) and asserts both the old loose-text span and the first quiet
  `px-2.5 py-1 text-xs` pill are gone. The focused Round 220 guard (renamed "…uses the strong compact treatment and
  readable light+dark semantic skins, no hover") now slices the pill span and requires `px-3` / `py-1.5` / `text-[13px]` /
  `leading-none` / `font-bold` / `whitespace-nowrap` / `rounded-full` / `border` with no `hover:` and no `active:` on it,
  still requires every status (pending/confirmed/preparing/ready/completed/cancelled/out_for_delivery) to have a light
  background + border + `dark:` variant, additionally asserts completed/delivered are **not** the faded
  `gray-100 / text-gray-600` skin, and keeps the unknown→pending fallback check.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **50 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — COMPLETED (Codex/operator, 1282×802, Greek/light).** Verified on the running standalone Orders route:
  status labels now read as clearly visible rounded pills, completed grey is solid/not disabled-looking, pending amber is
  readable, and the row height remains light. Order number, price, order-type icon/label, filters, pagination, and the
  round-218 pickup bag stayed unchanged. Dark contrast is covered by the explicit `dark:` skins and source tests.

### Supervisor round 221 — Orders route localizes room_service order type + gives it a Bed icon (no raw slug) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED (re-confirmed Round 284)

- **Problem (live QA, multilingual polish).** On the standalone Orders route in the Greek UI, a `room_service` order rendered
  the **raw slug** "room_service" as its order-type label (a multilingual-polish violation that looks unfinished), and the
  order-type icon fell through `getOrderTypeIcon`'s `default` to the pickup bag — so room service was indistinguishable from
  pickup.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/OrdersPage.tsx` + locale keys).** Two display-only changes plus locale keys:
  - **Label.** `getOrderTypeLabel` now collapses `-`/`_`/whitespace via the **ZReportModal slug idea**
    (`normalized.replace(/[\s_-]+/g, '_')`) so `room_service` / `room-service` / `room service` all resolve to one key:
    `dine_in → dineIn`, `room_service → roomService`, else the slug. It looks up `orders.type.${key}`, so room service now
    shows the localized label instead of the raw slug.
  - **Icon.** `getOrderTypeIcon` matches room service **first** (same collapse) and returns a dedicated `<Bed className="w-6
    h-6" />` (lucide, newly imported) at the same row footprint as the other order-type icons; it can no longer fall through
    to the pickup bag. Delivery (`Truck`), pickup (round-218 plain green `PickupOrderIcon` bag), and dine-in (`TableOrderIcon`)
    are unchanged — the room-service branch sits ahead of the untouched `switch`.
  - **Locales.** Added `orders.type.roomService` to **all five** POS locales using the existing
    `modals.zReport.orderTypes.roomService` wording: en "Room service", el "Υπηρεσία δωματίου", de "Zimmerservice",
    fr "Service en chambre", it "Servizio in camera".
  - Display-only: no change to `normalizeOrderType` / stored `order.order_type`, so filtering and data matching are
    untouched. The round-220 status pill, round-218 pickup bag, filters, pagination, and data flow are unchanged; `OrderCard`
    and the dashboard order-taking page were not touched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 221 guard asserting `Bed` is imported, that
  `getOrderTypeLabel` collapses separators and maps `room_service → roomService`, that `getOrderTypeIcon` has a
  `collapsed === 'room_service'` → `<Bed …>` branch resolving **before** the pickup default, that delivery/pickup/dine-in
  icons are unchanged, and that all five locales define `orders.type.roomService` (non-empty, not the raw `room[_-]service`
  slug, Greek contains Greek letters and differs from English). The stale localization-test assertion that pinned the old
  `getOrderTypeLabel` key line was updated to the new `collapsed === 'dine_in'/'room_service'` mappings. The round-218 icon
  guard still passes (the new branch sits before the byte-for-byte unchanged switch).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` →
  **61 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — DATA-LIMITED (Codex/operator, 1282×802, Greek/light).** Reopened the running POS, logged in with the
  test PIN, and opened the standalone Orders route. The current org/session only exposed one visible order (`ORD #00001`,
  pickup), so there was no `room_service` row available to visually confirm in this pass. The visible pickup row still
  uses the round-218 plain green bag, the status pill stayed intact, and filters/search layout remained unchanged. Keep
  this round pending until a `room_service` row is visible or we intentionally create/seed one for live QA.

### Supervisor round 222 — Settings "This register" overview: allowed-actions / active-areas as capped chips — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, Greek/light 1282×802).** In Settings → "Αυτό το ταμείο" / This register, the "Allowed actions" and
  "Active areas" cards rendered their long localized labels as a dense paragraph joined with bullets
  (`enabledFeatureLabels.join(' · ')` / `enabledModuleNames.join(' · ')`). It was hard to scan and read like a text dump.
  Round 194 had intentionally removed the earlier wall of equal-weight pills, so the fix had to be a middle ground — not a
  revert to an unlimited pill wall.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** Both cards now render a **capped, wrapping chip
  grid**: the first `OVERVIEW_CHIP_LIMIT` (8) localized labels become soft rounded chips (`flex flex-wrap gap-1.5`,
  `rounded-full border … px-2.5 py-1 text-xs`), followed by a calm **"+N more" summary chip** when there is overflow. The
  chips carry restrained semantic accents that work in light + dark via `dark:` variants (the modal already uses
  `darkMode: 'class'`): **allowed actions** use amber/yellow (`border-yellow-400/30 bg-yellow-400/10 text-yellow-900
  dark:text-yellow-100`); **active areas** use green (`border-green-500/30 bg-green-500/10 text-green-900
  dark:text-green-100`) for a distinct, scannable "active/on" identity; the "+N more" chip is neutral/muted. **No data is
  removed** — `enabledFeatureLabels` / `enabledModuleNames` still contain every entry (only the first 8 are *shown*); the
  count badges, the glass modal style, the empty-state fallbacks (`noAllowedActions` / `coreAreasOnly`), and
  `resolveNavigationLabel` localization are all preserved. No hover utilities (touch-first); the cards are informational so
  no tap feedback was added. Settings navigation, submodal wiring, and all other sections are untouched.
- **Locale keys.** Added `settings.deviceSetup.overview.moreCount` (the "+N more" label) to **all five** POS locales:
  en `+{{count}} more`, el `+{{count}} ακόμη`, de `+{{count}} weitere`, fr `+{{count}} de plus`, it `+{{count}} altri`.
  Called as `t('settings.deviceSetup.overview.moreCount', { count: total - OVERVIEW_CHIP_LIMIT })`, mirroring the existing
  `reservationForm.andMore` interpolation pattern.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`: the Round 194 admin-overview guard no longer requires
  `enabledFeatureLabels.join(' · ')` / `enabledModuleNames.join(' · ')`. It now scopes to the two overview cards
  (`data-register-allowed-actions` → `data-register-technical-details`) and asserts the **capped** chip rendering
  (`enabledFeatureLabels.slice(0, OVERVIEW_CHIP_LIMIT).map(` / same for modules), `rounded-full` chips, the **"+N more"**
  summary (`…length > OVERVIEW_CHIP_LIMIT … t('settings.deviceSetup.overview.moreCount'`) with the remainder count, that the
  bullet join is gone, that there is **no unlimited wall** (no bare `enabledFeatureLabels.map(` / `enabledModuleNames.map(`),
  **no `hover:`** in the chip section, that counts are still rendered, and that `moreCount` exists in all five locales
  (interpolating `{{count}}`, Greek ≠ English). The `resolveNavigationLabel` build assertion is preserved.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **18 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS at 1282×802, Greek/light).** Opened Settings → "Αυτό το ταμείο" after
  a renderer refresh. The allowed-actions card now shows amber rounded chips for the 8 enabled actions; the active-areas
  card shows green rounded chips for the first 8 areas plus the localized overflow chip `+8 ακόμη`. Counts still render
  (`8` and `16`), the cards are scannable instead of paragraph text, and the technical details remain below the first
  viewport. The explicit rollback surfaces remained correct in the same live session: logging out showed the warm yellow
  animated/orb login background intact, sidebar inactive icons stayed black with only the active icon neon, Dashboard order
  tabs kept grey inactive labels, and the order-type chooser table card still reads as table/chairs. Logged back in with the
  test PIN afterward so the app was left on the Dashboard.

### Supervisor round 223 — Navigation sidebar: persistent utility cluster (Settings/Logout/Z Report always reachable) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, Greek/light 1282×802).** With many modules enabled the sidebar rail scrolls, and Settings + Logout
  were rendered at the very end of the **same** scrollable nav list as all modules — so the operator had to scroll the icon
  rail to reach them. Z Report (a global close-day/report utility, not a module) sat in that scroll list too. Poor POS UX,
  especially on a touchscreen.
- **Fix (UI structure only, `components/NavigationSidebar.tsx`).** The rail card is now a **flex column** that no longer
  scrolls (`flex max-h-full flex-col overflow-hidden`, was `overflow-y-auto`). Inside the `<nav>` (`flex min-h-0 flex-1
  flex-col`):
  1. a **top persistent action area** (`data-navigation-top-actions`, `shrink-0`) holding the Check In button (kept as the
     top action, as before);
  2. a **module scroll region** (`data-navigation-module-scroll`, `min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto overflow-x-hidden
     touch-pan-y scrollbar-hide`) that is now the element carrying **`ref={scrollContainerRef}`** — only the module list
     scrolls and drags/reorders, exactly as before;
  3. a **bottom persistent utility cluster** (`data-navigation-utility-actions`, `shrink-0`) holding **Z Report → Settings →
     Logout**, outside the scroll region, so all three stay visible without scrolling the modules.
  The active-module auto-scroll (`const container = scrollContainerRef.current` + the nudge math) and the long-press
  drag-scroll (`scrollContainer.scrollTop = …`) now target the module-scroll region instead of the whole rail (more correct,
  since the pinned utilities are no longer part of the scrolled area). The 100-line module map, all drag handlers, the
  loading skeleton, dividers, and every button's markup are unchanged — only the wrapper boundaries moved.
- **Preserved exactly (no behaviour/visual drift):** route ordering, module filtering, locked/coming-soon behaviour, shift
  gating, Z Report gating, and drag storage are untouched. The user-approved nav colours are intact — `getNeonClass` still
  returns neutral (black in light / `text-zinc-400` in dark) for inactive unlocked icons and per-module neon only for the
  active route; Check In + Settings stay neutral via `getNeonClass('yellow', false, …)` (not always-on neon); Logout keeps
  its red outline. No hover utilities and no native `title=` tooltips were added (touch active scale only).
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 223 guard asserting the `data-navigation-
  module-scroll` + `data-navigation-utility-actions` markers exist, that `scrollContainerRef` lives on the
  `overflow-y-auto` module-scroll region (which also flexes `min-h-0`/`flex-1`), that the active-module auto-scroll and
  drag still reference `scrollContainerRef`, that the module map renders inside the scroll region while Z Report / Settings /
  Logout render inside the utility cluster **outside** it (ordered Z Report → Settings → Logout), that Check In stays above
  the scroll region, that the cluster has no `hover:` and the file no `title=`, and that Logout's red outline + Settings'
  neutral `getNeonClass('yellow', false, …)` are preserved. All prior sidebar guards (logout outline, drag/scroll,
  getNeonClass inactive-neutral/active-neon, aria-current, no title tooltips) still pass unchanged.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` → **9 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light 1282×802).** Verified on the running POS with many modules enabled:
  the module region scrolls independently while Check In remains pinned at the top and Z Report / Settings / Logout remain
  pinned at the bottom without scrolling the rail. Tapping the pinned Settings button opened the glass settings modal
  immediately. Inactive module icons stayed neutral black in light mode, the active Dashboard icon stayed neon blue, Logout
  stayed red, and the Orders pickup icon remained the same green bag used in the order-type chooser.

### Supervisor round 224 — TableSelector modal glass redesign (matches the Settings / Order Type glass modals) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, Greek/light 1282×802).** Dashboard → New Order → Table order opens
  `components/tables/TableSelector.tsx`, which was still an opaque white panel (`bg-white` / `bg-gray-900`) with flat pale
  cards, blue active accents (`bg-blue-600`, `focus:ring-blue-500`), and hover-only styling — visually disconnected from the
  rest of the glass POS modal system. The workflow was fine; this is a visual-consistency pass.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/tables/TableSelector.tsx`).** Rebuilt the modal onto the **shared `LiquidGlassModal` shell**
  (the same glass surface the Settings + Order Type modals use), replacing the hand-rolled `renderModalPortal` panel +
  manual backdrop + custom close button. The shell brings the translucent blurred surface, soft amber edge/glow, rounded
  edges, open/close animation, hidden scrollbars on the scrolling content, and the focus-trap/Escape a11y for free
  (`size="lg"`, `title` = the localized heading). The inner controls were restyled onto the same glass tokens so they read
  as one system: the **search field** uses `liquid-glass-modal-input` (glass surface, 44px touch height, amber focus ring —
  no more blue); the **min-capacity segmented buttons** use a neutral glass skin (`liquid-glass-modal-border bg-white/5
  liquid-glass-modal-text dark:bg-black/20`) with an **amber** active state (`border-amber-400/50 bg-amber-400/20`), `min-h-
  [40px]`, `active:scale-95`, and `aria-pressed`; the **legend** keeps the green/amber dots; and the **table cards** are now
  soft glass tiles (`rounded-2xl border backdrop-blur-md`, `active:scale-95`) keeping the **semantic** status colours —
  available **green** (`border-green-500/50 bg-green-500/10`), reserved **amber** (`border-yellow-500/50 bg-yellow-500/10`).
  Base stays neutral white/black/grey via the glass text tokens; accents are restrained green/amber (not "everything
  yellow"). All hover-only styling and the native blue focus rings were removed (touch active scale only); no native
  `title=` tooltips (the only `title=` is the `LiquidGlassModal` heading prop). Data flow, callbacks (`onTableSelect`),
  status filtering (`filterSelectableTables` / `filterStatuses`), search + capacity filtering, and all `tableSelector.*`
  translations are unchanged.
- **i18n.** No new keys needed — `tableSelector.*` already exists in all five POS locales (en/el/de/fr/it) with real
  translations (e.g. el title "Επιλογή Τραπεζιού"). No hardcoded Greek in the source; layout uses `flex-wrap` so it stays
  localization-safe.
- **Tests.** **Added** `tests/renderer/table-selector-ui.test.ts` (5 tests): glass shell (`LiquidGlassModal` import + usage,
  `size="lg"`, the old opaque panel / `renderModalPortal` gone) + glass tokens + the `data-table-selector` /
  `-capacity` / `-grid` markers; no `hover:` and exactly one `title=` (the heading prop); semantic green/amber status colours
  with the blue button/focus accents gone; the capacity segmented structure (All + `capacityOptions.map`, `aria-pressed`,
  `min-h-[40px]`) and preserved data flow (`onTableSelect`, `filterSelectableTables`, search); and `tableSelector.*` i18n
  (no hardcoded Greek, all five locales define `title`/`minCapacity`, Greek ≠ English).
- **Live-QA correction (clipped Greek placeholder + floor selector, UI only, `components/tables/TableSelector.tsx`).**
  First-pass live QA found the Greek search placeholder clipped at 1282×802 because the search field shared a row with
  min-capacity, and the user correctly asked for a **floor selector**. Two fixes (glass look unchanged):
  1. **Language-safe filter layout.** The filters block is now a vertical stack (`data-table-selector-filters
     space-y-3`): the **search gets its own full-width row** (`relative w-full` + `liquid-glass-modal-input w-full`), so the
     EN/EL/DE/FR/IT placeholders never clip — no locale-specific widths. The floor + min-capacity segmented controls sit on
     their own rows below, each `flex flex-wrap` so long localized labels (e.g. Greek "Ελάχ. χωρητικότητα:") wrap instead of
     squeezing the search.
  2. **Floor selector.** Added a compact glass segmented floor control reusing the TablesDashboard pattern: a module-level
     `getTableFloorValue(table)` (`table.floorLevel ?? floor_level ?? 1` → stable string), `selectedFloor` state (default
     `'all'`), a unique-sorted `floorOptions` memo from the selectable tables, and a floor filter applied inside
     `filteredTables` (tracked in its deps). The control (`data-table-selector-floor`, shown when there is any floor data)
     renders **All floors** + one **Floor {{floor}}** chip per option, same amber-active glass segmented styling as
     capacity, `min-h-[40px]`, `aria-pressed`, no hover. Data flow, callbacks, selection, status filtering, green/amber
     semantics, and the no-hover / no-DOM-`title` rules are unchanged.
- **Locale keys.** Added `tableSelector.floor` / `allFloors` / `floorNumber` (with `{{floor}}` interpolation) to **all five**
  POS locales with real translations (el "Όροφος" / "Όλοι οι όροφοι" / "Όροφος {{floor}}"; de "Etage"; fr "Étage"; it
  "Piano"), keeping the `table-selector-i18n` key-parity guard green. (Noted in passing: `tablesDashboard.floor*` keys are
  absent from the locale files and rely on `defaultValue` fallback — a separate latent i18n gap, left untouched.)
- **Live-QA follow-up (search matches the visible label, UI only, `components/tables/TableSelector.tsx`).** Live QA in
  Greek found that cards labelled with the formatted display value (e.g. `#TP03`) returned **0 results** when the operator
  typed `TP03`, because the filter only checked `table.tableNumber.toString()` + notes. Fixed so search matches **what the
  operator sees**: two small exported pure helpers — `normalizeTableSearch(value)` (lowercase + strip punctuation/separators but **preserve Unicode letters/numbers** via `/[^\p{L}\p{N}]/gu` so non-Latin notes/terms are not mangled, so
  `#TP03`/`TP03`/`tp03` collapse to one form) and `tableMatchesSearchTerm(table, rawTerm)` (matches the normalized raw
  `tableNumber`, the normalized `formatTableDisplayNumber(table.tableNumber)` label, **and** notes). The grid filter now
  calls `tableMatchesSearchTerm`, so `TP03`, `#TP03`, `tp03`, `03`, and `3` all find table `TP03`; notes search is kept;
  punctuation/case no longer break matching. Uses the existing shared display formatter only (no TP-/Greek-specific
  assumptions). The glass UI, full-width search row, floor + capacity selectors, no-hover / no-`title`, and data flow are
  unchanged.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/table-selector-ui.test.ts`: **added** a full-width/stacked search-row guard, two floor guards
  (state/options/filtering + the `getTableFloorValue` helper + `aria-pressed`; and floor labels localized in all five
  locales with `{{floor}}`), and a **search-matching guard** (slices `tableMatchesSearchTerm` to prove the raw number, the
  `formatTableDisplayNumber` label, and notes are all searched — i.e. the formatter participates in search, not only the
  card render — and that the old `tableNumber.toString().includes` filter is gone). `tests/renderer/inline-overlay-
  portals.test.ts`: the obsolete "TableSelector portals via renderModalPortal" test was rewritten to assert TableSelector
  now overlays via the shared `LiquidGlassModal` glass shell (no `renderModalPortal`). `table-selector-i18n.test.ts` passes
  unchanged (parity holds with the new floor keys).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/table-selector-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/table-selector-i18n.test.ts
  tests/renderer/inline-overlay-portals.test.ts` → **22 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, Greek/light 1282×802).** Reopened Dashboard → New Order → Table order after hot reload
  and login with the test PIN. The modal now uses the same blurry glass shell as the Settings / Order Type modals, the Greek
  search placeholder is no longer clipped because search has its own full-width row, the **floor selector** appears (`Όλοι οι
  όροφοι` + `Όροφος 1`) beside the capacity row, and the green/amber table-card semantics remain clear. Live typed `TP03`:
  the picker now returned the visible `#TP03` card instead of zero results. Tapping `#TP03` opened the table action modal,
  proving the selection handoff still works. This org only has Floor 1 data, so multi-floor filtering remains covered by the
  focused static tests.

### Supervisor round 225 — TableActionModal glass redesign + touch alignment (shared liquid-glass tokens) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA after Round 224).** Dashboard → New Order → Table order → `#TP03` opens `TableActionModal`. Behaviour
  works, but it used a hand-rolled translucent shell (`bg-gray-900/60 … ring-1`, custom header/close, two **manual SVG
  arrows**) that felt flatter than the new TableSelector / Settings / Order Type glass modals.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/tables/TableActionModal.tsx`).** Adopted the **shared liquid-glass tokens** rather than reusing
  the `LiquidGlassModal` component (direct reuse would break this modal's app-level portal + ref-counted background
  isolation, which the portal tests pin). The dialog shell now uses `liquid-glass-modal-shell` (premium blurred glass,
  `border-radius: 28px`, soft glow edge, the shared `liquidModalEnter` open animation), with `liquid-glass-modal-header` /
  `-title` / `-close` for the header and a `liquid-glass-modal-content scrollbar-hide overflow-x-hidden` scroll body
  (hidden scrollbar like the other glass modals). The table-details card, covers stepper, notes, and footer use the glass
  text/border tokens (`liquid-glass-modal-text` / `-text-muted` / `-border`) instead of ad-hoc `isDark` white/black text.
  The two hand-drawn `M9 5l7 7-7 7` arrows are now lucide **`ChevronRight`**, the icon-only close button gained a localized
  `aria-label={t('common.actions.close')}`, and the cover steppers are consistent `h-11 w-11` centred touch targets. Added a
  `data-table-action-modal` marker. Tap feedback is `active:` only — no hover utilities, no native `title=`.
- **Preserved exactly (behaviour/data unchanged):** the app-level `createPortal(document.body)` + `z-[1200]` +
  `MODAL_VIEWPORT_ATTR`, `role="dialog"` / `aria-modal` / `aria-labelledby`, the topmost-dialog Escape effect, the shared
  `useBackgroundAccessibilityIsolation` hook + viewport marker, the `bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm` backdrop, all action
  callbacks (parent-owned, handlers still defer `onClose`), guest-count rules, statuses, and `table.notes` (rendered raw,
  never translated). Semantic colours kept: available green, reserved amber/yellow, occupied red, maintenance orange,
  unavailable neutral; New Order yellow/black, reservation amber, destructive cancel red, set-available emerald.
- **i18n.** No new keys — all copy uses existing `tableActionModal.*` (with `defaultValue` fallbacks) and the close label
  reuses `common.actions.close`, which is already translated in all five POS locales (el "Κλείσιμο", de "Schließen", fr
  "Fermer", it "Chiudi"). No hardcoded Greek/English UI copy.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/tables-action-modal-portal.test.ts`: the 10 existing portal/isolation/Escape/i18n guards still
  pass unchanged; **added** three Round 225 guards — (1) the shell adopts `liquid-glass-modal-shell` + header/title/close/
  content tokens + glass text/border tokens + the `data-table-action-modal` marker, and the old `bg-gray-900/60 … ring`
  shell is gone; (2) lucide `ChevronRight` is used and the manual `M9 5l7 7-7 7` arrow is gone, the close has the localized
  `aria-label`, the steppers are `h-11 w-11`, and there is no `hover:` / `title=`; (3) the semantic status + action colours
  are preserved.
- **Cleanup (supervisor review, pre-live-QA).** (a) Design rule — negative letter-spacing is forbidden: set
  `letter-spacing: 0;` on **both** `.liquid-glass-modal-title` selectors in `styles/glassmorphism.css` (were `-0.02em`;
  these are the only negative-tracking declarations in the file). (b) `TableActionModal.tsx` — re-indented the Action
  Buttons block + footer to match the new glass content nesting (pure formatting, no behaviour/markup change) and converted
  the two new comment em-dashes to plain ASCII `--`. **Added** a focused guard asserting no negative letter-spacing anywhere
  in the glass CSS and that every `.liquid-glass-modal-title` block zeroes its tracking.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/tables-action-modal-portal.test.ts tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`
  → **65 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek / light theme, 1282×802).** Dashboard → New Order → Table →
  TableSelector → `#TP03` opened TableActionModal. The modal now visually matches the shared glass family — rounded
  translucent blurred shell, soft edge/shadow, labelled close, no visible native scrollbar — with the semantic
  green/yellow/amber/red action colours preserved. The covers stepper touch targets worked (+, +, − moved the guest count
  1 → 2 and stayed visually stable). New Reservation handoff worked: it opened ReservationForm for Table `#TP03` with 2
  guests, then cancelled/closed without saving. New Order handoff worked: it opened in-store order entry with the table
  badge "Τραπέζι #TP03"; no order was submitted. Separate future follow-up noted (not in scope here): ReservationForm itself
  is still solid white versus the glass direction.

### Supervisor round 226 — ReservationForm glass/touch redesign (shared liquid-glass tokens) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA after Round 225).** Dashboard → New Order → Table → New Reservation opens `ReservationForm`, which
  still rendered as an opaque white/dark solid panel with **purple** focus/save styling, `hover:` effects, and a broken
  (mojibake) bullet glyph in the warning lists — visually breaking the now-glass table-reservation flow.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/tables/ReservationForm.tsx`).** Adopted the **shared liquid-glass tokens** (same token
  approach as TableActionModal round 225, because the form's `renderModalPortal` + topmost-dialog Escape are pinned by the
  existing tests, so direct `LiquidGlassModal` reuse is not viable). The dialog shell now uses `liquid-glass-modal-shell`
  (premium blurred translucent glass, 28px radius, soft edge/glow, the shared `liquidModalEnter` open animation) with
  `liquid-glass-modal-header` / `-title` / `-close` and a `liquid-glass-modal-content scrollbar-hide` scroll body (hidden
  scrollbar). All inputs/labels use the glass tokens (`liquid-glass-modal-input` — which focuses **amber**, not purple — and
  `liquid-glass-modal-text` / `-text-muted` / `-border`). The icon-only close gained a localized
  `aria-label={t('common.actions.close')}`; the guest steppers became `h-11 w-11` centred touch targets with `active:scale`
  feedback and lucide **`Minus`/`Plus`** (with `defaultValue`-fallback aria-labels); the footer **Cancel** button is now red
  (`border-red-500/50 bg-red-500/10 text-red-600`) and the **Create/Save** button green (`bg-green-600 active:bg-green-700`),
  both `min-h-[44px]`, replacing the old **purple** submit. The mojibake `•` bullet was removed — the warning lists now use
  ASCII-safe `list-disc` CSS markers (the file is now 100% ASCII). All `hover:` utilities were replaced with `active:`; no
  native `title=`.
- **Preserved exactly (behaviour/data unchanged):** create/edit mode + submit DTO shape, validation (`validateForm`,
  `validateCapacity`, `validatePhone`), conflict checks + warning, upcoming-reservation selection
  (`selectUpcomingTableReservations`), the shared table display label, reset/cancel, the topmost-dialog Escape effect (routes
  through `handleCancel`, never submits), `renderModalPortal` + `z-[1200]`, backdrop cancel, capacity guard, the per-field
  clear-on-edit (`clearFieldError`, still ≥3 for party size), and the constrained scrollable body (`max-h-[90vh]`,
  `flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-y-auto`, `border-t flex-shrink-0` footer). The semantic warning panels stay blue (info) / red
  (conflict). `useTheme`/`isDark` were dropped (the glass tokens + `dark:` variants handle theming).
- **i18n.** All visible copy uses existing `reservationForm.*` keys; the close aria-label reuses the already-translated
  `common.actions.close`. No hardcoded Greek/English UI copy.
- **Supervisor correction (i18n, narrow).** The two new guest-stepper accessibility labels must not rely on `defaultValue`
  fallbacks in a five-locale app. Added `reservationForm.decreaseGuests` and `reservationForm.increaseGuests` to **all five**
  base locales with real, guest-noun-consistent translations (en "Decrease guests" / "Increase guests"; el "Μείωση ατόμων" /
  "Αύξηση ατόμων"; de "Gäste verringern" / "Gäste erhöhen"; fr "Diminuer les convives" / "Augmenter les convives"; it "Riduci
  ospiti" / "Aumenta ospiti"), preserving JSON validity and key parity. The reservation-form tests were extended so the
  representative-Greek coverage list includes both keys and the source is verified to call them via `aria-label`.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/reservation-form-ui.test.ts`: the 18 existing portal/behaviour/i18n/Escape guards still pass
  unchanged; **added** five Round 226 guards — shared glass tokens + `data-reservation-form` marker (old opaque shell gone);
  no `hover:` / no `title=` / active feedback / localized close label; semantic cancel-red + create-green with **no purple**;
  `h-11 w-11` active steppers with lucide `Minus`/`Plus`; and no non-ASCII bullet (pure-ASCII source) with `list-disc`
  markers and preserved i18n warning copy.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/reservation-form-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/tables-action-modal-portal.test.ts
  tests/renderer/table-selector-ui.test.ts` → **46 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`);
  `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek locale).** Logged in with the test PIN; the yellow login background
  animation/orb is present. The Dashboard navbar/order tabs still keep inactive neutral icons/tabs with selected colored/neon
  accents. Dashboard → New Order → Table showed the floor selector (`Όλοι οι όροφοι` / `Όροφος 1`). TableSelector → `#TP03` →
  TableActionModal → New Reservation opened ReservationForm. In light/system mode the form is now a blurry translucent glass
  modal — soft rounded shell, hidden scrollbar, centred close/steppers/footer controls; **Cancel is red, Create is green, no
  purple visible**. Tapped guest `+` then `-`; the controls stayed stable and the count returned to 2; Cancel closed without
  creating a reservation. Switched temporarily to dark mode (Settings → Display & Sound), reopened the same ReservationForm,
  and verified it remains readable glass with red cancel / green create and no purple, then restored the theme to System. No
  reservation/order/data was created or saved during QA.

### Supervisor round 227 — Settings switch glass redesign — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** The Settings → Display & Sound (and every other Settings) toggle used the shared `switchTrackClass`,
  which rendered a generic grey-to-solid-yellow pill the user called ugly — flat native-grey OFF, plain all-yellow ON.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).** Redesigned the **single shared `switchTrackClass`**
  into a premium touchscreen glass switch (one const, reused by all ~9 Settings switches): a **~58×34 rounded glass pill**
  with `backdrop-blur-sm`; **OFF** is a recessed neutral glass track (`bg-gray-200/80` light / `dark:bg-white/10`, `border-
  black/10` + `shadow-inner`) — not a flat native grey; **ON** is a lively semantic **green** track with a soft green glow
  (`peer-checked:bg-green-500` + `peer-checked:shadow-green-500/40`), replacing the old `peer-checked:bg-yellow-400`; the
  knob is a clean white glass disc (`after:h-7 after:w-7 after:bg-white`, `after:shadow-md`) with clear travel
  (`peer-checked:after:translate-x-[24px]`, geometry 58 − 28 knob − 6 inset). Keyboard focus shows a small **amber** accent
  ring (`peer-focus-visible:ring-amber-400/60`, focus-visible so touch shows no ring) and tap shows an active scale
  (`peer-active:scale-95`). No hover utilities. Each switch's `<label>` wrapper gained `min-h-[44px] justify-center` (single
  `replace_all`, 8 labels) so the **touch target is ≥44px** while the visible pill stays ~34px.
- **Preserved exactly:** every switch's `sr-only peer` checkbox, `aria-labelledby` to its visible title, `checked`/`onChange`
  bindings and setters, settings keys, labels, persistence, and save flow are unchanged (no input/markup edits beyond the
  label wrapper sizing). No new role/aria added (native checkbox semantics kept). No locale/text changes. Order-taking and
  dashboard pages untouched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`: the existing terminal-section guard (aria-labelledby + setters + no
  hover/title) still passes; **added** a Round 227 guard that slices `switchTrackClass` and asserts the new glass/touch
  dimensions (`w-[58px] h-[34px]`, `backdrop-blur-sm`), the neutral glass OFF state, the green ON state + glow, that the old
  `peer-checked:bg-yellow-400` is gone, the white knob + travel, the amber focus-visible ring + `peer-active:scale-95`, no
  `hover:`/`group-hover:`/`title=`, and the `min-h-[44px]` label touch target around the shared `{switchTrackClass}` track.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **19 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek locale, Settings → Display & Sound).** Light/System mode OFF: both
  terminal switches now read as neutral recessed glass pills with clean white knobs, not flat native grey. Light/System mode
  ON: tapped Audio on — the switch became green with a soft glow and clear knob travel; tapped Audio off again before
  leaving. Dark mode OFF: the neutral dark glass track remains readable and aligned. Dark mode ON: tapped Audio on — the
  green on-state stayed readable against the dark glass; tapped Audio off again. Restored the theme to System after the
  dark-mode check. Did not press Save Terminal Preferences; no terminal preference changes were persisted/saved.

### Supervisor round 228 — Payment Terminal config modal footer/scroll/buttons/switch polish — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Settings → Μηχανές καρτών → Ρύθμιση → Προσθήκη Τερματικού opens the Add/Edit Payment Terminal modal
  (`components/ecr/TerminalConfigModal.tsx`). At 1282×802 its lower Settings section (Transaction Timeout + the three switch
  rows) read as clipped/hidden behind the fixed footer; the footer used a neutral-grey **Cancel** + amber **Add**; and the
  three ECR switches still used an old yellow-on `SWITCH_TRACK_CLASS` (pre-round-227 style).
- **Fix (UI only, `components/ecr/TerminalConfigModal.tsx`).** Three scoped changes:
  1. **Footer clearance.** Added `pb-6` to the `<form>` (inside `LiquidGlassModal`'s scrollable glass content, which keeps
     the footer `flex-shrink-0` below it) so the last fields/switch rows clear the pinned footer and stay fully reachable —
     no native scrollbar exposed (the glass content already uses `scrollbar-hide`).
  2. **Footer buttons.** **Cancel** is now a red semantic button (`bg-red-500/10 active:bg-red-500/20 text-red-600
     dark:text-red-300 border-red-500/40`), **Add/Save** a green semantic primary (`bg-green-600 active:bg-green-700
     text-white border-green-600 shadow-green-600/25`); both centred (`inline-flex items-center justify-center`),
     `disabled`/saving state preserved, `active:scale-[0.98]` only — no hover. Replaces the old grey Cancel + amber Add.
  3. **ECR switch.** `SWITCH_TRACK_CLASS` was replaced with the round-227 premium glass switch: ~58×34 glass pill, recessed
     neutral glass OFF (`bg-gray-200/80` / `dark:bg-white/10`), semantic **green** ON + glow (`peer-checked:bg-green-500` +
     `peer-checked:shadow-green-500/40`, replacing the `peer-checked:from-yellow-300 … to-yellow-500` gradient), clean white
     knob with clear travel (`peer-checked:after:translate-x-[24px]`), amber `peer-focus-visible` ring, `peer-active:scale-95`,
     no hover. The switch rows already wrap in `min-h-[44px]` labels.
- **Preserved exactly:** the save payload/`deviceConfig` shape, validation + toasts, the bridge `onSave` call, every switch's
