  native `sr-only peer` checkbox + `checked`/`onChange` setter + `id` + visible label, all `ecr.config.*`/`common.actions.*`
  i18n keys/text, the `LiquidGlassModal` glass shell + its `title` heading prop, and parent `PaymentTerminalsSection`
  behaviour. No ECR data-model/persistence/settings-key changes. Order-taking/dashboard pages untouched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts`: the existing title-prop test (and the TerminalCard tooltip
  tests) still pass; **added** a Round 228 guard asserting the old amber-Add/grey-Cancel footer classes are gone, the
  red-Cancel + green-primary semantics + centred buttons, no `hover:`, the old yellow `peer-checked` gradient is gone, the
  neutral-off/green-on glass switch (`w-[58px] h-[34px]`, `bg-gray-200/80`, `peer-checked:bg-green-500` + glow, white knob +
  travel, amber focus-visible ring, active scale), preserved switch ids/setters, the `<form … pb-6>` footer-clearance guard,
  and the retained `LiquidGlassModal` shell.
- **Live-QA correction (footer still bled through; masking glass shelf, UI only).** First-pass live QA: buttons/switches
  fixed and fields reachable by scrolling, but on first open the Settings/Transaction-Timeout content was still visible
  *through* the translucent footer (the footer wrapper had only `border-t` and no surface), so it still read as clipped.
  Fixed by turning the footer wrapper into an intentional **glass shelf that masks** the body behind it: added a semi-opaque
  neutral glass background (`bg-white/85 dark:bg-black/55`), `backdrop-blur-xl`, and an upward separating shadow
  (`shadow-[0_-8px_24px_rgba(0,0,0,0.18)]`) on top of the existing `border-t liquid-glass-modal-border`. Theme-safe neutral
  glass, no blue/purple, no hover; the footer stays fixed/stable with red Cancel + green Add/Save and the `pb-6` form
  clearance is kept. The Round 228 test guard was strengthened to assert the footer wrapper carries the masking shelf
  classes (semi-opaque white/black bg + `backdrop-blur-xl` + `border-t` + upward `shadow-[...]`), not just `pb-6`.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts` → **4 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek locale, Settings → Μηχανές καρτών → Ρύθμιση → Προσθήκη Τερματικού).**
  First-open pass after the correction: the footer now reads as a solid masking glass shelf — Settings/Transaction Timeout
  text no longer bleeds through the footer area. Scrolled to the bottom: Transaction Timeout and all three switch rows are
  reachable and sit above the footer. Footer actions are correctly semantic — Ακύρωση is red, Προσθήκη/Add is green (no save
  was pressed). The ECR switches use the premium neutral-off / green-on glass style with white knobs, and on/off states are
  readable. Closed the Add Terminal modal and restored the theme to System; no terminal data was created or saved.

### Supervisor round 229 — Payment Terminals refresh/add CTA polish (a11y + semantic colour) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Settings → Μηχανές καρτών → Ρύθμιση (`components/ecr/PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx`): the small refresh
  icon button near the green "Συνδεδεμένο" status was exposed in the accessibility tree as an **empty unnamed button** (bad
  for touch + a11y), and the bottom primary "+ Προσθήκη Τερματικού" CTA was still **yellow** (per the current rules,
  Add/Save/Confirm should be semantic green; yellow stays an accent/selection/status colour).
- **Fix (UI + a11y only, `components/ecr/PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx`).**
  1. **Refresh button.** Now a 44×44 centred glass icon button (`inline-flex h-11 w-11 items-center justify-center rounded-xl
     border liquid-glass-modal-border bg-white/5 dark:bg-black/20 … active:scale-95 active:bg-white/10`) with a **localized
     `aria-label={t('ecr.refreshStatus', 'Refresh connection status')}`** — no longer an unnamed icon-only button. No native
     `title` tooltip, no hover; active tap feedback. `handleRefresh` / `disabled`/`isRefreshing` spin behaviour unchanged.
  2. **Add Terminal CTA.** Changed from yellow to **semantic green** (`border-green-600 bg-green-600 text-white
     shadow-green-600/30 active:bg-green-700`, `min-h-[44px]`, centred `Plus` icon + label). The **Discover** button stays
     the neutral/secondary glass button. The Add Terminal *modal* footer (round 228, already red-Cancel/green-Add) is
     untouched.
- **i18n.** Added `ecr.refreshStatus` to **all five** POS locales with real translations (en "Refresh connection status";
  el "Ανανέωση κατάστασης σύνδεσης"; de "Verbindungsstatus aktualisieren"; fr "Actualiser l'état de la connexion"; it
  "Aggiorna stato connessione"). No other i18n keys/text changed.
- **Preserved exactly:** the refresh handler/behaviour, the discover/add/edit flows, ECR data model, save payload, bridge/API
  calls, persistence, and parent behaviour. Order-taking/dashboard pages untouched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts`: the existing TerminalCard/ConfigModal guards still pass;
  **added** a Round 229 guard asserting the section has no `title=`/`hover:`, the refresh button has the localized
  `ecr.refreshStatus` aria-label + `h-11 w-11` centred sizing + preserved `handleRefresh`/icon, the Add CTA is green
  (`bg-green-600`/`active:bg-green-700`, `min-h-[44px]` centred, no `bg-yellow`/`bg-amber`), and `ecr.refreshStatus` exists in
  all five locales (Greek a real translation).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts` → **5 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek locale, Settings → Μηχανές καρτών → Ρύθμιση).** Before the fix the
  refresh control was an empty unnamed button; after hot reload the accessibility tree exposes it as button "Ανανέωση
  κατάστασης σύνδεσης". The refresh control is now visibly a comfortable glass ~44px icon button, still next to the green
  Συνδεδεμένο status, and no native tooltip appeared. Bottom actions: Αναζήτηση remains neutral/secondary; Προσθήκη Τερματικού
  is semantic green with a centred plus icon and label. Dark-theme spot check also passed — the named refresh button remained
  present and the green Add Terminal CTA stayed readable against dark glass. Theme restored to System afterwards; no
  Search/Add/Refresh data-changing action was triggered and no terminal data was created/saved.

### Supervisor round 230 — Salon AppointmentsView touch-first toolbar/date controls (a11y) — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Greek locale, sidebar → Ραντεβού (`pages/verticals/salon/AppointmentsView.tsx`): the top refresh
  button and the main date previous/next arrows were exposed in the accessibility tree as **empty unnamed buttons**; the page
  had many **hover-only** Tailwind classes; and the New-Appointment modal customer-clear button used a native **title**
  tooltip — all conflicting with the touchscreen-first POS rule.
- **Fix (UI + a11y only, `pages/verticals/salon/AppointmentsView.tsx`).**
  1. **Localized accessible names** on the icon-only buttons: top refresh (`aria-label={t('common.refresh', 'Refresh')}`),
     date previous/next day (`appointments.previousDay` / `appointments.nextDay`), the modal close
     (`common.actions.close`), the calendar previous/next month (`appointments.modal.previousMonth` /
     `appointments.modal.nextMonth`), and the customer-clear button — whose native `title` was **replaced with an
     `aria-label`** (`appointments.modal.clearCustomer`).
  2. **Touch-safe controls:** each of those icon buttons is now `inline-flex h-11 w-11 items-center justify-center` (≈44px,
     centred) with `transition active:scale-95` tap feedback.
  3. **Removed every hover-only utility** in the file — all 16 `hover:*` classes were converted to `active:*` (a single
     `replace_all` after the icon buttons were restyled), and **no native `title=` remains** (verified: 0 `hover:`/`title=`).
- **i18n.** Reused existing localized keys where possible — `common.refresh`, `common.actions.close`, and the existing
  `appointments.modal.clearCustomer` — and added **4 new keys** to all five POS locales: `appointments.previousDay`,
  `appointments.nextDay`, `appointments.modal.previousMonth`, `appointments.modal.nextMonth` (real translations, e.g. el
  "Προηγούμενη ημέρα" / "Επόμενη ημέρα" / "Προηγούμενος μήνας" / "Επόμενος μήνας").
- **Preserved exactly:** `refetch`, `navigateDate`, modal close (`onClose`), customer clear (`onClearCustomer`), calendar
  month navigation (`setCalendarDate`), the create-appointment flow, status actions, and date selection — behaviour/markup
  otherwise unchanged. No appointment data created or edited. Order-taking/dashboard pages untouched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts`: the 11 existing i18n/portal/escape guards still pass; **added**
  two Round 230 guards — (1) no `hover:`/`title=` in the view, and each icon button (refresh, date prev/next, modal close,
  customer clear, calendar prev/next month) carries the expected localized `aria-label` + `h-11 w-11` centred sizing; (2) the
  new date/month-nav keys + the reused refresh/close keys exist in all five locales (Greek a real translation).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` → **13 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek locale, sidebar → Ραντεβού).** The toolbar now exposes the icon
  buttons by name in the accessibility tree: Ανανέωση (refresh), Προηγούμενη ημέρα / Επόμενη ημέρα (date prev/next). Opening
  New Appointment, the modal exposes Κλείσιμο (close) and Προηγούμενος μήνας / Επόμενος μήνας (calendar month nav). No
  hover-only effect or native tooltip appeared. The modal was closed without creating any appointment data.

### Supervisor round 231 — Standalone TablesPage direct floor selector — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Greek POS, sidebar → Τραπέζια opens the standalone `pages/TablesPage.tsx` (not `TablesDashboard`).
  The page showed table-section cards (Bar, Terrace, Dining Room) and status filters but **no visible floor selector** — and
  the filter popover only carried status filters. For a touchscreen POS, floor selection should be direct and obvious.
- **Fix (UI + filtering only, `pages/TablesPage.tsx`).** Added a visible, touch-first **floor selector strip** under the
  status stats row and above the table grid (`data-tables-floor-selector`), so it is not hidden in the filter popover. A
  module-level `getTableFloorValue(table)` derives the floor from real metadata via the shared convention
  (`table.floorLevel ?? floor_level ?? 1` → stable string); `floorOptions` is the unique set sorted numerically (numbers)
  then lexically (strings). New local `floorFilter` state (default `'all'`) drives the strip — **All floors** + one button
  per floor, each with `aria-pressed` — and is applied inside `filteredTables` so it **composes with the existing status +
  search filters** (used by both the grid and floor-plan views). `floorFilter` is included in `hasActiveFilters` and reset
  in `handleClearFilters`. Buttons are `min-h-[44px]` centred (`inline-flex items-center justify-center`) with `active:`
  tap feedback only — yellow = selected, neutral glass = rest; no hover utilities, no native `title=`.
- **i18n.** The supervisor-named `tablesDashboard.floor` / `allFloors` / `floorNumber` keys do **not** exist in the locale
  files (the whole `tablesDashboard` namespace is absent — TablesDashboard itself renders those via `defaultValue`
  fallback). To keep the change inside the listed file scope (no locale edits) while staying fully localized, the strip
  reuses the **existing, fully-translated** `tableSelector.floor` / `allFloors` / `floorNumber` keys (added in round 224,
  present in all five POS locales with `{{floor}}` interpolation). No hardcoded Greek. (The absent `tablesDashboard.floor*`
  localization remains a separate, pre-existing gap.)
- **Preserved exactly:** table status behaviour, order creation, reservations, payments, all table data writes, and the
  existing `TableSelector` / `TablesDashboard` selectors are untouched (floor filtering here is display-only on
  `filteredTables`). Order-taking/dashboard pages untouched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: **added** a Round 231 guard (the existing OrdersPage guards still
  pass) asserting the `getTableFloorValue` helper + `floorFilter` state, floor options derived from table floor data with
  numeric-then-lexical sort, floor filtering composed into `filteredTables` (with `floorFilter` in its deps) alongside
  status + search, `floorFilter` in `hasActiveFilters` + reset in `handleClearFilters`, the `data-tables-floor-selector`
  strip (All floors + per-floor buttons, `aria-pressed`, `min-h-[44px]` centred, `active:scale-95`, no `hover:`/`title=`),
  the localized `tableSelector.floor*` labels, and those labels existing in all five locales (Greek a real translation).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **52 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check -- src/renderer/pages/TablesPage.tsx tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts
  planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek locale, sidebar → Τραπέζια).** The standalone TablesPage now shows
  a direct floor selector row under the status cards and above the grid with **Όροφος**, **Όλοι οι όροφοι**, and **Όροφος 1**.
  Tapping Όροφος 1 keeps the operator on the page, applies UI filtering only, shows active-filter state, and does not open a
  modal or change table data; resetting to Όλοι οι όροφοι was verified. Source review confirmed the floor derivation,
  status/search composition, Clear-filters reset, `aria-pressed`, 44px centred touch buttons, no hover/native tooltip, and
  the existing EN/EL/DE/FR/IT `tableSelector` floor labels.

### Supervisor round 232 — NavigationSidebar module-scroll affordance — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, Greek POS 1282×802, many modules).** The left module rail scrolls with a **hidden scrollbar** (round
  223), so after moving between Tables/Menu/Dashboard a module like Παραγγελίες can sit **above the visible rail** until the
  operator swipes it. Not a routing bug — once scrolled, Παραγγελίες opens fine — but a **discoverability** problem: there is
  no visible hint that the icon list can scroll.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/NavigationSidebar.tsx`).** Added subtle **top/bottom scroll-affordance caps** to the
  module-scroll region only. The scroll region is now wrapped in a `relative` frame (`data-navigation-scroll-affordance`)
  hosting two decorative caps (`data-navigation-scroll-hint-top` / `-bottom`): a neutral black/white edge **fade**
  (`bg-gradient-to-b/t from-black|from-white … to-transparent`) plus a small **amber pill** (`bg-amber-400/80` with a soft
  amber glow). New `canScrollUp` / `canScrollDown` state, computed by a scroll-listener effect from the container geometry
  (`scrollTop`, `scrollHeight`, `clientHeight`, 4px threshold): the **top** cap shows only when the list can scroll up, the
  **bottom** cap only when it can scroll down. The effect listens to `'scroll'` (passive) and re-runs on `currentView`,
  `orderedNavigationModules` (module list/order), and `availableHeight` changes — every trigger the supervisor named. The
  caps are **`pointer-events-none` + `aria-hidden="true"`** (never block taps, never pollute a11y) and add **no visible
  text**. Palette stays white/black/grey/yellow with the approved amber accent — no blue/purple/orange in the caps.
- **Preserved exactly:** module order persistence, drag/reorder, route guards, inactive-neutral / active-neon icon colours,
  labels, and the pinned Check In (top) / Z Report + Settings + Logout (bottom, outside the module scroll) are all unchanged
  — the caps live only inside the module-scroll frame. No native `title=` and no `hover:` anywhere in the file.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts`: the 9 existing guards (incl. the round-223 region/utility
  guards) still pass; **added** a Round 232 guard asserting the `canScrollUp`/`canScrollDown` state, the scroll-listener
  effect (`addEventListener('scroll', …, { passive: true })` + the up/down threshold setters + the
  `[currentView, orderedNavigationModules, availableHeight]` deps), the `data-navigation-scroll-affordance` frame + both
  conditional `data-navigation-scroll-hint-*` caps, that each cap is `pointer-events-none` + `aria-hidden` with only an amber
  pill (no blue/purple/orange, no hover/title, no text), the whole-file no-`title=`/no-`hover:` invariants, and that the
  utility cluster stays outside the frame with no scroll-hint markers.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` → **10 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running Greek POS, 1282×802, dark/system + light themes).** Dark/system: the
  Dashboard/Suppliers sidebar showed the bottom amber pill/fade when more modules existed below; after scrolling the module
  rail down, the top amber pill/fade appeared (content above) while the bottom hint remained (content below). The scroll
  hints do **not** appear in the accessibility tree (aria-hidden works); inactive icons stayed neutral grey/black with only
  the active module in neon; Check In / Z Report / Settings / Logout stayed pinned. Tap safety verified — with the hints
  visible, tapping a visible module opened Διαχείριση Προμηθευτών / Προμηθευτές normally, so the caps did not block sidebar
  interaction. Light theme (Settings → Οθόνη & Ήχος → Φωτεινό, temporary): the rail showed the light neutral white fade +
  amber pill, inactive icons stayed black, the active module stayed coloured; the previous Σύστημα theme was restored
  afterward. Evidence screenshots saved under `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-232/` (05, 06, 07, 08, 09).

### Supervisor round 233 — MenuManagementPage refresh glass button — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Greek/dark POS, sidebar → Μενού (`pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx`). The top-right refresh button was
  a **stark white square in dark mode** (`dark: bg-white text-black` / `light: bg-black text-white` inversion) — it read like
  a default web/admin button and broke the POS glass language. Evidence:
  `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-233/01-menu-management-refresh-before.png`.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx`).** Redesigned **only** the top-right refresh icon button into a touch-first
  glass icon button: `inline-flex h-12 w-12 items-center justify-center rounded-2xl border backdrop-blur-xl … shadow-amber-
  500/10` with a translucent surface + amber accent in both themes — dark `border-amber-400/30 bg-white/10 text-amber-300
  active:bg-white/20`, light `border-amber-400/40 bg-black/5 text-amber-600 active:bg-black/10` — replacing the opaque
  white/black inversion. The `RefreshCw` icon stays centred and `animate-spin`s while loading; the disabled/loading state is
  visible (`loading ? 'opacity-60 cursor-not-allowed' : 'active:scale-95'`). 48×48 target, no hover utilities, no native
  `title=`, active tap feedback only.
- **Preserved exactly:** the refresh behaviour (`onClick={loadData}`, `disabled={loading}`), the `aria-label={refreshLabel}`
  accessible name, the loading spin, the tabs (selected yellow / inactive grey-neutral), search, cards, category/product/
  ingredient/offer logic, data loading, translations, and module navigation. Sidebar icon colouring, order tabs, login
  background, and order-taking flow were not touched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts`: the existing guards still pass; the refresh-button assertions
  in the "icon-only header action" test were **updated** to lock down the new glass skin — 48×48 icon-centred `rounded-2xl`,
  `backdrop-blur-xl`, the dark + light amber-accent translucent skins — and to assert the old opaque `bg-white text-black` /
  `bg-black text-white` inversion is **gone**, with no `hover:` and no `title={refreshLabel}`, while keeping the
  `animate-spin`/`aria-label` behaviour.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` → **9 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check -- src/renderer/pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx
  tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek).** Dark/system: the refresh control is now a subtle amber glass
  icon button (no white square), icon centred, accessible name "Ανανέωση μενού" —
  `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-233/02-menu-management-refresh-dark-after.png`. Light theme: a quiet translucent
  amber-accent control, not a heavy black square —
  `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-233/03-menu-management-refresh-light-after.png`. Theme restored to System afterward (app
  returned to dark/system) — `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-233/04-menu-management-restored-system-after.png`. Static
  checks were independently re-run by Codex: `node --test tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` = 9 pass / 0 fail;
  `npm run type-check` = clean; `git diff --check` over the 3 touched files = clean.

### Supervisor round 234 — MenuManagementPage availability toggle touch targets — COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Greek/dark POS, sidebar → Μενού (`pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx`). The per-card availability controls
  (categories / menu items / ingredients / offers) are reachable as "Απενεργοποίηση" buttons, but visually they were tiny
  **borderless green eye glyphs** (`p-2 rounded-lg` text-only) on the right of each card — on a touchscreen they read like an
  icon decoration rather than a reliable 44px action target. Evidence:
  `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-234/01-menu-availability-icon-buttons-before.png`.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx`).** Added a shared `getAvailabilityToggleClass(active)` helper and pointed all
  four toggle call-sites at it. Each toggle is now an explicit ~44×44 glass touch target:
  `inline-flex h-11 w-11 items-center justify-center rounded-2xl border backdrop-blur-md … disabled:opacity-50
  disabled:cursor-not-allowed active:scale-95`, with semantic tinted-glass colours — enabled `border-green-500/40
  bg-green-500/15 text-green-500 active:bg-green-500/25`, disabled `border-red-500/40 bg-red-500/15 text-red-500
  active:bg-red-500/25`. Subtle active/tap feedback only; no hover utilities and no native `title=`.
- **Preserved exactly:** the toggle handlers (`toggleCategoryAvailability` / `toggleMenuItemAvailability` /
  `toggleIngredientAvailability` / `toggleComboAvailability`), the offline-disabled behaviour (`disabled={toggleAction.disabled}`),
  the `aria-label` accessible names, the `Eye`/`EyeOff` icons, the grid card layout, the tabs, the Round 233 refresh button,
  data loading, translations, and the availability logic. Sidebar, order tabs, login, order-taking, menu data, and locale files
  were not touched.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts`: added a focused guard asserting the four call-sites use the
  shared helper, the 44px centred `rounded-2xl` `backdrop-blur-md` glass surface with `active:scale-95` + disabled state, the
  semantic green/red tinted-glass colours, and that the old `p-2 rounded-lg …` / `text-green-500 active:bg-green-500/10` /
  `text-red-500 active:bg-red-500/10` text-only styling is **gone**, with no `hover:` / `title={` and the `Eye`/`EyeOff` +
  `toggleAction.disabled` behaviour intact.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` → **10 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check -- src/renderer/pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx
  tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator, running POS, Greek).** Dark/system: the availability eye controls are now explicit
  green glass touch buttons with centred icons (no tiny borderless glyphs); accessible names still read "Απενεργοποίηση" —
  `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-234/02-menu-availability-icon-buttons-dark-after.png`. Light theme: the same controls
  remain clear green glass touch buttons on the pale cards —
  `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-234/03-menu-availability-icon-buttons-light-after.png`. Theme restored to System afterward
  (app returned to dark/system) — `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-234/04-menu-availability-restored-system-after.png`. Static
  checks were independently re-run by Codex: `node --test tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` = 10 pass / 0 fail;
  `npm run type-check` = clean; `git diff --check` over the 3 touched files = clean.

### Supervisor round 235 — MenuManagementPage offers empty state — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Greek/dark POS, Menu Management → Offers. When there were no offers/combos (none synced, or the
  search filtered them all out), the page rendered the header/search/tabs and then a **huge empty black area** — it felt
  unfinished and confusing. Evidence: `planning/ui-audit-captures/round-235/01-menu-offers-empty-before.png`.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx`).** When `activeTab === 'combos'` and `filteredCombos.length === 0`,
  `renderCombos()` now returns a small **centered glass empty state** (`data-menu-offers-empty`) instead of an empty grid:
  a compact `max-w-md` card, `rounded-2xl border … backdrop-blur-md`, neutral black/white/grey base with an amber-accented
  `BadgePercent` (lucide) icon badge — no giant nested card, no emoji/SVG/ASCII, no hover utilities, no native `title=`. It
  distinguishes the two cases via `isSearching = searchTerm.trim().length > 0`:
  - **search active:** title `menu.offersEmpty.searchTitle` ("No offers match the search"), subtitle
    `menu.offersEmpty.searchSubtitle` ("Clear the search or try another term"), plus a touch-first
    (`min-h-[44px]`, `active:scale-95`) clear-search button whose `onClick` only calls `setSearchTerm('')`.
  - **no search:** title `menu.offersEmpty.title` ("No offers configured"), subtitle `menu.offersEmpty.subtitle`
    ("Create offers in the admin dashboard or refresh after syncing.") — no data-creation action.
- **i18n.** Added a `menu.offersEmpty` object (`title` / `subtitle` / `searchTitle` / `searchSubtitle` / `clearSearch`) to all
  five POS locales — `en`, `el`, `de`, `fr`, `it` — with real translations (no English-only fallbacks as the sole source).
- **Preserved exactly:** all loading/data/toggle logic, the tabs, search, refresh button (Round 233), availability toggles
  (Round 234), and order/dashboard/login/nav. No offer data is created, deleted, refreshed, or mutated by the empty state.
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts`: added two guards — (1) the empty-state branch
  (`filteredCombos.length === 0` + `isSearching`), the `data-menu-offers-empty` region's centered glass styling, the
  `BadgePercent` import/use, the search vs no-search copy keys, the `setSearchTerm('')`-only clear action, and no `hover:` /
  `title=` inside the region; (2) that `menu.offersEmpty.*` exists and is a non-empty, non-placeholder string in all five
  locales.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts` → **12 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check`
  → **clean** (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check -- src/renderer/pages/MenuManagementPage.tsx
  tests/renderer/menu-management-page-ui.test.ts src/locales/en.json src/locales/el.json src/locales/de.json
  src/locales/fr.json src/locales/it.json planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), 1282×802, Greek/dark.** Verified on the running POS, Menu Management →
  Offers: the Offers tab is reachable from Menu Management; the empty state is **centered in the content area** as a **dark glass
  card** with an **amber/yellow icon**, a clear Greek heading **"Δεν έχουν ρυθμιστεί προσφορές"** and helper copy; the search
  field and tab strip keep the existing **yellow/neutral** theme; **no native scrollbar**, **no hover-only affordance**, and **no
  data mutation**.

### Supervisor round 236 — Order-taking hub IA migration (tables/rooms/services) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Direction (founder).** Tables already live in the order-taking/dashboard grid tab, so the standalone Tables nav/page is no
  longer a primary surface. Rooms and Services must also be incorporated into the order-taking page, and New Order / order type
  must include Rooms and Services. Rooms flow supports **Room Order**, **Check-in**, and **Create Reservation**.
- **Order tabs hub (`components/OrderTabsBar.tsx`).** Extended `TabId` with `rooms`/`services`, added `showRoomsTab` /
  `showServicesTab` props and `orderCounts.rooms`/`.services`. Inactive tabs stay neutral grey; the new active tabs use
  distinct accents that fit the existing active-tab exception (rooms purple `#a855f7`, services teal `#14b8a6`). Centered
  text, no hover/title. `components/BulkActionsBar.tsx` now types `activeTab` as the shared `TabId`; non-order hub tabs fall
  through to `[]` actions so selection/actions don't break.
- **OrderDashboard integration (`components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Uses `hasRoomsModule` and
  `hasAppointmentsModule`/`hasServiceCatalogModule` from `useAcquiredModules` (`hasServicesModule = appointments ||
  service_catalog`). Added Rooms and Services tab branches beside Tables, rendering the **embedded** `RoomsView` and
  `AppointmentsView` inside a bordered card so only the content area scrolls. Rooms tab count comes from a light
  `useRooms({ enableRealtime: false })` snapshot (`occupiedRooms + reservedRooms`), blanked (→ 0, stable) when the module or
  org/branch context is missing; services count is `0` (no heavy duplicate fetch just for a badge).
- **New Order modal cards.** Added module-gated **Room** (purple, `BedDouble`) and **Service** (teal, `CalendarClock`) cards to
  the existing glass order-type modal; pickup/delivery/table cards and flows are unchanged. The modal width + grid now adapt to
  the visible card count (`!max-w-lg…!max-w-5xl`, `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3` for 4–5 cards) so 4–5 cards never
  cramp. New cards get localized titles/descriptions and aria-labels via the same `composeOrderTypeAriaLabel` helper.
- **Room flow modal.** The Room card opens a small glass chooser with **Room Order** / **Check-in** / **Create Reservation**.
  - **Room Order** opens a room selector limited to **occupied** rooms; a room with an active folio sets `selectedOrderType`/
    `orderType` to `dine-in` (menu pricing follows the table/dine-in branch), sets `customerInfo` to a localized room/guest
    label, sets a new `roomChargeContext` state `{ roomId, roomNumber, guestName, activeFolioId }`, and opens `MenuModal` with
    that context — **reusing the existing room-charge payment path** (`MenuModal`/`PaymentModal` already support it; no second
    cart/payment stack). Occupied rooms **without** an active folio are disabled with a "No active folio" note instead of
    creating a broken order. `roomChargeContext` is cleared on `handleMenuModalClose` so it can't leak to a later order.
  - **Check-in** switches to the Rooms tab in a `checkin` preset (reserved rooms first; a room tap opens the existing check-in
    path). **Create Reservation** switches to the Rooms tab in a `reservation` preset (available rooms first; a room tap opens
    the existing reservation path). Implemented as small `RoomsView` props (`embedded`, `hubPreset`, `hubPresetSignal`) rather
    than duplicating hotel logic in OrderDashboard.
- **Services flow.** New Order → Services switches to the Services tab and opens the existing Create Appointment modal via a new
  `AppointmentsView` `openCreateSignal` prop (`embedded` hides the internal FAB). The existing staff/service/day/time
  availability check inside `handleCreateAppointment` is untouched (not bypassed or duplicated).
- **Hidden nav (`components/NavigationSidebar.tsx`).** A module-scope `HUB_MIGRATED_NAV_IDS`
  (`tables`/`rooms`/`appointments`/`services`/`service_catalog`) filters `navigationModules` before everything downstream, so
  those entries are hidden from the primary rail. The underlying page components, routes, and route guards are untouched and
  remain reachable for direct/internal rendering and tests.
- **i18n.** Added `dashboard.tabs.rooms`/`.services` and an `orderFlow.*` set (room/service card titles + descriptions, the
  three room-flow options + descriptions, room-order selector strings, and `{{room}}`/`{{guest}}` room labels) to all five POS
  locales — `en`, `el`, `de`, `fr`, `it` — with real translations (no English-only UI).
- **Scope guard honoured.** No DB/API schema changes. Existing table order flow, delivery/pickup flow, login, dashboard order
  grid, menu checkout, and payment behaviour are unchanged except for passing `roomChargeContext` where needed. Not committed.
- **Tests.** Extended `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (adaptive width/grid; Room/Service cards + handlers;
  `roomChargeContext` → MenuModal; embedded RoomsView/AppointmentsView branches + room-flow region no hover/native-title),
  `rooms-view-i18n.test.ts` (embedded + hubPreset/hubPresetSignal + hubMode room-tap routing + FAB hidden when embedded),
  `appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` (`openCreateSignal` opens Create; availability path preserved; FAB `!embedded`-gated),
  `navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` (`HUB_MIGRATED_NAV_IDS` + filtered `navigationModules`), and `pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts`
  (all new keys present, non-empty, translated across the five locales, with placeholders intact).
- **Follow-up fixes (supervisor review, before approval).** Two room-order blockers fixed:
  1. **Payment was bypassed for room orders.** `MenuModal` auto-submits a pending table payment for any
     `orderType='dine-in'` via `shouldBypassPaymentForTableOrder`. Room orders deliberately use `dine-in` for pricing, so they
     were being silently completed without ever opening `PaymentModal` — the `room_charge` tender could never appear. Added a
     scoped `hasRoomCharge` exception to the helper (`src/renderer/utils/tableOrderFlow.ts`): when set it returns `false`
     (never bypass). `MenuModal` passes `hasRoomCharge: Boolean(roomChargeContext?.roomId && roomChargeContext?.activeFolioId)`,
     so a room-charge dine-in order now reaches `PaymentModal` (which already renders the room_charge tender). Normal table
     behaviour is unchanged when `roomChargeContext` is absent.
  2. **Stale table state could leak into a room order.** `handleRoomOrderRoomSelect` now explicitly clears
     `setSelectedTable(null)`, `setTableNumber("")`, and `setTableGuestCount(1)` before setting `roomChargeContext` / opening
     the menu, so a prior table flow can't leak `table_number`/`table_id`/`table_session` into the room-charge order.
  Added focused tests (in `orders-page-ui.test.ts`): the bypass helper short-circuits on `hasRoomCharge` while normal dine-in/
  table orders still bypass; `MenuModal` passes the folio-derived `hasRoomCharge` and still exposes the `PaymentModal` path
  with `roomChargeContext`; and the room-order handler clears the three table fields before `setRoomChargeContext`.
- **Follow-up fixes #2 (live POS QA — Rooms hub).** Two embedded-RoomsView issues fixed (`pages/verticals/hotel/RoomsView.tsx`):
  1. **Native scrollbar in the embedded rooms grid.** The grid keeps `overflow-y-auto` (scroll preserved) but now adds
     `scrollbar-hide` when `embedded`, so the native scrollbar no longer shows inside the hub card while scrolling still works.
  2. **Hub preset was sticky.** `hubMode` is now **one-shot**: `handleRoomClick` clears it (`setHubMode(null)`) right after a
     room tap consumes the check-in/reservation route, and a new `handleManualStatusFilterChange` clears it when staff manually
     change the status filter (the preset effect still sets `statusFilter` directly, so it does not self-clear). Both
     `StatusFilterButtons` groups now use the wrapped handler. After consumption/manual change, taps return to the normal
     action sheet.
  Added a focused test (in `rooms-view-i18n.test.ts`): the embedded grid uses `overflow-y-auto … scrollbar-hide`; both
  `handleRoomClick` routes call `setHubMode(null)` before opening the flow; `handleManualStatusFilterChange` clears `hubMode`
  and both filter groups use it (no raw `setStatusFilter` wiring left); and the preset effect still sets `statusFilter` directly.
- Verified (static): `node --test` over the five named files → **115 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**
  (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over all touched files → clean. (The full renderer suite has pre-existing,
  unrelated `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`/deleted-component failures on this WIP branch — e.g. `i18nLabels`, `CustomTitleBar` — none
  introduced by this round.)
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator).** Live POS, dark/Greek, 1282×802: New Order showed the order-type cards for
  Pickup/Table/Room/Service according to the org's acquired modules; the Services tab/card opened the New Appointment modal; the
  Rooms/Services tabs were present because the org owns those modules; module gating verified as source-of-truth via
  `useAcquiredModules`. (Round 236's Check-in/Create Reservation behaviour — switching to the Rooms tab — is **superseded by
  Round 237/238**, verified there.)

### Supervisor round 237 — New Order room flows stay in focused modals (no Rooms-page navigation) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Round 236's **New Order → Room → Check-in** and **→ Create Reservation** switched the dashboard to the
  Rooms hub **tab** and armed a preset there — i.e. they dumped staff onto the Rooms page to "go find the room". The user
  rejected this: the room flows must stay inside focused order-type modals.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Check-in and Create Reservation now open a **focused workflow modal**
  (a `LiquidGlassModal`, `showRoomWorkflowModal`) instead of `setActiveTab("rooms")`. The modal hosts the embedded
  `RoomsView` armed with the existing preset (`checkin` → reserved rooms first, `reservation` → available rooms first), titled
  by the preset; tapping a room opens the **existing** check-in / reservation form (RoomsView's hubPreset routing, already
  one-shot from Round 236 follow-up). Staff never leave the order-taking view.
  - **Room Order** is unchanged: its selector lists occupied rooms with an active folio and immediately opens `MenuModal`
    (dine-in pricing + `roomChargeContext`), with a clear empty state and no navigation when there are no eligible rooms.
  - The **Rooms tab is now browse-only** (`<RoomsView embedded />`, no preset) per the brief — the preset-armed instance lives
    only in the workflow modal, so browsing the tab can no longer inherit a stale New-Order preset.
- **Preserved:** glass/blur styling, touch-first controls, no hover-only effects; all existing room/locale keys (the modal
  titles reuse `orderFlow.roomFlowCheckin` / `orderFlow.roomFlowReservation`, already in EN/EL/DE/FR/IT); table/delivery/
  pickup/checkout/payment behaviour; the Round 236 room-charge + stale-table fixes.
- **Tests.** Updated the Round 236 guards (handlers no longer `setActiveTab`; tab is browse-only) and added a Round 237 test in
  `orders-page-ui.test.ts`: neither check-in nor reservation calls `setActiveTab`; both open `showRoomWorkflowModal`; the
  workflow modal hosts the preset-armed `RoomsView` titled by preset with no hover/native-title; the Rooms tab is a browse-only
  embedded `RoomsView`; and Room Order still opens `MenuModal` with `roomChargeContext` + an empty state.
- Verified (static): `node --test` over the five named files → **116 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**
  (`tsc --noEmit`); `git diff --check` over touched files → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator).** Live POS verified: New Order → Room opened the three-card Room chooser;
  Room Order stayed in a focused selector and showed a clear empty state requiring a checked-in room + active folio; Check-in
  stayed in a focused reserved-room selector and showed the empty reserved-room state; no navigation to the old Rooms page.

### Supervisor round 238 — New Order check-in / reservation use focused selector MODULES (no RoomsView shell) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA).** Round 237 still hosted the **full `RoomsView`** (stats + search + status/floor filters + grid) inside
  the workflow modal, armed with a `hubPreset`. The user rejected this: dumping the whole Rooms hub into a modal is not a
  focused order-taking flow. Check-in and Create Reservation must be **purpose-built selector modules**.
- **Fix (UI only).** New self-contained module `components/modals/RoomStayWorkflowModals.tsx` (NOT `RoomsView`, NO `hubPreset`):
  - `RoomStaySelectorModal` — a compact `LiquidGlassModal` listing **only the eligible rooms**: `variant="checkin"` → reserved
    rooms, `variant="reservation"` → available rooms (both by `getRoomEffectiveStatus`, matching the grid cards). Clear,
    localized empty state; no stats/search/filter/floor hub chrome.
  - `RoomCheckinModal` / `RoomReservationModal` — the check-in / reservation form for the **chosen** room (shown as a room
    chip, no room dropdown). They reuse the existing `roomsView.*` field/toast keys and call the **same** backend services as
    the Rooms-tab forms (`/pos/rooms/:id/checkin` + offline queue + guest-billing/reservation fallback; `reservationsService`),
    so behaviour stays at parity with `RoomsView` (the sibling that still owns the standalone Rooms page, untouched).
  - `OrderDashboard.tsx`: `handleRoomFlowCheckin` / `handleRoomFlowReservation` now open the focused selectors
    (`showRoomCheckinSelector` / `showRoomReservationSelector`); the rejected `roomsHubPreset` / `roomsHubPresetSignal` /
    `showRoomWorkflowModal` state and the embedded-`RoomsView` workflow modal are **deleted**. Selecting a room mounts the
    matching form. Room status + selector lists stay in sync via the dashboard's existing `useRooms` (`updateStatus` +
    `refetch`).
- **Room Order is unchanged** (occupied rooms with an active folio → `MenuModal` with `roomChargeContext`, with its empty state).
  **The Rooms tab stays browse-only** (`<RoomsView embedded />`, no preset) per the brief.
- **Preserved:** glass/blur (via `LiquidGlassModal` portal), touch-first controls (`active:scale-95`, no hover-only effects),
  EN/EL/DE/IT/FR keys (4 new `orderFlow.roomCheckin*/roomReservation*` selector strings + the reused `roomsView.*` form keys).
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: replaced the Round 237 guards with **Round 238** guards — the workflow
  modal **never** renders `<RoomsView … hubPreset>` (the only `RoomsView` left is the browse-only tab); check-in lists reserved
  rooms and reservation lists available rooms via the focused modules; the module is self-contained (no `RoomsView` import /
  `<RoomsView>` / `hubPreset=` prop, no hub chrome, touch-first), reuses the existing check-in/reservation i18n + service paths;
  and the new selector titles/empty states exist (Greek translated) in every locale.
- Verified (static): focused `node --test` over the 4 affected files (`orders-page-ui`, `rooms-view-i18n`,
  `vertical-modal-portals`, `table-order-flow`) → **136 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run
  locale:parity` → **passed** (0 missing/extra in all 5 locales); `git diff --check` → clean. (The full suite has 31 reds that
  pre-date this change — the in-flight UI-palette refactor + the deleted `CustomTitleBar.tsx`; none touch room workflows.)
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator).** Live POS, dark/Greek, 1282×802: Create Reservation showed available rooms
  in a compact glass selector with floor chips (All / Floor 1 / Floor 2 / Floor 3); Floor 2 filtered to rooms 201/202/203;
  selecting room 201 opened the glass New Reservation form with a red Cancel and a green (disabled-until-valid) Create — no
  RoomsView shell. Check-in (reserved-only) and Room Order behaved as specified.

#### Round 238 follow-up (live QA on the room-picker screenshots) — floor filter, hidden scrollbars, clearer empty state

- **Floor selector on every room picker.** New shared `RoomFloorChips` (in `RoomStayWorkflowModals.tsx`) + `deriveRoomFloors`
  helper — a compact, touch-first segmented control of **All Floors + each actual floor** (localized via the existing
  `roomsView.allFloors` / `roomsView.floor` keys, no new strings). It filters the displayed room cards in all three pickers:
  Room Order (occupied/open-folio, dashboard-owned `roomOrderFloor` state), Check-in (reserved) and Create Reservation
  (available) (module-owned `floorFilter`, reset to "all" on each reopen). The control hides itself when there are 0–1 floors
  (nothing to filter). A floor that hides every card shows a neutral `roomsView.noRooms` state with the chips still visible.
- **No more native white scrollbars.** Every scrollable room grid is `overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide` (scroll preserved, rail
  hidden — the same policy the embedded RoomsView/table floor plan already use; `scrollbar-hide` is defined in `index.css` and
  covers WebView2/Firefox/IE). The horizontal floor-chip row is `overflow-x-auto scrollbar-hide` too. (`LiquidGlassModal`'s own
  content wrapper was already `scrollbar-hide`; the visible rail came from the inner grid containers, now fixed.)
- **Clearer Room Order empty state.** `orderFlow.roomOrderEmpty` reworded ("…with an open folio **yet**") plus a new secondary
  `orderFlow.roomOrderEmptyHint` — "A room charge needs a checked-in guest with an active folio. Use Check-in first to open
  one." (EN/EL/DE/IT/FR). This explains why the test org's list is empty (no checked-in room with an open folio).
- **Preserved:** no RoomsView/hubPreset in the workflow; glass/blur; no hover-only effects; no native `title=` tooltips on
  buttons; touch-first `active:scale-95`; all five locales (locale parity passes, 0 missing/extra).
- **Tests** (`orders-page-ui.test.ts`): added Round 238 follow-up guards — every picker renders `RoomFloorChips` + filters by
  `room.floor`; the floor control hides under 2 floors and uses the existing floor keys; all room scroll grids carry
  `scrollbar-hide` (and no naked `overflow-y-auto sm:grid-cols-2` rail survives); the floor-chip row hides its rail; the Room
  Order empty state renders both the primary copy and the Check-in hint, present + Greek-translated in every locale.
- Verified (static): focused `node --test` (4 files) → **139 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run
  locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` → clean. (Full-suite reds remain pre-existing palette/CustomTitleBar work,
  untouched here.)
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator).** Live-verified on a multi-floor hotel POS: each room picker showed the floor
  chips and tapping a floor filtered the cards (Floor 2 → rooms 201/202/203); the modals scrolled with **no white rail**; Room
  Order's empty state explained the folio requirement and pointed to Check-in.

#### Round 238 follow-up #2 (supervisor code-quality correction) — cancel=red / confirm=green + JSX tidy

- **Button semantics in `RoomStayWorkflowModals.tsx`.** The Cancel buttons in `RoomCheckinModal` + `RoomReservationModal` were
  neutral `bg-white/10`; per the app rule (cancel = red) they now use a tasteful **glass red** treatment
  (`border-red-400/40 bg-red-500/15 text-red-300`, `active:scale-95 active:bg-red-500/25`, `disabled:opacity-50`). The
  **Create Reservation** submit was purple; confirm/save/create must be green, so it is now **emerald** (`bg-emerald-600`),
  matching the already-green Complete Check-in submit.
- **JSX tidy (`OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Re-indented the `visibleRoomOrderRooms.map(...)` block that was left visibly
  under-indented after the floor-filter wrapper was added — whitespace only, no behaviour change.
- **Tests.** Added a Round 238 guard in `orders-page-ui.test.ts`: both Cancel buttons use the red glass treatment (old neutral
  fill gone), both submits are emerald (no `bg-purple-500` submit), and the cancel/submit handlers stay wired
  (`onClose` / `handleCheckin` / `handleReservation`).
- Verified (static): focused `node --test` (4 files) → **140 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run
  locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` → clean. **LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED** (with Round 238 above).

### Supervisor round 239 — New Order verticals are module-gated (source-of-truth, not just visual) — LIVE QA PASSED

- **Context.** The explicit visual regressions were already restored (login lava-lamp orb, neutral inactive nav icons / tabs,
  the table/chairs `TableOrderIcon`). This round hardens **module availability** as the source of truth for the New Order
  verticals so a stale/out-of-band tab or chooser option can never surface a disabled vertical.
- **Room workflow chooser (`OrderDashboard.tsx`).** New derived gates `hasOrdersModule = hasModule(MODULE_IDS.ORDERS)` and
  `hasReservationsModule = hasModule(MODULE_IDS.RESERVATIONS)`. In the Room flow chooser: **Room Order** renders only under
  `hasOrdersModule` (it charges an order to a folio), **Create Reservation** only under `hasReservationsModule`, and
  **Check-in** stays unconditional inside the (already Rooms-gated) chooser. The chooser is a single-column grid, so 1–3
  options stay centered with no dead space.
- **Tab content + selection hardening (`OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Each content branch now requires its module —
  `activeTab === "tables" && hasTablesModule`, `… "rooms" && hasRoomsModule`, `… "services" && hasServicesModule` — falling
  through to the always-available Orders grid otherwise. `handleTabChange` ignores taps on module tabs whose module is not
  acquired (and gains those flags as deps), and a new effect resets `activeTab` back to `"orders"` if the active vertical's
  module becomes unavailable mid-session. The **Delivered** tab (gated by `showDeliveredTab={hasDeliveryModule}`) is hardened
  the same way — `handleTabChange` ignores `"delivered"` without the Delivery module and the reset effect snaps a
  module-revoked Delivered tab back to Orders.
- **Legacy `forms/OrderTypeSelector.tsx`.** It always seeded dine-in; now **dine-in** is gated by `hasTablesModule`, **pickup**
  is always available, **delivery** stays under `hasDeliveryModule`. Grid columns adapt to 1/2/3 options; the supplied
  `TableOrderIcon`/`PickupOrderIcon` are unchanged. (Component is legacy — only re-exported, no active consumer — so gating is
  safe.)
- **Preserved (explicitly not touched):** the login orb animation, neutral inactive nav icons/tabs, the `TableOrderIcon`; no
  hover effects or native `title=` tooltips; no new i18n keys (all gating reuses existing strings).
- **Tests** (`orders-page-ui.test.ts`): added Round 239 guards — Room Order/Create Reservation wrapped in the
  Orders/Reservations gates while Check-in is unconditional; the three content branches require their module and the bare
  conditions are gone; `handleTabChange` rejects unavailable module tabs and the reset-to-orders effect exists; and
  `OrderTypeSelector` gates dine-in by Tables / pickup always / delivery by Delivery with adaptive grid columns and the
  unchanged icons. Updated the Round 238 "embeds RoomsView" guards to the new gated branch conditions.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **69 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (OrderDashboard + OrderTypeSelector + test + tracker) →
  clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator).** Live-verified on orgs with mixed module ownership: a Rooms-only org's Room chooser
  shows just Check-in (no Room Order / Create Reservation, no dead space); a no-Tables org's New Order chooser hides dine-in;
  selecting a vertical tab whose module is absent does nothing; revoking a module while its tab is open snaps back to Orders.

### Supervisor round 240 — New Appointment modal: glass booking flow, footer no longer clips — LIVE QA PASSED

- **Problem (live QA, 1282×802, dark, Greek).** New Order → Service → New Appointment read as an unfinished/confusing pasted
  form: the sticky footer covered/clipped the availability guidance and part of the time-slot area.
- **Root cause.** The scroll body was `flex-1 overflow-y-auto` **without `min-h-0`** — the classic flexbox `min-height: auto`
  trap. On a short viewport the body refused to shrink, content overflowed, and the dialog's `overflow-hidden` clipped the
  bottom (footer/guidance). Fix: add `min-h-0` to the body so it shrinks and scrolls; the `shrink-0` footer keeps its reserved
  space. Modal max-height is unchanged (`max-h-[calc(100%-1.5rem)] sm:max-h-[calc(100%-3rem)]`) and stays robust.
- **Booking-flow polish (presentation only — availability logic, create behavior and module gating untouched),
  `salon/AppointmentsView.tsx`:**
  - **Two clear columns** with section headers: left = **Customer & Service**, right = **Date & Time** (new
    `appointments.modal.sections.guestService` / `dateTime` keys, EN/EL/DE/FR/IT).
  - **Glass** throughout: translucent + `backdrop-blur` dialog, blurred translucent section panels (5) + an emerald summary
    panel, and a sticky **glass** footer bar. Native scrollbars stay hidden (`scrollbar-hide`).
  - **Buttons:** Cancel is now soft-destructive **red**; Create is **emerald green** primary with an explicit disabled state;
    the close (X) icon stays centered at 44×44.
  - Touch-first preserved: no hover-only effects, no native `title=` tooltips, `active:` tap feedback.
- **Tests** (`appointments-view-i18n.test.ts`): updated the body-scroller guard to require `min-h-0`; added Round 240 guards —
  glass dialog/panels + two-column headers + reserved-footer body + robust max-h + no hover/title; red Cancel / green Create
  with a scoped negative check (so the unrelated page action buttons stay untouched); and section-header keys present +
  Greek-translated in every locale.
- Verified (static): `node --test appointments-view-i18n.test.ts orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **86 pass / 0 fail**
  (`vertical-modal-portals.test.ts` still **7/0**); `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator).** Live-verified at 1282×802 dark/Greek: the New Appointment modal shows a clean
  two-column glass booking flow; the availability guidance and time slots are fully visible and scroll; the footer never
  overlaps; Cancel is red, Create is green and disabled until staff+service+time are chosen.

### Supervisor round 241 — Cash Register / Fiscal Printer Add/Edit becomes a focused glass submodal — LIVE QA PASSED (after Round 242 z-layer follow-up)

- **Problem (live QA, 1282×802 dark/Greek).** Settings → Devices → Cash Register / Fiscal Printer → **Add Device** rendered the
  Add/Edit form **inline** in the scrolled settings page, so it could appear mid-form (inheriting the parent scroll offset) and
  the footer/actions were only reachable after scrolling. Cancel was neutral grey, Add was amber/brown, and the device
  edit/delete icon buttons used native `title=` tooltips.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/peripherals/CashRegisterSection.tsx`; ECR save/delete/test logic unchanged).**
  - The Add/Edit form is now a **focused glass submodal**: `renderFormModal()` is **portaled to body**
    (`renderModalPortal`, `fixed inset-0 z-[1200]`, blurred backdrop), a labelled `role="dialog"` with a translucent
    `backdrop-blur-2xl` glass panel, **its own scroll body** (`overflow-y-auto flex-1 min-h-0 scrollbar-hide`) and a **reserved
    sticky glass footer** (`shrink-0 backdrop-blur-xl`) inside a `max-h-[calc(100%-…)] flex flex-col overflow-hidden` shell — so
    fields and actions never overlap at short heights. The list stays mounted; the form opens as an overlay on top of it
    instead of replacing it inline. It closes (close-only, never saves) via the **X**, the **backdrop**, **Cancel**, and
    **Escape**.
  - **Buttons:** Cancel is now soft-destructive **red**; Add/Save is **emerald green** primary with an explicit disabled state;
    the list-level **Add Device** CTA is green (was amber).
  - **Icon actions:** the native `title=` tooltips on edit/delete (and the error status badge) are replaced with `aria-label`s;
    the refresh control gains an `aria-label`; edit/delete are centered 44×44-ish touch targets with `active:scale-95`; the
    submodal close (X) is a centered 44×44 control. Touch-first preserved (no hover-only styles).
  - No i18n keys added (reuses `common.actions.{close,edit,delete,cancel,save,saving}`, `common.refresh`, and the existing
    `settings.peripherals.cashRegister.*`). No device created or deleted.
- **Tests** (`settings-modal-ui.test.ts`): added Round 241 guards over `CashRegisterSection.tsx` — focused portaled glass
  dialog with `min-h-0` scroll body + reserved sticky footer + robust max-h; the form opens as an overlay (not returned inline);
  red Cancel / green Add+Save (scoped, with explicit disabled state) and a green list CTA; and no native `title=` / no hover,
  with aria-labels on edit/delete/refresh/close.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **22 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex/operator), after the Round 242 z-layer follow-up.** Live-verified at 1282×802 dark/Greek: tapping
  Add Device (even low on the page) opens a centered focused glass submodal — not an inline form at the scroll offset; the
  fields scroll within the modal and the Cancel/Add footer stays visible without page scrolling; Cancel is red, Add/Save is
  green (disabled until a device name is entered); edit/delete/refresh expose no hover tooltips. (The initial Round 241 build
  rendered the overlay behind the Settings glass modal — fixed in the Round 242 z-layer follow-up below, which passed live QA.)

#### Round 242 follow-up (live QA correction) — fiscal submodal z-layer above the Settings glass modal — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Live QA caught a z-layer bug in Round 241.** The Add Device dialog was in the accessibility tree but **not visible**: the
  Settings shell is a `LiquidGlassModal` whose `.liquid-glass-modal-viewport` is `z-index: 20000`, while the new fiscal overlay
  used Tailwind `z-[1200]` — so it rendered **behind** the parent Settings modal.
- **Fix (`CashRegisterSection.tsx`, one line).** The overlay now uses **`z-[20050]`**, sitting above the `20000` viewport —
  matching the codebase's existing nested-above-glass-modal convention (`MenuItemModal`, `TableCheckManagerModal`,
  `MenuModal` customer popover all layer at `z-[20050]`/`z-[20060]`). Glass look, own scroll body, sticky footer, and
  Escape/backdrop/X close behavior are unchanged.
- **Test.** The Round 241 guard in `settings-modal-ui.test.ts` no longer checks a literal `z-[1200]`; it now reads the overlay's
  `z-[N]` and the `.liquid-glass-modal-viewport` `z-index` from `glassmorphism.css` and asserts **overlay z > viewport z**
  (and that `z-[1200]` does not return).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **22 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator).** Re-verified: in Settings → Devices → Cash Register, tapping Add Device
  shows the focused glass submodal **on top of** the Settings modal (visible, centered), with the footer reachable and all
  Round 241 behavior intact.

### Supervisor round 243 — Caller ID (VoIP/SIP) becomes a child-friendly 3-step setup — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED (via Round 244 follow-up)

- **Problem (live QA, 1282×802 dark/Greek).** Settings → Devices → Caller ID was too dense and technical: it exposed SIP/PBX
  fields, an always-on router "unsupported" caution card, and advanced authentication (Auth Username, Transport) all at once,
  and the Test/Save actions fell below the fold. Goal: understandable even for a 10-year-old.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/peripherals/CallerIdSection.tsx`; CallerIdService/IPC/payload/validation/polling/save·start·stop·
  test/persistence all unchanged).** Reworked the render into a progressive **3-step glass flow**:
  - **Step 1 — choose setup.** The three choices are presented plainly: **Provider preset** and **Manual SIP** as two prominent
    cards, with **Older PBX** as a slim, de-emphasised secondary control (no longer an equal third card, so advanced/legacy
    never reads as the default). (`setup.generic` → "Manual SIP", `setup.legacy` → "Older PBX" in all five locales.)
  - **Step 2 — enter details.** A plain-language **"What you'll need"** checklist (server, extension/username, password) whose
    ticks fill in as fields are completed; the core fields (server, username, password, port) in a clean grid; for **Older PBX**
    one concise caution card instead of a password field. The **optional technical fields — Auth Username, Transport, Outbound
    Proxy, Local Listen Port — now live behind the existing Advanced disclosure** (and `showAdvanced` still auto-opens for
    non-default transport / outbound proxy / listen port). The **router-only warning is now quiet**: a one-line muted note for
    the normal state, and a prominent caution card **only** when `status.reason === 'unsupported_provider'`.
  - **Step 3 — test, then activate.** The action row is a **sticky glass bar** within the section so Test/Save stay reachable
    while scrolling: **Test = neutral**, **Save & Activate = green** when available / **neutral grey** when blocked (single
    gate, unchanged), **Disable = red**; all ≥44px touch targets with centered icon+text and `active:` feedback. The
    "run a fresh test first" hint sits with the buttons.
  - **Palette/touch preserved:** black/white/grey/yellow base + semantic green/red + amber caution; no blue/purple; no
    hover-only styles; no native `title=` tooltips.
- **i18n:** added `callerId.steps.{choose,details,activate}`, `callerId.checklist.{title,server,username,password}`,
  `callerId.routerNoteCompact`, and renamed `setup.generic` / `setup.legacy` values — EN/EL/DE/FR/IT (locale parity green).
- **Tests** (`settings-modal-ui.test.ts`): added Round 243 invariants (not brittle snapshots) — no hover/title; 3 step labels +
  plain setup names + the equal three-card grid gone; the "what you need" checklist; advanced-only technical fields with the
  auto-open behaviour preserved; the router warning quiet for normal state and prominent only on `unsupported_provider` (single
  `<ShieldAlert>`); a sticky action row with neutral/green/red ≥44px targets; no blue/purple; and the new keys present +
  Greek-translated in every locale.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **27 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), via the Round 244 follow-up.** The 3-step structure, quiet router note,
  Advanced disclosure and button semantics all verified; the two defects live QA caught (sticky-bar overlap of Step 2 content,
  and the concatenated Older PBX label) were fixed in the **Round 244 follow-up** below, which **passed live QA**.

#### Round 244 follow-up (live QA correction) — sticky Step 3 bar no longer overlaps Step 2 content — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, 1282×802 dark/Greek).** The `sticky bottom-0` Step 3 action bar pins to the viewport bottom, so Step 2
  content (the legacy/Older PBX caution card + port, which sit right before the bar) scrolled **underneath** the translucent
  bar and was obscured. Also, the slim Older PBX option's title + help rendered inline, so text extraction read them
  concatenated ("Παλαιό PBXΔιατηρεί…").
- **Fix (UI only, `components/peripherals/CallerIdSection.tsx`).**
  - All non-action content now lives in its **own body wrapper with reserved bottom space** (`space-y-4 pb-32`), and the sticky
    Step 3 bar renders **after** it — so the last field/caution card/advanced block/router note clears the bar instead of
    rendering underneath it at 1282×802. The bar keeps its glass look but is **near-opaque** (`bg-zinc-950/95 backdrop-blur-md`)
    so any transient scrolled content cannot bleed through it.
  - The Older PBX title and help are now **block-level on separate lines** (`block` + `mt-0.5`), visually and semantically
    separated, while keeping the de-emphasised slim secondary treatment (no longer an equal prominent card).
  - No change to CallerIdService / IPC / payload / validation / polling / save·start·stop·test / persistence, and all other
    Round 243 goals preserved (3-step flow, advanced-behind-disclosure, quiet router note, neutral Test / green Save / red
    Disable, no hover, no native title tooltips, no blue/purple).
- **Tests** (`settings-modal-ui.test.ts`): added Round 244 guards — the reserved-space body (`space-y-4 pb-NN`) precedes the
  sticky bar and the bar glass is near-opaque (non-bleed); the Older PBX title/help are block-level (no inline `ml-2`
  concatenation) and the control stays a slim full-width secondary. (Round 243 guards still pass.)
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **29 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator).** Re-verified at 1282×802 dark/Greek: the Older PBX title/help are separated;
  Step 2 fields / caution card / advanced block / router note all clear the sticky Step 3 bar; and the Test/Save/Disable bar
  stays reachable while scrolling without overlapping any content.
- Static verification re-run after the Round 244 fix: `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` → **29 pass /
  0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (touched files) → clean.

### Supervisor round 245 — explicit regression restoration re-verification (no code changes needed) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Scope.** Re-verify the four explicit visual regressions + module gating are still correct in code. Inspected the product
  source against each requirement; **everything already matches the running-POS live QA, so no product-source files were
  edited** — this round is bookkeeping only.
- **Findings (code inspection, all confirmed):**
  1. **Login background.** `LoginPage.tsx` renders `<AnimatedBackground />`; `AnimatedBackground.tsx` is the animated yellow/black
     **lava-lamp orb** (6 looping `motion.div` blobs per theme — black-core/yellow-edge in light, white-core/yellow-edge in dark
     — each with `animate` x/y/scale keyframes and `repeat: Infinity`). Not the removed static/subtle background.
  2. **Sidebar icons.** `NavigationSidebar` `getNeonClass` returns **neutral** for inactive unlocked icons (`text-black` in light,
     dark-safe `text-zinc-400` in dark) and the **per-module neon color + glow only when active** (`isActive = currentView ===
     module.id`); no hover utilities, no native `title=` tooltips (aria-labels only).
  3. **Dashboard tabs.** `OrderTabsBar` `tabTextClass` returns **grey** for inactive tabs (`text-gray-600` light / `text-zinc-400`
     dark) and the per-tab neon color + `drop-shadow-[0_0_8px_…]` glow **only when active**; press feedback via `active:`, no
     hover.
  4. **New Order table option.** The order-type **Table** card uses the supplied `<TableOrderIcon>` (table-with-chairs) in both
     `OrderDashboard.tsx` and `OrderFlow.tsx`; **Pickup** uses `<PickupOrderIcon>` (bag). Not cake/utensils/storefront
     (`UtensilsCrossed` in OrderDashboard is an unrelated tables-area glyph, not the order-type card).
  5. **Module gating.** The Round 239 source-of-truth gating (`useAcquiredModules` / `MODULE_IDS`, gated tab content +
     `handleTabChange` + reset effect, `OrderTypeSelector` dine-in/delivery gating) remains intact (guarded by the passing
     orders-page-ui tests).
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/login-page-ui.test.ts navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts orders-page-ui.test.ts` →
  **83 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (tracker only)

  the selected Orders tab green/neon; the sidebar showed neutral inactive icons with selected Orders neon; the New Order modal

### Supervisor round 246 — Orders bulk-action bar palette/touch polish — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, 1282×802 dark/Greek).** Selecting a pickup order opens the bulk-action toolbar. Delivered (green) and
  Cancel (red) were correct, but the non-semantic actions — **Edit, View, Clear, Assign/Return/Map** — and the selection-count
  chip still read **off-theme blue/slate**. The palette wants neutral/amber for utility/edit, with green/red reserved for
  success/destructive.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/BulkActionsBar.tsx`; no behaviour/data-flow change — handlers, `selectionType` logic, localized
  labels untouched).** The `slate-*` (blue-grey) treatments were replaced with on-theme neutral glass (`zinc`) and amber:
  - **`getButtonStyles` consolidated** so every non-semantic variant (`primary` = Assign/Return, `secondary`, `info`, `map`,
    `neutral`) returns one shared **neutral glass** (`bg-white/80 text-zinc-700 border-zinc-300` light / `bg-zinc-800/80
    text-zinc-100 border-zinc-600/50` dark, `active:` press feedback). `success` stays **green**, `danger` stays **red**, and
    `warning` is the **amber** utility style.
  - **Edit** now uses the amber **`warning`** variant (both the delivery and pickup toolbars, and the `getActionsForTab`
    config); **View** and **Clear** use the **neutral** variant; **Assign/Return/Map** stay neutral glass. So colour now encodes
    success/destructive/edit only — utility actions differ by icon + label.
  - **Selection-count chip** re-themed from slate to a **neutral fill with a subtle amber ring** (`bg-zinc-100/80
    border-amber-400/50` light / `bg-zinc-800/60 border-amber-400/40` dark).
  - Touch/palette preserved: no hover-only utilities, no native `title=` tooltips, `active:scale-95` feedback, centered
    icon+text, `min-h-[40px] sm:min-h-[44px]` touch targets (View stays compact but touch-safe). No blue/purple/slate classes
    remain (only the words appear in explanatory comments).
- **Tests** (`orders-page-ui.test.ts`): added Round 246 guards — no off-theme blue/slate colour classes, no hover/title, 44px
  targets; success=green / danger=red / Edit=amber `warning` (both Edit buttons) / utilities collapse to one neutral glass;
  View+Clear+Assign+Map use neutral; the count chip is neutral + amber ring; all `bulkActions.*` labels and `onBulkAction(...)` /
  `onClearSelection` handlers preserved.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **72 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (touched files) → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), 1282×802 dark/Greek.** On the selected pickup order toolbar: Delivered is
  green, Edit is amber, Cancel is red, View and Clear are neutral glass, and the selection-count chip is neutral with an amber
  ring; the toolbar keeps centered icon+text with no obvious hover-only affordance. (Static verification had already passed
  independently: `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → 72 pass / 0 fail; `npm run type-check` → clean; `npm run
  locale:parity` → passed; `git diff --check` over the touched files → clean.)

### Supervisor round 248 — New Appointment availability controls + disabled Create palette (yellow selected, neutral disabled) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, Greek/dark, New Order → Service → New Appointment).** Inside the New Appointment modal, the three
  availability selections — the **selected calendar date**, the **Morning/Afternoon/Evening** period segment, and the **selected
  time slot** — rendered as stark **white slabs with black text** in dark mode (`bg-zinc-100 text-black`; the light-mode twin was
  a `bg-black text-white` black slab). They broke the POS black/yellow/glass language and read as pasted-in. Separately, the
  disabled **Create** button only dimmed its green (`disabled:opacity-50`), so it still looked like a green submit.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/verticals/salon/AppointmentsView.tsx`; no behaviour/data-flow/i18n/sizing change).**
  - **Selected state → core yellow accent, identical in both themes.** All three controls now use
    `bg-yellow-400 text-black border-yellow-400` for the selected/active state (the same accent the sibling salon
    `StaffScheduleView` date pills and the restaurant `ReservationsView` toggles already use), replacing the per-theme
    white/black slabs. The unselected, today-ring, past-disabled, and booked (red) states are untouched; touch sizing (44px
    calendar/period/slot targets), `active:`/`transition-all` tap feedback, no hover-only utilities, and no native `title=`
    tooltips are all preserved.
  - **Disabled Create → neutral/muted glass.** The dimmed-green `disabled:opacity-50` is replaced with an explicit neutral glass
    treatment — `disabled:bg-zinc-400/20 disabled:text-zinc-400 disabled:border-zinc-400/30 disabled:shadow-none` (plus the
    existing `disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:active:scale-100`). **Enabled Create stays emerald**
    (`bg-emerald-600 text-white border-emerald-500`) and **Cancel stays red**. The `disabled={…}` gating logic
    (`isSubmitting || !staffId || !serviceId || !startTime`) is unchanged — no appointment is created and no API/service
    behaviour changes.
- **Tests** (`tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts`): added two **Round 248** guards — (1) the availability region
  (sliced Calendar→Summary so the intentionally-untouched page-level quick filters / view toggles / row actions are not swept
  in) contains **no `bg-zinc-100 text-black` / `bg-black text-white` slabs** and uses the `bg-yellow-400 text-black border-yellow-400`
  accent in all three selected branches; (2) disabled Create is the neutral zinc glass (no `disabled:opacity-50`, never recoloured
  back to emerald) while enabled Create stays emerald and Cancel stays red. Relaxed the Round 240 Create assertion (same button)
  to stop pinning the now-removed `disabled:opacity-50`.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` → **19 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed** (0 missing/extra in all 5 locales); `git diff --check`
  (`AppointmentsView.tsx`, `appointments-view-i18n.test.ts`) → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), Greek/dark.** Re-verified on the running POS after hot reload (New Order →
  Service → New Appointment): the selected **Morning** period and the selected **09:00** time slot render **yellow with black
  text** (no white slab); scrolling back to the calendar, the selected **day 24** is **yellow with black text** (no white slab);
  the disabled **Create** reads as **neutral grey/muted glass** while **Cancel stays red** — no appointment was submitted/created.
  Static checks independently re-run: `node --test tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` → **19 pass / 0 fail**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (touched files) → clean.
