### Supervisor round 250 — Menu/cart commit action polish (green Save/Update) + touch-control title cleanup — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, Greek/dark, pickup order edit flow).** Commit/save/update actions read as **amber/orange** instead of the
  POS's semantic **green**: the order-editor cart footer **"Save Changes"** button (`editMode`) and the item-customization
  modal's bottom **"Update Product"** button. Amber/yellow should stay for selected/attention states and the normal
  complete-order action — not for save/update commits.
- **Fix 1 — green commit actions (UI only, no behaviour/order-saving change).**
  - `components/menu/MenuCart.tsx`: the footer primary action's `editMode` branch flips from
    `bg-amber-600 text-white active:bg-amber-700` to **`bg-green-600 text-white active:bg-green-700 active:scale-[1.02]`**. The
    non-edit **complete-order** action stays intentional **yellow/black** (`bg-yellow-400 text-black active:bg-yellow-300`) and
    the disabled state stays **neutral** (`bg-black/10 … text-black/30`). `onClick={onCheckout}` + `disabled={isCheckoutBlocked}`
    are untouched.
  - `components/menu/MenuItemModal.tsx`: the commit button's `isEditMode` branch flips from the amber
    `liquid-glass-modal-warning` to the green **`liquid-glass-modal-success`** (the same green Add-to-Cart already uses — staying
    in the glass design system rather than a raw `bg-green-600`). Confirmed in `glassmorphism.css` that `…-warning` =
    `rgba(245,158,11)` (amber-500) and `…-success` = `rgba(34,197,94)` (green-500). Button also gains explicit
    `flex items-center justify-center` (centered), keeps `w-full py-3` (touch-safe) + `active:scale-98` (press feedback);
    `onClick={handleAddToCart}`, the update/add labels, ingredient/pricing/notes/quantity/close behaviour all unchanged.
- **Fix 2 — touch-control native `title=` cleanup (no hover tooltips on a touch terminal).** Removed every DOM native `title=`
  attribute from the cart and item-modal touch controls and preserved/added `aria-label` (or relied on existing visible text /
  `sr-only`), keeping `active:` press feedback and adding **no** `hover:` utilities:
  - **MenuCart** (10 removed): exit-selection, add-manual-item, select-all, discount-selected, clear-line-discount, line delete,
    coupon, loyalty, discount, and loyalty-redemption-remove. The coupon/loyalty/discount icon buttons already had `aria-label`
    (only the redundant `title` was dropped); icon-only controls got `aria-label`; the two text+icon buttons keep their visible
    label. **Zero `title=` remain.**
  - **MenuItemModal** (10 removed): Without/Little mode toggles (keep visible text), the selected-extra + without chip removes,
    item-quantity −/+, ingredient-quantity −/+, ingredient without-remove, and ingredient add. The 8 quantity/ingredient controls
    already had `aria-label` (redundant `title` dropped). **Zero `title=` remain.**
- **Scope guard.** No change to the approved neon order tabs/nav icons, pickup/table icons, room/service workflows, appointments
  fixes, or order-taking layout beyond these cart/item controls.
- **Tests.** New focused file `tests/renderer/menu-commit-actions-ui.test.ts` (4 tests) — MenuCart edit-mode save is green (not
  the old `bg-amber-600` branch), complete-order stays yellow/black, disabled stays neutral, `onCheckout`/`isCheckoutBlocked`
  wired; MenuItemModal commit uses `liquid-glass-modal-success` (no `liquid-glass-modal-warning`), centered + touch-safe, handler
  + labels intact; both files have **no** `title=["{]` DOM attributes and **no** `hover:` utilities, with the key aria-labels
  present.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/menu-commit-actions-ui.test.ts` → **4 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed** (0 missing/extra); `git diff --check`
  (`MenuCart.tsx`, `MenuItemModal.tsx`, `menu-commit-actions-ui.test.ts`) → clean.
  - **Note:** `tests/renderer/menu-category-tabs-ui.test.ts` had **3 pre-existing failures unrelated to this round** (assertions
    at lines 75/137/152 target MenuCategoryTabs.tsx, the cart's *non-edit* footer `hover:bg-yellow-300` — already `active:` from a
    prior hover→active migration — and MenuModal.tsx, **none of which Round 250 touched**). Left as-is here to keep the diff narrow;
    cleaned up separately in **Round 251** below (test-only).
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (supervisor), Greek/dark, existing pickup order edit flow.** On the running POS after the
  Round 250/251 changes: opened item customization for **Alpine Breakfast Plate** → the edit-mode bottom **"Ενημέρωση Προϊόντος"**
  (Update Product) button is **green and centered** (not clicked — no save); closed the item modal → the cart footer
  **"Αποθήκευση Αλλαγών"** (Save Changes) is **green and centered** (not clicked — no save); closed the order editor with **no live
  order changes saved**. Static verification re-run by supervisor: `menu-category-tabs-ui.test.ts` → **8/8**;
  `menu-commit-actions-ui.test.ts` → **4/4**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**;
  `git diff --check` → exit 0 (only the pre-existing CRLF warning on the untouched `MenuModal.tsx`).

### Supervisor round 251 — test-only cleanup of stale menu UI assertions (no source change) — COMPLETED

- **Scope.** Assertion-only cleanup so `tests/renderer/menu-category-tabs-ui.test.ts` reflects the **current approved source**.
  No product source touched — each stale assertion was first verified against the live source, not blindly rewritten. The 3
  failures (5 stale assertions) were leftovers from earlier approved palette/touch migrations the tests never caught up with.
- **Fixes (test file only):**
  - **Selected tab glow (line 75).** Source has **zero emerald**; selected tabs/subcategories use the approved yellow/black
    (`bg-yellow-400 text-black`, already asserted lines 71-72). The stale `assert.match(/bg-emerald-500\/75/)` → negative guard
    `assert.doesNotMatch(/bg-emerald-500\/75/)` (the old glow stays gone).
  - **Sweet flavor filter (line 86).** Source is now `border-slate-300/70 bg-slate-500` (was pink). Assertion updated to slate +
    a negative `doesNotMatch` guard against the old pink.
  - **Cart complete-order action (line 137).** Touchscreen uses `active:bg-yellow-300`, not `hover:`. `hover:bg-yellow-300` →
    `active:bg-yellow-300`; no positive hover requirement remains (the blue-hover negative guard on the next line is kept).
  - **MenuModal customer pill (lines 152-153).** Source is now green semantic (`border border-green-500/40 … text-white`,
    `<User … text-green-300 …>` — MenuModal.tsx ~2326-2327). Assertions updated blue→green; the negative blue guards (no
    `bg-blue-500/20 …`, no `focus:ring-blue-400`) are kept and still pass since the source is green.
- **Result:** `node --test tests/renderer/menu-category-tabs-ui.test.ts` → **8 pass / 0 fail** (was 5 pass / 3 fail).
- Verified (static): `menu-category-tabs-ui.test.ts` → **8/8**; `menu-commit-actions-ui.test.ts` (Round 250) → **4/4**;
  `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` → exit 0 (only the pre-existing CRLF

### Supervisor round 252 — Split Payment modal: footer overlap fix + yellow/black segmented controls — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, Greek/dark, 1282×802, Orders → pickup order → View → Split Payment).** In **by-item** mode the lower
  **Person 2** card was clipped/covered by the modal footer — the footer overlaid the content instead of the content having
  scroll/bottom breathing room. Separately, the top split-mode segmented control (**By Amount / By Items**) and the bottom
  receipt-mode toggle (**All Together / Separate**) used a grey/white selected slab instead of the POS's core yellow/black accent.
- **Fix 1 — robust bounded-body layout (`components/modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx`; no settlement/payment logic change).**
  - `LiquidGlassModal` now receives `contentClassName="flex min-h-0 flex-col overflow-hidden"`, so the modal content area is a
    bounded min-h-0 flex column (the shell is `flex flex-col` at `max-height: 92vh`; the footer is a sibling `flex-shrink-0`).
  - The inner content is `flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col`; the order-total header and the split-mode segmented control are
    `flex-shrink-0`; the tab content (`renderByAmountTab` / `renderByItemsTab`) is wrapped in a **single** real scroll region
    `min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide pb-4` — so the body scrolls and the `pb-4` (+ content padding) keeps the last
    person / Add-Person row reachable above the footer.
  - Removed the inner scrollers that fought the body and caused the clip: the by-amount people list dropped `max-h-[380px]
    overflow-y-auto`, and the by-item layout dropped `grid max-h-[500px]` + the two per-column `overflow-y-auto` (now a single
    bounded scroll, no nested competing regions, no content under the footer).
- **Fix 2 — yellow/black selected segmented controls (palette).** The **activeTab** tabs flip selected `bg-white/10
  liquid-glass-modal-text shadow-sm` → **`bg-yellow-400 text-black shadow-sm`**; the **receiptMode** toggles flip selected
  `border border-white/20 bg-white/10 liquid-glass-modal-text` → **`border border-yellow-400 bg-yellow-400 text-black`**.
  Unselected stays neutral with `active:` (touch) feedback. **Payment semantics untouched:** cash stays green, card stays slate,
  collected-by stays emerald/slate, and the **Confirm Split** button stays emerald green. No `hover:` and no DOM native `title=`
  were added (the existing `title=` is the `LiquidGlassModal` heading **prop**, not a DOM tooltip).
- **Tests.** New `tests/renderer/split-payment-modal-ui.test.ts` (5 tests): bounded flex-column content contract
  (`contentClassName` min-h-0/overflow-hidden + flex-shrink-0 header/segmented); single `flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-y-auto
  scrollbar-hide pb-4` body wrapping the tab render; no `max-h-[380px]`/`max-h-[500px]`/naked `grid max-h-[500px]`; activeTab +
  receiptMode selected branches yellow/black (old grey slab gone, unselected keeps `active:`); cash/confirm greens preserved; no
  `hover:` and no DOM-element `title=`.
  - **Tiny test-import fix (permitted by scope):** `split-payment-discount-persistence.test.ts` imported
    `../../src/renderer/utils/splitPaymentSettlement` without a `.ts` extension and failed `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` under a bare
    `node --test` (pre-existing, unrelated to this round — matches the project's explicit-`.ts`-extension rule). Added the `.ts`
    extension; no test logic changed.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/split-payment-modal-ui.test.ts` → **5/5**; regression
  `split-payment-discount-persistence.test.ts split-payment-currency.test.ts pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` → **25/25**
  (was 24 pass + 1 load failure before the `.ts` fix); `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**;
  `git diff --check` (touched files) → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), Greek/dark, 1282×802.** Verified after hot reload on the running POS
  Orders → pickup order → View → Split Payment: by-item mode scrolls so Person 2 and Add Person are fully reachable above the
  footer; the By Amount/By Items and All Together/Separate selected segments are yellow with black text; cash/card/confirm payment
  semantics stay green/slate/green; no native thick scrollbar is visible. No payment was submitted.

### Supervisor round 253 — Order Details modal: loading spinner off-theme blue/cyan → yellow/amber — COMPLETED (STATIC VERIFIED + LIVE NO-REGRESSION)

- **Finding (live review + source review).** A live review of the running POS **Order Details** modal found the visible card
  surfaces are already neutral/on-palette — the blue-looking spot on the **Total** card was just the pointer (hover) highlight,
  not a real off-theme fill. But a follow-up **source review** caught the **loading spinner** (the brief spinner shown while the
  modal fetches order/customer data) still using off-theme blue/cyan accents: dark `border-white/15 border-t-cyan-300`, light
  `border-zinc-200 border-t-blue-600`.
- **Fix (UI only, `components/modals/OrderDetailsModal.tsx`; one spinner branch — no behaviour/layout/order/payment change).**
  The spinner's top-border accent moves to the POS palette: dark → `border-white/15 border-t-yellow-400`, light →
  `border-zinc-200 border-t-amber-500` (neutral base ring kept; yellow on dark / amber on light reads cleanly on each theme).
  The unrelated neutral `historyLoading` spinner (zinc base + zinc top) was already on-palette and is left untouched. No `hover:`
  utilities or native DOM `title=` were added; semantic green/red/yellow behaviour is unchanged.
- **Tests** (`tests/renderer/order-modal-totals.test.ts`, which already reads `OrderDetailsModal.tsx`): added a **Round 253**
  guard — no `border-t-cyan` / `border-t-blue` anywhere in the modal, and the loading spinner keeps its shape with the
  yellow (dark) / amber (light) top accent on the neutral base. The existing subtotal/strikethrough total tests are unchanged.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/order-modal-totals.test.ts tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` →
  **15 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check`
  (`OrderDetailsModal.tsx`, `order-modal-totals.test.ts`) → clean.
- **Live QA — NO-REGRESSION (Codex/operator), 1282×802, Greek/dark.** Reopened the running POS Order Details modal after hot
  reload: the loaded modal remained neutral/glass, the **Total** card had **no actual blue/cyan styling** (the earlier
  blue-looking spot was only the pointer highlight), the footer actions stayed intact, and **no order/payment action was
  submitted**. The loading-spinner branch was too transient to capture live on this dataset, so the spinner-specific evidence is
  the **source diff plus the Round 253 test guard**, not a claimed visual sighting.
- **Static re-run (Codex/operator, independent):** `node --test tests/renderer/order-modal-totals.test.ts
  tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` → **15/15**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` →
  **passed**; `git diff --check` → exit 0 (only the known untouched `MenuModal.tsx` CRLF warning).

### Supervisor round 254 — Rooms tab search/filter bar: search gets its own full-width row (no clipped placeholder) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Problem (live QA, 1282×802, Greek/dark, Orders hub → Rooms tab).** The Rooms tab itself is usable and the floor selector
  exists, but the **search placeholder was visibly clipped/truncated** — Greek showed only "Αναζήτηση δωμα…" — because the search
  input shared **one desktop row** (`flex sm:flex-row`, search as `flex-1`) with the status chips, the floor select, and the
  refresh button. On a multi-language POS, a squeezed `flex-1` can't fit longer translated placeholders.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/verticals/hotel/RoomsView.tsx` — Search & Filter Bar layout only; no data/filter/module/card/modal
  logic change).** The bar now stacks vertically:
  - The outer bar is `data-rooms-filter-bar className="flex flex-col …"` (was `flex flex-col sm:flex-row`), so the search is
    always on its **own row**.
  - The search field is `data-rooms-search className="relative w-full …"` (was a squeezable `relative flex-1`) — full width, so a
    long localized placeholder can never be clipped.
  - The status filters + floor selector + refresh moved into a separate `data-rooms-filter-controls className="flex flex-wrap
    items-center gap-2"` row **below** the search; the desktop filter group is `hidden sm:flex flex-wrap gap-2` so many status
    chips wrap instead of squeezing. Same bar serves the **embedded Orders hub** and the **standalone** RoomsView.
- **Preserved:** all filtering behavior + `useRooms` data + module gates + room cards/stats/modals + reservation/check-in/checkout
  logic; the `StatusFilterButtons … setStatusFilter={handleManualStatusFilterChange}` one-shot wiring and the
  `roomsView.searchPlaceholder` i18n call; the selected-filter **yellow/black** + semantic green/orange/amber/red/purple palette
  (nothing turned all-yellow); touch targets (40px search/refresh/toggle), hidden scrollbars, `active:` feedback, and **no**
  `hover:` utilities or native DOM `title=`.
- **Tests** (`tests/renderer/rooms-view-i18n.test.ts`): added a **Round 254** layout-contract guard — the bar is `flex flex-col`
  (old `sm:flex-row` gone); the search is `data-rooms-search` / `w-full` (old `flex-1` gone) and keeps its localized placeholder;
  `data-rooms-search` precedes `data-rooms-filter-controls`; status/floor/refresh live in the controls row with the desktop group
  wrapping; and the relocated bar keeps `active:` feedback with no `hover:`/`title=`. Existing RoomsView i18n/effective-status
  tests are unchanged.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/rooms-view-i18n.test.ts tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts
  tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` → **106 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity`
  → **passed**; `git diff --check` (`RoomsView.tsx`, `rooms-view-i18n.test.ts`) → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), 1282×802, Greek/dark, Orders hub → Rooms tab, after hot reload.** The search
  field is now a **full-width row** and shows the full Greek placeholder **"Αναζήτηση δωματίου ή πελάτη…"** without
  clipping/ellipsis; the status chips, floor selector, and refresh sit on the **row below**; the selected **"Όλα"** chip remains
  **yellow/black**; room cards and floor headings remain readable; **no order/payment/reservation/check-in action was submitted**.
  Note: a standalone Rooms **sidebar route is intentionally not exposed** after the Orders-hub migration, so standalone behavior is
  covered by the **shared `RoomsView` source/test guard**, not a separate live route pass.
- **Static re-run (Codex/operator, independent):** `node --test tests/renderer/rooms-view-i18n.test.ts
  tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` → **106/106**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` → exit 0 (only the known untouched `MenuModal.tsx` CRLF
  warning).

### Supervisor round 255 — New Order → Room pickers roomier + neutral disabled room confirm + New Appointment side-by-side Date/Time (first-view availability) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Findings (Codex live QA, 1282×802, Greek/dark — the three New Order → Room workflows + Services booking).** Module-backed
  gating is correct (Room card Rooms-gated, Room Order Orders-gated, Create Reservation Reservations-gated, Check-in under Rooms);
  the empty states (no occupied/open-folio rooms for Room Order; no reserved rooms for Check-in) are correct; Create Reservation
  shows available rooms + floor chips and opens the New Reservation form. Three polish gaps remained: (1) the available-room
  selector at 1282×802 **clipped the last card** with the native scrollbar hidden, so content felt cut off; (2) the room
  reservation/check-in **disabled Create still read as a dimmed-green block** (should follow the Appointments standard:
  enabled emerald / disabled neutral glass); (3) the **New Appointment** date/time column **buried the time-slot availability
  below the fold** on first open.
- **Fix 1 — roomier room pickers (UI only; no data/gate/API/logic change).**
  - `components/modals/RoomStayWorkflowModals.tsx` `RoomStaySelectorModal`: modal `!max-w-lg` → **`!max-w-3xl`**; the room grid
    gains **`lg:grid-cols-3`** + **`pb-2`** (`grid max-h-[60vh] … overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide pb-2 sm:grid-cols-2
    lg:grid-cols-3`) so 9 rooms fit in 3 rows at 1282×802 and the last card scrolls fully into view; hidden native scrollbar and
    floor chips kept.
  - `components/OrderDashboard.tsx` Room Order selector: the **same** treatment (`!max-w-3xl`, `lg:grid-cols-3`, `pb-2`) since it
    reuses the room-card picker pattern.
- **Fix 2 — neutral disabled room confirm (matches `AppointmentsView` Create).** `RoomCheckinModal` + `RoomReservationModal`
  confirm buttons: enabled stays **emerald** (now `border border-emerald-500 bg-emerald-600`); disabled is explicit neutral glass
  — `disabled:bg-zinc-400/20 disabled:text-zinc-400 disabled:border-zinc-400/30 disabled:shadow-none disabled:cursor-not-allowed
  disabled:active:scale-100`, with the old **`disabled:opacity-50` removed** from confirm. **Cancel stays red** (its
  `disabled:opacity-50` is untouched).
- **Fix 3 — New Appointment first-view availability (`pages/verticals/salon/AppointmentsView.tsx`; layout only, no behaviour
  change).**
  - **First attempt (compacting) — FAILED live.** Tightening the date/time panels (calendar `p-4`→`p-3`, month header
    `mb-3`→`mb-2`, weekday grid `gap-1.5 mb-2`→`gap-1 mb-1.5`, day grid `gap-1.5`→`gap-1`, day cells `h-10`→`h-9`, time panel
    `p-4 … space-y-3`→`p-3 … space-y-2.5`) saved ~50px, but Codex live QA at 1282×802 still showed the **first slot row
    (07:00…08:00) behind the footer** — present in the accessibility tree but not visible to the operator without scrolling.
  - **Structural fix (the live correction).** The Date & Time section now wraps the **Calendar** and **Time Slots** panels in a
    **`grid grid-cols-1 gap-3 xl:grid-cols-2`** shell, so on **xl/desktop they sit side-by-side** (Calendar left, Time Slots
    right) instead of stacked. The Time Slots panel now starts at calendar-top height, so the period controls (Πρωί/Απόγευμα/
    Βράδυ) + first slot row land well above the footer at 1282×802. **Mobile/tablet stay stacked** (`grid-cols-1`). The compacting
    tweaks are kept (they help the now-narrower columns). **Summary + Notes stay full-width below** the grid.
  - The Round 240 footer-reservation body (`p-4 sm:p-6 … flex-1 min-h-0 scrollbar-hide`) and the slot grid's own `max-h-56`
    scroll are kept; selected calendar/period/slot stay **yellow + black**; enabled Create green, disabled Create neutral, Cancel
    red; no hover/title.
- **Preserved (explicitly):** all module gates + data flow + `useRooms` + API calls + order/payment/reservation/check-in logic +
  i18n; glass styling, floor chips, hidden scrollbars, `active:` feedback; no `hover:` or native DOM `title=` added; semantic
  green/orange/amber/red/purple untouched (nothing turned all-yellow).
- **Tests.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`: updated the Round 238 confirm guard to the new enabled-emerald /
  neutral-disabled pattern, and added **Round 255** guards — roomier pickers (`!max-w-(2xl|3xl)`, `lg:grid-cols-3`, `pb-2`,
  hidden-rail preserved, old naked 2-col gone) on both the module selector and the dashboard Room Order grid; both room confirm
  buttons neutral-when-disabled with no `disabled:opacity-50` (Cancel keeps red opacity); and the four module gates unchanged
  (Rooms/Orders/Reservations + Check-in not separately gated). `tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts`: the Round 255
  guard now protects the **side-by-side Date & Time shell** (`grid grid-cols-1 gap-3 xl:grid-cols-2`) plus the compacted panel
  spacing (p-3/h-9/gap-1/space-y-2.5), the old roomy spacing gone, the Round 240 body+slot-scroll contract preserved, and the
  yellow selected controls intact.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` →
  **96 pass / 0 fail**; `node --test tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` → **11/11**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**;
  `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (touched files) → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), 1282×802, Greek/dark.** All three areas verified on the running POS:
  (a) New Order → Room reservation selector shows a **3-column grid with all 9 rooms visible**, no native scrollbar / no clipped
  last card — **PASS**; (b) the room reservation form's **disabled Create Reservation is neutral grey glass** (turns emerald once a
  guest name is entered), **Cancel red** — **PASS**; (c) the **New Appointment** modal shows the **Calendar and Time Slots
  side-by-side**, with the period controls and the first slot row **07:00 / 07:15 / 07:30 / 07:45 / 08:00 visible above the footer
  without scrolling**, and Create neutral-when-disabled — **PASS**. For the record: the **first compacting attempt failed live**
  (the first slot row still sat behind the footer), and the **structural side-by-side Date/Time layout** (Fix 3 above) is what
  passed. No appointment/reservation/order/check-in/payment was submitted.

### Supervisor round 256 — Housekeeping (Καθαριότητα) board: full-width glass status sections + POS glass/yellow chrome — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (Codex live QA, 1282×802, Greek/dark).** Housekeeping is reachable but visually unfinished: the board showed a
  **narrow first pending column** with cards while most of the page was empty black space; the pending column had a **native white
  vertical scrollbar**; stats, filters, column headers, selects, and action buttons still used **old blue/navy/gray blocks** with
  **hover/title** patterns — a desktop kanban squeezed into a touchscreen POS.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/verticals/hotel/HousekeepingView.tsx`; no API/data-fetch/status-transition/assignment/fallback/module/
  i18n change).**
  - **Layout — full-width status sections, not narrow kanban lanes.** The 4-column grid
    (`flex-1 grid grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-4 … overflow-hidden`) is replaced by a single scrollable column
    (`flex-1 overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide space-y-5 pb-4`) of **stacked `<motion.section data-housekeeping-section=…>`** blocks,
    each with a **glass header (label + count badge)** and a **responsive card grid**
    `grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3 2xl:grid-cols-4`. Empty status sections are skipped, so the current 4 pending rooms
    **use the full width** instead of a single narrow lane. The body scrolls with the **hidden native scrollbar** (`scrollbar-hide`)
    — no white rail in the task area.
  - **Chrome — POS glass/yellow/neutral.** Stats cards, the filter selects, the section headers, the task cards, and the
    empty/error states now use translucent glass (`bg-zinc-900/50–60 border-white/10` dark, `bg-white/80 border-zinc-200` light,
    `backdrop-blur-md`). Semantic colors kept where useful: **Completed Today green**, priority badges keep their red/orange/amber/
    green inline palette, Avg-Time is now neutral (was `text-blue-500`).
  - **Refresh** is an **amber glass icon button** (`bg-amber-500/15 text-amber-300`) with an **aria-label** (the native `title=` is
    gone), `active:scale-95`, no hover.
  - **Action buttons — non-blue semantic glass + neutral disabled.** Start/Create → **amber glass**, Complete → **green glass**,
    Verify → **purple glass**; all carry the shared neutral disabled treatment
    (`disabled:bg-zinc-400/20 disabled:text-zinc-400 disabled:border-zinc-400/30 disabled:active:scale-100`, the old blue/
    `disabled:opacity-60` removed). The error Retry and empty-state Refresh buttons are amber glass too. No `bg-blue-*`, no
    `hover:`, no native `title=` remain anywhere in the file.
- **Preserved (explicitly):** all data fetching (`fetchTasks` dual-contract), status transitions + optimistic overrides,
  assignment, fallback-task logic (`buildHousekeepingFallbackTasks`, synthetic-id guards, create-task promotion), and every
  `housekeepingView.*` locale key (no new keys; only existing `defaultValue` strings).
- **Tests** (`tests/renderer/housekeeping-view-i18n.test.ts`): added 5 **Round 256** structural guards — no `bg-blue-`/`hover:`/
  native `title=` (aria-label refresh instead); full-width `<motion.section data-housekeeping-section>` sections with the
  responsive grid (old narrow kanban grid gone, empty sections skipped); task area `overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide` (old
  per-column native lane gone); glass chrome (no `bg-gray-700/800/900`, ≥4 glass surfaces, amber glass refresh); action buttons
  amber/green/purple glass with ≥4 neutral-disabled treatments. All 13 existing i18n/behaviour/fallback tests still pass.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/housekeeping-view-i18n.test.ts` → **18 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (`HousekeepingView.tsx`, `housekeeping-view-i18n.test.ts`)
  → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), 1282×802, Greek/dark.** Verified on the running POS, Καθαριότητα: the pending
  rooms now **spread across the full content width** in a responsive card grid (**3 cards first row, 1 second row** in this
  viewport) — no longer trapped in a narrow lane; **no native white scrollbar** is visible in the task area; the stats, filters,
  status header, task cards, refresh icon, and Start buttons read as **POS glass / yellow / neutral** with **no blue hover look**.
  **No housekeeping task actions were clicked or submitted.**

### Supervisor round 257 — Suppliers page: detail aside no longer clipped + amber-glass refresh — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (Codex live QA, 1282×802, Greek/dark).** The Suppliers route is mostly usable, but two visible problems remained:
  (1) the **right supplier detail aside was clipped at the bottom** — the **"Τιμολόγια" / invoice-count tile** began at the
  bottom edge and read like cut-off content rather than a finished panel; (2) the **header refresh icon button was a stark
  black/white square**, breaking the POS glass/yellow/neutral system used by the recent hotel and Menu/Housekeeping pages.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/SuppliersPage.tsx`; no fetch/filter/import/summary-modal/invoice-action/locale change).**
  - **Detail aside — compacted with bottom breathing room.** The desktop aside header `p-4`→**`p-3`**, the scroll body
    `p-4`→**`p-3 pb-4`** (compact sides + a generous bottom pad so the last tile clears the edge), the content stack
    `space-y-4`→**`space-y-3`**, and the invoice-count tile `p-4`→**`p-3`**. The body keeps `overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide`
    (hidden native scrollbar preserved), so the **invoice-count tile is fully visible** on first view at 1282×802 without a
    native rail. Supplier name/code/phone/email/address/payment-terms/invoice-count copy is unchanged (no cramming, no
    translation breakage).
  - **Header refresh — amber glass.** The stark `bg-white text-black` / `bg-black text-white` square is replaced with **amber
    glass** (`border-amber-400/30 bg-amber-500/15 text-amber-300` dark, `border-amber-400/40 bg-amber-50 text-amber-600` light),
    keeping its **aria-label** (no native `title=`), `active:scale-95` press feedback, and neutral `opacity-60` disabled state.
    No `hover:` utilities. The Import-items button (yellow outline) and the semantic stat icons (active=emerald, unpaid/owed=amber,
    overdue=red) are untouched.
- **Preserved (explicitly):** supplier/invoice fetches, filters, import preview/commit flow, the supplier-summary + invoice
  details modals (portal/Escape/close-only), invoice payment/status actions, and every `suppliers.*` locale key; no `hover:` or
  native `title=` introduced.
- **Tests** (`tests/renderer/suppliers-page-ui.test.ts`): updated the "hidden-scrollbar workbench" guard's refresh assertions
  from the old black/white square to the **amber glass** classes, and added 2 **Round 257** guards — refresh is amber glass (old
  black/white gone, aria-label kept, no hover/title); the desktop detail aside is compacted (`p-3` header/body/card, `space-y-3`)
  with `pb-4` bottom breathing room over `scrollbar-hide`, and the old roomy aside spacing (the clip source) is gone — all scoped
  to the aside so the summary modal's own `p-4`/`space-y-4` stay untouched. The 14 existing behaviour/import/modal/i18n tests
  still pass.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/suppliers-page-ui.test.ts` → **18 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` →
  **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (`SuppliersPage.tsx`, `suppliers-page-ui.test.ts`) → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), 1282×802, Greek/dark.** Verified on the running POS, Suppliers: the header
  **refresh button is amber glass** (not a black/white square); the right supplier detail aside now shows the **Τιμολόγια
  invoice-count tile fully** with **no native scrollbar and no bottom clipping** — it reads as a finished panel; the **semantic
  stat colors are unchanged** (active=green, unpaid/owed=amber, overdue=red). **No supplier/invoice data was created or edited.**

### Supervisor round 259 — Delivery Pro route (DeliveryZonesPage) header refresh → amber glass + stale-test repair — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (Codex live QA, 1282×802, Greek/dark).** The **visible** sidebar **Delivery Pro** route renders
  `pages/DeliveryZonesPage.tsx` (heading **"Ζώνες Διανομής"**), **not** `DeliveryPage.tsx` — Round 148 stays blocked for the
  unreachable `DeliveryPage`; this is a separate, visible page. It is otherwise usable (dark glass/neutral surfaces, hidden page
  scrollbar, semantic stat colors, clean no-zones empty state), but the **header refresh button was still a stark black/white
  square** (`bg-white text-black` dark / `bg-black text-white` light), unlike the amber-glass refresh now used by
  Suppliers/Menu/Housekeeping. It also carried a **native `title={t('common.refresh')}`** that **duplicated the accessible
  description** in the live tree (*"button Ανανέωση — Description: Ανανέωση"*); a touchscreen POS should use **aria-label only**.
  Separately, the focused test `delivery-zones-page-ui.test.ts` was **stale (3/4 failing)** — it still expected old `hover:` +
  blue-icon classes while the source had already moved to `active:` + emerald/amber/zinc icons.
- **Fix (UI only, `pages/DeliveryZonesPage.tsx` — header refresh button only; no routing/API/fetch/analytics/data/locale change).**
  - **Removed the native `title=`** prop entirely; the button keeps **`aria-label={t('common.refresh', 'Refresh')}`** as the sole
    accessible name (no more duplicated description).
  - **Restyled to amber glass** (matching SuppliersPage Round 257): dark `border border-amber-400/30 bg-amber-500/15 text-amber-300
    active:bg-amber-500/25`, light `border border-amber-400/40 bg-amber-50 text-amber-600 active:bg-amber-100`. Preserved
    `h-12 w-12`, centered icon, `onClick={() => void fetchData()}`, the loading spinner, `active:scale-95`, and the neutral
    `opacity-60 cursor-not-allowed` disabled state. No `hover:` utilities. The rest of the page (semantic stat/zone colors:
    green active, yellow validations, emerald success, red hotspots, amber time signals, neutral info) is untouched.
- **Tests (`tests/renderer/delivery-zones-page-ui.test.ts`) — repaired the stale guards to protect the desired state:** the
  shared-header guard now asserts the **amber glass** refresh + `active:scale-95` (old `hover:bg-zinc-*` / `hover:scale-[1.03]` /
  black-white square gone); the stat/zone icon guards now assert the **current** colors (success-rate Truck = **emerald**,
  estimated-time Clock = **amber**, bottom info = neutral **zinc**), not the stale blue. Added 2 **Round 259** guards: refresh is
  amber glass + aria-label only + **no `title=`** + **no `hover:`** anywhere + behaviour/shape preserved; and the semantic icon
  colors are current with **no `text-blue-500`** left on the page. The scroll/header/empty/status guards that were still valid are
  kept.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/delivery-zones-page-ui.test.ts` → **6 pass / 0 fail** (was 1 pass / 3 fail before
  the repair); `npm run type-check` → **clean**; `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check`
  (`DeliveryZonesPage.tsx`, `delivery-zones-page-ui.test.ts`, tracker) → clean. **No delivery/order data was created or edited.**
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), 1282×802, Greek/dark.** Verified on the running POS, Delivery Pro /
  **Ζώνες Διανομής**: the header **refresh button is amber glass** with **no harsh black/white square**; there is **no visible
  native page scrollbar**; the **no-zones empty state and semantic stat colors are intact**; and the **accessibility tree exposes
  only "button Ανανέωση" with no duplicated Description**. **No delivery/order data was created or edited.**

### Supervisor round 263 — New Order → Room workflows + module-acquired gating re-verification (no source changes needed) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Scope.** Re-verify (source + tests) that the three New Order → Room workflows and the module-acquired availability gating are
  source-of-truth correct. Inspected the product source against each expectation; **everything already matches, so no
  product-source files were edited** — this round is tracker/test-bookkeeping only.
- **Static findings (all confirmed in `OrderDashboard.tsx` / `useAcquiredModules` / `RoomStayWorkflowModals.tsx`):**
  - **Order-type cards are module-acquired gated.** The **Room** card renders only under `hasRoomsModule`; the **Service** card
    only under `hasServicesModule = hasAppointmentsModule || hasServiceCatalogModule` (derived gates at OrderDashboard ~434-451).
  - **Room chooser actions are individually gated.** **Room Order** renders only under `hasOrdersModule`
    (`hasModule(MODULE_IDS.ORDERS)`), **Create Reservation** only under `hasReservationsModule`
    (`hasModule(MODULE_IDS.RESERVATIONS)`), and **Check-in** stays available inside the (already Rooms-gated) chooser.
  - **Room Order is folio-charge, not navigation.** `handleRoomFlowOrder` opens the focused occupied/open-folio selector
    (`showRoomOrderSelector`); selecting a valid room sets `roomChargeContext` and opens the existing **MenuModal**
    (`setShowMenuModal(true)`) — it does **not** `setActiveTab("rooms")` / navigate to `RoomsView`.
  - **Check-in / Create Reservation use the focused modules.** `handleRoomFlowCheckin` → `RoomStaySelectorModal variant="checkin"`
    (reserved rooms) → `RoomCheckinModal`; `handleRoomFlowReservation` → `RoomStaySelectorModal variant="reservation"` (available
    rooms, with `RoomFloorChips`) → `RoomReservationModal`. Not the full `RoomsView` shell.
  - **Tab guards reject unavailable modules.** `handleTabChange` returns early for `rooms`/`services`/`tables`/`delivered` whose
    module is absent, and a reset effect snaps the active tab back to `orders` when a vertical's module becomes unavailable.
  - **Touch/glass invariants hold.** All room pickers use `overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide` (hidden native scrollbar), the
    `LiquidGlassModal` glass shell, **no `hover:`** utilities, and **no native DOM `title=` tooltips** — the only `title=` in
    `RoomStayWorkflowModals.tsx` are the `LiquidGlassModal` heading **props** (3) plus `<RoomChip subtitle=…>` substring matches
    (2), none of them DOM tooltips.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/users-page-ui.test.ts` → **19 pass / 0 fail**;
  `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` → **75 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` → **clean**;
  `npm run locale:parity` → **passed**; `git diff --check` (UsersPage + users test + OrderDashboard + RoomStayWorkflowModals +
  orders test + tracker) → clean.
- **Live QA — PASSED / COMPLETED (Codex/operator), 1282×802, Greek/dark — no data created.** Verified all three New Order → Room
  workflows on the running POS:
  - **New Order chooser** showed **Delivery, Pickup, Table, Room, Service** — confirming this org has those modules
    acquired/enabled.
  - **Room chooser** showed all **3 actions** — **Room Order, Check-in, Create Reservation** — confirming Room Order is
    **Orders-gated** and Create Reservation is **Reservations-gated** for this org; Check-in stays under **Rooms**.
  - **Room Order** opened the focused room selector and showed the **empty occupied/open-folio state with the Check-in hint** —
    **no order/menu opened**, no data created.
  - **Check-in** opened the focused **reserved-room** selector and showed the **empty reserved-room state** — **no check-in
    submitted**.
  - **Create Reservation** opened the focused **available-room** selector with floor chips **All / Floor 1 / Floor 2 / Floor 3**
    and the **3-column room grid**; selecting **Room 201** opened the **New Reservation** form with customer fields and **Create
    disabled until required info** — closed before save, **no reservation created**.
  - Modals stayed **glass/blurred**, the room picker's **scrollbars stayed hidden/modernized**, and **no `RoomsView` navigation**
    occurred.

    (blue removed entirely). Inactive stays **neutral glass/grey** (unchanged dark/light treatments).

    (`flex flex-col items-center justify-center`) with a **min touch size** (`min-h-[88px]`), and **active-only press feedback**

### Supervisor round 268 — Z Report modal: header action/close buttons drop native title tooltips (touch-first) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (rg / touch-first a11y).** This POS is touchscreen-first, so controls must not use native DOM `title=` tooltips
  (hover-dependent, and they leak a duplicated description into the accessibility tree). `components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx`
  carried native `title=` on the **header command-rail action buttons** (refresh/print/export, `title={action.label}`) and the
  **close button** (`title={t('common.actions.close')}`).
- **Fix (UI only, `ZReportModal.tsx` — those buttons' attributes only).** Swapped the two native `title=` attributes to
  **`aria-label`** (`aria-label={action.label}` on the mapped action buttons, `aria-label={t('common.actions.close')}` on the
  close button). Visible labels (`<span>` text), click handlers (`action.onClick` / `onClose`), `disabled` states, icons, and
  layout are **unchanged**. The legitimate component-prop `title=`s are **preserved**: `<LiquidGlassModal … title={title}>` (modal
  heading) and `<UnsettledPaymentBlockersPanel … title={t('modals.zReport.paymentIntegrityTitle', …)}>` (panel heading) — those
  are component props, not DOM tooltips. No business-day-close / submit / sync / locale change.
- **Tests (`tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts`):** added a guard — no `title={action.label}` /
  `title={t('common.actions.close')}` native tooltips remain; both controls now carry `aria-label`; the visible labels +
  handlers + `disabled` wiring are intact; and the two component-prop `title=`s (LiquidGlassModal + UnsettledPaymentBlockersPanel)
  are still present. The existing action-rail scroll + closeout-sync guards are unchanged.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts`
  → **10 pass / 0 fail**; `git diff --check` (`ZReportModal.tsx`, both Z-report tests, tracker) → clean. No business-day-close,
  submit, sync, or locale changes.
- **Live QA — PASSED (supervisor, Greek / dark, 1282×802).** Reopened the Z Report modal and inspected the live accessibility
  tree. The header command buttons read as **`button Ανανέωση αναφοράς`**, **`button Εκτύπωση`**, **`button Εξαγωγή CSV`**, and
  **`button Κλείσιμο`** — each exposing a **single accessible name with no duplicated Description text** from native `title`
  tooltips. Layout/behaviour unchanged. Closed the modal cleanly with **no Submit-to-Admin, Print, Export, payment, order,
  reservation, check-in, or close-day mutation** performed.

### Supervisor round 269 — Z Report modal: Cash Drawer tab "bright native rail" follow-up + scrollbar-hide regression guard — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (Codex live QA).** On the running POS the Cash Drawer tab appeared to show a bright native vertical scrollbar/rail at
  the edge of the central content panel, which would violate the touchscreen "no native scrollbars" policy.
- **Investigation (root cause).** Traced every scroll container in `components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx`. The modal has exactly
  **four** scrollable regions and **all four already carry `scrollbar-hide`**: the closeout checklist (`overflow-y-auto … scrollbar-hide`),
  the **central tab body that renders the Cash Drawer ledger** (`min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 scrollbar-hide`), the right
  action rail (`… overflow-y-auto … scrollbar-hide`), and the `LiquidGlassModal` content wrapper (`liquid-glass-modal-content
  scrollbar-hide overflow-x-hidden`, here also `!overflow-hidden`). The `.scrollbar-hide` rule in `src/index.css` is complete —
  `scrollbar-width: none` (Firefox), `-ms-overflow-style: none` (legacy Edge), **and** `::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none }`
  (Chromium/WebView2) — so WebKit cannot paint a bar where it is applied. No competing higher-specificity `::-webkit-scrollbar`
  rule targets Z-report descendants (the `.z-report-content *` rule only sets font-smoothing). The Cash Drawer tab body is a plain
  `grid` of cosmetic tiles (`modalInsetClassName` = border/bg/blur only — no `overflow`), so it has no nested scroller of its own.
  `git show HEAD` confirms all three `overflow-y-auto` lines already had `scrollbar-hide` at HEAD; the working tree adds no
  scrollbar change. **The source already complies — there is no bare native scroller to fix.** The live bright rail is most
  consistent with a **stale POS build / un-applied CSS** (build predating the central-scroller `scrollbar-hide`, or an HMR miss).
- **Change (test-only; no source change to `ZReportModal.tsx`).** Did **not** fabricate a no-op edit. Instead added a regression
  guard in `tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts` that locks the policy: **every** `overflow-y-auto` region in the
  modal (both double-quoted and template-literal `className` forms) must include `scrollbar-hide`, and the action rail must keep
  **exactly one** scroll container. This prevents any future edit from reintroducing a bare native scroller.
- Verified (static): `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts`
  → **11 pass / 0 fail**; `git diff --check` (`ZReportModal.tsx`, both Z-report tests, tracker) → clean. No close-day submit, sync,
  data-calculation, tab, label, locale, or business-behaviour change; the round 268 aria-label/native-title cleanup is preserved.
- **Live QA — PASSED (supervisor, Greek / dark, 1282×802).** Opened the Z Report modal from the sidebar **without** submitting
  close day. The right **action rail scrolled correctly** — Submit, Print, Export CSV, and the sales summary were all reachable
  with **no clipped Export button**. On the **Cash Drawer tab** the vertical edge at the central card was confirmed — by comparing
  the **Staff** and **Orders** tabs — to be the **glass panel / card border, not a native scrollbar thumb** (no bright native
  rail). **Staff** and **Orders** tabs also read coherently; the Orders selected tab/filter colors used the **yellow/green
  semantics** and **no native scrollbar was visible**. Closed the modal cleanly. **No Submit-to-Admin, Print, Export, payment,
  order, reservation, check-in, or close-day mutation was performed.** This matches the static analysis: every scroll region in the
  modal already hides its native scrollbar, so the "bright rail" was a misread of the glass border rather than a real leak.

### Supervisor round 272 — New Order Room/Service workflow module-gating verification — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Scope.** Verification only; no source or test change (every gate is already implemented and guarded). Targeted run of the
  orders-page UI suite + tracker append.
- **Source-of-truth gates (`components/OrderDashboard.tsx`).** Availability is driven by acquired modules via
  `hasModule(MODULE_IDS.*)`: Room card `{hasRoomsModule && (`; Room Order action `{hasOrdersModule && (` (Orders);
  Create Reservation action `{hasReservationsModule && (` (Reservations); Check-in is rendered **unconditionally inside the
  Rooms-gated chooser** (no separate Orders/Reservations gate); Service card + Services tab `{hasServicesModule && (` where
  `hasServicesModule = hasAppointmentsModule || hasServiceCatalogModule`, and `showServicesTab={hasServicesModule}` /
  `activeTab === "services" && hasServicesModule`.

  absent from the right column while Summary stays right; the light header/footer/close use translucent white glass and the old

### Supervisor round 280 — Settings modal: de-shouted Greek labels + dynamic sync-health status copy — COMPLETED

- **Live finding (Greek / light, Settings -> This register).** Two confusing/vibecoded things remained: (1) the hub still forced
  many labels into ALL-CAPS -- the left-rail group labels (Daily setup / Device / System) and the This-Register overview eyebrows
  (Register type / Sync / PIN / Allowed actions / Active areas / Technical details), which reads shouted/awkward in Greek across
  EN/EL/DE/FR/IT; and (2) the status card claimed the register was "set up" while the same card showed a red stale sync dot +
  "Sync: Stale" -- a mixed message.
- **Fix (`components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`).**
  - Removed forced `uppercase tracking-wide(r)` from the left-rail group label (`text-[11px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider`
    -> `text-[11px] font-semibold`) and the six This-Register overview eyebrows (`text-xs uppercase tracking-wide` ->
    `text-xs font-semibold`). Normal readable label styling that works in all five languages. **Danger badges untouched** (the
    "Cannot be undone" red badges keep their uppercase emphasis; they sit in the database section, outside the overview).
  - Replaced the CSS-class-string sync-dot IIFE with a clear derived boolean
    `const isSyncHealthy = ['healthy','online','ok','synced','connected','good','live'].some(s => (runtimeSyncHealth||'').toLowerCase().includes(s))`,
    and `const syncToneClass = isSyncHealthy ? 'bg-green-500' : 'bg-red-500'`. The **same boolean** now drives the status card
    title/help: healthy keeps the existing "This register is set up" copy; otherwise (stale/offline/failed/degraded/unknown) it
    shows plain warning copy ("Sync needs attention" / "Run sync to refresh this register before changing devices or payment
    settings."). The red dot + "Sync: Stale" tile are **not hidden**, and **no sync is triggered**.
- **Locales (EN/EL/DE/FR/IT).** Added `settings.deviceSetup.overview.statusTitleWarning` + `statusHelpWarning` (2 keys per locale,
  real translations; Greek is real Greek). Existing healthy `statusTitle` / `statusHelp` keys kept intact.
- **Preserved exactly (no behaviour change).** No sync calls, no settings saves, no data mutation, no navigation rewrite; all
  Settings sections + hardware integrations (PaymentTerminals / Printer / PrintQueue / CashRegister / CallerId / handlers) stay
  wired (existing guards still green). No hover/title regressions (0 `hover:`; only the 3 ConfirmDialog component-prop `title=`
  remain). Touch target sizes unchanged. `syncHealthLabel` still resolves the localized `syncHealth.${runtimeSyncHealth}` key.
- **Tests (`tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`).** Added 3 Round 280 guards: left-rail + admin-overview labels are normal
  case (no uppercase/tracking-wide in the overview slice; danger badges still uppercase outside it); the `isSyncHealthy` boolean
  drives the dot class + the dynamic `statusTitle`/`statusHelp` vs `statusTitleWarning`/`statusHelpWarning`; the new warning keys
  exist in all five locales with Greek a real translation; healthy keys intact. The esbuild tsx parse check passed (no syntax break).
- **Verified.** `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **32 pass / 0 fail**. `git diff --check`
  (modal, test, 5 locales, tracker) -> clean. (The 5 locale files were already dirty from prior rounds; this round adds exactly 2
  keys each.)

### Supervisor round 281 — Settings sync-health: substring matching -> safe normalized exact match (Round 280 correction) — COMPLETED

- **Supervisor rejection.** Round 280 derived `isSyncHealthy` with a substring shortcut --
  `['healthy','online','ok','synced','connected','good','live'].some(s => (runtimeSyncHealth||'').toLowerCase().includes(s))`.
  `.includes('connected')` is **true** for "disconnected" and "not connected", so negative states would be marked healthy and the
  status card would lie again (green "set up" headline while sync is actually down).
- **Fix (`components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`, sync-health derivation only).** Replaced the substring `.some(...includes)`
  with a normalized **exact** match: `const HEALTHY_SYNC_STATES = new Set(['healthy','online','ok','synced','connected','good',
  'live'])` and `const isSyncHealthy = HEALTHY_SYNC_STATES.has((runtimeSyncHealth || '').trim().toLowerCase())`. The runtime value is
  trimmed + lowercased and compared exactly, so only the explicit healthy states pass; `disconnected` / `not_connected` /
  `not connected` / `offline` / `stale` / `failed` / `degraded` / `unknown` are all non-healthy -> red dot + warning copy. The dot
  class, the dynamic status title/help, the locale keys, and all UI copy are **unchanged** from Round 280.
- **Tests.** Updated the Round 280 guard to the exact-match form and added a Round 281 guard that (a) proves no `.includes`-based
  healthy shortcut remains and (b) replicates the source's exact-match check to assert "connected"/"OK"/"  Connected " are healthy
  while "disconnected"/"not connected"/"not_connected"/"offline"/"stale"/"failed"/"degraded"/"unknown" are non-healthy -- plus a
  sanity assertion documenting that the old substring approach WOULD have mis-passed disconnected/not connected.
- **Preserved exactly.** No sync calls, no settings saves, no data mutation, no navigation change; locales untouched this round; no
  hover/title regressions. esbuild tsx parse check passed.
- **Verified.** `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **33 pass / 0 fail**. `git diff --check` (modal, test,
  tracker) -> clean.

### Supervisor round 282 — Orders route: stranded pagination shows empty state while orders exist — COMPLETED

- **Live finding (Greek / light, standalone Orders route).** Opened Orders, saw rows + pagination, tapped **Next**, then the page
  showed "1 order total" but rendered the empty state "No orders found". Cause: after the fetched/filtered result count shrank,
  `currentPage` stayed beyond the available page count, so `paginateOrders` sliced past the array (`(2-1)*20 = 20` into a 1-item
  list -> `[]`), hiding an order that exists.
- **Fix (`pages/OrdersPage.tsx`, pagination derivation only -- no data fetch / filter change).**
  - `paginateOrders` now clamps the page to the data before slicing:
    `const maxPage = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(input.length / pageSize)); const safePage = Math.min(currentPage, maxPage);
    const start = (safePage - 1) * pageSize;` -- a stale page can never produce an empty slice while orders exist (defense so there
    is no empty flash before the state resets).
  - Added a focused clamp effect that resets the stranded page from the result count:
    `useEffect(() => { const maxPage = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(total / pageSize)); if (currentPage > maxPage) setCurrentPage(maxPage); },
    [total, pageSize, currentPage])`. So `currentPage` can never stay above `totalPages`; when `totalPages` is 0/1 it lands on page 1,
    and the empty state renders only when there are truly zero filtered orders.
- **Preserved exactly.** Data fetching, filter semantics, order-type labels/icons, room_service display, payment/order-details
  modals, dashboard/order-taking tabs, login/nav, rooms/services, and locales are unchanged. Touch-first/no-hover behavior and the
  existing pagination labels/buttons (`Math.max(1, prev - 1)` / `Math.min(totalPages, prev + 1)`, disabled logic, aria-labels) are
  untouched. No data mutation (read/paginate only).
- **Test (`tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`).** Added a Round 282 guard that catches the source clamp pattern (the
  `paginateOrders` `safePage` clamp + the `total`-driven reset effect, and asserts the old `(currentPage - 1) * pageSize` slice is
  gone), and behaviourally replicates the clamped pagination + clamp to prove a page-2-over-1-item result still renders that order,
  truly-empty stays empty, and healthy multi-page navigation (page 2 of 2, a genuine 25-item page 2) is unaffected. The existing
  pagination-arrow aria-label test stays green (buttons untouched).
- **Verified.** `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` -> **77 pass / 0 fail**. esbuild tsx parse check passed.
  `git diff --check` (`OrdersPage.tsx`, the test, tracker) -> clean.

### Supervisor round 283 — Orders route: Next did nothing live; refactor to deterministic full-list pagination — COMPLETED

- **Room workflow live QA (Codex/operator, org geminixpro070@gmail.com) -- all correct, NO change made.** New Order -> Room
  chooser is visible (DB source of truth has rooms/orders/reservations acquired + POS-enabled). Room Order opens the focused
  selector with the correct "no occupied rooms with an active folio -> check in first" empty state. Check-in opens the focused
  reserved-room selector with the correct "no reserved rooms" empty state (no RoomsView). Create Reservation opens the available-
  room selector; floor chips filter correctly (Floor 2 = 201/202/203) and selecting Room 201 opens the focused New Reservation form
  with Create disabled until required data. Services is visible (appointments/service_catalog acquired) and opens the New Appointment
  modal with availability + disabled Create. No data submitted/mutated. These match the existing Round 238/255/272/275 guards.
- **Live finding (real bug, standalone Orders route).** Header "21 orders total", footer "Page 1 of 2", Next enabled + localized
  aria-label, but tapping Next (by a11y index and by coordinate) did **nothing** -- it stayed Page 1 and kept first-page rows.
  Round 282 fixed the stranded-empty case but pagination still did not advance live: `orders` state held only the current PAGE
  SLICE, and the page changed only via a refetch side effect (`paginateOrders` was a `fetchOrders` dependency), which did not
  re-slice reliably in the running POS.
- **Fix (`pages/OrdersPage.tsx`, deterministic data model -- no fetch/filter semantics change).**
  - `orders` state now stores the **full filtered/sorted list** (`setOrders(filtered)`, was `setOrders(paginateOrders(filtered))`).
  - Removed the `paginateOrders` useCallback and its `fetchOrders` dependency, so **changing the page no longer triggers a refetch**.
  - Added `const visibleOrders = useMemo(() => { maxPage = max(1, ceil(orders.length/pageSize)); safePage = min(currentPage, maxPage);
    start = (safePage-1)*pageSize; return orders.slice(start, start+pageSize); }, [orders, currentPage, pageSize])` -- the visible page
    is derived at render time, so Next/Previous update `currentPage` and the slice recomputes **immediately**.
  - The list renders `visibleOrders.map(...)`; the **empty state is driven by the full `orders.length`** (not the page slice). The
    Round 282 `currentPage` clamp effect (from `total`) is kept.
- **Preserved exactly.** Fetch + filter semantics, order-type labels/icons (incl. room_service Bed + localized label), module
  gating, payment/order-details modals, dashboard/order-taking tabs, room workflows, services flow, and locales are unchanged.
  Touch-first/no-hover, no native title; the pagination buttons (`Math.max(1, prev-1)` / `Math.min(totalPages, prev+1)`, disabled
  logic, localized aria-labels) are untouched. No data mutation, no DB writes, no commits.
- **Test (`tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`).** Updated the Round 282 clamp assertion to the new `visibleOrders` derivation
  (orders.length), and added a Round 283 guard that **fails if orders are stored/rendered as a stale paginated slice**: it asserts
  `setOrders(filtered)` (not `setOrders(paginateOrders(...))`), that `paginateOrders` is gone, that `visibleOrders` is a useMemo
  over `[orders, currentPage, pageSize]` returning `orders.slice(...)`, that the list maps `visibleOrders` (not `orders`), and that
  the empty state uses `orders.length`; plus a behavioural proof that a full 21-item list yields 20 rows on page 1 and item 21 on
  page 2 derived from `currentPage` alone (no refetch).
- **Verified.** `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` -> **78 pass / 0 fail**. esbuild tsx parse check passed.
  `git diff --check` (`OrdersPage.tsx`, the test, tracker) -> clean.

### Supervisor round 285 — room/service workflows + module-gated availability — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Live finding (Greek / light).** Room workflows (Room Order / Check-in / Create Reservation) are correct and modal-first (no
  RoomsView routing); the New Order chooser + module gating are correct. Two issues on the Service flow: (1) New Appointment time
  slots were selectable **before** staff/service were chosen (implying an availability check that had not run), and (2) the light
  shell read a bit grey/opaque vs the room glass modals.
- **(1) Module gates — verified + pinned (no behaviour change).** `OrderDashboard.tsx`: Room card `{hasRoomsModule && (`; Room Order
  `{hasOrdersModule && (` -> `handleRoomFlowOrder`; Create Reservation `{hasReservationsModule && (` -> `handleRoomFlowReservation`;
  Check-in stays unwrapped inside the Rooms-gated chooser. **Services gate decision: kept `appointments || service_catalog` (OR),
  documented in source.** The Service card opens the embedded AppointmentsView booking flow, but appointment **creation** is
  independently backend-validated in `handleCreateAppointment` (preserved), so a service-catalog org lacking the appointments
  backend cannot persist a booking. Tightening to require the appointments module would hide the Services surface from
  service-catalog orgs without backend certainty about the module taxonomy, so the gate stays OR with the booking-time validation as
  the real guard. **Caveat flagged:** if the backend does NOT gate appointment creation by the appointments module, a follow-up
  should tighten the Service card to `hasAppointmentsModule`.
- **(2) New Appointment availability UX fix (`pages/verticals/salon/AppointmentsView.tsx`).**
  - Added `const hasStaffAndService = Boolean(formData.staffId && formData.serviceId)`. Time slots are now
    `disabled={booked || !hasStaffAndService}` -- not selectable until BOTH staff and service are chosen.
  - Changing staff or service clears `startTime` (`{ ...prev, staffId/serviceId: e.target.value, startTime: '' }`) so availability is
    recalculated in context.
  - Guidance updated from staff-only to **staff + service** via a new key `appointments.modal.selectStaffServiceAvailability`
    ("Choose staff and service first to see real availability"), gated on `!hasStaffAndService`.
  - Pre-context slots read as **muted glass** (`bg-white/30 border-white/40 text-gray-400` light / `bg-white/[0.03] border-white/10
    text-zinc-600` dark) with a centered **min-h-[44px]** touch target; selected stays yellow, booked stays red; Create stays green
    when enabled and muted-zinc when disabled (Round 248, unchanged). **The backend availability validation in
    handleCreateAppointment is unchanged.**
  - **(3) Light glass:** the shell + 5 section panels are already glass (Round 278/279); the new pre-context muted-glass slots add
    transparency. The tappable period/slot button fills were left solid for legibility (Part 3 was "only if needed").
  - Touch-first preserved: no `hover:`/`group-hover:`/native `title=`; the slot grid keeps its hidden scrollbar.
- **Locales (EN/EL/DE/FR/IT).** Added `appointments.modal.selectStaffServiceAvailability` (1 key per locale, real translations,
  Greek is real Greek). Old `selectStaffAvailability` key left in place (harmless).
- **Tests.** `appointments-view-i18n.test.ts`: added Round 285 guards for the staff+service slot gating (disabled-until-both, muted
  glass, 44px, startTime-clear on staff/service change, new guidance key + condition, availability-validation preserved) and the
  new locale key in all five (Greek real). `orders-page-ui.test.ts`: added a Round 285 guard pinning the documented Services OR-gate
  rationale + the strict room-flow gates.
- **Verified.** `node --test tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` -> **26 pass / 0 fail**;
  `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` -> **79 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check` -> clean. esbuild tsx parse OK.
  `git diff --check` (AppointmentsView, OrderDashboard, 5 locales, both tests, tracker) -> clean. No appointment/room/reservation/
  payment was created; no DB writes; no commits.
- **Round 285 follow-up (Codex live QA -- found + fixed).** Live QA found that 09:00 still showed as the **yellow selected** slot
  before staff/service were chosen (the slots were disabled/muted, but `formData.startTime` still defaulted to `'09:00'`, so
  `selected` matched it). Fix (`AppointmentsView.tsx`): the create-form `startTime` initial value + the two reset paths (after create,
  customer-search selection) are now `''` instead of `'09:00'` (date still defaults to today; selectedDay/calendar stay visible), and
  `handleTimeSelect` now hard-guards `if (!formData.staffId || !formData.serviceId || isTimeSlotBooked(time)) return;` so a slot can
  only be picked once staff + service exist -- the yellow selected state can no longer appear before real availability context. Tests:
  updated the existing "no hidden default time" guard to pin `startTime: ''` (was `'09:00'`) and strengthened the Round 285 guard for
  the handleTimeSelect staff+service guard + no-`09:00`-default. `node --test tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` ->
  **26 pass / 0 fail**; `npm run type-check` -> clean; `git diff --check` -> clean. No data created/mutated; no commits.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex, running POS).** New Order chooser exposes the acquired **room/service** modules alongside Delivery /
  Pickup / Table. Room -> **Room Order** stays in the focused room selector with the correct empty state (no checked-in rooms with
  open folios). Room -> **Check-in** stays in the focused selector with the correct empty state (no reserved rooms ready for
  check-in). Room -> **Create Reservation** opens the available-room picker with the floor selector, and selecting **Room 101** opens
  the New Reservation modal with room context, required customer name, **red Cancel**, and **Create Reservation disabled** until
  required data exists. **Service** from New Order opens **New Appointment** with staff/service empty, **time slots disabled** with
  the instruction message, and **Create disabled** (no preselected 09:00). **No live data was created, checked in, charged, printed,
  paid, cancelled, or closed.** Previously-verified commands stayed clean (appointments-view-i18n + orders-page-ui tests,
  `npm run type-check`, `git diff --check`).

### Supervisor round 288 — Printer live preview native scrollbar leak — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Live evidence (Codex, running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** Settings -> Εκτυπωτής -> Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή ->
  Προσθήκη Εκτυπωτή rendered the receipt **Live Preview** with a bright **native** vertical scrollbar on the right edge.
- **Root cause.** The preview viewport (`PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`) hides its own rail with `overflow-auto scrollbar-hide`,
  and the **image** branch additionally masks a baked-in strip with a white `aria-hidden` edge mask. But the **HTML** branch
  renders `<iframe srcDoc={preview.html}>` — an iframe hosts its **own** document, so the parent viewport's `scrollbar-hide`
  utility cannot reach the iframe body's scrollbar. With a receipt taller than the 500px iframe, that document showed a native rail.
- **Fix (UI-only, scoped to the printer preview).** Added a module-level `PREVIEW_HIDE_SCROLLBAR_STYLE` (`<style>` with
  `html,body{scrollbar-width:none;-ms-overflow-style:none;}` + `html::-webkit-scrollbar,body::-webkit-scrollbar{...display:none;}`)
  and prepended it to the iframe payload: `srcDoc={PREVIEW_HIDE_SCROLLBAR_STYLE + preview.html}`. Covers Firefox, legacy Edge,
  and the WebKit/Chromium (WebView2) engine Tauri uses on Windows. The CSS suppresses scrollbar **chrome only** — no
  `overflow:hidden`, no margin reset — so **scrolling, inspectability, glass styling, the 500px preview dimensions, printer
  discovery / save / preview-data fetch / receipt-rendering semantics are all unchanged**. The image branch (viewport
  `scrollbar-hide` + white edge mask) is untouched.
- **Verification.** Focused renderer file `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **35 pass / 0 fail** (2 new Round 288
  guards: the iframe injects the cross-engine scrollbar-hiding `<style>` into `srcDoc` and never falls back to a raw
  `srcDoc={preview.html}`, preserving `sandbox`/min-height + the image branch's edge mask; the live-preview viewport keeps
  `scrollbar-hide`). `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (`PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`, `settings-modal-ui.test.ts`,
  tracker) -> clean. No unrelated UI changes; no data actions; no commits. (Pre-existing parity failures from other
  uncommitted branch work — deleted `CustomTitleBar.tsx`, `ExpenseModal`/`EditCustomerInfoModal`/`MenuCart`/`ServiceCatalogView`
  /`OrderDashboard` palette tests — are unrelated; none reference `PrinterSettingsModal`.)
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex, running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802, no data submitted).** Reopened Settings ->
  Εκτυπωτής -> Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή -> Προσθήκη Εκτυπωτή **fresh** after the iframe `srcDoc` fix: the receipt **Live
  Preview** no longer shows the bright native vertical scrollbar, scrolling inside the receipt preview still works, and no
  native rail reappeared. No data actions, printer save, discovery, or print performed.

### Supervisor round 289 — PaymentTerminalsPage touch-first palette + premium glass cleanup — STATIC VERIFIED / LIVE SETTINGS NO-REGRESSION PASSED / STANDALONE ROUTE NOT REACHABLE

- **Finding.** `src/renderer/pages/PaymentTerminalsPage.tsx` (a standalone "Card machines" page component, distinct from
  the already-cleaned `PaymentTerminalsSection` settings embed of Round 229) still carried pre-palette chrome: blue
  Discover/Test controls, a black/white inverted Refresh, native DOM `title=` tooltips, hover-only classes, and flat
  grey/white less-rounded cards.
- **Reachability (supervisor source search).** `rg PaymentTerminalsPage` finds **only the file itself** — this standalone
  page is **not currently imported or reachable** through the app routes. The live "Settings > Card machines" workflow
  renders `src/renderer/components/ecr/PaymentTerminalsSection.tsx` instead. So this round hardens a not-yet-wired
  component: the cleanup is correct for if/when it is mounted, but **live QA of the standalone page was not possible and is
  not claimed**. (`PaymentTerminalsSection` was already cleaned in Round 229 and is unchanged here.)
- **Fix (UI-only; IPC/data/handlers/gating unchanged).**
  1. **Tooltips.** Removed all 5 native `title=` (refresh / discover / add / empty-discover / test). Refresh keeps its
     `aria-label`; the discover/add/test disabled-reason now folds into `aria-label` **only when actually disabled**
     (`disabled && message ? message : undefined`) — no hover-dependent tooltip. Zero `title=` remain in the file.
  2. **Hover.** Removed every `hover:`/`hover:scale`/`transition-colors` and replaced with `transition-all` +
     `active:scale-95` / `active:bg-*` touch feedback. Zero `hover:` remain.
  3. **Palette.** Refresh -> amber-glass icon button (shared Round 286 pattern); Discover (header + empty state) ->
     amber-glass text button; Add Terminal -> semantic solid green (`border-green-500 bg-green-600 active:bg-green-700`);
     Test payment -> neutral zinc glass (kept secondary so the header is not a wall of yellow); loading spinner blue ->
     amber. Online green / Offline-Error red / disconnected grey (`#22c55e`/`#ef4444`/`#6b7280`) preserved.
  4. **Glass panels.** StatsCard + Test-Payment panel -> `rounded-2xl border backdrop-blur-sm` translucent glass; empty
     state -> `rounded-3xl` glass; stat icon wrapper `rounded-lg` -> `rounded-xl`. The flat `bg-gray-800/50`/`bg-white`
     admin boxes are gone. Works in both standalone and embedded (transparent) modes.
  5. **Modal contract preserved.** `TerminalConfigModal` (red Cancel, green Add/Save, Round-227 green glass switch,
     footer clearance) was **not** modified; re-asserted read-only in the new test and still covered by Round 228.

### Supervisor round 290 — Settings > Devices cash-register switches: yellow ON -> shared green premium glass — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

    with the exact shared track string: `w-[58px] h-[34px]`, neutral grey glass OFF (`bg-gray-200/80` / `dark:bg-white/10`,
    `shadow-inner`), **green ON** (`peer-checked:bg-green-500` + `peer-checked:shadow-green-500/40` glow), clean white knob

    (`translate-x-[20px]` symmetric travel), amber `focus-visible:ring-amber-400/60`, `active:scale-95`. `role="switch"`,
    `aria-checked`, `aria-label`, and `handleFiscalPrintToggle` are byte-for-byte unchanged.
  - The yellow-gradient ON tokens (`from-yellow-300` / `to-yellow-500` / `shadow-yellow-500`) are fully gone from the file.
    The **Print-Mode SELECTION toggles** (segmented `min-h-[44px]` button cards) legitimately keep yellow-selected -- those
    are selection states, not on/off switches, and were intentionally left untouched.

### Supervisor round 293 — Settings > Connection action bar: uneven/floating buttons -> balanced centered touch layout — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Greek UI, Settings > Connection).** The action area read as assembled/vibecoded: "Επικόλληση Κωδικού
  Σύνδεσης" and "Δοκιμή" sat on one row, "Συγχρονισμός ρυθμίσεων" dropped to a second row, and the green "Αποθήκευση"
  floated separately at the right. It did not satisfy the user's centered / same-size touch-button requirement.
- **Fix (UI layout only; handlers/translations/state/auth/sync/data ops unchanged).** Restyled only the connection action
  area (`data-connection-action-bar` / `data-connection-secondary-actions` / `data-connection-primary-action`) in
  `src/renderer/components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`:
  - The action bar is now a stable column (`flex flex-col gap-3 pt-2`).
  - The three secondary actions (Paste / Test-when-allowed / Sync) are a responsive flex (`flex flex-col gap-2 sm:flex-row
    sm:items-stretch`); each button is `flex-1 min-h-[44px] items-center justify-center text-center leading-tight` -- so
    they are equal-width, same-height, centered, wrap Greek neatly, and stack full-width on narrow screens. `flex-1`
    divides equally whether 2 or 3 buttons render, so it never wraps unevenly. They stay neutral glass
    (`liquidGlassModalButton('secondary','md')`).
  - Green Save now sits in its own **centered** primary slot (`data-connection-primary-action className="flex
    justify-center"`) as `SAVE_BTN_MD + ' min-h-[44px] w-full sm:w-auto sm:min-w-[240px]'` -- 44px+, full-width on narrow,
    a substantial centered button on desktop (no longer a floating leftover).
  - Palette preserved: neutral secondary glass, green save, yellow accents where the system uses them; no blue/purple/cyan;
    no hover utilities (active/tap feedback only); no native title.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` (connection action area only);
  `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` (new Round 293 guard + updated the Round 195 guard, which pinned the old
  `justify-between` / `sm:ml-auto` layout, to the new stacked/centered markup).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **41 pass / 0 fail** (new Round 293 guard
  proves the balanced/centered/same-height layout, the deliberate centered Save slot, preserved handlers/aria, and no

  (`ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`, `settings-modal-ui.test.ts`, tracker) -> clean. No behaviour/handlers/i18n/auth/sync
  changes; no POS actions run; no commits.
