### Supervisor round 294 — Settings > Card Machines Add Terminal modal: sticky footer overlap -> wider modal + compact stack + body clearance — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Greek UI, 1282x802, Settings > Card Machines > Configure > Add Terminal).** On first open the pinned
  glass footer (Cancel / Add) overlapped the lower Settings section -- the label "Χρονικό Όριο Συναλλαγής" (Transaction
  Timeout) was visibly cut behind the footer before scrolling. The scrollable form body only reserved `pb-6` (24px), far
  less than the ~72px sticky footer, so the previous guards treating `pb-6` as enough clearance were wrong.
- **First implementation FAILED live QA.** The initial fix only increased the form body bottom padding `pb-6` -> **`pb-28`**
  (112px). Supervisor re-checked after a fresh close/reopen: the extra padding helped bottom scrolling but did **not** fix
  the first-open overlap -- the Settings label was still cut behind the footer on first open.
- **Width follow-up — PARTIAL FAIL (live QA).** Root cause of the first failure (supervisor inspection): the modal forced
  `className="!max-w-lg"`, overriding `size="md"` down to ~512px, so the Greek title "Προσθήκη Τερματικού Πληρωμών" wrapped to
  two lines, ate header height, and cramped the first-open body. Removing `!max-w-lg` (so `size="md"` drives `max-w-2xl`,
  ~672px) made the title fit one line and the columns breathe -- but live QA after a fresh reopen still found the first
  Transaction Timeout **input** clipped by the footer on first open (the label now showed; the input was still cut). Width +
  `pb-28` alone were not enough.
- **Final fix (layout/class only; no save/connect/discover/test/payment/data/handler/validation/translation/prop changes).**
  In `src/renderer/components/ecr/TerminalConfigModal.tsx`: (a) removed the forced-narrow `className="!max-w-lg"` so `size="md"`
  drives `max-w-2xl`; (b) **compacted the form vertical stack `space-y-6` -> `space-y-4`** so the first Settings field
  (label + input) clears the footer at 1282x802 on first open; (c) kept `pb-28` footer clearance. Touch targets are unchanged
  (only the inter-field gap shrank, not control sizes), the form is not visually cramped (inner sections already used
  `space-y-4`), and the glass modal, sticky footer shelf, red Cancel, green Add/Save (`type="submit"
  form="terminal-config-form"`), green switch, and no-hover behaviour are all unchanged. No `contentClassName` was needed.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/ecr/TerminalConfigModal.tsx` (removed `!max-w-lg`; form `space-y-6` -> `space-y-4`;
  `pb-28`; short comment); `tests/renderer/payment-terminals-ui.test.ts` (Round 289 guard now pins `space-y-4 pb-28` and the
  stale "NOT modified this round" wording was removed; the Round 294 guard proves not-forced-narrow (no `!max-w-lg`, `size="md"`),
  the compact `space-y-4` stack with `>= pb-24` clearance, no lingering `space-y-6`/`pb-6`, footer colours/handlers/`form=`
  binding, glass shelf, no hover / no off-theme colour / only the one LiquidGlassModal heading `title` prop);
  `tests/renderer/terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts` (its Round 228 guard, supervisor-authorised, updated to `space-y-4 pb-28`).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/payment-terminals-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts`
  -> **14 pass / 0 fail** (updated Round 294 + Round 289; Round 228 still green). `npm run type-check` -> clean.
  `git diff --check` (`TerminalConfigModal.tsx`, `payment-terminals-ui.test.ts`, `terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts`, tracker)
  -> clean. No data/handler/i18n changes; no POS actions run; no commits.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex, running POS).** Add Terminal title renders on one line and the Transaction Timeout field clears
  the footer on first open.

### Supervisor round 295 — Settings switch unification: one shared fixed-geometry POSGlassSwitch — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, user screenshot, Settings > Devices / cash register).** The toggle switches still rendered at visibly
  different apparent geometry. Root cause: each surface built its own switch -- a `peer` + pseudo-element (`after:`) track in
  ConnectionSettingsModal/TerminalConfigModal, a native checkbox, and a bespoke `<button>` for Auto Fiscal Print -- so
  button/native/pseudo differences made nominally-58x34 switches look uneven. Treated as a shared-component bug.
- **Fix (UI component + migration only; handlers/aria/state/translations/validation/data logic all preserved).**
  - **New shared `POSGlassSwitch`** in `src/renderer/components/ui/pos-glass-components.tsx`: ONE fixed visible geometry
    everywhere -- a 58x34 pill, 28px white knob, 3px inset, 24px travel -- rendered as a `button[role=switch]` (no
    peer/pseudo-element). ON = semantic green glass + soft green glow; OFF = neutral grey glass (light + dark); amber
    keyboard-focus ring (`group-focus-visible`); active tap scale; no hover; never yellow. A transparent `min-h-[44px]
    min-w-[44px]` touch target wraps the fixed pill (the pill never resizes). `POSGlassToggle` now delegates to it (its old
    `h-8 w-14` geometry is gone).
  - **ConnectionSettingsModal:** removed the local `switchTrackClass`; all 8 Settings switches (terminal audio / receipt
    auto-print / session-timeout / scale / display / scanner / card-reader / loyalty) now render `<POSGlassSwitch>`,
    preserving each `checked`, `onChange` (boolean passed straight through), `aria-labelledby`, layout, and translations.
  - **CashRegisterSection:** removed the local cash-register switch-track class and the inline Auto Fiscal Print
    `button[role=switch]`; Auto Fiscal Print + Set-as-default + Enabled now render `<POSGlassSwitch>`, preserving
    `handleFiscalPrintToggle`, `updateForm({is_default/enabled})`, aria-labels, and disabled behaviour. The Print-Mode
    SELECTION toggles (segmented cards) are untouched (yellow-selected is a selection state, not a switch).
  - **TerminalConfigModal:** removed the local `SWITCH_TRACK_CLASS`; print-on-terminal / default / enabled now render
    `<POSGlassSwitch>` (ids + setters preserved); form submit + the Round 294 modal width (`size="md"`, no `!max-w-lg`) and
    spacing (`space-y-4 pb-28`) are unchanged.
- **Changed files.** `pos-glass-components.tsx` (new `POSGlassSwitch`; `POSGlassToggle` delegates); `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx`,
  `CashRegisterSection.tsx`, `TerminalConfigModal.tsx` (migrated, local switch classes removed);
  `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` (Round 196/197/227 updated to the shared switch; Round 290/291 consolidated to a
  migration guard; new Round 295 pinning the single source of truth + exact 58x34/28/3/24 geometry + no old `h-8 w-14` toggle
  geometry + no local switch classes + no yellow ON + no hover + handlers/aria preserved); `payment-terminals-ui.test.ts` and
  `terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts` (TerminalConfigModal switch assertions updated to the shared switch, Round 294 width/spacing
  kept).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/payment-terminals-ui.test.ts
  tests/renderer/terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts` -> **54 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check`
  (the 4 source files + 3 test files + tracker) -> clean. No behaviour/handler/i18n/validation changes; no POS actions run; no commits.
- **Accessibility follow-up (Codex rejection of the first migration).** Codex flagged that the five hardware/peripheral
  switches (scale, customer display, serial scanner, card reader/MSR, loyalty/NFC) migrated to `button[role=switch]` with NO
  accessible name (they had been unnamed siblings of a title column). Fix: each row's visible title `<span>` got a stable id
  (`peripheral-scale-label` / `peripheral-display-label` / `peripheral-scanner-label` / `peripheral-card-reader-label` /
  `peripheral-loyalty-reader-label`) and each matching `POSGlassSwitch` is named via `aria-labelledby` to it -- the same
  pattern the audio / receipt / session-timeout switches already used. No visual/behaviour change; the Round 227/295 test now
  asserts all five named switches. (Re-verified: 54 pass / 0 fail; type-check clean; diff-check clean.)
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex, running POS, 1282x802, Greek UI, dark theme, no data submitted).** Settings > Devices: Scale,
  Customer Display, Serial Barcode Scanner, Card Reader (MSR), and Loyalty/NFC switches all show the same fixed glass switch
  geometry. In the same scrolled section the Auto Fiscal Print ON switch matches the family and no longer looks larger/smaller
  than the MSR/NFC switches. The Add Device form was opened for visual QA only: Set-as-default OFF and Enabled ON switches also
  match the family. No Save, Add Device submit, Test Connection, Connect/Disconnect, or hardware action was clicked.

### Supervisor round 297 — Z Report clarity redesign — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Direction change.** The round began as a narrow footer/status-strip overlap fix (the bottom strip cropped the centre
  Overview cards; root cause was the 3-column workbench grid's implicit `grid-auto-rows: auto` letting the tallest column
  overflow behind the `shrink-0` strip). The user then asked for a full **clarity redesign** instead of a patch, so the
  3-column dashboard + overlapping strip are replaced outright by a single, scannable vertical flow (the overlap is fixed by
  construction -- the strip no longer exists as a separate overlay).
- **Design objective.** The first screen now answers three questions in order: (1) how much money, (2) is the day
  ready/blocked + what to press next, (3) the detail ledgers. Reduced density, short headings, big numbers, simple chips,
  progressive disclosure for the ledgers.
- **New structure (UI only; in `src/renderer/components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx`).**
  1. **Header** (unchanged command header): title + business date + live/historical pill + compact refresh / print / export /
     close actions (aria-labelled, no native `title=`).
  2. **Summary strip** (`data-z-report-summary-strip`, `shrink-0`): five large metric cards -- **Total sales, Expected cash,
     Variance, Orders, Issues** -- icon + concise label + big number; variance/issues go amber when non-zero, green when clean.
  3. **Decision card** (`data-z-report-decision-card`, `shrink-0`): the ready/needs-review status + a plain "next step" hint,
     a single prominent action **gated on `closeoutReady`** (see correction below), and -- when blocked -- a preview of the
     **top 3** blocking orders. The old bottom status strip is folded in here, so it can never overlap content.
- **Correction (live QA rejection -- contradictory action).** Codex caught that when the day was NOT ready for a non-blocker
  reason (e.g. an unreconciled cash drawer, so `paymentBlockers` is empty), the green Submit button stayed **enabled** while
  the card copy said "fix/review first" -- telling the cashier to fix yet offering the submit. The decision-card action is now
  strictly gated:
  - `canExecuteZReport && closeoutReady` -> the **green Submit** (`handleSubmitReport`, with the exact existing
    `disabled={submitting || loading || Boolean(resolvingBlockerKey) || paymentBlockers.length > 0}` gating).
  - `canExecuteZReport && !closeoutReady` -> **no submit**; instead an **amber "Review issues"** touch button wired to
    `setActiveTab('issues')` (new key `modals.zReport.clarity.reviewIssues`).
  - `!canExecuteZReport` -> the existing main-terminal / loading notice.
  - The top-3 blocker preview stays; the now-redundant "See the Issues tab" button was removed (the primary Review-issues
    action already points there). `handleSubmitReport` / print / export / data loading / business logic are unchanged.
  4. **Details tabs** (`data-z-report-details`, `flex-1 min-h-0`): **Money / Staff / Orders / Issues** over one hidden-scrollbar
     scroll body (`data-z-report-center-scroll`, `overflow-y-auto pb-6 scroll-pb-6 scrollbar-hide`). Money = reconciliation
     breakdown + drawer & expense ledgers; Staff/Orders reuse the existing ledgers + filters; Issues folds in the closeout
     checklist, the full `UnsettledPaymentBlockersPanel`, and the business-day picker. The Issues tab shows a count badge.
- **Behaviour preserved (verbatim).** `handleSubmitReport` / `handlePrintReport` / `handleExportReport` /
  `handleResolveBlocker` / `handleRefreshReport`, the date selection, order-type & payment filters, blocker resolution safety,
  data loading, and the live auto-refresh are all unchanged -- only the presentation was restructured. Palette stays
  black/white/grey/yellow + green/red/amber (no blue); touch-first, no hover-only effects, no native `title=`.
- **i18n.** All new visible copy uses `t(..., { defaultValue })`. Added `modals.zReport.clarity.*` (`totalSales`,
  `expectedCash`, `issues`, `nextStep`, `readyHint`, `reviewHint`, `tabMoney`, `tabIssues`, and the correction's
  `reviewIssues`) to **all five POS locales** (en/el/de/fr/it) with real translations (Greek is real Greek, not English).
  `locale:parity` passes (0 missing / 0 extra). The earlier `seeIssuesTab` key remains in all locales (now unused in code).
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx` (render restructured; tab state remapped to
  money/staff/orders/issues; removed now-unused `CreditCard` import); `src/locales/{en,el,de,fr,it}.json` (new
  `modals.zReport.clarity.*`); `tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts` (rewritten for the new structure -- 9
  guards: vertical-flow order, summary strip, decision card + gating + top-3 blockers, details tabs + single hidden scroll
  body, scrollbar-hide policy, handlers/filters preserved, no native title, portaled shell, and new copy is translation-keyed
  in all five locales); `tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts` (marker list updated to the new structure).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts`
  -> **14 pass / 0 fail** (the decision-card guard now asserts submit is the ready-only path and the not-ready branch is a
  non-submit Review-issues jump). `npm run type-check` -> clean. `npm run locale:parity` -> **passed** (0 missing / 0 extra).
  `git diff --check` (`ZReportModal.tsx`, the two Z-report test files, the five locale files, tracker) -> clean.
  (`i18n-labels.test.ts` fails only under standalone `node --test` with a pre-existing `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` runtime-import
  quirk -- unrelated to this round; it runs under the esbuild parity bundler.) No close-day / submit executed; no POS actions
  run; no commits.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex, running POS, Greek / light theme, 1282x802, no data submitted).**
  - POS was reloaded after HMR and recovered cleanly to login / dashboard.
  - Z Report opened to a simple vertical flow: summary metrics -> decision card -> details tabs.
  - With the report **not ready** (`Χρειάζεται έλεγχο`, a cash-drawer issue), the green submit button is **not shown**; the
    localized amber action is `Έλεγχος Θεμάτων`. Tapping it switches to the **Issues** tab and shows the issue list.
  - No native bright scrollbar was visible, and the former footer/status overlap is gone.
  - No submit / print / export / close-day action was clicked; no data mutation was performed.
- **Local verification (Codex, after the correction).**
  - `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts` -> **14 pass / 0 fail**.
  - `npm run type-check` -> clean.

### Supervisor round 298 — Split Payment modal footer/content overlap — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, 1282x802, Greek / light).** Open Orders -> existing order -> View -> Split Payment:
  the sticky modal footer (receipt-mode toggle / totals / green Confirm) covered the lower-right person/payment card --
  "Άτομο 2" started **under** the footer, so the last card could not be clearly read/tapped on first open. UI layout only.
- **Root cause.** The split modal's single scroll region (shared by both "By Amount" and "By Items" tabs) reserved only
  `pb-4` (16px) of bottom clearance -- far less than the modal footer's height -- so the last person / add-person card sat
  behind the footer and could not scroll clear. (Same class of bug as Round 294/297: padding that doesn't actually clear a
  pinned footer.)
- **First pass (padding only) -- FAILED live QA.** The initial fix raised the shared scroll region's clearance from `pb-4`
  to **`pb-24 scroll-pb-24`**. Codex re-checked the running POS: the By-Product "Άτομο 2" card was **still clipped** at the
  footer/body boundary, and **PageDown did not move the content** -- i.e. the modal was overflowing the viewport rather than
  scrolling, so more padding could never reveal the card. `pb-24` is necessary but not sufficient.
- **Root cause (corrected).** At 1282x802 the `.liquid-glass-modal-shell` max-height (92vh) + 2rem content padding left the
  bounded body too short for the tall By-Items "People" column, so the modal content exceeded the available height and the
  second person card sat at the body/footer boundary with no scrollable overflow.
- **Second correction -- compact, taller-capped first-open layout (layout/spacing only; no
  payment/split/bridge/confirmation/item-assignment/settlement/i18n changes).** In
  `src/renderer/components/modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx`:
  - Shell `!max-w-4xl` -> **`!max-w-4xl !max-h-[96vh]`** (lets the modal use more viewport height so the body has room).
  - contentClassName gains **`!px-6 !py-5`** (reduces the shell's default 2rem padding, reclaiming vertical space).
  - Tighter rhythm: outer column `space-y-4` -> **`space-y-3`**; order-total header `text-3xl` -> **`text-2xl`** (`mb-1` ->
    `mb-0.5`); tab buttons `py-2.5` -> **`py-2`**; By-Items two-column grid `gap-4` -> **`gap-3`**.
  - Kept the single hidden-scroll body **`min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide pb-24 scroll-pb-24`** (no nested
    competing scrollers, no visible native rail). Glass style, rounded cards, yellow selected segments, semantic green
    Confirm, active/tap feedback, and the no-`hover:` / no-native-`title=` policy are all unchanged.
- **Second correction -- FAILED live QA.** Codex re-checked the running POS after the compact shell/header pass. The modal
  was better, but at 1282x802 Greek/light the By-Product (By Items) first open STILL clipped the lower person card
  (`Άτομο 2`) at the footer/body boundary -- more visible than before, but its lower details were still cut off, so it still
  read unfinished. Shell/header/padding shaving had reached its limit; do not chase this with more of it.
- **Root cause (final).** The real height hog is the by-items "People" cards themselves: in the initial empty state BOTH
  people have no assigned items and 0,00 €, yet each card still rendered the full `renderPortionDetails` settlement stack
  (subtotal + discount + payable rows, the `Collected By` owner, and the discount-editor button). Two unnecessarily tall
  empty cards cannot both clear the footer no matter how much the shell/header is shaved.
- **Third correction -- compact empty by-items person cards (UI only; no payment/split/bridge/handler/item-assignment/
  confirmation/settlement/locale changes).** In `src/renderer/components/modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx`:
  - Added a module-level predicate `isEmptyByItemsPortion(portion)` = `status === 'draft' && items.length === 0 &&
    amount <= 0.009 && discountAmount <= 0.009`.
  - The by-items "People" card now branches on it. An empty/compact portion renders a SHORT card only: the person label,
    the italic `No items assigned` line (`splitPayment.noItems`), the `0,00 €` amount, and the cash/card `MethodToggle`. It
    no longer renders the full `renderPortionDetails` subtotal/discount/payable/`Collected By`/discount-editor stack.
  - The moment a portion has assigned items, a positive amount, a discount, or a paid|processing state, it flips back to the
    existing FULL details + payment controls, so no real functionality is removed from active/assigned portions.
  - Result: the two initial empty People cards are short enough to fit above the footer on first open. The footer-safe scroll
    body (`pb-24 scroll-pb-24`) and the second-pass shell/header compaction stay in place as belt-and-braces, but the compact
    empty-card state is the actual fix. Glass style, rounded cards, yellow selected segments, semantic green Confirm, no
    `hover:` utilities, no native `title=` tooltips, and the hidden touch scrollbar are unchanged.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx` (new `isEmptyByItemsPortion` predicate + a
  compact/full branch in the by-items "People" card; the second-pass shell max-h / reduced padding / tighter rhythm remain);
  `tests/renderer/split-payment-modal-ui.test.ts` (a new Round 298 third-correction guard pins the compact empty-card branch
  -- predicate shape, the compact branch shows label/`No items assigned`/`0,00 €`/`MethodToggle` and does NOT call
  `renderPortionDetails`, the full else-branch keeps it, exactly two `renderPortionDetails(portion)` call sites -- and records
  that the first pass and second correction both failed live QA); `planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md` (this entry).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/split-payment-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **8 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check`
  -> clean. `git diff --check -- src/renderer/components/modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx tests/renderer/split-payment-modal-ui.test.ts planning/POS_UI_POLISH_TASKS.md`
  -> clean. No payment/split/settlement logic touched; no POS actions run; no commits.
- **Codex live re-QA — PASSED (running POS, 1282x802, Greek/light).** By Product first open now shows both empty person
  cards plus the Add Person row fully above the footer; no native scrollbar is visible; Confirm remains disabled because no
  items are assigned.

### Supervisor round 299 — Navigation sidebar scroll hint loses standalone yellow dash — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, light theme, Orders page, 1282x802).** The collapsed left nav rail showed a
  standalone yellow dash between the calendar icon and the Z icon (while the active route was the blue grid icon). Source:
  the Round 232 module-scroll-hint caps each rendered a `<span class="h-1 w-6 ... bg-amber-400/80 shadow-...">` pill inside
  the `data-navigation-scroll-hint-top` / `-bottom` fade. In the collapsed rail that amber pill read like a broken inactive
  nav item / selected indicator -- violating the selected-only neon rule (inactive nav area must be black/white/neutral;
  only the active route shows distinct neon colour).
- **Fix (UI only; scroll behaviour unchanged).** In `src/renderer/components/NavigationSidebar.tsx`, both scroll-hint caps
  now render **only** the neutral black/white edge-fade overlay (`bg-gradient-to-b|t from-black|white via .../75
  to-transparent`); the amber pill `<span>` (and the now-pointless `flex justify-center` / `items-end justify-center`
  centering) were removed. The fade still signals "more list to scroll", but there is no coloured dash. Hidden scrollbar,
  module scroll, drag/reorder drop-slots, pinned Check In / Z Report / Settings / Logout, selected-only neon active icon,
  neutral inactive icons, no hover, and no native `title=` are all unchanged. (The drag drop-slot `h-1 w-8 bg-amber-400/85`
  and the coming-soon badge dot are separate elements, not scroll-hint caps, and were left untouched.)
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/NavigationSidebar.tsx` (removed the amber pill span from both caps + the stale
  comment); `tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` (the Round 232/299 guard no longer expects `bg-amber-400/80` --
  it now asserts each cap is a neutral gradient fade with no inner `<span>` and no `bg-amber`/`text-amber`/`yellow`, and that
  no `h-1 w-6 ... bg-amber` pill remains in the affordance frame; documents that Round 299 live QA removed the dash because
  it looked like an inactive nav item).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` -> **11 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check`
  -> clean. `git diff --check` (`NavigationSidebar.tsx`, `navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts`, tracker) -> clean. No behaviour
  changes; no POS actions run; no commits.
- **Codex live QA — PASSED (running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** The standalone yellow dash between the calendar and Z
  icons is gone. The module-scroll affordance is only a neutral fade, inactive rail icons remain neutral, the active Orders
  route remains blue neon, and the Orders tabs keep their approved green/grey treatment. No order, Z Report, or data action
  was submitted.

### Supervisor round 300 — Z Report modal second-pass clarity redesign — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (user live feedback).** The Round 297 Z Report was still too text-heavy: the header competed with many actions +
  long business-window/terminal text; five equal metric cards + the tabs read like a dashboard rather than a guided
  close-day flow. The cashier should instantly see (1) is the day ready, (2) what money matters, (3) what must be fixed,
  (4) where the final close-day action is.
- **Fix (UI/presentation only; report generation / submit / print / export / blocker resolution / date selection / filters /
    live refresh / data logic all unchanged).** In `src/renderer/components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx`:
  - **Quieter header.** Title + date + a small at-a-glance status pill (`data-z-report-header-status`, green when ready /
    amber when needs-review). The live-mode label, business window, and terminal are demoted to small neutral meta chips
    instead of dominating the first line. Refresh / Print / Export / Close stay compact centered icon+text controls with
    aria-labels (no native `title=`).
  - **Three primary cards, not five.** The summary strip is now **Sales / Expected cash / Issues** in a 3-column grid
    (`grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3`) so the first screen breathes; **Orders** moved to a quiet chip
    (`data-z-report-orders-chip`) and **Variance** moved off the strip (it lives in the Money tab).
  - **Status hero + single gated action (kept from the Round 297 correction).** The decision card shows Ready-to-close vs
    Needs-review with one short hint; **green Submit only when `closeoutReady`**, otherwise an amber "Review issues" action
    that jumps to the Review tab. No enabled green submit unless ready. Top-3 blockers still preview when present.
  - **Step tabs: Review / Money / Staff / Orders.** The old Issues tab became **Review** (first tab, with the issue-count
    badge) and the modal now **defaults to Review on open** (guided start). The Review tab is a concise **to-do list** -- one
    row per closeout check, with the long description shown only where action is still needed -- plus the payment-blocker
    panel (only when needed) and the business-day picker. Money keeps the cash equation + drawer/expense ledgers. All
    existing info stays accessible behind tabs; the single hidden-scrollbar scroll body + footer clearance are unchanged.
- **i18n.** Added `modals.zReport.clarity.tabReview` to all five POS locales (en "Review" / el "Έλεγχος" / de "Prüfen" /
    fr "Vérifier" / it "Verifica"); all new copy is `t(..., { defaultValue })`. No raw visible literals in JSX. `locale:parity`
    passes (6169 keys, 0 missing/extra). (The now-unused `clarity.tabIssues` key is left in all locales -- harmless, parity-safe.)
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx` (header/strip/tabs/review restructure; tab state
    `issues` -> `review`, default `review`); `src/locales/{en,el,de,fr,it}.json` (new `clarity.tabReview`);
    `tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts` (updated the 4 Round 297 structure guards -> Round 300: 3 cards +
    orders chip, Review/Money/Staff/Orders tabs, default-to-Review, review-tab jump); `tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts`
    (markers unchanged -- still green).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts`
    -> **14 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check` -> clean. `npm run locale:parity` -> **passed** (6169 keys; 0 missing / 0
    extra / 0 support errors in de/el/fr/it). `git diff --check` (modal + 5 locales + 2 test files + tracker) -> clean. No
    report/submit/data logic touched; no POS actions run; no commits.
- **Live QA — PASSED (Codex, running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802, no data submitted).** After a hard renderer reload and
    login with the test PIN, Z Report opened with the 3 primary cards + Orders chip, a clearer status hero, and the
    Review / Money / Staff / Orders tabs; Review showed the concise to-do list; Money and Orders tabs switched correctly;
    the Review scroll worked with no visible native scrollbar. No Submit, Print, Export CSV, close-day, payment resolution,
    or data mutation was clicked.
- **Round 300 cleanup (post-QA, comments/test-names only).** Updated the stale Round 297 "five metric cards" / "Issues tab"
    wording in `ZReportModal.tsx` comments and `z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts` test names/header to the current
    structure (3 primary cards + Orders chip; Review / Money / Staff / Orders). No runtime/behaviour/class/locale changes;
    14 pass / 0 fail; `git diff --check` clean.

### Supervisor round 301 — Printer receipt-action switches migrate to shared POSGlassSwitch — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** Settings -> Printer -> "Διαμόρφωση Εκτυπωτή"
    (PrinterSettingsModal) -> the "Automatic Receipt Actions" rows (e.g. "Μετά την παραλαβή παραγγελίας", "Μετά την πληρωμή")
    still rendered the **bespoke yellow pill** ON switch (`h-8 w-14` track, `bg-yellow-400` ON, `translate-x-6` knob),
    contradicting Round 295 (Settings switches unified to the shared green-on / neutral-off `POSGlassSwitch`).
- **Fix (UI only; no save/print/detect/test/persistence/i18n changes).** In
    `src/renderer/components/modals/PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`, `renderReceiptActionSwitchRow` now imports and renders the
    shared **`POSGlassSwitch`** (58x34 green-on / neutral-off, 28px knob, no hover) and the bespoke right-side pill track/knob
    span was removed. To avoid nested interactive controls / double toggles, the row changed from a `button[role="switch"]`
    to a presentational `<div>`, and `POSGlassSwitch` is the **single** interactive control -- it carries `role="switch"` +
    `aria-checked` (from the shared component) + `aria-label={`${label}: ${stateLabel}`}` and calls
    `onChange={() => handleReceiptActionToggle(key)}` (exact same toggle behaviour, one tap = one toggle). The enabled
    row/icon/state-label/AUTO-chip accent moved **amber -> emerald** so the ON state reads as semantic green; the warm/yellow
    accent stays reserved for selected tabs/buttons, not switch ON. `receiptActions` state, persistence, toasts, labels, and
    all printer business logic are unchanged.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/PrinterSettingsModal.tsx` (import + `renderReceiptActionSwitchRow`
    migration + emerald re-theme); `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` (new Round 301 guard scoped to the
    receipt-action row block: imports POSGlassSwitch, renders it with `checked`/`onChange`/`aria-label`, no bespoke
    `h-8 w-14` / yellow / `translate-x-6` track, presentational `<div key={key}>` with no nested `<button>`, emerald ON
    accent, no hover / no native `title=`).
- **Codex review correction (touch UX).** First pass made only the small switch tappable
    (the old whole-row toggle was lost). Corrected: the row stays a `<div>` (no nested buttons) but is tappable again --
    `onClick={() => handleReceiptActionToggle(key)}` + `cursor-pointer active:scale-[0.99]` (touch feedback, no hover) -- so
    tapping the label/card toggles once. `POSGlassSwitch` is wrapped in `<span onClick={(event) => event.stopPropagation()}>`
    so a tap on the switch toggles exactly once via `POSGlassSwitch.onChange` and does not also bubble to the row onClick (no
    double toggle). `POSGlassSwitch` `checked`/`onChange`/`aria-label` are unchanged; no business/persistence/i18n/style-system
    changes beyond this row-tap correction. The Round 301 test now also asserts the row `onClick` and the stopPropagation
    wrapper.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **41 pass / 0 fail** (new Round 301 guard +
    the existing Settings/switch/printer guards still green). `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check`
    (`PrinterSettingsModal.tsx`, `settings-modal-ui.test.ts`, tracker) -> clean. No printer business logic touched; no POS
    actions run; no commits.
- **Codex live QA — PASSED (running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** Reopened Settings -> Printer -> "Διαμόρφωση
    Εκτυπωτή" and inspected the Automatic Receipt Actions rows. The old yellow ON pills are gone; both visible receipt-action
    rows now show the shared green-on glass switch geometry with emerald row/icon/status accents, matching the Settings switch
    family. No save, print, detect, test, or toggle action was clicked, so no printer settings were changed.

### Supervisor round 302 — Navigation sidebar misleading native focus boxes -> input-modality-gated focus ring — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, touchscreen).** After closing Settings / returning to the dashboard, the inactive
    Settings control kept a heavy **native black focus rectangle**. On a touch POS that reads like a selected state even
    though the icon colour is neutral -- a pointer/touch tap should leave no lingering focus box, while keyboard focus must
    stay visible.
- **Fix (focus styling only; no colour/drag/aria/selected-state changes).** In
    `src/renderer/components/NavigationSidebar.tsx` a shared `SIDEBAR_FOCUS_RING =
    'rounded-xl focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-yellow-400/70'` is applied to every sidebar icon
    button -- Check In, the module buttons, Z Report, Settings, and Logout. `focus:outline-none` drops the native rectangle
    that lingered after pointer/touch; `focus-visible` adds a deliberate **keyboard-only** yellow glass ring (so it does not
    appear after a touch/pointer tap). No `hover:` utilities; `active:scale-95` tap feedback is preserved. The `rounded-xl`
    only shapes the ring (the icon buttons have no background, so the inactive/active icon appearance is unchanged); the
    Logout button's old `rounded-lg` was folded into the shared `rounded-xl` so all five focus rings are uniform. `getNeonClass`
    inactive-neutral logic (grey in dark / black in light, neon only on the active route), drag/drop, aria-labels, and
    selected-state semantics are untouched.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/NavigationSidebar.tsx` (new `SIDEBAR_FOCUS_RING` const + applied to the 5 icon
    buttons; Logout className -> template literal, `rounded-lg` -> shared `rounded-xl`); `tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts`
    (new Round 302 guard: const defined with `focus:outline-none` + keyboard-only `focus-visible:ring`, NO plain `focus:ring-`,
    ring referenced on >=5 buttons incl. Check In / module / Z Report / Settings / Logout, no hover, inactive-neutral logic
    intact).
- **Correction (Codex live QA FAILED the first pass).** First pass used a plain
    `focus-visible` ring. Live QA: tap Settings, then tap the modal's close X (pointer) -> focus returns to the Settings
    button via `.focus()` and Chromium's `focus-visible` heuristic STILL painted the yellow ring, so the inactive button
    looked selected. `focus-visible` alone is not a reliable input-modality signal for programmatic focus-return. Fix: an
    explicit input-modality guard in `NavigationSidebar.tsx` -- a `keyboardMode` state + **window-level capture-phase**
    listeners (`keydown` -> keyboard mode, `pointerdown` -> pointer mode). `focus:outline-none` stays on always
    (`SIDEBAR_FOCUS_BASE`); the yellow ring (`SIDEBAR_FOCUS_RING_KEYBOARD`) is attached to the buttons only while
    `keyboardMode` is true (`sidebarFocusRing = keyboardMode ? ... : SIDEBAR_FOCUS_BASE`). Because the listeners are
    capture-phase, a pointerdown anywhere (including the modal-close tap) flips to pointer mode and drops the ring class
    BEFORE focus returns to the opener -- so a tapped-then-closed control shows no ring, while Tab/keyboard navigation still
    gets the ring. No hover; restored inactive/active nav colours, aria-current/aria-label, and drag/reorder are unchanged.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` -> **12 pass / 0 fail** (the Round 302 guard
    now pins the modality guard: `SIDEBAR_FOCUS_BASE` always-on outline reset, keyboard-gated `SIDEBAR_FOCUS_RING_KEYBOARD`,
    `keyboardMode` state + capture-phase keydown/pointerdown listeners, gated `sidebarFocusRing`, NO plain `focus:ring-`,
    >=5 button refs, no hover, inactive-neutral logic intact; existing nav guards incl. Round 299 still green).
    `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (`NavigationSidebar.tsx`, `navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts`, tracker) ->
    clean. No behaviour changes beyond focus styling; no POS actions run; no commits.
- **Codex live QA — PASSED (running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** Reproduced the failed touchscreen path after HMR:
    logged in with the provided PIN, tapped Settings, then tapped the modal close X. The dashboard returned with the inactive
    Settings icon neutral and no lingering native black rectangle or yellow ring. A blank dashboard pointer tap also clears
    any prior keyboard-visible ring. Restored nav colors and order-tab colors stayed intact; no settings were changed.

### Supervisor round 303 — Embedded 2D table floor-plan native scrollbar rails -> fully hidden (touch-first) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** Orders dashboard -> Τραπέζια tab -> 2D floor-plan
    mode rendered ugly native WebView2 scrollbars inside the floor-plan canvas: a thick horizontal rail across the bottom and
    a vertical native rail with arrow buttons on the right. The `.floor-plan-scrollbar` class only *partially* styled the
    WebKit scrollbar (an 8px thumb with a thin Firefox rail), which on Chromium/WebView2 still draws the default
    arrow-button rails. It also carried pointer-only `:hover` thumb colours, which make no sense on a touchscreen POS.
- **Fix (scrollbar visibility only; no table data / action / layout changes).** The 2D floor-plan viewport now hides the
    native rail in every engine while keeping overflow pan/scroll:
  - `src/renderer/components/tables/TableFloorPlanView.tsx`: the scroll container className drops the
        `floor-plan-scrollbar-light` / `floor-plan-scrollbar-dark` colour variants and pairs the scoped class with the shared
        `scrollbar-hide` utility (`floor-plan-scrollbar scrollbar-hide ... overflow-auto`). `overflow-auto`, `min-h-[360px]`,
        the rounded/glass background, and the top-left scroll-reset `useEffect` are untouched.
  - `src/renderer/styles/globals.css`: `.floor-plan-scrollbar` now uses `scrollbar-width: none` + `-ms-overflow-style: none`
        (Firefox / legacy Edge) and a single combined WebKit rule that sets `display: none` (width/height 0, transparent) for
        `::-webkit-scrollbar`, **`::-webkit-scrollbar-button`** (the arrow-button rail), `-track`, `-corner`, and `-thumb`.
        The old `scrollbar-width: thin`, the 8px thumb rule, the light/dark colored-thumb variants, and **both
        `:hover::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb` selectors** are removed. The touch affordances `-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch`
        and `overscroll-behavior: contain` are preserved.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/tables/TableFloorPlanView.tsx` (container className),
    `src/renderer/styles/globals.css` (hardened `.floor-plan-scrollbar` block), `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts`
    (the stale "scoped modern scrollbars" test that pinned `thin`/`8px` is replaced by a Round 303 guard: container pairs
    `floor-plan-scrollbar` + `scrollbar-hide` and keeps `overflow-auto`, no `-light`/`-dark` variant refs, CSS sets
    `scrollbar-width: none` + `-ms-overflow-style: none` + `::-webkit-scrollbar`/`-button` `display: none`, NO `thin`/`8px`
    rail, NO floor-plan `:hover` selector, NO `hover:` utility in the view).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` -> all pass. `npm run type-check` -> clean.
    `git diff --check` (`TableFloorPlanView.tsx`, `globals.css`, `orders-page-ui.test.ts`, tracker) -> clean. No table data,
    table-action behaviour, floor filtering, list mode, order-taking colours, nav/tab restoration, or table-card business
    actions changed; no destructive POS actions run; no commits.

    `Τραπέζια` tab and 2D mode, and verified the thick bottom rail and right-side WebView2 scrollbar/arrow rail are gone.
    Horizontal pan still works inside the floor-plan canvas and no rail appears while panning; table nodes remain visible and
    the restored order tab/sidebar colours stayed intact.

### Supervisor round 304 — Z Report modal third-pass clarity redesign (close-day assistant) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** After Rounds 297/300 the Z Report first screen
    still read like an accounting dashboard: the header action buttons (Refresh / Print / CSV) were the same size as the
    close button and competed with the title; live-mode / business-window / terminal chips added a wall of text; the money
    cards used ALL-CAPS micro-labels; the decision card repeated a "NEXT STEP" label plus a helper sentence; and the Review
    list still read like many small rows. The founder asked for a redesign so an operator can answer in under 3 seconds:
    (1) can I close the day? (2) if not, what is the one thing to fix first? (3) where is the final close-day action?
- **Fix (UI/presentation only in `ZReportModal.tsx`; report generation, submit/close-day, print/export, blocker
    resolution, date filtering, calculations, feature gating, and IPC contracts all unchanged).**
  - **Quiet header.** The header is now identity-only: file icon + title + business date + one at-a-glance status pill
        (`data-z-report-header-status`) on the left; on the right a compact **secondary** tools cluster
        (`data-z-report-utility-tools`) holds Refresh / Print / CSV as small muted icon buttons, plus a quiet icon-only
        close (X). Handlers stay wired through the same `action.onClick` array; `aria-label`s preserved; no native `title=`.
        The long business-window / terminal / live-mode chips were removed from the header.
  - **Close-day hero (new, first element).** `data-z-report-close-day-hero` replaces the old `data-z-report-decision-card`
        and is now the first thing on screen: a large status icon, a big status line (`closeoutStatusLabel`), and AT MOST
        one sentence -- ready -> `clarity.readyHint`; blocked -> `clarity.fixFirst` naming the single first non-ready
        checklist item (`primaryIssue`), or the count fallback (`clarity.reviewHint`). The green submit renders ONLY in the
        `closeoutReady` branch with the exact preserved gating (`submitting || loading || resolvingBlockerKey ||
        paymentBlockers.length > 0`); the not-ready branch is a non-submit amber "Review issues" jump to the Check tab.
        Top-3 blocker preview + submit result stay inside the hero.
  - **Three calm money facts.** `data-z-report-summary-strip` (now below the hero) keeps Sales / Expected cash / Issues +
        a quiet Orders chip, with **natural-case** labels (the `uppercase tracking-[0.12em]` micro-label styling is gone).
        The hero names the one issue to fix; the Issues card only shows the raw count, so the two never repeat a sentence.
  - **Step tabs.** The four tabs read like steps with icons: **Check** (`ListChecks`, keeps the `review` key; EN
        `clarity.tabReview` value changed "Review" -> "Check"; el/de/fr/it already read Check/Verify) / **Money**
        (`Banknote`) / **Staff** (`Users`) / **Orders** (`Receipt`). The four state/content branches are unchanged.
  - **Check tab checklist.** Each row is icon + label + a short localized status word (`closeoutStateLabel`:
        Ready / Needs attention / Action needed / Checking), with the explanatory sentence shown only on rows needing
        attention; long paragraphs remain reserved for the payment-blocker panel. The detailed business window + terminal
        now live here as quiet on-demand reference text (the relocated header clutter).
  - **Palette / touch.** Blurry glass, rounded containers, hidden native scrollbars (`scrollbar-hide`), and `active:`
        feedback preserved; black/white/grey/yellow + semantic green/red/amber only; no new blue/cyan/purple, no
        `hover:`/`:hover`, no native `title=` tooltips. All visible copy is i18n-keyed in en/el/de/fr/it.
- **New i18n keys (all five locales).** `modals.zReport.clarity.fixFirst` (`{{issue}}` interpolation), `statusReady`,
    `statusAttention`, `statusAction`, `statusChecking`; EN `tabReview` value "Review" -> "Check".
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx`; `src/locales/{en,el,de,fr,it}.json`;
    `tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts` (Round 304: hero-first ordering, quiet-header guard, natural-case
    money facts, hero non-contradiction + removed `nextStep` micro-label, icon step tabs + per-row status word);
    `tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts` (marker `data-z-report-decision-card` -> `data-z-report-close-day-hero`).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts`
    -> **15 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check` -> clean. `npm run locale:parity` -> passed (6174 keys aligned, 0 missing/
    extra across all 5 locales). `git diff --check` (ZReportModal.tsx, 5 locales, 2 tests, tracker) -> clean. No report
    generation, submit/close-day, print/export, blocker resolution, date filtering, calculations, feature gating, IPC
    contracts, order-taking dashboard, or Round 303 floor-plan files changed; no destructive POS actions run; no commits.
- **Codex live QA — PASSED (running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** Logged back in after the renderer refresh with the
    provided test PIN, opened Z Report from the sidebar, and verified the redesigned first screen: quiet title/date/status
    header, compact Refresh/Print/CSV tools, new close-day hero first, one amber "Review issues" action while blocked, natural-
    case Sales / Expected cash / Issues cards, no long business-window/terminal chip clutter in the header, and no visible
    native scrollbar. Switched only safe tabs (Money, Staff, Orders): content remained readable, icon+text tab labels stayed
    centered, and no overlap/clipping appeared. Did not click Submit, Print, Export CSV, resolve payment blockers, close day,
    or any other data-mutating action.

### Supervisor round 305 — Settings > Printer PrintQueuePanel touch/palette cleanup — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live/static QA, Codex).** Settings > Printer embeds `PrintQueuePanel.tsx`, but the panel still broke the
    global touch-POS rules: pointer-only `hover:bg-*` affordances on every queue action (Refresh, Pause/Resume,
    Cancel pending, row Cancel, row Retry); an off-theme sky/blue tone on the Printer icon (`text-sky-300`) and the
    Pause/Resume button (`border-sky-400/30 bg-sky-500/10 text-sky-100`); sub-44px top action buttons; and a
    `max-h-72 overflow-y-auto` queue list with no `scrollbar-hide`, so native rails appear when jobs overflow.
- **Fix (presentation only; queue behaviour, bridge calls, job filtering, toasts, loading/busy states, and
    translations all unchanged).** In `src/renderer/components/printing/PrintQueuePanel.tsx`:
  - **No hover.** Every `hover:bg-*` is replaced with an `active:bg-*` pressed state (touch feedback only). No
        `hover:` / `:hover` / `group-hover:` / `dark:hover:` remain.
  - **On-palette.** The sky tokens are gone. The Printer icon is now `text-amber-300`. A clean 3-tier semantic mapping:
        Refresh = neutral glass (`border-white/15 bg-white/10`), Pause/Resume = amber (`border-amber-400/30
        bg-amber-500/10 text-amber-100` -- the brand accent replacing sky), Cancel pending + row Cancel = rose
        (destructive), row Retry = emerald (success). No blue/cyan/purple/indigo/violet/sky anywhere.
  - **Touch targets.** The three top action buttons are `inline-flex min-h-[44px] items-center justify-center
        rounded-2xl` (icon+text centered, Settings rounding); the two row buttons also get `min-h-[44px]
        justify-center rounded-xl`. Disabled states (`disabled:opacity-60` + the existing disabled conditions) preserved.
  - **Hidden rail.** The queue list region is now `max-h-72 overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide divide-y divide-white/5`
        -- max-height + scrolling kept, native rail hidden.
  - The empty state, table columns, active-job Cancel, failed-job Retry, and all `bridge.printer.*` methods are intact.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/printing/PrintQueuePanel.tsx`; `tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`
    (new Round 305 guard: no hover selectors, no sky/blue/indigo/violet/cyan/purple tokens, Printer icon amber, the three
    top actions are centered >=44px rounded-2xl with active feedback + disabled states, queue overflow region has
    `scrollbar-hide`, cancel stays rose / retry stays emerald, and every bridge method + empty state stay wired).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **42 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check`
    -> clean. `git diff --check` (PrintQueuePanel.tsx, settings-modal-ui.test.ts, tracker) -> clean. No queue behaviour,
    bridge calls, filtering, toasts, loading/busy states, translations, or parent modal wiring changed; ZReportModal and
    the Round 303 floor-plan files untouched; no POS actions run; no commits. Initial live QA was resolved in the
    follow-up below.
- **Follow-up (Codex live QA) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED.** The palette / hover / scrollbar cleanup passed,
    but live QA rejected the action layout: in Greek the three top queue actions rendered as a mixed-width vertical stack
    (the `flex flex-wrap` cluster sized each button to its own label, so the unequal Greek labels wrapped into ragged
    chips). Fix (presentation only): the cluster is now a deliberate **equal-width grid** -- `grid w-full shrink-0
    grid-cols-1 gap-2 md:w-48`, i.e. full-width stacked equal cards on narrow widths and a compact fixed-width column on
    desktop -- and each top button gained `w-full`, so the grid track (not the label) sets the width and all three are
    identical in every language, with centered icon+text, >=44px height, smooth rounded-2xl glass edges. No `flex-wrap`
    chip row remains. Refresh / Pause-Resume / Cancel-pending behaviour, disabled states, bridge calls, toasts, the queue
    list, and the retry/cancel row actions are unchanged; no hover utilities; no blue/sky/cyan/purple/violet/indigo
    tokens. The Round 305 test was strengthened to guard the equal-width cluster (`grid ... grid-cols-1 ... md:w-48`, no
    `flex-wrap`, each top button `w-full`) alongside the existing no-hover / on-palette / scrollbar-hide / rose-cancel /
    emerald-retry rules. Verification: `node --test tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **42 pass / 0 fail**;
    `npm run type-check` -> clean; `git diff --check` (PrintQueuePanel.tsx, settings-modal-ui.test.ts, tracker) -> clean.
    No commit.
- **Codex live QA.** Re-opened Settings -> Printer in the running POS after HMR. The Print Queue buttons now render as
    an intentional equal-width right-side column in Greek; icon and labels are centered, all three actions share the same
    height/width/radius, palette is neutral / amber / rose with no blue/sky drift, and the queue list shows no native rail.
    Did not click Refresh, Pause queue, Cancel pending, Configure printer, or any print/device action.

### Supervisor round 306 — Restoration hardening pass (login orbs / sidebar neon / order tabs / order-type icons) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Context.** Codex live QA (running POS, Greek / light) confirmed the restored state is correct: login shows the warm
    yellow animated orb background; the sidebar has neutral inactive icons with only the selected icon neon; the order
    dashboard top tabs show active Orders green/neon and inactive tabs grey; the New Order order-type chooser uses the
    proper table/chair `TableOrderIcon`, not cake/utensils/store/package. The brief was to HARDEN this exact state
    without churn -- restore only if something was structurally missing, otherwise add/strengthen guards only.
- **Inspection result — code is already correct on all five points (no source changes needed).**
  1. **Login / AnimatedBackground.** `LoginPage.tsx` renders `<AnimatedBackground />`; `AnimatedBackground.tsx` keeps
        6 framer-motion lava-lamp orbs per theme on the warm radial gradient (`#92400e`/`#eab308`/`#fde68a`,
        `repeat: Infinity`). Already guarded by `login-page-ui.test.ts` (orb count, infinite loop, yellow gradient, both
        light + dark branches).
  2. **NavigationSidebar.** `getNeonClass(color, isActive, theme)` returns `theme === 'dark' ? 'text-zinc-400' :
        'text-black'` when inactive (neutral; not all-yellow), and the per-module neon + glow only when active. Already
        guarded by `navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts` (inactive-neutral, active-neon, Check In + Settings same rule).
  3. **OrderTabsBar.** `tabTextClass` returns `isDark ? 'text-zinc-400' : 'text-gray-600'` for inactive tabs; only the
        active tab gets its neon (orders green, etc.) + glow. Already guarded by `orders-page-ui.test.ts`.
  4. **Order-type icons.** `OrderTypeSelector`, `OrderDashboard`, and `OrderFlow` all use `TableOrderIcon` (chair/table
        Flaticon mask) + `PickupOrderIcon` (shared ShoppingBag) at matching footprints (`w-6 h-6` selector, `w-16 h-16`
        choosers); `Truck` for delivery. No Cake/Utensils/Store/Package fallback for table/dine-in. The `UtensilsCrossed`
        in OrderDashboard is only the empty-"no tables configured" placeholder, not an order-type glyph.
- **Hardening (guard-only; no component/business changes).** Found one coverage asymmetry: the OrderDashboard order-type
    chooser had a restoration guard (round 186) but the parallel **OrderFlow** chooser -- the second New Order path -- was
    unguarded, so a refactor could reintroduce an off-theme glyph on that path alone. Added a mirror guard in
    `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (new `orderFlowSource()` reader + `Round 306` test): OrderFlow imports +
    renders `TableOrderIcon`/`PickupOrderIcon` at `w-full h-full`, the pickup bag and table/chair share the `w-16 h-16`
    icon box (matching footprint, >=2 boxes in the chooser region), and the chooser region has no
    `Utensils/UtensilsCrossed/Fork/Cake/Store/Package` glyph. No source files were changed.
- **Changed files.** `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (new OrderFlow chooser guard + reader); this tracker. No
    component, locale, or business-logic files changed; the protected order-taking page (incl. its green/blue/purple
    card accents) was not redesigned; payments/closing-day/report flows untouched.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/navigation-sidebar-ui.test.ts
    tests/renderer/login-page-ui.test.ts` -> **96 pass / 0 fail** (incl. the new Round 306 OrderFlow guard).
    `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (orders-page-ui.test.ts, tracker) -> clean. No POS actions run; no
    commits.
- **Codex live QA.** Running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802: reopened the New Order chooser and confirmed Pickup uses the
    bag icon, Table uses the table/chair mark with matching footprint, and no cake/utensils/store/package glyph appears
    in the table card. Closed the chooser and confirmed the dashboard keeps neutral inactive sidebar icons with only the
    selected nav item colored/neon, plus active Orders tab green with all inactive tabs grey. Logged out for the login
    check and confirmed the warm yellow animated/orb background is visible behind the glass PIN card. Did not create an
    order, select an order type, enter payment, print, close day, or run any data-mutating POS action.

### Supervisor round 307 — New Appointment Date & Time pre-context empty state (no fake disabled slot grid) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / light).** Orders hub > Services > New Appointment opens a good glass
    booking modal, but the Date & Time panel rendered a grid of disabled time-slot buttons before staff AND service were
    selected. Because `selectedDay` is seeded to today (not null), the slot panel showed immediately, and with no
    staff/service every slot hit the `!hasStaffAndService` disabled branch -- a wall of greyed times that reads like
    broken / fully-booked availability.
- **Fix (presentation only; `AppointmentsView.tsx`). Appointment creation, `handleCreateAppointment`, backend
    availability validation, module gates, submit conditions, API calls, and data models are all unchanged.** The Date &
    Time right column now branches on the staff+service gate FIRST instead of `selectedDay`:
  - **Pre-context (`!hasStaffAndService`)** renders `data-appointment-slots-empty`: a warm-glass (amber, rounded-2xl,
        blurred) empty state -- a clock icon, a short title (`appointments.modal.slotsEmpty.title`), one help sentence
        (`slotsEmpty.help`), and a two-step **checklist** of Staff + Service chips that flip from neutral (pending, domain
        icon) to emerald (complete, check) from `Boolean(formData.staffId)` / `Boolean(formData.serviceId)`. No period
        buttons, no slot grid, no fake disabled times.
  - **In-context (`hasStaffAndService`, then `selectedDay`)** renders the unchanged period buttons + actual time slots
        with booked/available disabled logic (`disabled={booked}`), the selected yellow slot, the legend, and the
        Summary. The now-dead pre-context per-slot muted-glass styling + the inline `selectStaffServiceAvailability` hint
        were removed (their guidance is fully served by the empty state). `handleTimeSelect` keeps its Round 285 hard
        guard (refuses no-context clicks); changing staff/service still clears the picked time; Create stays disabled
        until staff + service + time; Cancel stays red.
  - Styling stays touch-first: blurry/warm glass, rounded-2xl, no `hover:`/`:hover`, no native `title=`, and only
        black/white/grey/yellow + semantic amber/emerald/red (no blue/sky/purple drift).
- **i18n.** New `appointments.modal.slotsEmpty.{title,help,staffStep,serviceStep}` added to en/el/de/fr/it (Greek is real
    Greek). The existing `selectStaffServiceAvailability` key is retained (no longer rendered inline).
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/pages/verticals/salon/AppointmentsView.tsx`; `src/locales/{en,el,de,fr,it}.json`;
    `tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` (Round 285 test updated for the in-context-only grid; the
    seeds-visible-date test's grid-gate assertion re-pointed to the nested `) : selectedDay ? (`; new Round 307 structure
    guard -- empty state + checklist present, NO `timeSlots.map`/`isTimeSlotBooked` in the pre-context branch, real grid
    + selected/booked behavior in the staff+service branch, on-palette/no-hover/no-title; new Round 307 locale guard).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` -> **28 pass / 0 fail**.
    `npm run locale:parity` -> passed (6178 keys aligned across all 5 locales). `npm run type-check` -> clean.
    `git diff --check` (AppointmentsView.tsx, 5 locales, appointments-view-i18n.test.ts, tracker) -> clean. Codex live
    QA on the running POS (Greek / light) opened Orders hub > Services > New Appointment and confirmed the pre-context
    Date & Time side shows the clock empty state + Staff/Service checklist, with no fake disabled time-slot grid. No
    appointment creation / availability / module-gate / submit / API / data-model changes; no reservation or submit action
    was run; no commits.

### Supervisor round 308 — Z Report modal full clarity redesign (close-day assistant) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

    day is step 1 of closing. It read like an accounting report, not a guided workflow.

    issue calculations, filters, module/terminal gating, submit/print/export/refresh/resolve handlers, API calls, and date
    selection behavior are all unchanged).** The first screen is now a literal numbered close-day assistant:
  - **Step 1 — `data-z-report-day-card`** (above the fold): the business-day date input (moved UP from the Check tab) +
        a live/historical pill + terminal + the business window, behind a "1" badge titled `clarity.dayStepTitle`.
  - **Step 2 — `data-z-report-readiness-card`** (beside the day card in a `lg:grid-cols-2` grid): ONE readiness answer
        behind a "2" badge titled `clarity.readyStepTitle` -- ready -> emerald state + the green submit; blocked -> amber
        icon + the first-issue sentence (`clarity.fixFirst`) + an amber "Review issues" jump to the Check tab. The green
        submit renders ONLY in the `closeoutReady` branch, `handleSubmitReport` is wired **exactly once** in the whole
        modal, and the gating `submitting || loading || Boolean(resolvingBlockerKey) || paymentBlockers.length > 0` is
        preserved verbatim. A compact top-3 payment-blocker preview stays when blockers exist.
  - **Compact stat chips — `data-z-report-summary-strip`** is now a `flex flex-wrap` row of four `data-z-report-stat-chip`
        chips (Total sales / Expected cash / Orders / Issues, each >=44px) for quick scanning -- not four competing giant
        cards. The old 3-card grid + separate orders chip are gone.
  - **Tabs unchanged** (Check / Money / Staff / Orders with icons). The Check tab is now purely the kid-readable checklist
        (icon + label + short status word, description only when action is needed) + the rare full blocker panel; the
        date picker no longer lives there. Single hidden-scrollbar scroll body retained.
  - Header stays quiet (Round 304): identity + status pill + the demoted Refresh/Print/CSV utility cluster + close.
  - Touch/glass invariants: no `hover:`/`:hover`, no native `title=`, >=44px targets, rounded-2xl glass, on-palette
        (black/white/grey/yellow + semantic amber/emerald/red; no blue/sky/purple/cyan/indigo).
- **i18n.** Two new `modals.zReport.clarity` keys -- `dayStepTitle` + `readyStepTitle` -- added to en/el/de/fr/it (Greek
    is real Greek). All other copy reuses existing keys.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx`; `src/locales/{en,el,de,fr,it}.json`;
    `tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts` (Round 308: day-card-first flow + Step 1 day-card guard +
    compact-chip strip + readiness-card non-contradiction + a whole-modal submit-once guard; details test no longer
    expects `type="date"`); `tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts` (markers
    `data-z-report-close-day-hero` -> `data-z-report-day-card` + `data-z-report-readiness-card`).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts
    tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` -> **28 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run locale:parity` -> passed.
    `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (ZReportModal.tsx, 5 locales, both Z-report tests, tracker) ->
    clean. Codex live QA on the running POS (Greek / light) opened Z Report and verified the new Step 1 business-day card
    appears above the fold, Step 2 readiness card is beside it, compact stat chips replace the old giant-card strip,

### Supervisor round 309 — Edit Customer Info modal action visibility (bounded flex column + fixed footer) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** Orders > selected order > Edit Options > Edit
    Customer Info: the modal opened with the notes textarea clipped at the bottom and **Save/Cancel not visible on first
    open** -- they only appeared after scrolling. Root cause: the intro, every field, AND the action row were all inside a
    single `overflow-y-auto max-h-[70vh]` wrapper, so the actions scrolled with the content and sat below the fold.
- **Fix (UI/layout-only in `EditCustomerInfoModal.tsx`; `onSave`, validation, `customerInfo` payload shape, address
    autocomplete/geocoding, order-update bridge, delivery-zone checks, module gating, and initial form state are all
    unchanged).** The modal is now a bounded flex column via `LiquidGlassModal`'s structured slots:
  - The custom inner `overflow-y-auto max-h-[70vh]` wrapper is removed. The form body is now the modal's own
        `liquid-glass-modal-content` (already `flex: 1; min-height: 0; overflow-y: auto` + `scrollbar-hide`), inside the
        `flex flex-col` shell capped at `max-height: 92vh` -- a single inner scroll body with no clipping.
  - The Save/Cancel actions moved into the `footer` prop, which `LiquidGlassModal` renders `flex-shrink-0` BELOW the
        scroll body, so they are visible on first open without scrolling. The footer is a subtle glass bar (top border +
        translucent bg + backdrop blur). Cancel = red (`liquid-glass-modal-error`), Save = green
        (`liquid-glass-modal-success`), both `flex-1` (same height/visual weight), rounded, touch-sized; the disabled save
        state stays clear (`disabled:opacity-50 disabled:saturate-0 disabled:cursor-not-allowed`).
  - Minor footprint trim: intro margin `mb-6` -> `mb-4`, the form's bottom `mb-6` dropped (the footer is now separate).
        No fields removed, no spacing made cramped.
  - **Palette fix noticed along the way:** the address loading spinner (`border-blue-500/30 border-t-blue-500`) and the
        "validating address" row (`text-blue-500`) were off-palette blue; both moved to amber (on-palette, in-progress
        accent). The green address pin + grey fields are unchanged.
  - Touch/glass invariants: no `hover:`, the only `title=` is the modal heading prop (no native tooltips), >=44px action
        targets (the `liquid-glass-modal-button` base is min-height 44px), rounded, on-palette
        (black/white/grey/yellow + semantic green/red; no blue/cyan/sky/purple/violet/indigo).
- **i18n.** No new strings -- all copy reuses existing keys.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/EditCustomerInfoModal.tsx`;
    `tests/renderer/edit-customer-info-modal-ui.test.ts` (rewritten: the stale yellow+hover save expectation is gone;

### Supervisor round 310 — Order cancellation modal glass action footer — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / light, 1282x802).** Orders > selected pickup order > Cancel: the modal
    is safe (it requires a cancellation reason before the destructive confirm enables), but the enabled confirm rendered as
    a flat solid-red block (`bg-red-600 active:bg-red-700 text-white`) that looked heavier/less glassy than the rest of the
    modal system, and the action row was hand-rolled inside the body instead of the shared `LiquidGlassModal` footer slot.
- **Fix (UI/presentation only in `OrderCancellationModal.tsx`; reason validation, `onConfirmCancel`, reason reset, focus,
    close behavior, cancellation logic, translations, and data flow are all unchanged).**
  - The action buttons moved into `LiquidGlassModal`'s `footer` prop -- the same fixed glass action bar pattern as
        `EditCustomerInfoModal` (rendered `flex-shrink-0` below the scroll body; subtle top divider + translucent bg +
        backdrop blur).
  - Shared glass button classes: the safe close is `liquid-glass-modal-button liquid-glass-modal-secondary` (neutral
        translucent glass, visually lighter); the destructive confirm is `liquid-glass-modal-button liquid-glass-modal-error`
        (red glass) when enabled and `disabled:opacity-50 disabled:saturate-0 disabled:cursor-not-allowed` (desaturated +
        dimmed, clearly disabled) when no reason is typed. The flat `bg-red-600` block + its conditional neutral-zinc twin
        are gone.
  - Both buttons are `flex-1` (same width/visual weight), rounded, touch-sized (the `liquid-glass-modal-button` base is
        min-height 44px), centered text.
  - The confirm stays `disabled={!cancelReason.trim()}` (unchanged); the reason textarea + character count stay in the
        scroll body (not clipped).
  - Touch/glass invariants: no `hover:`, the only `title=` is the modal heading prop (no native tooltips), palette stays
        white/black/grey/yellow + semantic red (no blue/cyan/sky/purple/violet/indigo/slate).
- **i18n.** No new strings -- all copy reuses existing keys.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/OrderCancellationModal.tsx`;
    `tests/renderer/order-edit-modals-ui.test.ts` (the OrderCancellationModal red/neutral test rewritten to guard the
    footer-prop glass action bar -- red-glass enabled confirm, subdued-disabled confirm, neutral secondary safe close,
    same-size flex-1 buttons, flat-red block gone; the shared no-hover/no-off-palette/single-title loop + the
    title-prop/business-callback guards + the EditPaymentMethodModal tests are unchanged).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/order-edit-modals-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/order-modal-plural-i18n.test.ts`
    -> **8 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (OrderCancellationModal.tsx,
    order-edit-modals-ui.test.ts, tracker) -> clean. No reason-validation / confirm / reset / focus / close / cancellation
    behavior changed; no POS actions run; no commits. Codex live QA on the running POS (Greek / dark, 1282x802) passed:
    the empty-reason state keeps the confirm disabled/subdued, a temporary typed reason enables the red glass destructive
    confirm in the fixed footer, the safe Cancel remains neutral, labels stay centered/readable, and the modal closed
    without submitting cancellation.
