### Supervisor round 311 — Split Payment By-Amount first-view footer clearance (compact person cards) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / dark, 1282x802).** Orders > #00001 > View > Split Payment > By Amount:
    on first open the second person card was partially hidden/clipped under the fixed footer (scrolling revealed the rest,
    but the first impression read as footer overlap). By Items was already acceptable after Round 298.
- **Root cause.** In By Amount the default 50/50 split creates two positive-amount, zero-discount portions, yet each card
    rendered the full `renderPortionDetails` stack -- a Subtotal row, a `Discount: -0,00 €` row, AND a Payable row -- even
    though with no discount Subtotal == Payable. Two over-tall cards plus the Add Person affordance couldn't all clear the
    footer at first open.
- **Fix (UI/layout only in `SplitPaymentModal.tsx`; payment recording, bridge calls, confirm, terminal handling, discount
    logic, receipt mode, and order state are all unchanged).**
  - `renderPortionDetails` is now a block body with a `const hasDiscount = portion.discountAmount > 0.009` gate: the
        Subtotal + Discount rows render ONLY when a discount exists; the Payable line always shows. A zero-discount portion
        therefore shows a single Payable line instead of three rows (~2 rows saved per card). The rows reappear the moment a
        discount is applied -- no info or functionality removed. (Shared helper, so by-items full cards get the same compaction.)
  - Detail rhythm tightened (`space-y-2` -> `space-y-1.5`); the by-amount person card and the portions-list wrapper
        tightened (`space-y-3` -> `space-y-2`); the discount button shortened (`py-1.5` -> `py-1`).
  - Preserved: blurry glass modal, yellow/black selected segments, green cash/confirm semantics, neutral card semantics,
        the single bounded footer-safe scroll body (`min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide pb-24 scroll-pb-24`),
        `renderPortionDetails` still wired exactly twice, no hover-only effects, no native title tooltips.
- **i18n.** No new strings.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/SplitPaymentModal.tsx`;
    `tests/renderer/split-payment-modal-ui.test.ts` (new Round 311 guards: hasDiscount-gated Subtotal/Discount rows,
    space-y-1.5 detail / space-y-2 card+list rhythm, shorter discount button, footer-safe single-scroll body preserved,
    `renderPortionDetails` call count still 2). The Round 252/284/298 guards remain green.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/split-payment-modal-ui.test.ts` -> **10 pass / 0 fail** (incl. both new
    Round 311 guards). Codex follow-up ran `node --test tests/renderer/split-payment-modal-ui.test.ts
    tests/renderer/split-payment-discount-persistence.test.ts` -> **21 pass / 0 fail** and `npm run type-check` -> clean.
    Codex live QA on the running POS (Greek / dark, 1282x802) passed: By Amount now shows both default person cards and the
    Add Person affordance above the footer on first open, with no payment/settlement/discount/order behavior changed and no
    POS actions run. No commits.

### Supervisor round 312 — Z Report modal "close-day assistant" simplification (one panel + checks row) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / dark, 1282x802).** Even after Round 308 the first view still asked the
    cashier to parse too much at once: a large business-day card, a large readiness card, four equal-weight stat chips,
    four tabs, and a checklist. The user wants it readable by a 10-year-old: which day, are we ready, what's the next action.
- **Fix (UI/presentation only in `ZReportModal.tsx`; report loading, generateZReport, submitZReport, print/export,
    resolvePaymentBlocker, logout/reload, blocker gating, filters, date locking, terminal gating, and calculations are all
    unchanged).** The Round 308 day card + readiness card + four-chip stat row are merged into ONE calm panel, with the
    checklist moved to a compact row:
  - **`data-z-report-assistant-panel`** (one panel): a compact day selector (date input + live/historical pill + a tiny
        quiet window/terminal meta line); ONE large verdict (`closeoutStatusLabel` + status icon); issues + orders as small
        inline badges (`data-z-report-issues-badge`), not equal-weight cards; exactly TWO money facts (Total sales +
        Expected cash); and ONE primary action. A one-line explainer renders ONLY when not ready (no ready-state paragraph).
        The green submit renders ONLY in the `closeoutReady` branch with the EXACT preserved gating
        (`submitting || loading || Boolean(resolvingBlockerKey) || paymentBlockers.length > 0`); not-ready shows an amber
        Review jump (`setActiveTab('review')`), never a submit. `handleSubmitReport` is wired exactly once in the whole modal.
  - **`data-z-report-checks`** (compact checks row): the five closeout checks (Sync / Payments / Drawer / Expenses / Staff)
        as chips -- icon + label + a short status word; a description renders ONLY for a non-ready check, so a clean day is a
        row of green ticks with no text wall.
  - **Details tabs** (Check / Money / Staff / Orders) unchanged in structure. The Check tab now drills into ONLY the
        non-ready checks (`closeoutChecklistItems.filter((item) => item.state !== 'ready')`) + the full payment-blocker
        resolve panel + a brief all-clear when ready -- "very short unless blockers exist." The whole checklist + the date
        picker no longer live in the tab.
  - Header stays quiet (identity + status pill + demoted Refresh/Print/CSV + close). Blurry glass, rounded, `scrollbar-hide`
        single scroll body, >=44px targets, no `hover:`/`:hover`, no native `title=`, palette black/white/grey/yellow +
        semantic green/amber/red (no blue/cyan/sky/purple/violet/indigo) all preserved.
- **i18n.** No new strings -- all copy reuses existing `modals.zReport.*` / `clarity.*` keys (Greek is real Greek). The
    `clarity.readyStepTitle` key is now unused but retained.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/ZReportModal.tsx`;
    `tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts` (Round 312: one-panel-then-checks-then-tabs flow; panel holds day
    selector + verdict + badges + two money facts; panel submit-safety; compact checks row; Check tab drills into only
    non-ready checks; old day-card/readiness-card/summary-strip/stat-chip markers asserted gone);
    `tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts` (markers `data-z-report-day-card`/`-readiness-card`/`-summary-strip`
    -> `data-z-report-assistant-panel` + `data-z-report-checks`).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts`
    -> **17 pass / 0 fail** (4 new Round 312 guards green). `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (ZReportModal.tsx,
    both Z-report tests, tracker) -> clean. Codex live QA on the running POS (Greek / dark, 1282x802) passed: the first view
    now reads as one assistant panel, two money facts, compact checks, and clear detail tabs; Money, Staff, and Orders tabs
    stayed readable inside the hidden-scrollbar body. No reporting/business behavior changed; no POS actions run; no commits.

### Supervisor round 313 — Staff check-in PIN step touch keypad + calmer eyebrow labels — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED

- **Finding (live QA, Codex, running POS, Greek / dark, 1282x802).** Sidebar Start Shift -> select staff -> PIN step showed
    a dots input + Continue but NO on-screen numeric keypad, so a touchscreen cashier couldn't enter a PIN without a physical
    keyboard. The check-in path also used shouty uppercase / letter-spaced micro-labels (selected member / current role /
    Enter PIN), which read generated and are harder to scan in Greek.
- **Fix (UI/presentation only in `StaffShiftModal.tsx`; `handlePinSubmit`, staff auth, check-in, role selection, active-shift
    management, bridge calls, eligibility, QR, cashier-first gate, checkout, and close-shift behavior are all unchanged).**
  - **Touch numeric keypad (PIN step only):** a `data-testid="staff-pin-keypad"` 3-column grid -- 1-9, then Clear / 0 /
        Backspace -- inside the existing PIN inset surface. Each key is a >=44px centered rounded glass button with
        active/tap feedback (no hover), base black/white/grey/yellow palette (slate/white neutral; no blue/cyan/purple/
        indigo). It updates the SAME `enteredPin` state: digits append capped at length 4
        (`setEnteredPin((prev) => prev.length >= 4 ? prev : prev + digit)`), Clear empties (`setEnteredPin('')`), Backspace
        removes the last digit (`prev.slice(0, -1)`). It does NOT auto-submit -- the Continue button stays the only
        click-submit. Keyboard entry is untouched: the numeric `onChange` + Enter-at-length-4 submit still work, and the
        hidden/visible input is unchanged. Clear/Backspace reuse existing `login.clear` / `login.backspace` keys (with an
        aria-label and the lucide `Delete` icon) -- no new locale strings.
  - **Calmer check-in typography:** the shared `checkInEyebrowClass` changed from `text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.22em] …`
        to `text-xs font-semibold …` (normal-case readable small label). This fixes every check-in eyebrow (selected member
        / role / Enter PIN) in one place and deliberately does NOT touch the inline `uppercase tracking-[…]` labels in the
        checkout / report / table sections of this 7k-line modal.
- **i18n.** No new strings -- keypad Clear/Backspace reuse the existing login keypad keys; all other copy is unchanged.
- **Changed files.** `src/renderer/components/modals/StaffShiftModal.tsx` (lucide `Delete` import; `checkInEyebrowClass`
    normal-case; PIN keypad); `tests/renderer/staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts` (new Round 313 guards: keypad exists +
    digit/clear/backspace wiring to `enteredPin`, no auto-submit / Continue is the only submit, >=44px keys, no
    hover/native-title/off-palette in the PIN step, eyebrow class no longer uppercase/tracking).
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts tests/renderer/staff-shift-closeout-inline-forms.test.ts`
    -> **14 pass / 0 fail** (both new Round 313 guards green). `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check`
    (StaffShiftModal.tsx, staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts, tracker) -> clean. NOTE: `staff-shift-checkout-print.test.ts`
    and `staff-shift-duration.test.ts` cannot load under bare `node --test` -- they import source utils with extensionless
    relative paths (a transitive chain that resolves only under the project's esbuild test runner, not Node's type-strip
    loader); this is pre-existing on HEAD and unaffected by this round (those files were not edited here). No POS actions
    run; no commits. This first implementation was superseded by the follow-up live-QA corrections below.
- **Follow-up correction (live QA FAILED first pass) — SUPERSEDED BY 2ND FOLLOW-UP.** Live QA (Greek/dark,
    1282x802): the PIN step opened too tall -- at the top scroll position only digits 1-9 showed; Clear/0/Backspace and the
    Back+Continue footer were below the fold (one scroll revealed the bottom keypad row, another the footer). For a touch
    POS that fails. The keypad keys are fixed at 44px (touch targets), so the vertical budget was reclaimed from the chrome
    around them (UI-only; `handlePinSubmit`, staff auth, bridge calls, role-selection, check-in, checkout/closeout, and all
    live workflows unchanged; keyboard entry + no-auto-submit preserved):
  - Shared check-in consts: `checkInSurfaceClass` `p-5` -> `p-4`; `checkInInsetSurfaceClass` `p-4` -> `p-3`;
        `checkInFooterClass` `mt-6`/`pt-4` -> `mt-3`/`pt-3`.
  - `renderSelectedStaffSummary` densified: name `text-2xl`/`mt-2` -> `text-xl`/`mt-1`; helper -> `mt-1 text-xs
        leading-snug`; role row `mt-5 gap-4` -> `mt-3 gap-3`; icon `h-16 w-16 rounded-[20px]` -> `h-14 w-14 rounded-2xl`,
        `User h-8 w-8` -> `h-7 w-7`; role value `mt-2 text-lg` -> `mt-1 text-base`; available-roles `mt-5` -> `mt-3`, badges
        `gap-2` -> `gap-1.5`.
  - enter-pin branch: wrapper `space-y-6` -> `space-y-3`; grid `gap-6` -> `gap-4`; right heading `text-2xl`/`mt-2` ->
        `text-xl`/`mt-1`; helper -> `mt-1 text-xs leading-snug`; inset `mt-6` -> `mt-3`; PIN input wrapper `mt-4 p-4` ->
        `mt-2 p-2.5`; input `py-5 text-4xl tracking-[0.9em]` -> `py-2.5 text-3xl tracking-[0.7em]` (inline `paddingLeft`
        matched to `0.7em` for centering); empty dots `gap-4`/`h-3 w-3` -> `gap-3`/`h-2.5 w-2.5`; keypad `mt-4 gap-2` ->
        `mt-3 gap-1.5`. Every keypad button keeps `min-h-[44px]`.
  - Test: `staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts` gains a Round 313 follow-up guard (no tall `py-5 text-4xl` input, compact
        `py-2.5 text-3xl` input, keypad `mt-3 gap-1.5`, `min-h-[44px]` kept, section `space-y-3` not `space-y-6`); existing
        Round 313 keypad + eyebrow guards preserved.
  - Verification: `node --test tests/renderer/staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts tests/renderer/staff-shift-closeout-inline-forms.test.ts`
        -> **15 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (StaffShiftModal.tsx,
        staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts, tracker) -> clean. No commits.
- **2nd follow-up correction — LIVE QA FAILED AGAIN; superseded by the 3rd follow-up below.** Live QA (Greek/dark,
    1282x802): the keypad (all 12 controls) was now visible, but Back + disabled Continue were STILL below the fold. Root
    cause: the keypad keys carried `min-h-[44px]` AND `py-3`, so the 44px floor was overshot (~52px/row), and the step
    repeated the "Enter PIN" label three times (eyebrow + h3 + inset label). Reclaimed the height (UI-only; touch targets,
    behavior, keyboard entry, no-auto-submit, glass/theme, localization all preserved):
  - **Keypad keys: dropped `py-3`** (kept `min-h-[44px] items-center`), so every key is exactly 44px (was ~52px) -- ~32px
        reclaimed across the four rows, touch target unchanged.
  - **Single PIN heading:** removed the duplicate eyebrow + inset `<label>` that both repeated "Enter PIN" (the `h3`
        heading + localized helper line remain); removed the input box's now-unneeded `mt-2`.
  - **Tighter shell:** the shared check-in scroll region `space-y-6` -> `space-y-4` and the stepper wrapper `mb-4` ->
        `mb-2` (consistent across check-in steps).
  - Tests: `staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts` gains a "Round 313 (2nd follow-up)" guard (keypad keeps `min-h-[44px]` but no
        `py-3` overshoot, the Enter PIN heading renders once, the helper + the 12 keypad controls stay wired);
        `staff-shift-closeout-inline-forms.test.ts` main-scroller assertion made spacing-tolerant (`space-y-\d+`) for the
        `space-y-6 -> space-y-4` change. Existing Round 313 + 1st-follow-up guards preserved.
  - Verification: `node --test tests/renderer/staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts tests/renderer/staff-shift-closeout-inline-forms.test.ts`
        -> **16 pass / 0 fail**; esbuild-style Staff Shift bundle (duration + role-labels + checkout-print +
        closeout-inline-forms) -> **39 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (StaffShiftModal.tsx,
        staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts, staff-shift-closeout-inline-forms.test.ts, tracker) -> clean. No commits.
  - Codex live QA REJECTED this pass: the keypad (all 12 controls) was visible, but Back + disabled Continue were STILL
        below the fold. Height-shaving alone was insufficient -- the real cause was structural (the footer's
        `sticky bottom-0` was trapped inside the animated step pane's transform), fixed in the 3rd follow-up below.
- **3rd follow-up correction (root-cause layout fix) — LIVE QA PASSED / COMPLETED.** Diagnosis: the check-in
    action footer (`<div className={checkInFooterClass}>` with `sticky bottom-0`) lived INSIDE each step body, which is
    rendered inside a framer-motion `<motion.div variants={...}>` pane. The pane's `transform` creates a CSS containing
    block for `position: sticky`, so the footer could only stick within the (content-height) pane -- it could never pin to
    the scroll viewport, which is why Back + Continue fell below the fold regardless of how much chrome was shaved. Fix
    (UI/layout only; handlers, gating, auth, keyboard entry, no-auto-submit, glass/theme, localization unchanged):
  - Extracted the three per-step footers (enter-pin / select-role / enter-cash) into one `renderCheckInFooter()` and
        removed them from the step bodies. The footer is now rendered as a SIBLING of `<AnimatePresence>` (outside the
        transformed pane), so its containing block is the scroll region and `sticky bottom-0` pins it to the bottom of the
        viewport -- Back + disabled Continue stay visible on first open. The footer JSX (Back button + Continue/skip/start
        actions + exact disabled gating) is copied verbatim, only relocated.
  - Tests: `staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts` -- the Round 313 keypad test now asserts the PIN body has only the Enter-key
        `handlePinSubmit` (no auto-submit) and finds the Continue submit + its `disabled={loading || enteredPin.length !== 4}`
        gating + the Back action in the hoisted `renderCheckInFooter`; a new "3rd follow-up" guard pins the footer
        out-of-pane (rendered after `</AnimatePresence>`, `checkInFooterClass` used exactly twice, never inside the
        pin-step body). 1st/2nd follow-up + eyebrow guards preserved.
  - Verification: `node --test tests/renderer/staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts tests/renderer/staff-shift-closeout-inline-forms.test.ts`
        -> **17 pass / 0 fail**; esbuild-style Staff Shift bundle (duration + role-labels + checkout-print +
        closeout-inline-forms) -> **40 pass / 0 fail**. `npm run type-check` -> clean. `git diff --check` (StaffShiftModal.tsx,
        staff-shift-role-labels.test.ts, tracker) -> clean. No commits.
  - Codex live QA on the running POS (Greek / dark, 1282x802) passed after HMR: first-open Ana Novak PIN step shows the
        selected-staff summary, PIN input, all 12 keypad controls, and the hoisted Back + disabled Continue footer in one
        view with no scroll and no visible native scrollbar. Safe interaction check: tapped two digits only, Continue
        remained disabled, then cleared the PIN back to empty; no shift was started and no submit/checkout/report action
        was run.

    sent because the live app did not expose a failing screen that needed code changes.

    no blue/cyan tokens), but live visual QA is still externally blocked by module availability rather than a UI defect.
- **Delivery.** Scrolled the module rail and opened the visible **Delivery Pro** route. It still maps to
    `DeliveryZonesPage` ("Ζώνες Διανομής"), not the older `DeliveryPage.tsx`, so Round 148 remains a route-exposure
    blocker. The reachable Delivery Zones no-zones branch visually passes: neutral blurry-glass surface, rounded cards,
    amber refresh, semantic green/yellow/emerald/red icons, centered empty state, and no visible native scrollbar. No

### Supervisor round 327 - Z report cashier-checkout blocker copy - TEST QA PASSED / CLAUDE IMPLEMENTED

- **Scope (Claude Code implementation supervised by Codex).** User asked why the close-day panel showed the cash drawer
    blocker like an error when the visible variance was 0.00. Codex identified that `ZReportModal.tsx` treated every
    unreconciled drawer as the same "cash drawer needs review" condition, so a cashier checkout/reconciliation blocker
    looked like a money discrepancy.
- **Implementation result.** Claude split the cash-drawer closeout messaging into three cases: unreconciled drawer with
    zero variance means cashier checkout is required, variance means drawer reconciliation is required, and both states
    mention checkout plus variance review. The primary amber action now uses a specific cash-drawer action label instead
    of the generic "Fix: Cash Drawer" fallback, while the close-day gate and blocker counts stay unchanged.
- **Safety guard re-check.** The state remains an amber warning/review state, not a hard error, and submit is still
    blocked until the existing closeout gates pass. Codex did not click refresh, print, export, blocker resolution,
    submit, close-day, or Z-report commit actions during this round.
- **Verification.** Supervisor reran `node --test tests\renderer\z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts tests\renderer\z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts`
    -> 20/20 pass, `npm run locale:parity` -> passed with 0 missing/extra keys for EN/EL/DE/FR/IT, `npm run type-check`
    -> passed, and `git diff --check` was clean for the touched Z report files.

### Supervisor round 340 - Sync recovery assistant cashier-facing IDs - STATIC-VERIFIED / CLAUDE IMPLEMENTED

    `organizationId` UUID when the friendly name was absent.

       (`recovery.center.thisTerminal`) instead of `Terminal {{terminalId}}`; `branchDisplayName` /
       `organizationDisplayName` prefer the friendly `branchName` / `organizationName` and otherwise fall back to a
       localized "This branch" / "This business" label (`recovery.center.branchFallback` /
       `organizationFallback`) -- never the raw `branchId` / `organizationId`.
    2. The raw `terminalId` remains only inside the persisted recovery action log
       (`actor.staffName: terminalContext?.terminalId`, recorded via `bridge.recovery.recordActionLog`), which is
       allowed diagnostics context and was left intact. No IDs were removed from internal recovery logic.

    `branchFallback`, `organizationFallback` across `en/el/de/fr/it` with locale parity intact.

    `scripts/run-parity-tests.mjs`) which fails if the visible summary region renders
    `terminalContext.terminalId/branchId/organizationId`, if the display-name derivation falls back to a raw id, if
    the `terminalContextLine` key reappears, or if the friendly keys are missing/untranslated in any POS locale. It
    also asserts the internal action-log `terminalId` + `recordActionLog` path is preserved. Guard teeth were
    proven by a temporary injected `terminalContext?.branchId` render (test failed as expected) then reverted.
- **Safety guard re-check.** Claude performed no recovery action in the app -- no restore, export, reset, refresh,
    sync, close-day, create snapshot, open folder, or any privileged/data-changing action. Static source/test work

### Supervisor round 342 - New Order Room check-in empty-state helper - LIVE QA PASSED / CLAUDE IMPLEMENTED

    δωμάτια για άφιξη") with no guidance, unlike Room Order which already explains the prerequisite.

    `src/renderer/components/modals/RoomStayWorkflowModals.tsx` (`RoomStaySelectorModal`). It explains in plain cashier
    language that check-in needs an existing room reservation, and that if none appear the cashier should create a
    reservation first and then return to check the guest in. The helper is gated to the check-in variant; the
    reservation variant keeps its single-line empty state. Glass/touch style preserved (centered icon + title + muted
    helper, no hover-only affordance, no native scrollbar).
- **Behavior preserved.** No change to room-order/reservation selection, room filtering, room status logic, backend
    calls, reservation creation, check-in submit, or order creation -- presentation/i18n only.
- **Locale changes.** Added `orderFlow.roomCheckinEmptyHint` across `en/el/de/fr/it` with locale parity intact. Greek
    uses arrival language ("Η άφιξη ... καταχωρίσετε την άφιξη"), never the Latin word "check-in".
- **Regression guard.** Extended `tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts` (Round 238 component + locale tests) to assert
    the helper is rendered (`orderFlow.roomCheckinEmptyHint`, the `emptyHelper` const, and the gated render), that the
    key exists + is Greek in every locale, that English names the reservation prerequisite, and that Greek uses arrival
    language with no Latin "check-in". Added the key to the `pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts` Round 236 required-key list;
    the existing Round 317 room* sweep already forbids Latin "check-in" in the new Greek value. Guard teeth proven by
    temporarily setting the Greek value to English (tests failed on "should be Greek" + Latin "check-in"), then
    reverted.

    static source/i18n/test work only.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts`
    -> 93/93 pass, `npm run locale:parity` -> pass, `npm run type-check` -> pass, `npm run build` -> pass (fresh

### Supervisor round 343 - Delivered/history order rows show a calm timestamp, not an age timer - LIVE QA PASSED / CLAUDE IMPLEMENTED

- **Scope (Claude Code implementation supervised by Codex).** Live QA on the delivered orders tab found a completed
    pickup row rendering a huge red elapsed timer ("4570λ") plus an age-based red left edge -- on a completed/cancelled
    history row this reads like a broken/raw urgency counter. Active orders still need the live age/urgency timer.
- **Implementation result (presentation only, `src/renderer/components/order/OrderCard.tsx`).**
    1. Terminal rows are detected via `isTerminalOrder = isDeliveredOrCompleted || isCancelledTerminal` (completed,
       delivered, and both `cancelled`/`canceled` spellings).
    2. The time line is now a ternary: terminal rows render a calm, neutral, display-capitalized timestamp
       (`{localized status verb} {HH:MM}`, e.g. "Completed 22:53" / "Ολοκληρώθηκε 22:53"; a short `dd/mm` date is
       prepended when not today) instead of `orders.time.minutes` + `getElapsedMinutes`. Active rows keep the live
       elapsed timer and `getTimeColorClass` urgency color unchanged.
    3. The left edge uses a guarded `leftEdgeColorClass`: active rows keep the age-based `getLeftEdgeColor`; terminal
       rows use a neutral edge, except cancelled keeps a calm status-based semantic red (`border-l-red-400/50`) that
       is never derived from elapsed age. `deliveryOlderThan40` now also excludes `canceled`.
    4. The terminal timestamp prefers `completed_at/completedAt -> delivered_at/deliveredAt ->
       cancelled_at/canceled_at -> updated_at/updatedAt`, falling back to `created_at` only if nothing else exists.
- **Behavior preserved.** No business logic, status actions, API calls, or order mutation; order type/payment icons
    untouched. The calm timestamp reuses existing `orders.status.*` labels, so no locale keys changed.
- **Regression guard.** Added three tests to `tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts`: terminal branch renders the
    timestamp and never `getElapsedMinutes`/`orders.time.minutes`/`getTimeColorClass`; the active branch still does;
    `getLeftEdgeColor` is only the active branch of `leftEdgeColorClass` and is no longer called directly in the row
    className; and the timestamp field-preference chain falls back to `created_at`. Guard teeth proven by temporarily
    restoring the old direct `getLeftEdgeColor` className call (edge test failed), then reverted.
- **Safety guard re-check.** No live order/status/sync/export/restore/close-day action performed; static
    source/test work only.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/order-card-currency.test.ts` -> 7/7 pass, `npm run type-check`
    -> pass, `npm run locale:parity` -> pass (no keys changed), `git diff --check` for the touched scope -> clean.
    Supervisor later live-verified the running Tauri app after reload: the delivered order row now renders a calm
    localized completion timestamp (`Ολοκληρώθηκε 25/06 22:49`) instead of the old giant elapsed-minute counter.

### Supervisor round 344 - Settings Data recovery raw IDs stale-binary guard - LIVE QA PASSED / CLAUDE SUPERVISED

    `data-recovery-technical-details` UI is gone, and the current bundle no longer contains the old recovery raw-ID
    labels/keys. The durable root cause was an old built Tauri executable / embedded frontend path: rebuilding `dist`
    alone does not refresh an already-built binary, so an old binary can keep serving the stale raw-ID recovery UI.
- **Live result.** Codex activated the running `the-small-pos.exe`, opened Settings -> Data -> Local Recovery, and
    verified the selected recovery detail now shows only snapshot counts, snapshot size, business day, restore-note
    copy, export/restore buttons, and `Αποθηκεύτηκε αναφορά υποστήριξης`. It does not show a recovery point id,
    terminal id, branch/substore UUID, organization UUID, or the old technical-details disclosure.

    folder, export selected, restore selected, reset, sync, print, close-day, or any data-changing action.

    when `dist/assets/*.js` exists, it must not contain `data-recovery-technical-details`, the Greek recovery-point-id
    label, `Recovery point ID`, or `recoveryPointId`, and it must contain `data-recovery-support-note`. This catches the
    exact stale embedded-frontend class before it can ship or be embedded again.
- **Verification.** Fresh renderer bundle `dist/assets/App-CcHU8zzI.js` passed the new dist guard. `node --test
    tests/renderer/recovery-panel-ui.test.ts` -> 6/6 pass; the combined recovery/order-card focused test run -> 13/13
    pass; `npm run type-check` -> pass; `git diff --check` for the touched scope -> clean. Live POS accessibility text
    also returned zero matches for the reported raw-id labels on Settings -> Data. The debug Tauri binary rebuild
    (`tauri build --debug`) compiled successfully but failed only at the final remove/replace step because the
    currently running `the-small-pos.exe` locks `target/debug/the-small-pos.exe`; closing the POS and
    rebuilding/relaunching is required to replace that binary.

### Supervisor round 345 - Order-type table icon optical weight - STATIC-VERIFIED / CLAUDE IMPLEMENTED

- **Scope (Claude Code implementation supervised by Codex).** Presentation-only polish. Supervisor live QA already
    confirmed the restored contracts are intact and must NOT be undone: the login `AnimatedBackground` yellow
    lava-lamp orb animation is restored, the navigation sidebar shows neutral inactive icons with selected-only neon,
    and the order dashboard inactive tabs are grey with selected-only neon. The remaining issue: the order-type
    chooser dine-in/table card uses the approved Flaticon table/chairs PNG (not cake/fork) but read optically
    lighter/smaller than the pickup bag / truck / bed / service icons.
- **Implementation result.**
    1. `src/renderer/components/icons/TableOrderIcon.tsx` gains an optional `opticalScale?: number`. Omitted keeps the
       unchanged default (mask-size `contain`), so the order-row table icon, table grid, and every other call site
       render byte-identically. When provided, the mask is painted at `opticalScale * 100%` inside the SAME box so the
       table/chairs glyph reads with the pickup bag's optical weight. Same local PNG asset, `currentColor` mask,
       `aria-hidden`, no network fetch, no asset replacement.
    2. Both order-type chooser table cards (`OrderDashboard.tsx`, `OrderFlow.tsx`) pass `opticalScale={1.12}` while

### Supervisor round 349 - Visible recovery raw IDs = stale embedded debug binary; closed + rebuilt + relaunched - RESOLVED / READY FOR VISUAL QA

- **Scope (Claude Code implementation supervised by Codex).** User live QA still saw Settings > Data > Local recovery
    selected snapshot details showing a raw recovery checkpoint id + branch UUID in the running desktop app. Treated as
    a shipped-artifact (stale embedded binary) verification issue, not a redesign.
- **Verification result (source/locale/dist all clean).** `RecoveryPanel.tsx` renders only the calm support note --
    `selectedPoint.id` appears solely in the export/restore handlers and `selectedPoint?.path` solely in the
    open-folder handler (never in the rendered selected detail); no `data-recovery-technical-details`, no
    `recoveryPointId`/`terminalId`/`branchId` rendered. All five locale files carry no `settings.recovery` technical
    keys. The rebuilt `dist/assets/App-DtsTYOyD.js` contains zero true-leak strings
    (`Αναγνωριστικό σημείου ανάκτησης`/`Recovery point ID`/`recoveryPointId`/`data-recovery-technical-details` ->
    none) and carries the `data-recovery-support-note` marker. `node --test recovery-panel-ui.test.ts
    settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> 50/50 pass; `npm run type-check` -> pass.
- **Root cause confirmed = stale embedded debug binary.** The running `target/debug/the-small-pos.exe` (PID 4213884,
    mtime 2026-06-26 04:50) embeds a pre-fix frontend. `npm run build` regenerated the clean dist, but
    `npm run pos:tauri:build -- --debug` compiled and then FAILED at the final replace with
    `error: failed to remove file ...target\debug\the-small-pos.exe -- Access is denied (os error 5)`: the running
    process locks its own binary. The debug exe timestamp is therefore UNCHANGED (still 04:50) -- it was NOT
    refreshed. A Tauri binary embeds the frontend at build time, so rebuilding dist never updates a running/locked
    binary.
- **Resolution (round 349 continuation, supervisor-authorized).** With explicit authorization to stop only the local
    debug POS process, the locking process was reconfirmed (PID 19580, ExecutablePath
    `...\pos-tauri\src-tauri\target\debug\the-small-pos.exe`) and stopped via `Stop-Process -Id 19580 -Force` (no
    unrelated apps touched). `npm run pos:tauri:build -- --debug` then SUCCEEDED ("Built application at:
    ...target\debug\the-small-pos.exe"); the debug exe timestamp changed from `2026-06-26 04:50` to
    `2026-06-26 05:26`, so the binary now embeds the clean dist (App-DtsTYOyD.js: support-note marker present, zero
    raw-ID strings). The rebuilt exe was relaunched (new PID 26468) so the POS window is visible. Visual QA is ready
    on the refreshed app: Settings > Data > Local recovery selected snapshot must now show the calm support-note
    detail with no recovery checkpoint id or branch UUID.
- **Safety guard re-check.** Only the local debug POS process was stopped (supervisor-authorized); no
    checkout/restore/export/close-day clicked, no production data touched, no destructive flow. Nothing staged or
    committed.
- **Verification.** Source/locale/dist scans clean (above); 50/50 focused guards pass; type-check pass; renderer +
    debug exe rebuilt clean; debug exe timestamp 04:50 -> 05:26 (refreshed); rebuilt window relaunched (PID 26468).
    `git diff --check` for the tracker -> clean.

### Supervisor round 350 - New Order room workflow regression guards - LIVE QA PASSED / CLAUDE GUARDS ADDED

- **Scope (Claude Code implementation supervised by Codex).** Codex live QA in the refreshed debug POS (Greek/dark,
    Dashboard > New Order) verified all three room branches: (1) the main order-type chooser's Room card opens the
    in-modal Room action chooser, NOT the standalone Rooms page; (2) the chooser has exactly three glass actions --
    Room Order (amber/orange), Check-in/Arrival (green), Create Reservation (purple) -- with no hover dependency;
    (3) Room Order opens a compact occupied/open-account room picker whose empty state explains a checked-in room
    with an active account is required first (not a broken empty modal); (4) Check-in opens a compact reserved-room
    picker whose empty state explains an existing reservation is required first; (5) Create Reservation opens a
    compact available-room picker inside the modal with floor chips (All/Floor 1/2/3) and no native scrollbar, and
    does NOT navigate to the Rooms/Housekeeping page; (6) selecting an available room opens the New Reservation form
    with the selected-room chip, required guest name, phone/dates/notes, red Cancel, green/disabled Create
    Reservation. (No data-changing action was clicked.)
- **Guards added (tests only, no production source changed).** New focused file
    `tests/renderer/room-stay-workflow-ui.test.ts` (8 Round 350 tests) source-reads
    `RoomStayWorkflowModals.tsx` + `OrderDashboard.tsx`: the 3-action chooser modal with its semantic colors +
    handlers (modal, not Rooms page); room actions route to the focused selector/form MODULES with no
    RoomsView/hubPreset (RoomsView only as the browse-only embedded hub tab); `RoomFloorChips` resets to all-floors
    on open, hides below 2 floors, and hides the native scrollbar (chip rail + room grid); room-order + check-in
    empty helpers exist and are localized (el Greek, != en); selector cards use `formatCurrency` + `translateRoomType`;
    the New Reservation and New Check-in forms have the selected-room chip, required guest name, red cancel, and an
    emerald Create that is disabled until a name is entered (+ submit fails closed on empty name); and the room
    workflow modals are touch-first (no hover/group-hover, no native title tooltips -- only LiquidGlassModal heading
    props). Guard teeth proven by temporarily breaking the floor-reset-on-open (`setFloorFilter('all')` -> `1`); the
    floor guard failed, then reverted (source net-unchanged).

    teeth-break was reverted, so no production source changed and no Tauri rebuild was needed.

### Supervisor round 351 - Z Report header day-chip mismatch - LIVE QA FIX / CLAUDE IMPLEMENTED

- **Scope (Claude Code implementation supervised by Codex).** Live QA (Greek/dark, refreshed debug POS) found the
    Z Report header showed the business date "Πέμπτη 25 Ιουνίου 2026" while the day chip said "Σήμερα" (system date
    Friday 2026-06-26): a past/open business day read as today, eroding trust in close-day decisions.
- **Root cause.** `ZReportModal` rendered `friendlyBusinessDate` from `resolvedBusinessDate = zReport?.date ||
    selectedDate`, but `isLiveDay` was derived from the INTENT flag `isUsingLiveDefaultDate && !lockDate`. When the
    report payload returned a different (past) date than the live-default `selectedDate`, the displayed date and the
    chip disagreed.
- **Fix (display-only, `ZReportModal.tsx`).** The chip is now derived from the SAME date shown in the header:
    `const isLiveDay = !lockDate && isBusinessDateValid && resolvedBusinessDate === localToday;` where
    `localToday = toLocalDateString(new Date())` and `isBusinessDateValid = !Number.isNaN(businessDateValue.getTime())`
    (`businessDateValue = parseLocalDateString(resolvedBusinessDate)`). It says "Today" (dayLive) only when the
    displayed date is valid and equals the terminal-local today; a returned past `zReport.date` shows "Past day"
    (dayHistorical) even if `isUsingLiveDefaultDate` is true. `lockDate`/submit/closeout logic and all five locales
    are unchanged (no copy change; existing dayLive/dayHistorical keys reused).
- **Regression guard.** `tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts` adds a Round 351 test: the
    `const isLiveDay =` line must NOT be `isUsingLiveDefaultDate && !lockDate`, and must be
    `!lockDate && isBusinessDateValid && resolvedBusinessDate === localToday` with
    `localToday = toLocalDateString(new Date())`; the header date (`friendlyBusinessDate`) and the chip both derive
    from `resolvedBusinessDate`/`isLiveDay`. The existing Round 322 header guard still passes. Teeth proven by
    temporarily restoring the buggy derivation (Round 351 failed), then re-applying the fix.
- **Safety guard re-check.** No Z-report submit/close-day, restore/export/reconcile, or order/reservation
    create/modify performed.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts
    tests/renderer/z-report-closeout-sync.test.ts` -> 22/22 pass; `npm run type-check` -> pass; `git diff --check`
    for the touched files -> clean. Production source changed, so the debug Tauri exe was rebuilt + relaunched (see
    timestamp/PID in the round report). Live header now shows "Past day" for a 2026-06-25 report on 2026-06-26.

### Supervisor round 352 - Payment Terminal config modal: disable Add until required fields valid - LIVE QA FIX / CLAUDE IMPLEMENTED

- **Scope (Claude Code implementation supervised by Codex).** Live QA (Settings > Card Machines > Payment Terminals
    > Add Terminal) found the green Add button active while required fields were empty (Terminal Name + the visible
    connection field, e.g. COM Port / MAC Address / IP Address): the cashier could tap Add and only then get a toast
    error -- sloppy on a touchscreen.
- **Fix (`TerminalConfigModal.tsx`).** Added a derived `requiredFieldsComplete = name.trim() && <connection field
    for the current connectionType>` (`btAddress` for bluetooth, `serialPort` for serial_usb, `networkIp` for
    network). The submit button is now `disabled={isSaving || !requiredFieldsComplete}` (was `isSaving` alone), and a
    calm on-palette amber inline hint (`data-terminal-required-hint`, key `ecr.config.missingRequired`) renders just
    above the footer buttons ONLY while incomplete + idle, so the footer never feels crowded. `handleSubmit` keeps
    its per-field validation toasts as a safety fallback. Visual contract preserved: glass modal, red Cancel, green
    Add/Save, shared POSGlassSwitch, no hover/group-hover/native title, no off-theme blue/indigo/violet/cyan/purple.
- **Locales.** Added `ecr.config.missingRequired` to all five POS locales (en/el/de/fr/it) -- no Greek-only
    hardcoding; locale parity passes.
- **Regression guard.** `payment-terminals-ui.test.ts` Round 352 test guards: the `requiredConnectionField` /
    `requiredFieldsComplete` derivation, the submit gate `isSaving || !requiredFieldsComplete` (and that
    `disabled={isSaving}` alone is gone), the localized amber hint (`data-terminal-required-hint` +
    `ecr.config.missingRequired`), handleSubmit's nameRequired fallback, and that the new key exists + is Greek (el
    != en) in all five locales. The earlier footer-shelf guards (Round 294 / Round 228) were updated for the new
    structure (shelf holds the glass; Cancel/Add live in an inner `flex justify-end gap-3` row). Teeth proven by
    temporarily reverting the gate to `disabled={isSaving}` (Round 352 failed), then re-applying.
- **Safety guard re-check.** No Add/Save/Restore/Export/Checkout/Close-day clicked; no orders/reservations/terminals/
    rooms/services/settings created or modified in the live POS.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/payment-terminals-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/terminal-card-tooltip-ui.test.ts
    tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts` -> 59/59 pass; `npm run type-check` -> pass; `npm run locale:parity`
    -> pass; `git diff --check` for touched files -> clean. Production source/locales changed, so the debug Tauri exe
    was rebuilt + relaunched (timestamp/PID in the round report). The live Add button is now disabled on first open
    (empty name + empty connection field) and enables once both are filled.

### Supervisor round 355 - Service appointment modal glass redesign - LIVE QA FIX / CLAUDE IMPLEMENTED

- **Scope (Claude Code implementation supervised by Codex).** Live QA: New Order -> Service opens the New
    Appointment modal (`AppointmentsView.tsx` `CreateAppointmentModalContent`). It worked but read too opaque +
    panel-heavy versus the order/room/Z glass modals. Focused redesign of the Create Appointment modal only.
- **Fix (presentation-only).** (1) The dialog shell is now a `motion.div` with a subtle scale+fade open animation
    (`initial opacity:0 scale:0.97 -> animate 1/1`, 0.2s ease) to match the shared liquid-glass modal feel. (2) The
    shell is more translucent in both themes -- dark `bg-zinc-950/80 -> /65`, light `bg-white/55 -> /45` (keeping
    `backdrop-blur-2xl`, `rounded-3xl`, ring, shadow). (3) The five form section panels are lighter glass --
    `bg-zinc-900/50 backdrop-blur-md -> /40 backdrop-blur-xl` (dark) and `bg-white/45 -> /35` with `border-white/55
    -> /50` (light); `backdrop-blur` is KEPT on each card so the Round-333 stacking-context fix (staff/service
    listbox over Notes) still holds. Cancel stays semantic red, Create stays semantic green with the clear
    disabled state (gated on staff + service + start time).
- **Behavior preserved.** Translations, accessibility (role=dialog / aria-modal / aria-labelledby / titleId),
    GlassSelect listbox + capture-phase Escape, topmost-[role=dialog] Escape close-only, availability checks,
    `openCreateSignal` hub flow, form fields/validation, and backend calls are all unchanged. Hidden-scrollbar
    pattern (`scrollbar-hide`) kept; no native scrollbars; buttons stay centered; no hover/native-title. No
    appointment/order/reservation was created or submitted during checks.
- **Regression guard.** `tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts`: Round 240/255/278/279 updated to the new
    translucent shell/card tokens; new Round 355 test guards the motion open animation, the rounded/ringed
    translucent shell, red Cancel + green Create classes, the disabled-until-valid Create, and the preserved
    openCreateSignal path + touch-first invariants. Teeth proven by neutralizing the open animation (Round 355
    failed), then reverting.
- **Safety guard re-check.** No Add/Save/Restore/Export/Checkout/Close-day clicked; no orders/reservations/
    terminals/rooms/services/settings created or modified. Only the local debug process was rebuilt/relaunched.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts` -> 32/32 pass; `npm run type-check`
    -> pass; `git diff --check` for touched files -> clean. Production source changed, so the debug Tauri exe was
    rebuilt + relaunched (timestamp/PID in the round report).

### Supervisor round 356 - Codex direct pass after Claude rate limit - VERIFIED

- **Scope (Codex implementation, no Claude available).** Finished the small defects still visible after the prior
    supervised passes: the service appointment modal still read too opaque in light mode, Print Queue still exposed
    technical job/printer identifiers to staff, the reservation create modal could still be submitted with incomplete
    fields, and Housekeeping light-theme cards/buttons were too low-contrast.
- **Fixes.** `AppointmentsView.tsx` light create modal now uses a lighter glass scrim and more transparent shell/card
    surfaces while keeping the blur, ring, red Cancel, green Create, and modal open animation. `PrintQueuePanel.tsx`
    now converts technical UUID/long identifiers into staff-readable labels, hides printer profile IDs, and maps
    hardware/profile-resolution errors to localized operator instructions, with locale keys added for English, Greek,
    German, French, and Italian. `ReservationsView.tsx` create/service modals now share
    a consistent translucent glass shell and the Create action is disabled until the required fields are ready.
    `HousekeepingView.tsx` light cards/controls/actions now use readable white glass surfaces with stronger semantic
    amber/emerald/purple action buttons.
- **Behavior preserved.** No order, appointment, reservation, room, printer, recovery, close-day, or settings-save
    action was submitted. These changes are presentation/guard-copy only; existing data fetching and submit handlers
    were left intact.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/appointments-view-i18n.test.ts tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts
    tests/renderer/reservations-view-i18n.test.ts tests/renderer/housekeeping-view-i18n.test.ts
    tests/renderer/vertical-modal-portals.test.ts` -> 129/129 pass; `npm run type-check` -> pass; `npm run build` ->
    pass with the existing Vite chunk-size/dynamic-import warnings only; `git diff --check` over touched source,
    locales, and tests -> clean. Live POS visual QA passed for New Appointment glass, Reservation create glass/disabled
    Create, Housekeeping light readability, Settings Data raw-ID cleanup, and Printer Queue localized issue copy.

### Supervisor round 360 - New Order chooser card accents + table icon recovery - LIVE QA PASSED

- **Finding (founder screenshot, Greek/light).** In the New Order chooser, the Table card's supplied table/chair
    icon disappeared, the Delivery truck icon was still yellow while the other card icons were white, and Table/Room/
    Service did not carry the same visible accent-card treatment as Delivery/Pickup.
- **Fix.** `TableOrderIcon.tsx` now uses the founder-supplied transparent PNG as a `currentColor` alpha mask,
    replacing the previous traced SVG so the exact requested table/chair artwork appears in the POS. In
    `OrderDashboard.tsx` and `OrderFlow.tsx`, Delivery/Pickup/Table cards now use explicit arbitrary accent
    borders/backgrounds (`#facc15`, `#34d399`, `#60a5fa`) that bypass the neutral Tailwind remap, the Delivery
    truck glyph is white, and the dashboard Room/Service cards get matching purple/cyan accent surfaces
    (`#a855f7`, `#22d3ee`). Icons remain white; color lives on the card shell and title.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/orders-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/pos-greek-flow-i18n.test.ts`
    -> 97/97 pass; `npm run type-check` -> pass; `git diff --check` over touched files -> clean. Live POS visual QA
    passed: Table icon is visible, Delivery icon is white, and all five chooser cards have clear accent borders/tints.

### Supervisor round 361 - Table icon optical size correction - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder feedback).** The founder-supplied table/chair PNG was correct artwork, but it rendered smaller
    than the neighboring order-type icons because most of its 512px canvas is transparent vertical padding.
- **Fix.** The bundled table PNG was normalized to white-on-transparent for reliable CSS masking, then
    `TableOrderIcon.tsx` keeps the caller's stable icon footprint and scales the masked PNG with
    `transform: scale(...)` plus `overflow: visible`. The wide table/chair mark now matches the bag/bed/calendar
    icons by apparent height without clipping. `OrderDashboard.tsx` and `OrderFlow.tsx` both use
    `opticalScale={1.62}` for the large Table card.
- **Verification.** Updated `orders-page-ui.test.ts` to lock the 1.62 chooser scale and the non-clipping transform
    mask implementation.

### Supervisor round 362 - Transparent New Order chooser trial - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder request).** The founder asked to try the New Order window fully transparent so clicking New
    Order shows only the order-type buttons, without the large grey modal container.
- **Fix.** `OrderDashboard.tsx` and `OrderFlow.tsx` now add `order-type-transparent-modal` and zero content padding
    only on the New Order chooser. `glassmorphism.css` uses that scoped class to remove the glass shell background,
    border, shadow, header divider, and title, leaving the five floating colored order-type
    cards plus a small floating close button.
- **Scope guard.** This is a trial scoped to the order-type chooser. Settings, payment, customer, room, Z-report,
    and all other `LiquidGlassModal` users keep their existing blurry glass shells.

### Supervisor round 363 - Transparent chooser with blurred page backdrop - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder correction).** The first transparent-shell trial made the New Order container disappear, but
    also removed the page blur, so the background stayed sharp behind the floating order-type cards.
- **Fix.** The New Order chooser now separates the layers correctly: the modal shell remains fully transparent,
    while the invisible backdrop uses `blur(18px) saturate(1.08)` with only a near-clear tint. This keeps the
    "only the buttons" look while still softening the dashboard behind it.
- **Verification.** `orders-page-ui.test.ts` now asserts the scoped chooser backdrop keeps blur in both theme
    paths while the shell itself stays transparent with no border or shadow.

### Supervisor round 364 - Transparent chooser light-mode contrast + no close X - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder screenshot QA).** Dark theme looked right, but light theme washed out the order-type cards
    because pale translucent cards sat over a pale blurred dashboard. The floating close X also looked like extra
    window chrome in the "only the buttons" trial.
- **Fix.** The transparent New Order chooser now uses stronger light-mode backdrop dim/blur and smoky per-card
    accent fills so white icons/descriptions stay readable. Dark theme explicitly keeps the previous lighter neon
    card treatment the founder liked. The close X is hidden only for this chooser; backdrop tap and Escape remain.
- **Verification.** `orders-page-ui.test.ts` guards the light/dark split, the per-card style hooks, and the hidden
    chooser close button.

### Supervisor round 365 - Light chooser dark icon/subtitle ink - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder screenshot QA).** Light theme card bodies were better after the smoky fills, but the white
    icons and subtitle text still lacked enough contrast against pale yellow/green/blue glass.
- **Fix.** The transparent New Order chooser now paints light-theme card icons near-black and subtitles dark grey
    while preserving colored titles. Dark theme explicitly resets icons and subtitles to the previous white layer.
- **Verification.** `orders-page-ui.test.ts` guards the light-mode dark ink and dark-mode reset for the chooser.

### Supervisor round 366 - Light chooser ink fallback for already-running DOM - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder screenshot QA).** The close X was gone, but icons/subtitles were still white in the running
    light-theme desktop window. Root cause: the window had picked up the CSS-level close-button rule, but its
    chooser buttons were still the older DOM without the new `data-order-type-card` hooks.
- **Fix.** Added a late stylesheet fallback targeting the existing transparent chooser buttons directly:
    `button[type="button"] svg`, the bitmap-mask `span[aria-hidden="true"]`, and subtitle `p` nodes. Light theme
    now forces dark ink through that older DOM too; dark theme still resets to white.
- **Verification.** `orders-page-ui.test.ts` now guards both the data-hook path and the late non-data fallback.

### Supervisor round 367 - Settings nav icon chips solid yellow + black strokes - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder screenshot QA).** Settings left-rail icon wrappers still used translucent amber chips with
    yellow icon strokes, which looked weak against the glass Settings surface.
- **Fix.** `ConnectionSettingsModal.tsx` now renders all Settings left-rail icon chips as full yellow tiles with
    black lucide strokes. The row active state remains separate, so selection still reads through the row shell.
- **Verification.** `settings-modal-ui.test.ts` guards all nine nav icons as black-stroke icons and the chip as
    full yellow, with the old translucent yellow chip classes forbidden in that rail.

### Supervisor round 368 - Settings page header icon chips solid yellow + black strokes - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder screenshot QA).** Settings detail pages still had the older translucent yellow icon chips in
    their section headers, so the Connection and This Register page headers did not match the newly corrected left rail.
- **Fix.** The shared `sectionHeader(...)` helper now uses the same full yellow chip treatment with black icon ink,
    and all eight Settings detail header icons were switched from yellow strokes to black strokes.
- **Verification.** `settings-modal-ui.test.ts` now guards the detail-header helper and all eight page-header icons,
    forbidding the old translucent yellow chip classes and the old yellow-stroke icon class.

### Supervisor round 369 - Missing hero titles on Appointments / Housekeeping / Reservations - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder screenshot QA).** Housekeeping opened directly into stat cards, and the booking hub screenshot
    opened directly into tabs; both lacked the standard visible page title at the top of the rounded page surface.
    The standalone Appointments page had the same missing-title pattern.
- **Fix.** Added a first-row `h1` using the existing localized navigation labels to `AppointmentsView`,
    `HousekeepingView`, and `ReservationsView`. Existing stats, filters, tabs, create/refresh handlers, and module
    gates were left unchanged.
- **Verification.** `appointments-view-i18n.test.ts`, `housekeeping-view-i18n.test.ts`, and
    `reservations-view-i18n.test.ts` now guard that each title renders above its controls.

### Supervisor round 370 - Slim app frame restores update visibility + window controls - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder correction).** Removing the old custom title/menu row also removed the persistent place where
  operators could see update status and reach window controls. The app is intentionally Tauri-frameless
  (`decorations: false`), so native Windows controls will not appear by themselves.
- **Fix.** Added `AppWindowFrame.tsx`: a 40px touch-first custom frame with a draggable region, compact update chip
  wired to the existing `useAutoUpdater` / `UpdateDialog`, and minimize / maximize / fullscreen / close controls
  using the existing bridge window commands. No desktop File/Edit/View menu row was reintroduced, and the frame
  uses tap feedback instead of hover styling.
- **Layout correction.** `FullscreenAwareLayout` now reserves the frame and constrains child content to the remaining
  height. Login, onboarding, new-order, and `RefactoredMainLayout` were adjusted away from full-viewport height
  assumptions so the POS content does not clip below the new frame.
- **Verification.** `custom-titlebar-ui.test.ts` was rewritten to guard the actual shell contract: frameless Tauri
  config, slim app frame, update chip wiring on login and main POS routes, native bridge window controls, no `hover:`
  or native `title=`, and main-layout height under the frame.

### Supervisor round 371 - Transparent draggable frame refinement - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder screenshot QA).** The first restored frame read too much like a toolbar: placeholder `S` logo,
  unnecessary app title, always-visible "up to date" chip, boxed window controls, and a visible white frame strip.
- **Fix.** `AppWindowFrame.tsx` now uses the real app logo asset (`logo-black` / `logo-white`) with no title text,
  keeps the bar background fully transparent, and overlays the frame on top of the app instead of reserving a white
  strip. The empty bar surface stays a Tauri drag region so click-hold can move the window. Minimize / maximize /
  fullscreen / close are bare icon hit targets with no box wrappers; the fullscreen icon now uses the same simple
  screen-size icon family as the other window controls.
- **Update behavior.** The frame update chip is centered and only renders for actionable update states: available,
  downloading, downloaded, install-pending, installing, or an update-specific error. It no longer shows "up to date" or
  background checking in the frame, and the update chip remains visible after an update is found until the update is
  applied/resolved.
- **Verification.** `custom-titlebar-ui.test.ts` now guards the real logo, transparent drag bar, centered conditional
  update chip, no placeholder `S` / app title text, bare icon controls, no hover, and no native tooltip attributes.

### Supervisor round 372 - Shell loading / shift-blocker hover cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static shell audit).** `App.tsx` still used blue loading spinners in the configuration and initial-session
  loading paths. `RefactoredMainLayout.tsx` still used a blue lazy-view spinner and hover-only color changes on the
  pending-Z-report / no-active-shift blocker actions.
- **Fix.** Shell loading spinners now use the app amber accent. The blocker action buttons now use `active:` tap
  feedback instead of `hover:` and keep semantic colors: amber for completing the pending Z report, neutral for
  check-in from the pending-Z-report choice, and red for the no-active-shift blocker.
- **Verification.** `custom-titlebar-ui.test.ts` now guards these shell paths: no blue spinner tokens, no `hover:bg-`
  in `RefactoredMainLayout`, and the expected amber/neutral/red active states.

### Supervisor round 373 - Transparent frame native drag behavior - COMPLETED

- **Finding (Computer Use live QA).** The transparent frame looked correct in the running Tauri app, but click-hold
  on the empty top bar did not move the window. The frame only had `data-tauri-drag-region`; this Windows/Tauri build
  needs the official renderer drag API called directly from the pointer event.
- **Fix.** `AppWindowFrame` now calls `getCurrentWindow().startDragging()` from pointer-down on the transparent bar.
  Update chip and window controls are marked as no-drag so their tap actions remain clean.
- **Verification.** Computer Use confirmed the pre-fix drag failure (`dx: 0`, `dy: 0`) and the post-fix live drag
  success (`dx: -20`, `dy: -2`). `custom-titlebar-ui.test.ts` now guards the direct Tauri drag API, frame handler,
  and no-drag control guards. `npm run type-check`, `cargo check`, and `npm run build` passed.

### Supervisor round 374 - Caller-ID toast + dashboard card hover/palette cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** `CallerIdPopup.tsx` still had hover-only color changes and a blue unknown-caller
  Add Customer action. The shared `DashboardCard` still used blue/purple visual mappings plus hover opacity/scale and a
  blue focus ring. These are real operator surfaces: caller-ID notifications and product/service/hotel dashboard cards.
- **Fix.** Caller-ID toast actions now use tap feedback, amber focus rings, amber for Add Customer, green for Start Order,
  and an accessible close button label. `DashboardCard` keeps the public `blue`/`purple` variant names for callers, but
  maps them to amber and zinc visuals, uses smoother `rounded-2xl` cards, and replaces hover animation with active tap
  feedback plus an amber focus ring.
- **Verification.** Added `tests/renderer/callerid-dashboard-ui.test.ts` to guard no `hover:` / blue tokens on the
  caller-ID popup and no hover / blue-purple mappings on `DashboardCard`. `node --test
  tests/renderer/callerid-dashboard-ui.test.ts`, `npm run type-check`, scoped `rg`, and scoped `git diff --check`
  passed. `npm run build` also passed with the existing Vite chunk-size/dynamic-import warnings only.

### Supervisor round 375 - Update dialog palette + touch cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** `UpdateDialog.tsx`, the modal opened from the restored app-frame update chip,
  still used cyan accents for checking/available/downloading/up-to-date states and hover-only styling on the install
  ready buttons. That left the update affordance visually outside the app's amber/grey/green/red POS palette.
- **Fix.** Checking spinner, available icon, and download progress now use amber. Up-to-date uses green success semantics.
  Install Now remains green, Install Later remains neutral grey, and both use active tap feedback, smooth `rounded-2xl`
  corners, and visible focus rings instead of hover state.
- **Verification.** Extended `tests/renderer/update-changelog-ui.test.ts` to guard no `hover:`, no cyan/blue tokens,
  amber update states, green/neutral action buttons, rounded corners, and focus rings. `rg` and
  `node --test tests/renderer/update-changelog-ui.test.ts` passed. `npm run type-check`, scoped `git diff --check`,
  and `npm run build` also passed; build emitted only the existing Vite chunk-size/dynamic-import warnings.

### Supervisor round 376 - Secondary update notification/progress surfaces aligned - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** The main `UpdateDialog` was on-palette after round 375, but the sibling update
  surfaces still drifted: `UpdateNotification` used a cyan available icon, `UpdateProgressModal` used a cyan progress
  bar, and `UpdateToast` used a cyan filled toast with no tap/focus polish.
- **Fix.** `UpdateNotification` now uses amber for available-update emphasis, `UpdateProgressModal` uses an amber
  progress bar, and `UpdateToast` is a dark blurry glass card with amber icon/border, rounded-2xl shape, active tap
  feedback, and an amber focus ring. Space key handling now prevents page scroll before opening the dialog.
- **Verification.** Extended `tests/renderer/update-changelog-ui.test.ts` to cover these secondary update surfaces.
  Scoped `rg "hover:|cyan-"` across `UpdateDialog.tsx` and `components/updates` found no matches, and
  `node --test tests/renderer/update-changelog-ui.test.ts` passed. `npm run type-check`, scoped
  `git diff --check`, and `npm run build` also passed; build emitted only the existing Vite chunk-size/dynamic-import
  warnings.

### Supervisor round 377 - Transparent app frame drag + theme logo mapping - COMPLETED

- **Finding (live supervisor QA).** The restored transparent app frame looked right, but dragging from the top strip did
  not move the Tauri window reliably. The light theme also used the dark logo, opposite of the founder's requested
  mapping.
- **Fix.** The frame now uses `logo-black.png` in dark theme and `logo-white.png` in light theme. Native Tauri
  `data-tauri-drag-region` handling was removed from the custom bar because it intercepted pointer delivery. The frame
  now owns a deterministic manual pointer-drag path: pointer down reads the native window position, pointer movement
  calls typed `window.getPosition` / `window.setPosition` bridge commands, and Rust applies `window.set_position`.
  The existing `window.startDrag` command remains as a fallback if position lookup fails.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/custom-titlebar-ui.test.ts`, `npm run type-check`, `cargo build`,
  scoped `git diff --check`, and `npm run build` passed. Computer Use live verification on the rebuilt Tauri app:
  a `-80px` drag in the transparent bar moved the window `-80px`; a `-50px` drag from the very top strip moved the
  window `-50px`. Final passive capture confirmed the white logo in light theme and the transparent bar/controls.

### Supervisor round 378 - Delivery validation override UI touch/glass cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** `DeliveryValidationComponent` still had hover-only button states, blue focus and
  validation accents, orange warning accents, small-radius controls, and an opaque override modal card. This contradicted
  the touchscreen-first and blurry-glass modal rules.
- **Fix.** Address validation now uses amber focus/validation states, green/red semantics where appropriate, rounded
  glass result panels, and active tap feedback instead of hover. The override modal now portals into a darker blurred
  backdrop with a translucent glass card, amber confirm/warning treatment, and a red cancel action.
- **Verification.** Added `tests/renderer/delivery-validation-component-ui.test.ts` and updated the portal guard in
  `tests/renderer/inline-overlay-portals.test.ts`. `node --test tests/renderer/delivery-validation-component-ui.test.ts
  tests/renderer/inline-overlay-portals.test.ts`, `npm run type-check`, scoped `git diff --check`, and scoped `rg` for
  `hover:|blue-|cyan-|purple-|orange-|focus:ring-blue|rounded-lg` all passed.

### Supervisor round 379 - Shared ConfirmDialog amber/touch cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** The shared `ConfirmDialog` still used cyan info styling, yellow warning tokens,
  a hover-only checkbox row, cyan checkbox focus, and `rounded-lg` geometry. Because this dialog is reused by settings,
  staff, expense, recovery, and customer flows, that leak affected multiple modals.
- **Fix.** Info and warning states now use amber glass icon chips, the confirmation checkbox uses amber focus/checked
  color, and the checkbox row has a rounded-2xl active tap state instead of hover. The dialog remains on the shared
  `LiquidGlassModal` shell.
- **Verification.** Added `tests/renderer/confirm-dialog-ui.test.ts`. `node --test
  tests/renderer/confirm-dialog-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/customer-search-modal-ui.test.ts
  tests/renderer/settings-modal-ui.test.ts`, `npm run type-check`, scoped `git diff --check`, and scoped `rg` for

### Supervisor round 380 - Shared UI primitive palette/touch cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** Several shared primitives still leaked the old blue/cyan interaction language
  into many workflows: both loading spinners used blue defaults, `ErrorAlert` used blue/yellow alert tones plus hover-only
  detail/close controls, `ReceiptScaleSlider` used a blue reset action and blue range fill, `PulseAnimation` defaulted to
  blue rings, and `UnsettledPaymentBlockersPanel` used sky/fuchsia/cyan payment badges and hover-only resolve buttons.

  tests/renderer/shared-ui-primitives-palette.test.ts tests/renderer/z-report-action-rail-scroll.test.ts

### Supervisor round 384 - Recovery center panel palette/touch cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** The larger `RecoveryCenterPanel` still used sky/blue informational surfaces,
  orange error/blocker cards, and hover-only action feedback. This left the guided recovery assistant inconsistent with
  the cleaned modal shell and the POS touch rules.

### Supervisor round 385 - App frame drag and theme logo correction - COMPLETED

- **Finding (hands-on founder feedback).** The transparent custom frame looked right, but click-hold on the top bar did
  not reliably move the Tauri window. The frame code also used asset names that made the requested dark-theme and
  light-theme logo mapping harder to audit.
- **Fix.** The frame now uses a fixed transparent top hit layer with explicit pointer/z-index styling, Tauri's official
  `getCurrentWindow().startDragging()` API, and a mouse-down fallback so Windows/WebView input paths both start drag.
  The update pill and window controls remain excluded from drag. Logo imports are explicit: dark theme uses the dark app
  logo and light theme uses the white app logo.
- **Verification.** Extended `tests/renderer/custom-titlebar-ui.test.ts` to assert native drag, mouse fallback, controls
  staying non-draggable, and explicit theme logo mapping. Computer Use live QA confirmed dragging the top strip moved the
  running POS window from `(364, 227)` to `(384, 237)`, confirmed the dark app logo in dark theme, and restored light
  theme with the white app logo.

### Supervisor round 386 - Upgrade prompt modal amber glass cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** `UpgradePromptModal` still used the old blue-to-purple lock badge and
  blue/purple plan-comparison panel, plus broken mojibake emoji text in console messages. That made locked navigation
  prompts feel separate from the amber/neutral module-gate surfaces already cleaned in round 382.
- **Fix.** The modal now uses amber/neutral glass styling for the lock tile and plan comparison, keeps green benefit
  checks as semantic success, removes the mojibake console text, and preserves the same module metadata fallback,
  required-plan copy, alerts, close-on-backdrop/escape behavior, and CTA button wiring.
- **Verification.** Added `tests/renderer/upgrade-prompt-modal-ui.test.ts` to guard against old blue/purple/hover
  styling and to keep the behavior wiring covered.

### Supervisor round 387 - Tables dashboard and reservation warning palette cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** The standalone `TablesDashboard` still leaked the old blue/purple/orange
  utility palette into selected view/floor/status controls, occupied-table chips, and maintenance accents. The
  `ReservationForm` existing-reservations warning also used blue admin-style info chrome. A focused test run exposed a
  related mounted `OrderDashboard` regression where cleaning/out-of-service cards had an action but no simple
  explanatory status line.
- **Fix.** Tables selected controls and occupied accents now use yellow/amber; maintenance uses red; preparing uses
  amber; touched table controls moved from small `rounded-lg` corners to smoother `rounded-xl`/`rounded-2xl`. The
  reservation warning is amber glass. `OrderDashboard` cleaning/out-of-service cards now show a compact amber
  explanation above the action, keeping the card easy to understand without restoring the old bulky duplicate block.
- **Verification.** Added `tests/renderer/table-dashboard-reservation-palette-ui.test.ts`. `node --test
  tests/renderer/table-dashboard-reservation-palette-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/tables-cleaning-card-ui.test.ts
  tests/renderer/table-grid-scroll-reset.test.ts tests/renderer/reservation-form-ui.test.ts`, source-only palette/mojibake
  grep on `TablesDashboard.tsx` and `ReservationForm.tsx`, and scoped `git diff --check` passed.

### Supervisor round 388 - Kitchen display touch/palette cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (static supervisor audit).** `KitchenDisplayPage` still carried native `title=` tooltips on icon controls,
  hover-only feedback on KDS actions, and old blue/cyan/purple/pink/orange accents across order type labels, station
  icons, external-display buttons, stats, and retry actions. The display-page regression test still asserted those old
  hover/blue/cyan patterns instead of guarding the touchscreen rules.
- **Fix.** KDS controls now expose aria labels only and use active press feedback. Order type and station accents moved
  to yellow/amber/neutral plus semantic green/red. The pending bump, retry, external display, station filter, stats,
  item, and note surfaces now use smoother rounded corners and on-palette styling. Added localized labels for view,
  sound, and live-refresh toggles in English, Greek, German, French, and Italian.
- **Verification.** Updated `tests/renderer/display-pages-ui.test.ts`. `node --test tests/renderer/display-pages-ui.test.ts`,
  source-only grep for `title=|hover:|blue-|cyan-|purple-|pink-|indigo-|orange-|rounded-lg|mojibake` on
  `KitchenDisplayPage.tsx`, JSON parsing for all five POS locale files, and scoped `git diff --check` passed.

### Supervisor round 389 - App frame drag and theme logo correction - COMPLETED

- **Finding (founder live QA).** The transparent app frame looked visually correct, but click-hold on the top strip
  still did not move the unmaximized Tauri window. Live Computer Use measurement confirmed the failure twice
  (`deltaX: 0`, `deltaY: 0`). The frame also needed an explicit guard that dark theme uses the dark app logo asset and
  light theme uses the white app logo asset.
- **Fix.** `AppWindowFrame` now keeps the real app logo assets (`logo-black.png` for dark theme, `logo-white.png` for
  light theme), removes the Tauri `data-tauri-drag-region` marker that blocked reliable input delivery, keeps direct
  pointer/mouse handlers on the transparent drag layer, and adds `WebkitAppRegion: 'drag'` so Windows WebView moves the
  frameless window natively. The update chip remains centered and conditional; window controls remain unwrapped.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/custom-titlebar-ui.test.ts`, `npm run type-check`, scoped
  `git diff --check`, source-only grep for removed native/title/hover tokens, and live Computer Use drag verification
  passed. Final live drag moved the window by `deltaX: -120`, `deltaY: 70`, then the window was restored to its previous
  position.

### Supervisor round 390 - Settings locked behind staff login - COMPLETED

- **Finding (live/source supervisor audit).** The login screen still rendered a pre-login connection/settings button and
  `App.tsx` mounted `ConnectionSettingsModal` in the unauthenticated branch. That made settings accessible before staff
  authentication, contrary to the current security/UX direction.
- **Fix.** `LoginPage` no longer accepts or renders `onOpenSettings`; the unauthenticated app branch renders only the
  login screen, update dialog, toaster, and theme toggle. `openConnectionSettings()` now refuses to open while `user` is
  null, so event-driven settings requests are also ignored before login. Authenticated Settings access through the
  sidebar/main shell is unchanged.
- **Verification.** `node --test tests/renderer/login-page-ui.test.ts tests/renderer/custom-titlebar-ui.test.ts`,
  `npm run type-check`, scoped `git diff --check`, and live Computer Use QA passed. The live login screen showed only the
  theme toggle in the top-right and no settings shortcut; keypad login with `111111` returned to the authenticated POS
  shell with Settings available only after login.

### Supervisor round 400 - Customer order history modal hover/blue cleanup - COMPLETED

- **Finding (source audit).** The checklist had `CustomerOrderHistoryModal` marked as audited, but the current modal still
  used hover-only row feedback plus blue/purple header, spinner, status, and close-button accents.
- **Fix.** `CustomerOrderHistoryModal.tsx` now uses a yellow/black header icon, yellow loading spinner, yellow glass close
  action, smooth `rounded-2xl` row/icon surfaces, active tap feedback, and semantic status colors (yellow/amber/green/red)
  with no blue/purple styling. The order rows are real `<button>` press targets instead of clickable card divs.
- **Verification.** Added `tests/renderer/customer-order-history-modal-ui.test.ts`; focused test, source grep for hover /
  blue / purple / small-radius tokens, scoped `git diff --check`, and `npm run type-check` passed.

### Supervisor round 403 - Product catalog modal touch/palette correction - COMPLETED

- **Finding (source audit).** `ProductCatalogModal` was marked audited, but the current modal still used hover-only
  category/product/cart controls, blue selected tabs/icons/focus ring, and smaller `rounded-lg`/`rounded-xl` surfaces.
- **Fix.** Category tabs now use yellow selected state with active tap feedback, product cards use smooth `rounded-2xl`
  press styling, product icons use yellow, cart remove/quantity buttons use active feedback, the manual delivery fee input
  uses a yellow focus ring, and checkout keeps semantic green with tap feedback. Cart, offer, delivery-fee, payment, and
  checkout failure behavior were left unchanged.
- **Verification.** Added `tests/renderer/product-catalog-modal-ui.test.ts`; focused UI test plus the existing checkout
